title: EEOC: Employer rescinded job offer based on ADHD meds, violated ADA
summary: The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) filed a lawsuit against International Paper Co., alleging a violation of the Americans with Disabilities Act. The company rescinded a job offer to an applicant with ADHD because he tested positive for amphetamines due to prescribed Adderall, despite being informed of the medication. This case highlights the EEOC's stance against discriminating based on legal prescription drug use, even in permissible drug tests, when the medication treats a disability and doesn't affect job performance.

title: Tax Breaks Cushion Tesla’s Texas Landing
summary: Tesla received approximately $64 million in state and local tax breaks to build its Giga Texas factory in Austin, sparking debate over the efficacy of such incentives in generating promised economic benefits and jobs. The article highlights concerns that communities often give up more than they gain from these deals, drawing parallels to Tesla's Buffalo factory where job creation targets were not met. Community groups and local residents have raised issues regarding the deal's lack of oversight, potential for wage theft and unsafe conditions, and the challenge of local hiring due to language barriers, leading some local lawmakers to question the wisdom of future incentive deals.

title: The Trusted Yelp ‘Elite’ Reviewers Who Sell Their Reviews for Cash
summary: An investigation has revealed that some Yelp Elite members, who are designated as more trustworthy reviewers, are selling their positive reviews for cash. Former federal investigator Kay Dean uncovered instances where Elite members posted fake reviews for businesses like Regenics, receiving payment from third parties. This practice undermines Yelp's system of trust and distorts the marketplace, benefiting businesses that pay for reviews over those that don't, although Yelp states that only a small percentage of Elite reviewers have been tied to such activities and the company actively investigates and removes fraudulent accounts.

title: Marcelo Affonso and Rebecca Weekly: why we joined Cloudflare
summary: This article features Marcelo Affonso (VP of Infrastructure Operations) and Rebecca Weekly (VP of Hardware Systems) discussing their reasons for joining Cloudflare. They share their professional journeys, highlight Cloudflare's mission to build a better Internet through security, speed, and privacy, and express excitement about expanding Cloudflare's global infrastructure and hardware innovation in a "new golden age of computer architecture."

title: Amazon Signs Deal to Bring One Championship MMA Fights to Prime
summary: Amazon has secured a five-year deal with One Championship to exclusively stream mixed martial arts fights on Prime Video in the U.S. and Canada. The agreement will bring at least 12 live prime-time events annually, marking Amazon's continued expansion into live sports content. Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.

title: Amazon Freight now offers money back through American Express
summary: Amazon Freight has partnered with American Express to offer cash back to freight brokers and forwarders. Eligible Amazon Business American Express cardholders can receive 3% back per load, while Amazon Business Prime American Express card members can earn 5% back on shipments booked through Amazon Freight. This initiative aims to provide cost savings in the freight industry, a sector Amazon has increasingly made its mark in despite initial skepticism from carriers and brokers.

title: Amazon Just Quietly Added What Might Be Its Single Most Important Feature Yet
summary: Amazon has launched "Buy With Prime," a new feature that allows online retailers to offer Prime shipping and payment benefits directly on their own websites. This move is presented as potentially more significant than Amazon's original Prime membership, extending its fulfillment advantages beyond its own platform. The feature aims to simplify purchases for customers while also benefiting Amazon by increasing the utility and reach of its Prime service.

title: Ansys simulation software to be used in CU Boulder graduate program
summary: Ansys Inc., an engineering simulation software company based in Pittsburgh, is partnering with the University of Colorado Boulder. Their software will be integrated into the university's new Professional Master’s Program in High-Speed Digital Engineering. This collaboration aims to provide students with advanced simulation tools relevant to their field.

title: Amazon’s biggest drop since 2006 caps dismal month for tech
summary: Amazon.com Inc. shares experienced their largest one-day drop since 2006, sinking 14% and erasing $206.2 billion from its market value, following a disappointing outlook. This slump contributed to a dismal April for major tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, and Tesla, which collectively lost a trillion dollars in market value due to tumultuous earnings, rising costs, and concerns over Federal Reserve policy. The Nasdaq 100 Index also saw its biggest one-month decline since October 2008.

title: Amazon results and outlook fall short as fuel costs soar
summary: Amazon reported a disappointing quarter and outlook due to higher operating costs for warehouses and package delivery, leading to a 9 percent drop in shares after hours. Despite a long-running surge in sales during the COVID-19 pandemic, the e-commerce giant faces increased expenses from higher pay, its first US unionization, and soaring fuel prices. The company expects to lose as much as $1 billion in operating income this quarter, down from $7.7 billion last year, though its Amazon Web Services unit saw significant revenue growth.

title: 3 Nasdaq Stocks to Buy Now for Growth
summary: The Nasdaq Composite has significantly declined in 2022, primarily due to rising interest rates impacting tech shares. This article identifies three Nasdaq-listed investments—Incyte (INCY), Roku (ROKU), and First Trust Nasdaq Semiconductor ETF (FTXL)—as potential growth opportunities despite current market pressures. Each pick is analyzed for its fundamental strengths, recent performance, and future outlook.

title: Amazon to reimburse U.S. employees who travel for abortions, other treatments
summary: Amazon announced it would reimburse U.S. employees up to $4,000 annually for travel for non-life-threatening medical treatments, including abortions, if the procedure is unavailable within 100 miles of their home and virtual care is not possible. This decision makes Amazon the latest company to help employees navigate state laws restricting abortion access, following similar moves by Citigroup and Yelp. The policy, retroactive to January 1, applies to employees and covered dependents enrolled in specific health plans, regardless of whether they work in a corporate office or warehouse.

title: Woman says Amazon.com fired her because she got 'long COVID' - lawsuit
summary: A former Amazon.com Inc employee has sued the online retailer, alleging she was wrongly fired and forced to repay wages after contracting "long COVID." Brittany Hope, a former brand manager, claims Amazon terminated her for "job abandonment" and billed her for overpaid wages after she requested medical leave due to severe long COVID symptoms, which she developed after being diagnosed with the flu in February 2020. The lawsuit, filed in Manhattan federal court, seeks damages for alleged violations of federal, state, and New York City disability laws.

title: Amazon announces major SoCal expansion, including 1,000 jobs in Santa Monica
summary: Amazon plans to create 2,500 new corporate and technical jobs across Southern California, with over 1,000 in Santa Monica and 800 in Irvine. These roles include positions in cloud infrastructure, Alexa development, and video game design. The expansion involves new office leases in both Santa Monica and Irvine, with additional jobs also planned for San Diego.

title: Amazon Announces SoCal Expansion, Including 700 Jobs in San Diego
summary: Amazon.com Inc. is expanding its operations in Southern California, creating 2,500 new corporate and technical jobs, with over 700 of those located in San Diego. To accommodate this growth, Amazon has signed a lease for a 123,000-square-foot office space in University Town Center, expected to open in early 2023. The new roles include positions in cloud infrastructure, Alexa development, video game design, software engineering, human resources, finance, and IT.

title: Focus: Amazon's union: Two warehouses, two outcomes
summary: Amazon's two warehouses on Staten Island, JFK8 and LDJ5, despite being near each other and hiring from the same communities, had opposing outcomes on unionization. JFK8 voted to unionize due to worker dissatisfaction with conditions and the efforts of ALU founders who had worked there. LDJ5, a newer facility with different job roles and schedules, rejected unionization, with some workers finding the work easier and expressing concerns about union dues and goals.

title: Amazon says it has too much warehouse space. What's that mean for the broader industrial market?
summary: Amazon.com Inc., a dominant force in the U.S. industrial market, appears to have reached its maximum capacity for warehouse and fulfillment space. This development could signal significant shifts for the broader industrial real estate sector. The article prompts a discussion about the implications of Amazon's changing needs on the overall market.

title: Tech sell-off pushes Nasdaq to steepest drop since June 2020
summary: A tech sell-off on Thursday, May 5, 2022 pushed the Nasdaq Composite to its steepest drop since June 2020, wiping out hundreds of billions in market value. This downturn was driven by disappointing earnings reports from cloud and e-commerce companies like Shopify, Amazon, and Meta, coupled with investor concerns following the Federal Reserve's interest rate hike to combat inflation. The rotation out of tech began in late 2021 due to inflation fears and escalated with events like Russia's invasion of Ukraine, leading to significant year-to-date losses for many "Covid winner" stocks.

title: AES announces deals to provide 450 MW of renewable energy to Amazon
summary: The AES Corporation announced two Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) with Amazon to provide 450 MW of solar and 225 MW of 4-hour battery energy storage. These projects will support Amazon's goal to power its global operations with 100% renewable energy by 2025 and achieve net-zero carbon by 2040. AES has partnered with Fluence for energy storage solutions for one of the projects.

title: Amazon fires managers at its pro-union New York City warehouse
summary: Amazon fired managers at its JFK8 warehouse in New York City after workers successfully voted to join the Amazon Labor Union (ALU). While Amazon stated the changes were due to evaluating operations and leadership, reports suggest six managers were dismissed because the union won the election. Amazon is currently fighting to overturn the union election results.

title: 7 Charts for Making Sense of These Markets
summary: This article analyzes current market volatility through seven charts, highlighting the Federal Reserve's aggressive interest rate hikes as a primary driver. It discusses the impact on both stock and bond markets, the underperformance of growth stocks versus value stocks, and offers insights on potential opportunities for investors amidst the market adjustments.

title: Starbucks asks White House for equal time after Biden met with union leaders
summary: Starbucks has requested a meeting with the Biden administration after the White House hosted unionized workers without inviting Starbucks officials, expressing concern that the meeting did not represent the majority of its employees who oppose unionization. This comes as the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) issued a complaint alleging misconduct by Starbucks against union members, which Starbucks denies. Workers United, representing Starbucks employees, stated they were honored to represent all partners at the White House meeting.

title: AES Corp seals two renewable PPAs with Amazon
summary: The AES Corporation has signed two Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) with Amazon to deliver renewable energy to the CAISO market. These agreements involve 450MW of solar and 225MW of 4-hour duration battery energy storage projects, helping Amazon achieve its goal of powering global operations with 100% renewable energy by 2025. With these projects, Amazon further solidifies its position as the largest corporate buyer of renewable energy, advancing its commitment to net-zero carbon by 2040.

title: 15 Top Cities for Machine Learning Engineer Salaries
summary: This article identifies the top 15 U.S. cities offering the highest salaries for Machine Learning Engineers, based on data from Blind. It highlights that major tech hubs like Silicon Valley and Seattle lead due to high demand from companies like Meta and Apple. The piece also notes the increasing importance and compensation for AI and machine learning skills across industries.

title: Prologis offers to buy Duke Realty in $23.7 billion deal as warehouse demand soars
summary: Prologis Inc has offered to acquire Duke Realty Corp in an all-stock deal valued at $23.7 billion to capitalize on the surging demand for industrial warehouse space. The offer values Duke at $61.68 per share, representing a 29% premium, as Prologis seeks to maintain its growth amid a tight supply chain and increased e-commerce activity from companies like Amazon.

title: Rackspace is looking to sell itself and exit the public markets, again
summary: Rackspace Technology Inc. is exploring a sale of all or part of its business to return to private ownership, less than two years after re-entering the public market. The company believes a sum-of-the-parts valuation could exceed its current enterprise value, driven by attractive growth in its public cloud sector. This move follows tepid first-quarter financial results, with a net loss of $64 million and modest revenue growth, prompting concerns about its growth trajectory in comparison to the broader cloud market.

title: Google Cloud launches AlloyDB, a PostgreSQL-compatible database for mission-critical workloads
summary: Google Cloud has introduced AlloyDB, a new fully managed, PostgreSQL-compatible relational database service designed for mission-critical workloads. Unveiled at Google I/O, AlloyDB is built on open-source PostgreSQL and Google Cloud's infrastructure, aiming to offer superior performance, scalability, and reliability compared to standard PostgreSQL and AWS Aurora. It emphasizes transparent pricing without I/O charges and includes AI/ML capabilities for management and analytical acceleration, positioning it as a strong competitor to Oracle and AWS in the database market.

title: Rackspace CEO’s comments fuel speculation about another sale of tech company
summary: Rackspace Technology CEO Kevin Jones' recent comments to analysts have sparked speculation about a potential sale or restructuring of the company. Jones indicated that a "sum of the parts valuation" could exceed Rackspace's current enterprise value, leading to considerations of strategic alternatives. While Rackspace later downplayed a full sale, the company is exploring reorganizing its on-premise server and public cloud services into separate entities to "unlock value."

title: Global 2000: Berkshire Hathaway Takes Down JPMorgan As America’s Largest Company
summary: Berkshire Hathaway has surpassed JPMorgan Chase to become America's largest public company in the Forbes Global 2000 list, driven by nearly $90 billion in profits. Despite global volatility, U.S. companies like Amazon and ExxonMobil showed significant growth, with Amazon rising to third in the U.S. and ExxonMobil rebounding strongly due to surging energy prices. The article highlights the resilience of top U.S. companies amid inflation and geopolitical challenges.

title: Starbucks Baristas Are Unionizing, and Even Howard Schultz Can’t Make Them Stop
summary: Starbucks baristas are rapidly unionizing, undeterred by the return of longtime CEO Howard Schultz. This movement is inspiring other workers, including those at Amazon, and demonstrates the growing power of organized labor. The article highlights an instance where Schultz's return was met with a mix of corporate welcome and employee activism regarding social issues.

title: WD Targets Cloud Challenges
summary: Western Digital (WD) is launching new SSDs and HDDs to address the exponential data growth and performance demands in cloud environments, particularly for hyperscale customers. The company's new 22TB and 26TB UltraSMR HDDs leverage OptiNAND and enhanced SMR technology, while their Ultrastar NVMe PCIe 4.0 SSDs target disaggregated storage in public clouds to improve utilization and density. WD also notes a strong demand for client SSDs driven by remote work and gaming, reinforcing the critical role of storage innovation across the board.

title: Exclusive: Maker of Walmart, Amazon store-brand baby formulas sees shortages through rest of 2022
summary: Perrigo Company PLC, a major producer of store-brand baby formulas for retailers like Walmart and Amazon, anticipates ongoing shortages and heightened demand throughout the remainder of 2022. This scarcity is largely attributed to a significant recall by Abbott Laboratories in February. Perrigo's manufacturing facilities are operating at 115% capacity, and the company is collaborating with retailers to ensure weekly stock allocations.

title: Sonos Ray vs Beam (Gen 2) vs Sonos Arc: Which soundbar is best for you?
summary: This article compares Sonos' three soundbar models: the Ray, Beam (Gen 2), and Arc, to help consumers choose the best option for their needs. It details the differences in design, connectivity, sound capabilities (including Dolby Atmos support), and smart features. The article highlights price points and suggests which soundbar is best suited for various TV sizes and desired audio experiences, from basic TV audio enhancement to immersive home cinema.

title: QVC in the digital age: Tech giants like YouTube embrace livestream shopping
summary: Tech giants like YouTube are embracing livestream shopping, reimagining the QVC model from the 1990s. YouTube aims to streamline the existing organic behavior of users seeking product reviews on its platform by integrating live video shopping experiences with influencers. This move comes as livestream shopping has become a multi-billion dollar industry, particularly significant in China, and presents a win-win opportunity for creators and viewers by making the process easier and more interactive.

title: The PG 100: Ranking Top Food Retailers in North America
summary: The "PG 100" report ranks the top 100 food retailers in North America, highlighting an 11% increase in combined sales to $2.275 trillion in 2021. The report discusses challenges and benefits faced by retailers due to COVID-19 and rising fuel prices, as well as significant merger and acquisition activity in the industry. It provides a detailed table with sales, store counts, and top executives for each of the ranked companies.

title: Amazon’s unpaid time off practices violate USERRA, class-action suit claims
summary: A class-action lawsuit alleges that Amazon discriminated against military service members by counting military leave as unpaid time off (UTO), leading to negative UTO balances and subsequent termination. The plaintiff, a former Alabama Army National Guard member, claims Amazon violated the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act (USERRA) when she was fired after taking military leave. USERRA prohibits employers from denying employment benefits or retention based on military service and generally requires comparable benefits for military and non-military leaves.

title: Starbucks Labor Controversy Sparks Discussion About Unionizing and Company Reputation
summary: Starbucks is facing a labor controversy with Starbucks Workers United, which has filed numerous unfair labor practice charges against the company for allegedly interfering with unionization efforts. This situation has sparked a broader discussion about company reputation and the historical context of labor relations, with experts like Robert Josephson highlighting the importance of companies addressing employee concerns to avoid reputational damage. The article suggests that genuine empathy from leadership and effective communication can bridge the gap between management and employees in such disputes.

title: Amazon, Kong Studios Opening Offices In Santa Monica
summary: Amazon and game developer Kong Studios Inc. are establishing new offices in Santa Monica, a move city officials expect to bolster the local workforce and expand technology, game development, and corporate job opportunities. Amazon plans to open a "tech hub" in mid-2023, bringing over 1,000 new tech and corporate jobs to Santa Monica, while Kong Studios is relocating its headquarters to the city. This expansion is seen as a positive sign for Santa Monica's economic growth and its strong technology sector.

title: JD.com Opens First Overseas Service Centers in the US
summary: JD.com has opened two new overseas service centers in Los Angeles and New Jersey, marking its first such venture in the overseas market. These centers will offer comprehensive aftersales and maintenance services for electronics and appliances, supported by JD's air cargo lines and self-operated warehouses in the US. The initiative aims to provide end-to-end supply chain logistics services and reduce repair times for brands and cross-border merchants.

title: FedEx expands pilot of self-driving trucks
summary: FedEx is expanding its pilot program for self-driving trucks by adding a new commercial lane in Texas, collaborating with Aurora Innovation Inc. This expansion is part of FedEx's strategy to compete with Amazon in autonomous cargo hauling, following successful earlier pilots that completed 60,000 miles with zero safety incidents. The company is actively integrating autonomous technology into its operations to enhance efficiency and meet growing customer demand.

title: D-Market Elektronik Hizmetler ve Ticaret (Nasdaq:HEPS) - Stock Analysis
summary: This analysis of D-Market Elektronik Hizmetler ve Ticaret (Nasdaq:HEPS) from Simply Wall St provides a detailed overview including valuation, growth forecasts, past performance, financial health, and dividend information. The report highlights that HEPS is trading significantly below its estimated fair value with projected revenue growth, despite facing a forecast decline in earnings and current unprofitability. Key takeaways point to expanded delivery services and strategic partnerships as drivers for future success, while also noting challenges from intensifying competition and macroeconomic volatility.

title: Will smart shopping carts transform the shopping experience at Albertsons?
summary: Albertsons is expanding its pilot program for smart shopping carts, manufactured by Veeve, across several dozen stores after successful initial tests in California and Idaho. These carts allow customers to complete their shopping and checkout without needing a traditional register, aiming to enhance the grocery experience through technology. While the concept offers convenience, industry experts and commentators raise concerns about the high cost of implementation, potential user adoption challenges, and whether such advanced technology distracts from fundamental store operational improvements.

title: Broadcom in Talks to Acquire VMware, the Cloud Computing Company (Published 2022)
summary: Broadcom, a semiconductor giant, is reportedly in discussions to acquire VMware, a cloud computing company known for its virtualization software. This potential acquisition would expand Broadcom's presence in cloud computing and continue CEO Hock E. Tan's strategy of diversifying beyond chip companies following a blocked deal with Qualcomm in 2018. Following the news, VMware's stock saw a significant increase, while Broadcom's shares experienced a slight dip.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Announces New Fire 7 Tablets
summary: Amazon has announced its new Fire 7 and Fire 7 Kids tablets, which are available for pre-order and will start shipping on June 29, 2022. These tablets serve as budget-friendly alternatives to the Apple iPad mini, with prices starting at $59.99 and $109.99 respectively. The new Fire 7 boasts significant upgrades in battery life, RAM, and responsiveness compared to its 2019 predecessor, while only increasing in price by $10.

title: Amazon gains on UPS in package delivery volume
summary: Amazon's package delivery business, Amazon Logistics, is rapidly gaining on UPS in terms of delivery volume, having already surpassed FedEx in 2020. While Amazon Logistics held a 22% share of the parcel delivery market by volume last year, its revenue share is significantly lower at 12% due to its Prime free shipping model. Amazon plans to invest $1 billion in other logistics companies to further its growth.

title: Amazon.com Inc. delays hiring by months at new 1 million-square-foot facility in Clarksville
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has delayed hiring for its new 1 million-square-foot fulfillment center in Clarksville, Tennessee. The company is reportedly waiting to invest in manpower until there is a clear inbound supply of materials for the facility. This delay indicates a revised hiring schedule compared to initial plans.

title: How Amazon, Microsoft, Costco and more Seattle-area brands rank by reputation
summary: Amazon ranked highest among Seattle-area companies in a recent Axios and Harris Poll survey assessing the reputations of the 100 most visible corporate brands in the U.S. Conversely, Costco, based in Issaquah, experienced a 17-place drop in the same survey. The article highlights how local companies performed in this national reputational ranking.

title: Our verdict on the Garmin Instinct 2S GPS smartwatch
summary: This review covers the Garmin Instinct 2S Solar smartwatch, highlighting its robust features, impressive battery life (especially the solar model), and comprehensive health and fitness tracking capabilities. The article notes its affordability compared to other high-end Garmin watches, praising its utility for keen runners and triathletes despite some limitations in music storage and advanced mapping.

title: Kroger Extends Its Delivery Reach in Ohio
summary: Kroger is expanding its delivery network in Ohio with a new 61,000-square-foot facility in Lockbourne, powered by Ocado Group technology. This "spoke" facility will serve central Ohio and act as a last-mile cross-dock location, extending delivery services up to 200 miles. The expansion is expected to create 200 jobs and further Kroger's digital shopping experience, including the Boost by Kroger Plus service for customers.

title: Hyperion looks to slower growth for a helping hand
summary: Australia's largest growth manager, Hyperion, is betting on a counterintuitive outcome—an economic downturn and anemic growth—to revive its performance. Despite a 36% year-to-date loss, Hyperion's CIO Mark Arnold believes that high inflation and high interest rates are temporary and will retreat as the economy cools, providing a tailwind for growth stocks. The firm argues that its disruptive holdings, like Tesla and Amazon, will outperform in a cooler economy where growth is scarce, forcing capital allocators to invest in companies with double-digit earnings growth.

title: Coty launches first-ever Chloé Fragrances e-commerce store
summary: Coty Inc. has launched the first-ever Chloé Fragrances digital store in partnership with e-commerce platform Lazada. The digital store, hosted on LazMall Prestige, will feature Chloé Signature and Nomade lines, along with exclusive launch sets and will be supported by a digital marketing campaign and a live stream event. This move is part of Coty's strategy to expand its premium digital footprint across Southeast Asia, leveraging the growth in luxury e-commerce.

title: How Chatbots are Revolutionizing Customer Experience
summary: Modern AI-powered chatbots are transforming customer experience by handling various tasks from ordering taxis to booking appointments, driven by advancements in AI, Machine Learning, and Natural Language Processing (NLP). Unlike traditional rule-based bots, NLP allows chatbots to understand complex conversations, retain context, and interpret human sentiment by integrating with external knowledge sources. Conversational AI, built on neural networks, enables chatbots to learn and improve continuously, process vast amounts of unstructured data to predict user behavior, and perform repetitive tasks, making them indispensable for businesses and freeing up human employees for more complex issues.

title: Five Below opening location in East Setauket this summer
summary: Five Below is set to open a new store in East Setauket on July 1st, located in the Suffolk Plaza shopping center. The store will occupy approximately 20,000 square feet next to a future Amazon Fresh, in a space previously housing a Waldbaum’s grocery store. This new location will bring low-cost products to the community and is also hiring staff.

title: Boeing will move global HQ to Arlington
summary: Boeing announced its decision to relocate its global headquarters from Chicago to its existing campus in Arlington, Virginia. This move will make Boeing the largest defense contractor headquartered in Virginia, leveraging its proximity to government stakeholders and a skilled workforce. The company also plans to develop a research and technology hub in Arlington, supported by a significant gift to Virginia Tech.

title: Industry backs Lloyd’s of London’s $465m DXC Technology contract extension
summary: The insurance industry has largely welcomed Lloyd’s of London's $465 million contract extension with DXC Technology, viewing it as a crucial step towards digital transformation. This agreement aims to modernize Lloyd's IT systems by transitioning around 400 London market insurance companies to a cloud-native digital platform running on Amazon Web Services. This move is expected to significantly improve speed, efficiency, and operational resilience, transforming the market from a paper-based system to a data-focused, automated, and cost-efficient one.

title: American Eagle’s Quiet Platforms partners with global shipper Pitney Bowes
summary: American Eagle's logistics platform, Quiet Platforms, has partnered with global shipper Pitney Bowes to enhance its delivery and fulfillment capabilities. This collaboration aims to offer faster delivery services and logistics to its network of retailers and brands, strengthening Quiet Platforms' position as an "anti-Amazon" open-sharing logistics model. The partnership will leverage Pitney Bowes' Designed Delivery service to customize delivery networks for Quiet Platforms' clients like Steve Madden, Kohl’s, and Peloton.

title: ZIM Logistics China (ZLC), Ladingo and ZIM Announce the Launch of ZL MAX™ - a New Service Package for Amazon Sellers and eCommerce Businesses
summary: ZIM Logistics China (ZLC), Ladingo, and ZIM Integrated Shipping Services have partnered to launch ZL MAX™, a new comprehensive service package designed for Amazon sellers and eCommerce businesses operating on the China-US West Coast trade route. This service provides end-to-end logistics, including a dedicated digital platform from Ladingo for instant quotes and LCL shipment booking, and leverages ZIM's eCommerce Xpress Service for fast transit times. The collaboration aims to offer reliable, digital, and efficient cross-border shipping solutions amidst growing eCommerce demand and supply chain challenges.

title: ADM’s flaxseed products ship globally
summary: The Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) plant in Red Wing, Minnesota, which began as Zumbro Linseed Company in 1902, continues to process flaxseed for global distribution. ADM, also founded in 1902, acquired the Red Wing facility in 1971, making it the company's last remaining flaxseed processing plant. The article details how flaxseed oil is used in industrial applications, supplements, and increasingly in human consumption, with the Red Wing team selling products like "Linsheen" and "Knwble Grwn" online.

title: Quantrepreneurs: Wall Street Quants Who Left Trading to Build Startups
summary: This article profiles eight "quantrepreneurs"—quantitative finance professionals who transitioned from lucrative Wall Street trading careers at firms like Citadel and Renaissance Technologies to found or join tech startups. Inspired by Jeff Bezos, these individuals sought more impactful work, moving into diverse fields such as AI in insurance, medical technology, sustainable energy, decentralized finance, and real estate tech. Their stories highlight a growing trend of quants applying their analytical skills to real-world problems, often leveraging non-compete periods to explore entrepreneurial ventures.

title: Walmart is using its thousands of stores to battle Amazon for e-commerce market share
summary: Walmart is leveraging its extensive network of nearly 4,700 stores across the U.S. as "shoppable fulfillment centers" to boost its e-commerce market share and compete with Amazon. The retailer is transforming its physical locations into hubs for drone deliveries, automated online grocery order fulfillment, and direct-to-fridge drop-offs, aiming to provide faster and more efficient service. This strategy also involves wooing third-party sellers by offering fulfillment services from its stores and expanding its delivery and membership programs like GoLocal and Walmart+.

title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy earned 6,474 times the median Amazon employee’s salary
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy received a compensation package of nearly $213 million in 2021, which was 6,474 times the median Amazon employee's salary of $32,855. This high compensation was primarily due to a special restricted stock award tied to his promotion to CEO. While several other Fortune 500 CEOs also earned significantly more than their median employees, Jassy's ratio was exceptionally high, sparking controversy among shareholder advisory firms.

title: Amazon consumer CEO Dave Clark resigns
summary: Dave Clark, Amazon's consumer CEO, has resigned after 23 years with the company, effective July 1. His departure signals potential changes in the consumer division, which faced challenges with overbuilt warehouse space and a weak profit outlook. CEO Andy Jassy acknowledged the need for further improvements and will announce a succession plan in the coming weeks.

title: What Does Amazon's Stock Split Mean for Investors?
summary: Amazon is executing a 20-1 stock split, its first in over two decades, effective June 3. The split will adjust Morningstar's fair value estimate for Amazon to $192 per share from $3,850, but senior analyst Dan Romanoff's long-term view remains unchanged, anticipating continued growth in e-commerce, AWS, and advertising. Other major tech companies like Alphabet and Tesla are also planning stock splits.

title: Ford Motor Company Will Start Online, Fixed-Price, No Haggle Sales For EVs (Updated with Video!)
summary: Ford CEO Jim Farley announced that the company will transition to 100% online, fixed-price sales for its electric vehicles, including direct delivery to customers' homes. This move aims to bypass the traditional dealership experience and excessive markups, following a model similar to Tesla and drawing inspiration from companies like Target and Amazon. While dealerships will still exist, they will need to adapt to this new online-focused, no-haggle sales approach for EVs, with potential expansion to all vehicle sales if successful.

title: Toxic Products Sold at Dollar Stores
summary: A report by the Campaign for Healthier Solutions reveals that over half of products tested from dollar stores like Family Dollar, Dollar General, and Five Below contain hazardous chemicals. These toxic products, some targeted at children, include substances like lead, phthalates, PVC, BPAs, and PFAS, which are linked to various health issues. The article lists 30 specific toxic products found in dollar stores, ranging from children's headphones and toys to food packaging and cooking pans.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Gains as Stock Split Lowers Price Tag for Retail Buyers
summary: Amazon's stock surged after a 20-for-1 split, making shares more accessible to retail investors. This marks the company's first split in over two decades, leading to increased trading volume and outperforming the Nasdaq 100 Index. The move is expected to attract greater retail interest, potentially acting as a sentiment catalyst.

title: U.S. Supreme Court rules Southwest Airlines cannot force wage suit into arbitration
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court ruled 8-0 that Southwest Airlines cannot force a baggage handler's class action lawsuit over overtime pay into private arbitration, exempting them from a federal law due to their involvement in interstate commerce. This decision has potential implications for other companies like Amazon and Uber, as it suggests that various transportation workers may also be exempt from mandatory arbitration for wage claims, leading to more class actions rather than individual arbitrations.

title: XPO Logistics Appoints Yoav Amiel as Chief Information Officer
summary: XPO Logistics has appointed Yoav Amiel as Chief Information Officer for its planned spin-off of the asset-light brokered transportation platform. Amiel, who previously led the technology organization for XPO’s North American transportation division, has been critical in developing the XPO Connect digital brokerage platform. This move supports the spin-off's creation of two separate, publicly traded companies focused on North American growth.

title: Teladoc Health faces class action lawsuit alleging it misled investors
summary: Teladoc Health is facing a class action lawsuit alleging the company, its CEO Jason Gorevic, and CFO Mala Murthy misled investors. The suit claims they failed to disclose that increasing competition was negatively impacting its BetterHelp mental health and chronic care segments, leading to unsustainable growth projections. This alleged misrepresentation resulted in significant investor losses and a $6.6 billion non-cash goodwill impairment charge related to its 2020 Livongo acquisition.

title: Amazon Sued Over 'Bait-And-Switch' Whole Foods Fee
summary: Amazon is facing a putative class action lawsuit filed by an Amazon Prime member in Washington federal court. The lawsuit alleges that Amazon engaged in "bait-and-switch" false advertising by promising free grocery services for Whole Foods Market orders but then added a $9.95 service fee. The plaintiff claims this practice constitutes false advertising.

title: Amazon’s former No. 2 Dave Clark just joined a Bay Area logistics startup as co-CEO
summary: Dave Clark, formerly Amazon's consumer chief, has joined Flexport Inc. as co-CEO. He will serve in this role for six months starting September 1st, after which he will become the sole CEO, with current CEO Ryan Petersen transitioning to executive chairman. Clark's departure from Amazon comes amidst slowing online sales growth and an excess of warehouse space, concluding a two-decade career where he championed innovation in Amazon's fulfillment and transportation sectors.

title: Kroger is now delivering in South Florida
summary: Kroger has launched online grocery delivery services in South Florida through a new 60,000-square-foot spoke facility in Miami, which supports its automated customer fulfillment center in Groveland, Florida. This expansion is part of Kroger's aggressive growth strategy in Florida, utilizing a hub-and-spoke network to deliver groceries to areas hundreds of miles from its CFCs. The company aims to compete with rivals like Amazon and Walmart by focusing on next-day delivery, a wide assortment, competitive pricing, and its partnership with Ocado, particularly in markets where it lacks physical stores.

title: Dow dives 800 points, S&P 500 posts worst week since January after inflation hits 40-year high
summary: U.S. stocks fell sharply on Friday, with the Dow dropping 880 points and the S&P 500 completing its worst week since January, following a higher-than-expected inflation report for May. The consumer price index reached its highest level since 1981, showing an 8.6% year-over-year increase, fueling concerns about a potential recession and prompting fears of a more aggressive Federal Reserve. Tech stocks, banks, and cyclical stocks were particularly impacted as the 2-year Treasury yield surged above 3%.

title: Hack Into Quantum Computing Boom With QQQ
summary: Quantum computing, a disruptive technology with significant growth potential, is still in its early stages but offers various use cases. While some speculative companies exist, major players in this field are large-cap companies. The Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) provide investors with exposure to quantum computing through their holdings in established tech giants like Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Nvidia, and Honeywell.

title: Fortinet (FTNT) to Split Stock in 5 to Attract More Investors
summary: Fortinet (FTNT) announced a five-for-one forward stock split to make its shares more accessible to employees and retail investors, with stockholder approval scheduled for June 17. If approved, the stock price will drop from approximately $300 to $60 per share. This move follows similar stock splits by other major tech companies like Alphabet, Amazon, and NVIDIA, aiming to boost demand and potentially increase share prices.

title: Walmart struggles to solve consumer needs in China
summary: Walmart has faced persistent challenges in the Chinese market since opening its first store in 1996, struggling to compete with local retailers known for their diverse product offerings and fast home deliveries. The company's large hypermarket format is not appealing to Chinese consumers who prefer online shopping and local products. Additionally, Walmart has found itself navigating complex political tensions, including controversies over product sourcing from Xinjiang and increased regulatory scrutiny, despite China being crucial for its manufacturing supply chain and for observing e-commerce trends.

title: Oracle’s Database Dominance Eroded by Rise of Cloud-First Rivals
summary: Oracle's long-standing dominance in the database market is being challenged by cloud-first rivals offering more flexible and cheaper options. Companies like Shutterfly are migrating their data to cloud providers such as Amazon.com Inc., moving away from Oracle's traditional database products due to ease of use. Despite this trend, Oracle is still projected to report 4% sales growth.

title: Amazon Prime Air prepares for drone deliveries
summary: Amazon's Prime Air drone delivery service is preparing to launch in Lockeford, California, marking a significant step in making drone deliveries a reality after nearly a decade of development. The service aims to deliver packages quickly and safely, leveraging advanced sense-and-avoid technology to navigate obstacles and ensure safe landings without human observers. Lockeford residents will provide crucial feedback to help scale the service, which has earned an FAA air carrier certificate through rigorous testing and innovation.

title: Drones will start delivering Amazon packages this year, company says
summary: Amazon announced that it will begin delivering packages via its Prime Air drone service to residents in Lockeford, California, later this year. The company has spent nearly a decade developing its drone technology, which includes a sophisticated sense-and-avoid system to ensure safe operation. Amazon is working with the FAA and local officials to implement the service and plans to invest in the Lockeford community.

title: eBay Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) stock dropped 0.82% to $42.58 on Tuesday, underperforming the broader market as the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw declines. This marked the fourth consecutive day of losses for eBay, with the stock closing significantly below its 52-week high. Competitors showed mixed performance, with Amazon.com Inc. falling, while Walmart Inc. and Costco Wholesale Corp. rose.

title: The Surprising Case for Stronger E-commerce Growth
summary: Despite a recent slump in e-commerce stocks and slowing economic activity, Morgan Stanley forecasts continued strong growth for e-commerce, projecting an increase from $3.3 trillion today to $5.4 trillion in 2026. This growth is driven by a permanent behavioral shift to online shopping, increasing penetration in both developed and emerging markets, and technological improvements in logistics and digital payments.

title: Meet Naima Greenwood | President & Chief Operating Officer, Yardstick Management
summary: The article features an interview with Naima Greenwood, President & Chief Operating Officer of Yardstick Management, the nation's leading Black-owned management consulting firm. Greenwood discusses the firm's significant impact on diversity, equity, and inclusion within global organizations like Amazon and Netflix, and their commitment to leveling the playing field for marginalized communities. She also shares personal insights about Atlanta, where Yardstick Management is headquartered, and highlights the important community work of the Villages of Carver YMCA.

title: Sonos tips: how to stereo pair, connect to a TV, and more
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to getting the most out of a Sonos system, covering frequently asked questions and offering 25 tips and tricks. It details aspects like Bluetooth connectivity, using Sonos without Wi-Fi, connecting to a TV, app usage, waterproofing, stereo pairing, and enhancing sound quality with Trueplay and external DACs. The guide also touches on streaming services, voice control, and integrating vinyl with Sonos, demonstrating the system's versatility.

title: hololive production Announces New Apparel Sales on Merch by Amazon
summary: hololive production has announced a new line of apparel featuring various talents, which will be available globally via Merch by Amazon starting June 16, 2022. The white T-shirts depict talents like Tokino Sora, Robocosan, and Hoshimachi Suisei, and are shippable to customers in France, Germany, Italy, Japan, Spain, the United Kingdom, and the United States. Merch by Amazon handles production, sales, and shipping of these creator-designed products, offering standard Amazon shipping options.

title: Amazon Prime announces 2022 Prime Day dates will be held on July 12, 13
summary: Amazon announced that its 2022 Prime Day sales event will take place on July 12 and 13. The 48-hour event, starting at 3 AM ET, will offer discounts across various categories and include Poland and Sweden for the first time. The annual event aims to boost Prime subscriptions and generate significant sales, impacting rival retailers who often hold their own sales concurrently.

title: Dick’s Sporting Goods Expanding, Burlington Moving to Town Square
summary: Dick's Sporting Goods is set to expand significantly in the Town Square Mall, taking over the neighboring Dollar Tree space. Additionally, Burlington is relocating its store from the Oakdale Mall in Johnson City to the Town Square Mall, occupying the former A.C. Moore Arts & Crafts location, with construction already underway.

title: Ariana Grande Debuts Chapter 3 of R.E.M. Beauty with a Bang(s)
summary: Ariana Grande's R.E.M. Beauty launched its third collection, 'Chapter 3: on your collar,' focusing on lip products. This collection, which includes five new formulas like lip oil, balm, liner, and lipsticks, follows the brand's 'drops' model and continues its extraterrestrial aesthetic. R.E.M. Beauty's initial collections, Chapters 1 and 2, are also available at Ulta stores.

title: Former Amazon employee convicted in Capital One hack
summary: A former Amazon Web Services employee, Paige Thompson, has been convicted of hacking into Capital One and stealing the data of over 100 million people, making it one of the largest data breaches in the U.S. Thompson was found guilty of seven federal crimes, including wire fraud, and her sentencing is set for September 15. Capital One has previously agreed to pay $190 million to settle a class-action lawsuit and an additional $80 million in regulatory fines related to the breach.

title: Ex-Amazon employee convicted over 2019 Capital One breach
summary: A former Amazon Web Services (AWS) employee, Paige Thompson, has been convicted of seven federal crimes related to the 2019 Capital One data breach that affected over 106 million accounts. The breach led to Capital One paying an $80 million penalty and a $190 million settlement for customer lawsuits. Thompson, who is scheduled for sentencing in September, exploited misconfigured AWS accounts to steal personal data and mine cryptocurrency.

title: False Advertising Lawsuit Against Vital Farms Wins Right to Proceed
summary: PETA Foundation lawyers and co-counsel achieved a legal victory as a motion to dismiss a class-action lawsuit against Vital Farms Inc. was denied. This decision allows the case to proceed, brought by consumers alleging they were misled by the company's false claims of ethical and humane treatment of hens. Vital Farms' eggs are widely sold at major grocery chains.

title: Former AWS engineer convicted over hack that cost Capital One $270m
summary: A former AWS engineer, Paige Thompson, has been convicted of seven counts of fraud for stealing personal data from over 100 million people, including Capital One customers, after identifying and exploiting misconfigured AWS accounts. The breach has cost Capital One more than $270 million in compensation and regulatory fines. Thompson, who also used the breached accounts for cryptojacking, is awaiting sentencing in September.

title: Paige Thompson found guilty in 2019 Capital One data breach
summary: Paige Thompson, a former Amazon engineer, has been convicted in the 2019 Capital One data breach that exposed data of over 100 million customers. The hack involved misconfigured AWS accounts and cost Capital One hundreds of millions in fines and settlements. Thompson was found guilty of wire fraud and multiple counts of unauthorized access to a protected computer and damaging a protected computer, though acquitted on other charges.

title: Oracle introduces lower-cost version of its full on-premises cloud stack
summary: Oracle has introduced a more affordable version of its on-premises cloud stack, Dedicated Region Cloud@Customer, alongside a new rack-level offering called Oracle Compute Cloud@Customer. The new Dedicated Region Cloud@Customer reduces the required space and annual cost significantly, making Oracle's full public cloud services, including Fusion applications, accessible to a broader range of global companies for workloads that cannot fully migrate to the public cloud. Oracle Compute Cloud@Customer provides managed on-site computing and storage resources for smaller workloads, similar to offerings from AWS, Microsoft, and Google.

title: Build a data pipeline on AWS with Kafka, Kafka connect and DynamoDB
summary: This article provides a step-by-step guide to building a data pipeline on AWS using Kafka, Kafka Connect, and DynamoDB. It covers setting up MSK, MSK Connect, and integrating Datagen source connector with a DynamoDB sink connector. The guide also details infrastructure preparation using CloudFormation and testing the end-to-end pipeline.

title: Barack, Michelle Obama ditch Spotify for Amazon-owned Audible
summary: Former President Barack Obama and former First Lady Michelle Obama are moving their podcast content from Spotify to Amazon-owned Audible. Their production company, Higher Ground, will create new audio content for Audible, a platform primarily known for audiobooks. The Obamas cited Audible's investment in lifting up voices and telling stories that entertain and inspire as reasons for the switch.

title: Despite Shopify’s Stock Split, Investors Should Look Elsewhere
summary: Morningstar analyst Sandy Ward advises investors to look beyond Shopify's stock split, suggesting Amazon shares as a better alternative. Shopify's stock has plummeted significantly, and concerns about its financial outlook and a new share class cementing founder control make it a less attractive investment compared to Amazon, which offers greater financial certainty and diversified revenue streams. Despite a potential upside, Shopify is not among Morningstar's top stock picks due to slowing revenue growth and quarterly losses.

title: Amazon has a plan to make Alexa mimic anyone's voice
summary: Amazon is developing a new Alexa feature that will allow the voice assistant to mimic any voice after hearing less than a minute of audio. The company's senior vice president, Rohit Prasad, stated the goal is to "make the memories last" for those who have lost loved ones, particularly after the pandemic. This technology, however, raises concerns about its potential for abuse, such as creating deepfakes, an issue Microsoft has also addressed with its voice-parroting software.

title: Here’s how much the Jif peanut butter recall could cost parent company JM Smucker
summary: The J.M. Smucker Company could face a $125 million impact from the massive recall of nearly 50 Jif peanut butter products due to a salmonella outbreak. The recall, originating from the company's Lexington plant, led to a 15% drop in stock price. Production has resumed, and products are expected back on shelves shortly, with heightened food safety measures in place.

title: Por qué un empresario propone colocar "estatuas de Cristina Kirchner en cada rincón de Uruguay"
summary: A Brazilian venture capitalist, Paulo Passoni, has provocatively suggested erecting statues of Cristina Kirchner across Uruguay. He argues that her policies have inadvertently driven a significant number of Argentine entrepreneurs and tech companies to relocate to Uruguay, appreciating its stable economic environment and clear rules. This migration has fostered a thriving tech ecosystem in Uruguay, benefiting the country by attracting talent and investment.

title: Seattle Pride’s Biggest Sponsors Also Give Big to Homophobic Politicians
summary: This article exposes how major corporate sponsors of Seattle Pride, specifically Alaska Air Group, Chateau Ste. Michelle, and T-Mobile, also donate significant amounts of money to politicians who oppose LGBTQ+ rights. It details specific examples of these donations and the voting records of the politicians supported. The article questions the alignment of these corporations' actions with Pride values and notes that Seattle Pride is reviewing its corporate partnerships.

title: Omnicom banks on Walmart, Instacart in latest commerce expansion
summary: Omnicom Media Group (OMG) has expanded its commerce offerings by partnering with Walmart Connect and Instacart. The Walmart partnership focuses on cross-screen planning and connecting first-party advertiser data to Walmart's DSP, while the Instacart collaboration includes account management, product insights, and utilizing Omni's clean room infrastructure. These moves aim to deepen Omnicom's connected commerce capabilities and help advertisers navigate a privacy-centric marketing landscape.

title: 3 Reasons to Buy TJX Companies, and 1 to Sell
summary: TJX Companies, the parent company of TJ Maxx and Marshalls, is highlighted as a resilient brick-and-mortar retailer with an evergreen business model benefitting from the "retail apocalypse" and offering deeply discounted goods. The article outlines three reasons to buy the stock: its solid business model, robust post-lockdown recovery, and attractive valuation coupled with a decent dividend. The primary reason to sell is potential macroeconomic headwinds such as inflation, higher freight costs, and supply chain disruptions, which could impact its margins and inventory management.

title: CVS, Rite Aid ration contraceptive pills as demand surges
summary: CVS Health Corp. and Rite Aid Corp. are limiting purchases of emergency contraceptive pills like Plan B to three per customer due to a surge in demand following the US Supreme Court decision overturning Roe v. Wade. Other chains, such as Walgreens, have not implemented purchase limits at this time, though some Walmart products have online purchase limits that may change with demand fluctuations. The increased demand for emergency contraception reflects preparations people are making in response to the ruling, which is expected to make abortions largely illegal in many states.

title: Federal Realty Pandemic Program Produced Permanent Deals for 50% of Tenants
summary: Don Wood, CEO of Federal Realty Investment Trust, discussed the company's temporary leasing program during the pandemic, which resulted in over half of the tenants transitioning to permanent deals. He also highlighted the acquisition of Kingstowne Towne Center in Virginia and the importance of ESG principles, noting the company's 13 megawatts of solar capacity.

title: AWS: Amazon (AMZN) Cloud Unit on Course for $3 Trillion Value, Redburn Says
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is projected by Redburn Ltd. to reach a $3 trillion valuation, a figure nearly triple Amazon.com Inc.'s current total market capitalization. Analyst Alex Haissl suggests AWS is so dominant that Amazon might eventually consider spinning it off from its slower-growing e-commerce business. The report initiates coverage on the cloud-computing industry but does not specify a timeline for AWS to achieve this valuation.

title: BP, Shell to cover employee travel for treatment after Roe v. Wade
summary: BP PLC has joined Shell PLC in committing to cover employee travel expenses for healthcare treatment following the Supreme Court's overturning of Roe v. Wade. Both European oil producers updated their healthcare plans to extend benefits for women needing to travel for services, though neither explicitly named abortion. This move comes as other major companies like Amazon, JPMorgan Chase, Tesla, and Walt Disney also updated their policies to cover travel costs for out-of-state abortion services, while U.S. oil majors have largely remained silent.

title: Corsair TC200 gaming chair review: "A great chair for both work and play"
summary: The Corsair TC200 gaming chair offers significant improvements over its predecessor, the T3 Rush, particularly for larger users with its wider seat and back, and internal lumbar support. Reviewer Frederick Hillinger praises its all-day comfort for both work and gaming, despite the lumbar support not being adjustable. The chair is highly recommended for its strong value, high-quality materials, and thoughtful design that incorporates user feedback.

title: Amazon, Disney, AT&T Gave to Abortion Foes Like DeSantis While Vowing to Help Employees
summary: Amazon, Disney, and AT&T, despite pledging to cover travel costs for employees seeking reproductive medical care, have also contributed financially to anti-abortion governors and political figures like Ron DeSantis. State campaign-finance records show these corporate giants supported leaders actively working to reduce access to abortions, creating a contradiction between their public stances and political donations.

title: Zscaler and AWS to help customers accelerate their secured transition to the cloud
summary: Zscaler has extended its relationship with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to help customers securely transition to the cloud using a Zero Trust approach. The collaboration introduces new solutions, including a Cloud-Native Application Protection Platform (CNAPP) built on AWS, extended Zero Trust security for cloud workloads, and Zero Trust for Private 5G connections with AWS Wavelength. These innovations aim to simplify cloud security operations, mitigate workload risks, and advance security architecture for enterprises.

title: How Much Worse Can It Get for Growth Stocks?
summary: The second quarter of 2022 saw growth stocks suffer their largest losses since the 2008 financial crisis, with the Morningstar US Growth Index dropping 25.3%. Value stocks significantly outperformed growth, marking the widest margin since the dot-com bubble collapse, as rising interest rates and inflation dampened the appeal of growth companies' future earnings. While many growth stocks are now at their cheapest valuations in years, experts suggest value stocks may continue to outperform in the short term despite growth's long-term lead.

title: Walt Disney Co. Creates ‘Disney Storytellers Fund’ At Howard University To Spark Opportunities
summary: The Walt Disney Company has established the "Disney Storytellers Fund" at Howard University. This initiative aims to provide opportunities for underrepresented students to pursue careers in media and entertainment, offering stipends, mentorship, and internships across various creative fields. The fund will also support a new collaborative creative space within Howard's Chadwick A. Boseman College of Fine Arts and Cathy Hughes School of Communication.

title: Top 15 Affirm Competitors and Alternatives
summary: This article identifies and details the top 15 competitors and alternatives to Affirm, a leading buy now, pay later (BNPL) service provider. It highlights various companies like Sezzle, PayPal, Klarna, Afterpay, and Zip, outlining their services, financial performance, and unique features. The piece also discusses alternatives that cater to specific needs, such as travel payments (Uplift) or those for consumers with poor credit (Perpay).

title: 'A certification that you've made it': How United Natural Foods Inc became the DTC CPG gatekeeper
summary: United Natural Foods, Inc. (UNFI) has established itself as the leading wholesale distributor for health and specialty foods in North America, becoming a crucial gateway for DTC brands aiming for national retail presence. Despite challenges like antiquated processes and margin compromises, working with UNFI is often a necessary "certification" for CPG startups seeking to expand beyond e-commerce into major grocery chains like Whole Foods. The article highlights UNFI's significant influence and role in bridging digitally-native brands with traditional physical retail, as demonstrated by brand experiences and financial figures, including $27.27 billion in revenue in 2021.

title: Springfield trucking company settles lawsuit with Amazon over use of word 'Prime'
summary: Springfield-based trucking company Prime Inc. has settled a lawsuit with Amazon regarding Amazon's use of the word "Prime" on its shipping vehicles. Prime Inc. had sued Amazon in 2019, claiming the retailer's use of "Prime" caused confusion and potential "irreparable injury" to its business, which has used the name on its semi-trailers since at least 1980. The terms of the confidential settlement were not disclosed, and representatives from both companies declined to comment on whether Amazon would cease using "Prime" on its vehicles.

title: Amazon Celebrates Prime Members With New, Exclusive Offers and Experiences
summary: Amazon announced new exclusive offers and experiences for Prime members, ahead of Prime Day on July 12-13. These include a free one-year Grubhub+ membership, an exclusive 60-second sneak peek of "The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power" for 48 hours, and special deals on electronics and fan gear for the upcoming Thursday Night Football on Prime Video. The company emphasizes the enhanced value and expanded benefits now available to its Prime members.

title: FMET Makes It Simple to Take Long View of Metaverse
summary: The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) offers investors a way to engage with the growing metaverse theme, which is projected to be worth over $800 million by 2028. Despite a retrenchment in growth stocks, the long-term outlook for the metaverse remains compelling, especially with established companies like Meta Platforms, Alphabet, Apple, and Nvidia investing heavily in its development. FMET provides a diversified basket approach to this theme, including exposure to gaming and numerous mega-cap growth companies, with an affordable annual fee.

title: Amazon Fresh store now open steps from HQ2
summary: Amazon has opened a new Amazon Fresh grocery store in Crystal City, just steps from its HQ2. This location marks the third Amazon Fresh store to open in the last month. The store was reported on by Hannah Denham of the Washington Business Journal.

title: Canadian Solar sells two Australian solar farms to US interests
summary: Canadian Solar has sold its 150 MW Suntop Solar Farm and 110 MW Gunnedah Solar Farm in New South Wales, Australia, to CalEnergy Resources, a subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway Energy. The deal also includes a multi-year development services agreement for Canadian Solar to build out CalEnergy's renewable energy pipeline in Australia. These solar farms are backed by long-term offtake agreements with Amazon and are expected to generate over 700,000 MWh annually, avoiding significant CO2 emissions.

title: The Weekly Closeout: Ulta, The Allure Store partner and UK antitrust watchdog takes on Amazon
summary: This weekly retail wrap-up covers Ulta Beauty's pop-up shop at The Allure Store in NYC, focusing on highlighting newer brands and experiential programming. It also details Deckers' hiring of former Nike chief merchant Anne Spangenberg to lead its fashion and lifestyle brands. Additionally, the article reports on the UK's Competition and Markets Authority launching an antitrust probe into Amazon's marketplace practices, following similar investigations in the EU, and touches on other retail news like E.l.f.'s gaming-inspired makeup and Aldi's anti-theft measures.

title: The next evolution of the Dash Cart: New features and expansion to first Whole Foods Market Store
summary: Amazon has released an upgraded version of its Dash Cart, a smart shopping cart designed to make grocery trips quicker by allowing customers to skip checkout lines. The new Dash Cart offers increased capacity, weather resistance, improved product recognition, and extended battery life, based on customer feedback and technological advancements. This enhanced version will be introduced at a Whole Foods Market store in Westford, Massachusetts, and subsequently rolled out to more Whole Foods Market and Amazon Fresh locations.

title: Behind the scenes: Same-day Amazon shipping facility in Utah preps for Prime Day 2022
summary: Amazon's same-day fulfillment center in Salt Lake City, Utah, is gearing up for Prime Day 2022, anticipating a significant spike in demand despite rising inflation. The 150,000-square-foot facility, which delivers within an hour's radius, stores essential household goods and handles 20,000 to 25,000 packages daily, a number expected to surge during the two-day sales event. The center utilizes 2,500 robotic drives to efficiently process orders, ensuring quick delivery by flex drivers using their own vehicles.

title: Cisco SD-WAN with AWS Cloud WAN for an on-demand global cloud network
summary: Cisco and AWS are collaborating to integrate Cisco SD-WAN with AWS Cloud WAN, aiming to simplify and accelerate cloud journeys for their joint customers. This integration allows organizations to connect multi-region and cloud-to-on-premises workloads with enhanced automation, unified management, and secure cloud networking. The solution will reduce deployment times, lower TCO, and provide a single portal experience for secure connectivity across AWS cloud regions.

title: AWS Selected as Delta’s Preferred Cloud Provider
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has been selected as Delta Air Lines' preferred cloud provider in a multi-year agreement. This partnership aims to accelerate Delta's digital business transformation by using AWS's cloud technologies to enhance customer experience, streamline processes, and provide cloud training to its employees globally. The collaboration will also modernize Delta's core technology platforms, including its customer engagement center operations using Amazon Connect, and extends to Amazon being Delta's most preferred carrier for business travel.

title: Amazon, Moov to have offices ready at new Tempe high-rise early next year
summary: Amazon and Moov are set to occupy offices at the new 100 Mill high-rise in Tempe starting early next year. Developed by Hines and Cousins Properties, the building is already 92% leased, with Amazon taking five floors. This development highlights ongoing growth in the Tempe office market.

title: Stock-Split Watch: Is Adobe Next?
summary: Adobe could be the next high-profile company to announce a stock split, following a trend set by others like Alphabet and Amazon in 2022. While stock splits don't alter a company's fundamental valuation, they can make shares more accessible to retail investors and help companies attract/retain talent. Adobe has a history of 2-for-1 splits, with its last one occurring 17 years ago, and its strong financial performance in Q2 2022 suggests a healthy business that could support such a move.

title: How to Buy Google Stock After the Split: GOOG vs. GOOGL
summary: Alphabet (Google's parent company) completed a 20-for-one stock split on July 15, 2022, making its shares more accessible to retail investors by lowering the per-share price. The article explains that Alphabet trades under two ticker symbols, GOOG (Class C, non-voting) and GOOGL (Class A, voting rights), both of which were affected by the split. Existing shareholders did not need to take any action as their accounts were automatically updated to reflect the new share quantity and price.

title: Prime Day 2022 was the biggest Prime Day event ever
summary: Prime Day 2022 proved to be the largest Prime Day event in Amazon's history, with members purchasing over 300 million items worldwide and saving more than $1.7 billion. This year also marked the biggest Prime Day for Amazon's small and medium-sized selling partners, whose sales growth outpaced Amazon's retail business. Customers spent over $3 billion on small business items, highlighting a significant focus on supporting these businesses during the event.

title: Online sales nationwide spike during Amazon Prime Day event
summary: Online spending in the U.S. during the 48-hour Amazon Prime Day event reached almost $12 billion, as other retailers also leveraged the period. Amazon sold over 300 million items during its Prime Day, with these sales to be reported in its fiscal third-quarter earnings. The article also touches on Amazon's expanding e-commerce lead and related sales trends.

title: Medtronic expands Health Equity Assistance Program for colon cancer screening with support from Amazon Web Services; Completes first installation of donated GI Genius™ intelligent endoscopy modules
summary: Medtronic announced the expansion of its Health Equity Assistance Program for colon cancer screening, supported by Amazon Web Services, increasing the number of donated GI Genius™ intelligent endoscopy modules from 50 to 133 across 62 facilities. Grady Memorial Hospital in Atlanta received the first installation of these modules, which use AI to detect colorectal polyps, addressing disparities in communities with low screening rates. This initiative aims to improve early detection and outcomes for colon cancer, particularly among vulnerable populations that disproportionately experience higher rates of the disease.

title: Daily deals July 17: $130 Apple TV 4K, $700 off 55-inch LG OLED Smart TV, $27 Netgear router, more
summary: This article lists the best daily deals for July 17, including significant discounts on an Apple TV 4K, a 55-inch LG OLED Smart TV, and a Netgear router. It compiles offers from various online stores like Amazon and Walmart, advising readers to act quickly as deals can be short-lived. The piece also mentions ongoing deals from the previous day.

title: Amazon’s Prime Video streamlines its user interface with a new look
summary: Amazon's Prime Video is updating its user interface and home screen, introducing new features like a "Top 10 most popular shows" list. The redesign will initially roll out to TV and Android app users in mid-August, with web and iOS users receiving the update later this year. This overhaul aims to improve the user experience ahead of major fall releases.

title: The Critiques Against the American Innovation and Choice Online Act Miss the Mark
summary: Yelp's General Counsel, Aaron Schur, argues that criticisms against the American Innovation and Choice Online Act (AICOA) are unfounded and focus on highly unlikely hypothetical abuses. He contends that concerns about the bill hindering content moderation are misguided, as existing antitrust laws and judicial precedent would prevent such misapplications. Schur asserts that the proposed edits to AICOA are unnecessary, politically unfeasible, and would weaken antitrust enforcement, urging legislators to pass the bill as is to combat real anticompetitive harms.

title: LinkedIn Still Number One Brand to be Faked in Phishing Attempts while Microsoft Surges up the Rankings to Number Two Spot in Q2 Report
summary: Check Point Research's Q2 Brand Phishing Report reveals that LinkedIn remains the most imitated brand in phishing attempts, accounting for 45% of all such attacks. Microsoft has significantly risen to the second spot with 13%, surpassing DHL, while new brands like Adidas, Adobe, and HSBC have entered the top 10. The report highlights the dangers of these scams, with examples provided for LinkedIn, DHL, Outlook, and Amazon phishing attempts.

title: This is how much more an S&P 500 CEO earned than the typical worker last year
summary: A new report from the AFL-CIO reveals that S&P 500 CEOs earned an average of $18.3 million in total compensation in 2021, which is 324 times more than their median-paid workers. This compensation marked a significant increase that outpaced inflation, further widening the gap between executive and worker pay. The report highlights this disparity at major companies like Amazon, Expedia Group, McDonald’s, and The TJX Companies.

title: 3 Reasons Why Investing in the Nasdaq-100 Is Effective
summary: This article highlights three core reasons why investing in the Nasdaq-100 is effective for long-term asset management. It explains how index investing, particularly in the Nasdaq-100, offers exposure to rapidly growing, innovative global companies with strong brand power and historically high growth rates. The article details various investment products available, such as mutual funds and ETFs, and discusses strategies like currency hedging and leverage.

title: SnapMirror in the Cloud: New Uses for NetApp Data Replication
summary: NetApp's SnapMirror, a long-standing enterprise data replication solution, is now extended to the cloud through Cloud Volumes ONTAP. This integration opens up new use cases for SnapMirror, including cloud-based disaster recovery, efficient cloud migration, application development and analytics, and multicloud architecture. It also plays a role in NetApp Cloud Backup, enhancing data protection and efficiency across hybrid and multicloud environments by leveraging block-level replication and storage efficiencies like deduplication and compression.

title: Amazon to Buy One Medical for $3.49 Billion in All-Cash Deal
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has announced its intention to acquire the primary-care company One Medical for $18 per share, an all-cash deal valued at $3.49 billion. This acquisition marks Amazon's continued expansion into the healthcare sector, making it one of the company's largest deals to date. One Medical operates medical clinics across various markets in the United States.

title: Amazon (AMAZ) to Use Rivian Vans as EV Maker Works Toward Delivery Goal
summary: Amazon.com Inc. is beginning to deliver packages to U.S. customers using electric vans manufactured by Rivian Automotive Inc. The retail giant expects to deploy thousands of these vans this year, out of a total order of up to 100,000 vehicles. This rollout marks a significant step for Rivian as it works to meet its delivery goals.

title: Walmart partnership expands The Honest Company’s reach
summary: The Honest Company is expanding its distribution network by partnering with Walmart, making its products available on Walmart's website and in physical stores this fall. This move aims to increase accessibility for customers and generate capital to support the company's growth, which has historically relied significantly on retail partnerships, including Target, Amazon, and Costco. The expansion is part of Honest Company's omni-channel retail strategy and Walmart's effort to offer trustworthy products.

title: NVDS Share Price & Live Chart | AXS 1.25X NVDA Bear Daily ETF
summary: This article provides live share price data, historical performance charts, and technical analysis for the AXS 1.25X NVDA Bear Daily ETF (NVDS). It details key metrics such as dividend yield, expense ratio, holdings, and long-term performance trends. Investors can access current share price, market data, and evaluate the ETF's suitability for their portfolio through InvestingPro's WarrenAI.

title: Receiving money via apps? Selling items online? It may affect your taxes
summary: New tax laws, specifically the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), have changed the reporting requirements for online sales. For the 2025 calendar year (2026 filing season), a 1099-K form will be issued if transactions exceed $20,000 and there are over 200 transactions. This article from H&R Block explains how to report online sales, whether for personal items, hobbies, or businesses, and provides guidance on tax forms like 1099-K, Schedule D, and Form 8949.

title: Tuya Announces Matter-compatible Solutions in Close Collaboration with Amazon Alexa
summary: Tuya Smart announced its collaboration with Amazon Alexa to implement Frustration-Free Setup (FFS) based on the Matter connectivity protocol, aiming to enhance the IoT device setup experience and allow direct Wi-Fi connections to Amazon Echo devices. Tuya, an initial FFS partner for Matter, is participating in interoperability tests with Amazon, Google, and Apple and plans to launch Matter-compatible devices by the end of 2022. The company is committed to promoting universal open standards in IoT and supporting its ecosystem partners in adopting Matter, enabling a wider variety of smart home solutions for consumers.

title: News | Amazon Buys Up Thousands of Acres As It Eyes Future Real Estate Needs
summary: Amazon is shifting its real estate strategy from leasing to owning, having spent billions on acquiring over 5,000 acres since early 2020 for logistics centers. This move comes as the e-commerce giant slows its warehouse leasing and plans to sublet millions of square feet amid economic shifts. Despite fears of a pullback, this strategic change could offer opportunities for other businesses to occupy Amazon's vacated or subleased spaces in a tight industrial market.

title: Amazon to raise Prime prices in Europe as retailer wrestles with costs
summary: Amazon announced it will increase prices for its Prime delivery and streaming service in Europe by up to 43% annually, effective September 15.
