This move follows a similar hike in the U.S. and is attributed to rising inflation, increased operating costs, and investments in faster delivery and more content. The decision comes as Amazon faces pressure to improve profitability amidst a challenging economic environment, having reported its first quarterly loss in seven years in April.

title: Rumors of Oracle Layoffs Highlight Company's Position in Cloud Race
summary: Rumors are circulating about potential Oracle layoffs affecting thousands of workers across multiple divisions, following reports from The Information and CRN. This comes as Oracle intensifies its efforts to compete in the cloud market against giants like AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud, despite facing an uphill battle in developer preference. While Oracle has secured wins like TikTok as a cloud client and offers competitive salaries for in-demand tech roles, its broad transformation to keep pace in the rapidly evolving cloud landscape is under scrutiny amid tech industry hiring slowdowns.

title: Corsair K70 Pro Mini wireless review: "one hell of a feature list"
summary: The Corsair K70 Pro Mini is the brand's first hot-swappable 60% gaming keyboard, offering numerous features like 50 onboard profiles, Bluetooth and 2.4GHz wireless connectivity, and an 8,000Hz polling rate when wired. While praised for its design and gaming performance, the typing experience can be "scratchy" due to the stock Cherry MX switches and stabilizer rattle, making it less ideal for productivity. Despite its higher price, it's considered a strong contender for gamers seeking a miniature powerhouse, though alternatives exist for those prioritizing typing feel or a lower cost.

title: RYU’s Wholesale Strategy an Unprecedented Success with Addition of Former Saks Fifth Ave SVP, National Expansion, and New Partnerships with Wholesale Icons such as Zappos, Urban Outfitters, Nordstroms, and Others
summary: RYU Apparel Inc. is experiencing dramatic growth in its Q2 2022 wholesale strategy, exceeding expectations due to the addition of Thomas Ott, former Saks Fifth Avenue SVP, to lead its wholesale push. The company announced new partnerships with major retailers like Nordstroms, Urban Outfitters, and Zappos, complementing existing relationships with Altitude Sports and Winners. RYU plans to expand its wholesale channels through traditional stockholding retailers, drop-shipping partnerships, and premium clearance accounts, with strategic goals for its Impact and Rebel collections through 2024.

title: FedEx Ground affirms ban on 3rd-party negotiations with contractors
summary: FedEx Ground has reaffirmed its policy against negotiating with third parties representing groups of contractors, insisting on individual agreements. This comes in response to a contractor, Spencer Patton, attempting to organize other contractors to collectively negotiate financial terms, citing rising costs. FedEx Ground CEO John Smith stated that such group negotiations breach existing contracts and emphasized that service reliability has improved, contrary to Patton's claims about turbulence impacting deliveries.

title: How Zoom security incident response survived the pandemic
summary: During the COVID-19 pandemic, Zoom experienced an exponential increase in users, forcing a massive, rapid scaling of its incident response operation and observability infrastructure. The company addressed security concerns by significantly expanding its security data log infrastructure and implementing new security protocols, executive hires, and a freeze on non-security related features. Leveraging AWS services like Kinesis, Lambda, and S3, Zoom built a scalable, event-driven architecture to manage petabytes of data, and further enhanced its security posture with Splunk's SOAR tool to automate incident triage and improve alert efficiency.

title: EHR on Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP
summary: This article discusses how Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP helps healthcare organizations migrate electronic health records (EHRs) to the cloud, addressing challenges like cost reduction, performance, and data management. It emphasizes the partnership between NetApp and AWS in enabling this transition, highlighting benefits such as faster upgrades and improved data protection. The solution aims to free healthcare providers from data center burdens, allowing them to focus on patient outcomes and innovation.

title: H|T: The Healthtech Times – Amazon is buying into primary care with One Medical acquisition
summary: This article from "H|T: The Healthtech Times" reports on Amazon's acquisition of primary care provider One Medical for $3.9 billion. It also covers significant developments in the healthtech sector, including Oncoustics securing $5.5 million CAD for AI-powered liver disease detection, TA Associates investing over $400 million in Intelerad, and Invitae laying off more than 1,000 employees. The piece highlights a cooling trend in digital health investment after a highly active year.

title: PLDT activates trans-Pacific cable system seen to boost Philippine int’l data capacity
summary: PLDT Inc. has activated the Jupiter Cable System, a trans-Pacific cable network that will significantly boost the Philippines' international data capacity. This $136 million investment connects the Philippines to Japan and Los Angeles, tripling PLDT's international capacity to about 60 terabits per second. The system is expected to enhance internet speed and reliability for Filipinos, supporting the nation's digital economy and data-driven industries.

title: How Amazon, Starbucks and other companies fight unions | Robert Reich
summary: Robert Reich details four common union-busting tactics employed by large corporations such as Amazon and Starbucks: anti-union propaganda, hiring expensive anti-union consultants, intentionally delaying unionization votes, and outright breaking labor laws. He highlights that despite these efforts, workers are increasingly unionizing and public opinion supports labor rights. Reich advocates for the PRO Act to strengthen protections for union organizers and level the playing field.

title: Amazon Got a Big Boost During the Pandemic—And So Did Its Carbon Emissions
summary: Amazon's carbon footprint increased by 18% in 2021, reaching 71.54 million metric tons of carbon-dioxide equivalent, marking a 40% rise since 2019. This surge is attributed to the company's rapid growth during the pandemic, despite nascent efforts to reduce its environmental impact. While Amazon's carbon intensity fell, indicating improved efficiency in operations, achieving its net-zero goal by 2040 remains a significant challenge given its expansive business model.

title: Amid economic and stock volatility, Warren Buffett remains No. 3 investor in Kroger
summary: Legendary investor Warren Buffett maintains his position as Kroger's third-largest shareholder, holding over $3 billion in shares, despite current economic and stock market volatility. Buffett, known for his "value investing" approach, favors financially sound companies like Kroger that tend to perform steadily even during tough times. His continued investment is seen as a tacit endorsement, and Kroger's shares have shown resilience, being up double digits this year, following a significant jump after his initial investment disclosure in early 2020.

title: How Capital One improves visibility into Snowflake costs
summary: Capital One has launched Capital One Software, a new business selling its internal cloud cost management and governance tools, with its first product, Slingshot, targeting the Snowflake platform. This move allows Capital One to monetize its extensive cloud migration work and helps other enterprises manage Snowflake costs and automate governance, while also showcasing Snowflake's capabilities for highly regulated industries. Slingshot has already helped Capital One reduce Snowflake costs by 27%, improve cost per query by 45%, and save over 50,000 hours of work.

title: AEO’s Supply Chain Platform Cozies Up to DHL E-Commerce
summary: American Eagle Outfitters' subsidiary, Quiet Platforms, has partnered with DHL eCommerce Solutions to offer a new delivery service to retailers and brands within its network, including Peloton, Steve Madden, Li & Fung, and Fanatics. This collaboration aims to provide affordable last-mile services and date-definitive delivery across 93 percent of U.S. postal codes without custom integration. The move furthers Quiet Platforms' goal of democratizing the national carrier network and expanding its parcel delivery footprint.

title: Etsy India appoints former Amazon executive Pankaj Jathar as VP, country head
summary: Etsy India has appointed Pankaj Jathar, a former Amazon executive and CEO of Prione, as its Vice President and Country Head for India operations. Jathar will be responsible for scaling Etsy's marketplace capabilities, identifying growth opportunities in India, and connecting local sellers with global buyers. Etsy considers India one of its seven core markets and has been investing in the country.

title: Microstrategy Outperforms Every Asset Class and Big Tech Stock Since Adopting Bitcoin Strategy, Says CEO
summary: Microstrategy CEO Michael Saylor highlights that the company's bitcoin strategy has led to significant outperformance against all asset classes and major tech stocks since its adoption. Saylor attributes this success to bitcoin's digital property characteristics and its appeal as a safe haven asset. The company's unique approach involved acquiring substantial bitcoin holdings and integrating it into their corporate strategy.

title: Veritone Creates AI-Driven Revenue Opportunities for Enterprise Media & Entertainment Companies on AWS
summary: Veritone announced an extension of its collaboration with Amazon and AWS to support its Media & Entertainment (M&E) customers, leveraging its aiWARE platform to create AI-driven revenue opportunities. By integrating with AWS, Veritone's solutions help M&E companies modernize content production, management, and monetization, including processing vast amounts of media, creating metadata, and enabling Web3 activations. The collaboration aims to provide IP owners and content creators with tools to maximize revenue and engage audiences in increasingly immersive digital environments, as evidenced by clients like the San Francisco Giants and CBS News.

title: Online Home Decor Market size to grow by USD 52.95 billion, Market Research insights highlight the rise in online spending and smartphone penetration as Key Driver - Technavio
summary: The online home decor market is projected to grow by USD 52.95 billion between 2021 and 2025, exhibiting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 9%. This growth is primarily driven by increased online spending and smartphone penetration. Europe is expected to account for 42% of this market expansion, with key contributions from the UK and Germany.

title: Amazon.com Inc. – Cost pressures surpassing healthy demand
summary: Amazon's Q2 2022 revenue exceeded expectations, but earnings were negatively impacted by a significant pre-tax valuation loss from Rivian Automotive and substantial incremental costs due to inflation, including higher fuel and freight rates. In response to these pressures, the company is focusing on improving fulfillment efficiency and adjusting staffing levels, while maintaining a NEUTRAL rating and an unchanged target price of US$133.00 due to expected suppressed earnings in the near term. AWS continues to be a strong growth driver and a focus for investment, despite overall margin pressure.

title: Amazon to Buy Maker of the Roomba for $1.7 Billion (Published 2022)
summary: Amazon announced an agreement to acquire iRobot Corp., the maker of the Roomba robotic vacuum, for $1.7 billion. This acquisition adds to Amazon's expanding smart home product portfolio, which already includes Alexa and Ring doorbells. The deal, Amazon's fourth-largest recorded acquisition, highlights an ongoing trend of tech giants expanding their reach into automated home technologies and services.

title: Better Buy: iShares S&P Core 500 Small Cap ETF or Vanguard S&P 500 ETF?
summary: This article compares the iShares S&P Core 500 Small Cap ETF (IJR) and the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), discussing their features, risks, and benefits for investors. While VOO offers stability with large-cap companies, IJR provides potential for higher upside with small-cap stocks, albeit with increased volatility. The choice between them depends on an investor's strategy, risk tolerance, and investment goals.

title: Amazon is reportedly buying Roomba vaccum maker iRobot Corp.
summary: Amazon announced its acquisition of iRobot Corp., the maker of Roomba vacuums, for $1.65 billion. This move reinforces Amazon's push into internet-connected home devices and robotics, integrating iRobot's technology with its smart home ecosystem like Alexa and Echo speakers. The acquisition comes as iRobot faces waning demand post-pandemic and patent infringement battles, while Amazon continues its strategy of acquiring companies in hot or adjacent markets, following its recent bid for One Medical's parent company.

title: CVS plans bid for Dallas-based home health provider Signify Health
summary: CVS Health Corp. is reportedly planning to bid for Dallas-based Signify Health Inc. to expand into home-health services. This move aligns with CVS CEO Karen Lynch's strategy to increase direct patient care and compete with companies like Amazon, which recently acquired One Medical. Signify Health, which went public in 2021 and recently exited the bundled health insurance payments business to focus on home health, saw its shares rise by 40% this year and reported $773.4 million in revenue in 2021.

title: Gymboree, the iconic brand of The Children’s Place, launches on Amazon
summary: Gymboree, a brand under The Children's Place, has expanded its reach by launching on Amazon.com, offering its playful apparel to a broader customer base. This strategic move aims to leverage Amazon's platform for significant growth, complementing Gymboree's existing online presence and availability in over 100 Children's Place retail stores. Jane Elfers, president and CEO, highlighted the excitement about the collaboration and its potential for brand exposure.

title: iRobot and Its Roombas Could Give Amazon a Window Into Your Home
summary: Amazon's acquisition of iRobot for $1.7 billion raises concerns about user data privacy, specifically regarding the information Roombas gather about homes. The article suggests Amazon is interested in this data to enhance targeted advertising and potentially integrate iRobot's technology into its smart home ecosystem. It highlights how Roomba's mapping capabilities and object recognition, combined with Amazon's history of data utilization, could provide the tech giant with unprecedented insights into consumers' living spaces.

title: Overtime Raises $100 Million in Series D Funding Round Led by Liberty Media Corporation and Counterpoint Global (Morgan Stanley)
summary: Overtime announced it has raised $100 million in Series D funding led by Liberty Media Corporation and Counterpoint Global (Morgan Stanley), bringing its total funding to over $250 million. The investment will fuel the expansion of its disruptive sports leagues, Overtime Elite (OTE) and OT7, and support the creation of new leagues, e-commerce, and web3 initiatives. Notable investors include Bezos Expeditions, Blackstone, and Sapphire Sport, reflecting confidence in Overtime's growth potential and its ability to engage Gen Z and Millennial sports communities.

title: Chuck Schumer’s Family Is Getting Nice Work on Wall Street
summary: The article highlights how Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer's family members and former staffers are being hired by major corporations like Blackstone, Amazon, Intel, and Google to lobby on legislation before the Senate. This raises concerns about potential conflicts of interest, especially regarding Schumer's role in advancing or delaying legislation that impacts these companies. Specifically, it details Blackstone's hiring of Schumer's son-in-law around the time a tax loophole benefiting private equity was being debated and Schumer's delay in bringing antitrust legislation against tech monopolies to a vote, given his daughters' employment by these tech giants.

title: Tech, Cyber Companies Launch Security Standard to Monitor Hacking Attempts
summary: A consortium of 18 technology and cybersecurity companies, including AWS, Splunk, and IBM, have launched a new common data standard called the Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework (OCSF). This initiative aims to standardize the sharing of cybersecurity information, addressing a long-standing issue for corporate security chiefs regarding the lack of integration between various proprietary cyber products. The goal is to improve the assessment of hacking threats by creating a unified approach to data.

title: Megazone Cloud receives $343 mn from MBK, IMM PE in Series C funding
summary: Megazone Cloud, South Korea’s largest managed cloud service provider, has closed its Series C funding round with 450 billion won ($343 million) from MBK Partners and IMM Private Equity. This investment, combined with an earlier one from KT Corp., brings the company's valuation to 2.4 trillion won, making it a unicorn. Megazone Cloud is also preparing for an IPO by the end of 2023.

title: Amazon is partnering with GE and GE Appliances to stop counterfeiters
summary: Amazon, alongside General Electric (GE) and GE Appliances, has filed a joint lawsuit against 16 defendants for selling counterfeit GE-branded water filters. This action is part of Amazon's broader effort to protect customers and brands from counterfeiting through proactive controls, brand protection tools, and holding fraudsters accountable. GE Appliances emphasized the risks associated with fake filters, which fail to provide adequate filtration and can damage appliances.

title: How Microsoft secures its spot among the world's most valuable brands
summary: Microsoft has become one of the world's most valuable brands, with its brand value reaching $661 billion in 2022, securing the number four spot in Kantar BrandZ Most Valuable Global Brands. The company's success is attributed to its meaningful, different, and salient offerings, including superior cloud, software, and hardware products, as well as its commitment to sustainability. Under CEO Satya Nadella, Microsoft has focused on specific market segments, leading to a tenfold increase in brand value and solidifying its position as the world's most valuable business services brand, further boosted by the continued digital transformation and remote work trends.

title: The Forrester Wave™: Anti-Money Laundering Solutions, Q2 2025
summary: SAS has been recognized as a leader in The Forrester Wave™: Anti-Money Laundering Solutions, Q2 2025 report. Forrester notes that SAS's recent acquisition of Hazy synthetic data software is expected to enhance its Financial Crime Compliance portfolio, especially with large data models and Generative AI. The report highlights SAS's AML solution as suitable for enterprises with existing SAS and data science skills, emphasizing its strong framework for quantifying ROI.

title: Walmart Reaches Video-Streaming Deal to Offer Paramount+ to Members
summary: Walmart Inc. has partnered with Paramount Global to offer its Paramount+ streaming service to subscribers of Walmart's membership program, Walmart+. This move is part of Walmart's strategy to attract more members and compete with Amazon.com Inc.'s successful Prime membership program. The deal makes an ad-supported version of Paramount+ available as a perk for Walmart+ subscribers.

title: Walmart partners with Paramount+ to better compete with Amazon Prime
summary: Walmart announced a deal with Paramount Global to include the Paramount+ streaming service as a perk for its Walmart+ shipping subscription members, aiming to better compete with Amazon Prime. This addition will not change the Walmart+ annual fee of $98 or monthly fee of $12.95, and comes as many other streaming services are increasing prices. The move helps Walmart enhance its membership offering, which also includes free shipping and gasoline discounts.

title: Future Farm launches at Amazon Fresh and Sprouts
summary: Future Farm, a leading Brazilian plant-based meat company, has expanded its U.S. distribution by launching products nationwide on Amazon Fresh and making its retail store debut at Sprouts Farmers Market in Southern California. This move follows initial e-commerce sales in July 2021 and aligns with the company's mission to democratize plant-based food and accelerate international growth. The company also plans to introduce new products like Future Chick'n and Future Tuna in the U.S. and expand into plant-based drinks and dairy.

title: Google Strikes Back at Sonos; SmartSpeaker War Gets Ugly
summary: Google has sued Sonos for patent infringement, escalating an ongoing battle over smart home audio technology. This comes after Sonos previously won a ruling against Google regarding similar claims. The conflict highlights broader issues of antitrust concerns and data privacy in the smart speaker market, with Sonos launching its own privacy-focused voice assistant.

title: Amazon was Roomba marketer's biggest customer long before $1.7 billion deal
summary: Amazon was the largest customer for Roomba's marketer well in advance of its $1.7 billion acquisition deal. This indicates a pre-existing significant business relationship between the two companies.

title: BigCommerce Named as a Challenger in 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Digital Commerce Platforms for Third Consecutive Year
summary: BigCommerce has been recognized as a Challenger in the 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Commerce for the third consecutive year. This recognition highlights BigCommerce's ability to provide flexible e-commerce solutions for enterprise businesses, supported by recent acquisitions and global expansion. The evaluation was based on the company's Completeness of Vision and Ability to Execute in providing core technology for interactive digital commerce experiences.

title: Amazon (AWS) and Microsoft Data Centers in Virginia
summary: This article details the extensive presence of Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Microsoft data centers across Virginia, highlighting locations in both Northern Virginia and emerging areas like Boydton. It outlines specific data center sites, their sizes, and ongoing development plans for both companies, emphasizing their investments and strategic growth in the region. The report also covers their respective cloud regions, availability zones, and cloud on-ramps to support demand for compute, storage, and database capabilities.

title: Qualcomm planning return to server market with new chip - Bloomberg News
summary: Qualcomm Inc. is reportedly planning a return to the server market with a new chip, aiming to reduce its dependence on smartphones. The company is seeking customers for a product based on its Nuvia Inc. acquisition, with Amazon Web Services (AWS) agreeing to evaluate the offerings. This move comes as Qualcomm faces a slowdown in smartphone demand, impacting its core handset chip business.

title: How Amazon’s DSP program has created $26 billion in revenue for owners
summary: Amazon's Delivery Service Partner (DSP) program has generated over $26 billion in revenue for small businesses since its inception four years ago. The program has enabled nearly 3,000 entrepreneurs to scale their operations, employ 275,000 drivers, and deliver over 10 million packages daily across more than 14 countries. Amazon continues to expand the DSP program, focusing on supporting underrepresented entrepreneurs and developing sustainable, carbon-neutral delivery methods such as electric vehicles.

title: Amazfit、新スマートウォッチ「GTS 4 Mini」。薄型軽量／15日間バッテリー
summary: Zepp Health CorporationがAmazfitブランドから新スマートウォッチ「Amazfit GTS 4 Mini」を発売しました。このスマートウォッチは、31.2gの薄型軽量デザインと最大15日間のロングバッテリーライフが特徴で、価格は16,800円（税込）です。直販ストアや主要ECサイトでのオンライン限定販売となります。

title: Amazon’s Signify Health Interest Another Indicator Of At-Home Care Capabilities Buildout
summary: Amazon is reportedly interested in acquiring Signify Health, a value-based care platform with significant home-based care capabilities, just a month after purchasing One Medical. This move signals Amazon's continued expansion into the healthcare sector, particularly strengthening its at-home care offerings. The acquisition could position Amazon to better appeal to payers by leveraging Signify's in-home assessments and integration with the healthcare ecosystem.

title: Signify Health shares jump as Amazon, UnitedHealth reportedly among bidders for takeover deal
summary: Signify Health's shares surged after reports indicated Amazon and UnitedHealth Group are among the bidders for the home health technology and services provider in a deal potentially exceeding $8 billion. This comes as Signify focuses on its home and community services segment and other major players like CVS, Walgreens, and Walmart intensify their efforts in the healthcare market. A potential acquisition by Amazon would mark its second major healthcare deal in 2022, while for UnitedHealth, it aligns with its vertical integration strategy.

title: You may be eligible for part of a $190M Capital One settlement: How to know
summary: Capital One has reached a $190 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit following a 2019 data breach that affected over 100 million customer accounts. Eligible individuals, who were notified by Capital One, can claim reimbursement for lost time and out-of-pocket expenses related to the breach, or enroll in identity defense services. The deadline to file a claim has been extended to September 30, 2022, with a final approval hearing for the settlement scheduled for September 8.

title: These Wide-Moat Stocks Are Gaining Ground
summary: Quality stocks with wide economic moats, including Microsoft and Amazon, have outperformed since the bear market low in June, despite still being undervalued. While narrow-moat stocks have seen stronger recent gains due to market-cap weighting of companies like Apple and Tesla, wide-moat companies are better positioned to handle inflation due to their pricing power. Overall, Morningstar analysts consider wide-moat stocks to be deeply undervalued compared to overpriced narrow-moat stocks.

title: Amazon says it will shut down Amazon Care, its primary and urgent health care business.
summary: Amazon announced it is shutting down its primary and urgent health care service, Amazon Care, by the end of the year. This decision comes shortly after the company revealed plans to acquire One Medical, a larger health care competitor, for $3.9 billion. Amazon Care, which began in 2019 for Amazon employees and later expanded, struggled to attract large enterprise customers, leading to the conclusion that it was not a complete enough offering for long-term viability.

title: Amazon Plans to Shut Amazon Care Service Amid One Medical Overlap (AMZN)
summary: Amazon.com Inc. is shutting down its Amazon Care primary care and telehealth service by the end of the year. This decision comes shortly after the company announced its intention to acquire the One Medical chain of clinics, indicating an overlap in services. Senior Vice President Neil Lindsay communicated the closure to the Amazon health care team, stating that Amazon Care was not a "complete enough offering."

title: Amazon strikes green hydrogen deal with fuel cell maker Plug Power, sending shares up
summary: Amazon has struck a deal with fuel cell maker Plug Power to power some of its operations with green hydrogen, causing Plug Power's stock to surge 9%, while Amazon's shares also closed up 2.6%. Under the agreement, Plug will provide 10,950 tons of green hydrogen annually for Amazon's transportation and building operations starting in 2025. This deal includes a warrant for Amazon to purchase up to 16 million shares of Plug Power, contingent on Amazon spending $2.1 billion on Plug products over seven years.

title: Amazon mothballing virtual health care service after admitting, ‘It is not a complete enough offering’
summary: Amazon is discontinuing its telehealth service, Amazon Care, by December 31, 2022, citing that it is "not a complete enough offering" for large enterprise customers. This decision comes despite the company's broader expansion into healthcare through acquisitions like PillPack and One Medical, and marks a potential setback for the telehealth industry as demand has reportedly declined. However, Amazon's other healthcare initiatives remain unaffected.

title: Musk's SpaceX and T-Mobile plan to connect mobile phones to satellites, boost cell coverage
summary: T-Mobile US Inc. announced a partnership with Elon Musk's SpaceX Starlink to provide satellite-based mobile network access to users in remote areas of the United States. This service will allow mobile phones to connect directly to Starlink satellites, initially supporting texting services in a beta phase, thereby extending coverage beyond traditional cell towers. The plan aims to use T-Mobile's mid-band spectrum with SpaceX's next-generation satellites, eliminating the need for new phones for existing T-Mobile customers.

title: Amazon not expected to bid for Electronic Arts, says CNBC
summary: CNBC reported that Amazon.com Inc is not expected to bid for Electronic Arts Inc, refuting an earlier report about a potential takeover. This news caused EA's stock to fluctuate, initially jumping 15% on the acquisition rumor before settling to a 4% gain. Amazon has recently been active in acquisitions, diversifying its business, while analysts see EA as an attractive target due to its strong licenses and metaverse potential, despite a struggling global gaming industry.

title: Amazon announces 750-job sortation center in Liberty Commerce Center
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has opened a new 517,000-square-foot sortation center in the Liberty Commerce Center. This facility is Amazon's 11th announced and 12th known location in the Kansas City metro area. The new center is expected to create 750 jobs.

title: Thermo Fisher Opens $105M Nashville-Area Tech Plant
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. has opened a new 400,000-square-foot bioprocessing production plant in Lebanon, Tenn., near Nashville. This $105 million facility, developed by Panattoni within Speedway Industrial Park, is the company's largest single-use manufacturing site and is expected to create up to 1,400 jobs. The development highlights Nashville's growing prominence as a life science hub, supported by institutions like Vanderbilt University Medical Center.

title: Amazon Fresh to open in revamped Montgomery County shopping center, documents show
summary: Amazon Fresh is planning to open a new grocery store in a redeveloped shopping center in Montgomery County, Pennsylvania. This location marks another expansion for Amazon Fresh in the Philadelphia region, following other announced area stores. The information was revealed through official documents.

title: Announcing VMware Cloud on AWS integration with Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP
summary: NetApp announces the integration of VMware Cloud on AWS with Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP, offering an integrated, certified, and supported external NFS datastore. This solution allows for independent scaling of compute and storage in the cloud, leading to significant cost savings (up to 25-50% TCO reduction) and simplified data management for data-intensive VMware workloads. The partnership leverages 20 years of collaboration between NetApp and VMware to reduce barriers to cloud migration.

title: Songwriters and Music Publishers to Be Paid More by Streaming Services
summary: Music publishers and streaming services have reached an agreement to increase the rates paid to songwriters for their music played on digital platforms like Spotify. This deal, struck between the National Music Publishers Association and the Digital Media Association, means publishers will receive up to 15.35% of music streamers’ revenue, which will be phased in over a five-year period. The agreement was finalized just before a trial was set to begin in front of the Copyright Royalty Board.

title: SpaceX, T-Mobile race to put an end to cell phone dead zones with help from outer space
summary: T-Mobile and SpaceX are collaborating to launch "Coverage Above and Beyond" service by late 2023, aiming to eliminate cellular dead zones across the continental U.S., Hawaii, parts of Alaska, and surrounding waters using SpaceX's Starlink satellites. The service will initially offer basic text and voice communications through existing smartphones, leveraging T-Mobile's 1900 MHz frequency. This initiative marks a significant step toward ubiquitous cellular coverage, with other companies like Apple and AST SpaceMobile also developing similar satellite-based phone connectivity solutions.

title: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP with VMware Cloud now available on AWS
summary: NetApp, VMware, and Amazon Web Services (AWS) have announced the general availability of Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP with VMware Cloud on AWS. This new solution allows enterprises to cost-effectively move data-demanding workloads to VMware Cloud on AWS, providing independent scaling of cloud storage and compute. This offering aims to optimize costs, enable new application deployment, and enhance data management with ONTAP's features.

title: American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) embraces fulfillment at the edge
summary: American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) is innovating its e-fulfillment strategy to meet increasing customer expectations for speed. The article details AEO's evolution from a traditional distribution center model to a more decentralized "fulfillment at the edge" approach, including the acquisition of Quiet Logistics and AirTerra to position inventory closer to customers and leverage automation and optimization software. This strategy aims to overcome labor challenges and compete with larger retailers by enhancing delivery speed and efficiency.

title: Lakeland is home to world-class companies including Publix, Amazon, GEICO
summary: This article highlights Lakeland, Florida's status as a hub for major companies and identifies its 15 largest employers. It details key information about each employer, including their employee count and their contribution to the local economy. The city's strategic location between Orlando and Tampa is noted as a factor in attracting such businesses.

title: The Untold Truth Of Steak 'N Shake
summary: Steak 'n Shake, a burger and milkshake chain founded in 1934, has experienced significant transformations and challenges despite its longevity. Changes include its parent company, Biglari Holdings, also owning Maxim magazine, a unique and inexpensive franchise partner program, and efforts to operate internationally. The chain has faced recent struggles with numerous store closings, a shift from full-service dining to self-service kiosks, lawsuits from franchisees and employees, and a near bankruptcy experience.

title: It’s not just you. Tommy Bahama beach chairs are everywhere
summary: Tommy Bahama has unexpectedly dominated the beach chair market, controlling an estimated 80% of chairs priced over $30, despite initially struggling as a brand. Their success stems from a savvy licensing deal with ShelterLogic Group, strategic distribution through Costco, and innovative chair features like backpack straps. The chairs have become a powerful marketing tool, reinforcing Tommy Bahama's lifestyle brand and driving sales for other beach gear.

title: Amazon’s ‘Lord of the Rings’ Series Draws 25 Million on Debut
summary: Amazon's "Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power" series attracted over 25 million viewers globally on its premiere day, marking the largest series debut in the history of Prime Video. The show is set thousands of years before "The Hobbit" within J.R.R. Tolkien's universe. This significant viewership highlights the massive anticipation and Amazon's investment in the fantasy epic.

title: CVS takes lead in talks to acquire Signify Health: report
summary: CVS Health Corp. has reportedly taken the lead in talks to acquire home healthcare company Signify Health Inc., with a bid of around $8 billion. This move aligns with CVS's strategy to expand its in-home health services, an area also eyed by competitors like Amazon.com and UnitedHealth Group. A deal could be announced next week, valuing Signify at over $30 per share.

title: Amazon Fresh to Open in Revamped Montgomery County Shopping Center, Documents Show
summary: Amazon Fresh is planning to open a new grocery store in the Willow Grove Shopping Center in Montgomery County, Pennsylvania, as part of a significant renovation of the retail complex. Amazon has applied for a liquor license at the site of a former Barnes & Noble, and their Fresh stores offer a lower-priced alternative to Whole Foods. This new location is one of several Amazon Fresh stores planned for the greater Philadelphia area.

title: Amazon discriminating against Asians, whites in grant program, class action lawsuit says
summary: A class action lawsuit alleges that Amazon discriminates against Asian and white applicants in its "Diversity Grant" program, which offers stipends to Black, Latinx, and Native American entrepreneurs. The lawsuit, filed by Crystal Bolduc, claims the program violates the Civil Rights Act of 1866 by forcing Asian and white hopefuls to cover their own startup costs. Bolduc seeks to end the program and obtain damages for those who have experienced racial discrimination due to it.

title: CVS Health to acquire Signify Health for $8B
summary: CVS Health announced its plan to acquire home health care company Signify Health for approximately $8 billion. The deal involves CVS acquiring Signify for $30.50 per share in cash, intending to enhance its connection to consumers in their homes and expand product offerings. The acquisition, which faced competition from industry giants like Amazon and UnitedHealth, is expected to be accretive to CVS's earnings and close in the first half of 2023.

title: Beating Other Bidders, CVS Health Inks Deal To Acquire Dallas’ Signify Health in $8B Deal
summary: CVS Health is acquiring Dallas-based Signify Health for approximately $8 billion, beating out other bidders like Amazon. This acquisition will expand CVS's footprint in the value-based care market and enhance its connection to consumers in their homes through Signify's tech-enabled home health services. Signify Health CEO Kyle Armbrester will continue to lead the company's 600-person team as part of CVS Health.

title: Qurate Snags Amazon, Macy’s Execs to Accelerate Video Commerce Growth
summary: Qurate Retail Group, parent company of QVC and HSN, has appointed Soumya Sriraman (formerly of Amazon) as President of Streaming and Stacy Bowe (formerly of Macy's) as Chief Merchandising Officer for QVC U.S. These strategic hires aim to accelerate the company's video commerce growth and differentiate its home shopping brands. The appointments are part of Qurate's broader restructuring efforts and Project Athens turnaround plan.

title: How to Buy NASDAQ Stocks
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to buy NASDAQ stocks, detailing what the NASDAQ is, who owns its shares, and the pros and cons of investing in them. It covers the steps to purchase NASDAQ stocks, from fixing a budget and choosing a broker to placing an order, and includes a brief history of the exchange. The article also mentions specific index funds and prominent companies listed on the NASDAQ.

title: Amazon Breaches TV’s Last Stronghold With $13 Billion Bet on NFL
summary: Amazon is making an aggressive move into live sports broadcasting with an 11-year, $13 billion exclusive deal for "Thursday Night Football" on its Prime Video streaming service. This marks a significant shift in how NFL games are consumed, requiring fans to subscribe to Amazon Prime to watch. The first regular-season game under this new agreement will be between the Los Angeles Chargers and Kansas City Chiefs on September 15.

title: Amazon CEO Says the Next Decade Will See Company Growth in Every Area
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy articulated a vision for the company's significant growth across various sectors in the next decade, including retail, AWS, Alexa, and healthcare. He emphasized Amazon's focus on customer experience and addressed concerns regarding antitrust legislation and unionization. Jassy believes the company's current business is merely a "shadow" of its future potential.

title: Why Allstate's CHRO believes HR needs to run more like a business
summary: Robert Toohey, Allstate's new CHRO, is leveraging his background in tech and leadership roles outside of HR to transform the company's human resources department. He emphasizes the importance of HR operating with a business-first mindset, focusing on data and technology to improve employee experience and contribute directly to business outcomes. Toohey aims to treat HR as a service provider that adds value to the organization by aligning talent management with digital transformation goals.

title: Here's Why This E-commerce Stock is Undervalued
summary: MercadoLibre Inc (NASDAQ:MELI), a Latin American e-commerce giant, is showing signs of recovery despite recent dips, with indicators suggesting low volatility expectations among options traders. The company boasts significant revenue and earnings growth, justifying its high valuation metrics. Analysts anticipate continued strong growth for MELI in fiscal years 2022 and 2023, making it an attractive option for growth investors.

title: AP: American Eagle Exec Works To Modernize The Supply Chain
summary: Shekar Natarajan, American Eagle Outfitters' chief supply chain officer, is modernizing retail supply chains by creating a shared platform accessible to other companies, including over 100 partners and 14 retailers. This initiative aims to address the inefficiencies and unsustainability of current logistics networks, particularly accelerated by the pandemic. Natarajan believes small companies can pool resources to compete with larger entities like Amazon and Walmart, leveraging a model that consolidates packages for more efficient delivery and reduced shipping times and miles.

title: The miracle of streaming
summary: The article discusses the transformative impact of streaming technology on the world, from early internet speeds to today's high-resolution video consumption and its role in daily life and global data usage. It explores the history, underlying technology, and future implications of streaming, including its integration with AR/VR, 5G, and the Internet of Things, highlighting how networking has enabled this "miracle."

title: Amazon Prime Members Score Exclusive Deals and Get Access to VIP Experiences This Football Season
summary: Amazon is offering Prime members exclusive deals and VIP experiences for the upcoming Thursday Night Football season, streaming on Prime Video. Members can find discounts on home entertainment devices and NFL-licensed products at amazon.com/TNFcentral, alongside unique gameday experiences, including a kickoff party with Jerry Rice and VIP stadium access in 15 cities. These initiatives aim to enhance the gameday viewing experience and provide greater value for Prime subscribers.

title: DraftKings Will Be Exclusive Odds Provider for Amazon NFL Games
summary: DraftKings (DKNG) has secured a multiyear agreement to be the exclusive pregame and in-game odds provider for Amazon.com Inc.'s (AMZN) Thursday Night Football games. This partnership includes DraftKings content featuring in all 15 NFL telecasts on Amazon this season, starting September 15, and will offer same-game parlays through the DraftKings Sportsbook app. Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.

title: Here’s why the founder of the Zara clothing empire is buying up U.S. warehouses
summary: Amancio Ortega, founder of Zara, has invested hundreds of millions of dollars in U.S. warehouse properties through his family investment company, Pontegadea. This move signals his confidence in the future of warehousing despite some companies like Amazon downsizing their logistics. Ortega's acquisitions come as the pandemic has increased demand for goods, leading to a heated market for warehouse space and higher rental rates, although some experts question if this trend will continue.

title: Emerge EMPWR Unified Sustainable Equity ETF
summary: This article provides a detailed profile of the Emerge EMPWR Unified Sustainable Equity ETF (EMPW), noting it is no longer active. It covers key financial data, including its expense ratio, inception date, AUM, and historical trading data. The profile also lists its top holdings, concentration analysis, performance metrics, geographic and sector exposures, and technical indicators, offering a comprehensive overview for investors.

title: Amazon.com sued by California state over alleged price competition antitrust violation
summary: California has filed a lawsuit against Amazon.com Inc, alleging the company violated antitrust laws by preventing price competition. The suit claims Amazon's policies prohibit merchants from offering lower prices on their own websites or rivals' platforms, thus establishing a price floor that inflates costs for consumers. Amazon, which argues the lawsuit could lead to higher prices for customers, states it has the right not to highlight uncompetitively priced offers.

title: Meet Dave & Buster’s New, Futuristic Revenue Stream
summary: Dave & Buster's is piloting a new "Game & Go" micro-market, developed in partnership with Coca-Cola and powered by Zippin's checkout-free technology, to address issues with food and beverage attachment among gamers. This automated store, located in Hollywood, Florida, offers snacks and drinks, including alcohol with ID monitoring, and aims to increase revenue without cannibalizing existing sales. The initiative reflects Dave & Buster's broader strategy to diversify revenue streams beyond traditional restaurant offerings, following significant financial growth and the acquisition of Main Event.

title: Capital One released from consent order related to 2019 data breach
summary: Capital One has been released from a consent order imposed in 2020 by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) after a data breach in 2019 exposed personal information of 100 million credit card applicants. The OCC determined that Capital One had achieved a level of "safety and soundness" that no longer required the additional oversight. The original consent order required Capital One to pay an $80 million fine and form a compliance committee to address risk management, board accountability, and auditing issues.

title: FedEx warning drives worst decline in stock, deepens slowdown fears
summary: FedEx Corp's shares experienced their worst day ever, closing at their lowest price since the early pandemic months, after the company pulled its forecast due to fears of a global demand slowdown. This outlook fueled investor anxiety regarding a potential recession as the U.S. Federal Reserve raises interest rates to combat inflation. The stock drop resulted in a $12.5 billion market capitalization loss and put increased pressure on CEO Raj Subramaniam for a quick turnaround amidst existing company-specific challenges.

title: OCC frees Capital One from consent order tied to 2019 breach
summary: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) has terminated a 2020 consent order against Capital One, stemming from a 2019 data breach that affected 106 million accounts. The OCC determined that Capital One had achieved a sufficient level of "safety and soundness" and compliance, no longer requiring additional oversight. This move follows Capital One's payment of an $80 million penalty and its commitment to improving risk management and compliance practices after the breach.

title: Colgate Marches On With AI-Infused Product Innovation
summary: Colgate-Palmolive is using AI and AR for a new tooth-whitening experience, partnering with Perfect Corp. for an AR tool that lets consumers visualize teeth whitening results on their smartphones. This innovation, focusing on the Colgate Optic White Overnight Teeth Whitening Pen, aims to enhance the consumer journey and support both online and in-store sales by making products more accessible and engaging. The company anticipates this is the first of many such partnerships as it seeks to be a leader in teeth whitening.

title: Federal Regulators Request More Information on Amazon-IRobot Deal
summary: The Federal Trade Commission has requested additional information regarding Amazon's $1.7 billion acquisition of iRobot, maker of Roomba. This request extends the antitrust waiting period and effectively turns a formality into a full investigation. Both Bedford, Massachusetts-based iRobot Corp. and Amazon.com Inc. received the request.

title: FTC seeks more data on Amazon's $1.7-billion deal for vacuum maker iRobot
summary: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has requested more information from Amazon.com Inc and iRobot Corp regarding Amazon's proposed $1.7-billion acquisition of the Roomba vacuum maker. The FTC's request for details, which came on September 19, follows concerns raised by privacy and worker organizations about the deal's impact on privacy and Amazon's market influence in smart home devices and retail. The probe will examine both head-to-head competition and potential illegal boosts to Amazon's market share.

title: PR News | WPP Scoops Up JeffreyGroup - Wed., Sep. 21, 2022
summary: WPP has acquired Miami-based JeffreyGroup, an agency with over 330 staffers and a significant presence in Latin America. JeffreyGroup, founded in 1993, will retain its brand within Hill+Knowlton Strategies, with its current CEO, Brian Burlingame, leading H+K's Latin American operations. This acquisition aims to strengthen WPP's regional presence and leverage JeffreyGroup's established experience in delivering tailored communications.

title: R2 is now Generally Available
summary: Cloudflare announced the general availability of R2, its object storage service. R2 differentiates itself by eliminating egress fees, addressing a major cost concern for developers using traditional cloud providers. The service offers S3 compatibility, automatic regional optimization, integration with Cloudflare Workers, and transparent pricing based on storage volume and operation types, with a free tier for initial usage.

title: American Eagle logistics arm Quiet Platforms partners with FourKites
summary: American Eagle Outfitters' logistics arm, Quiet Platforms, has partnered with supply chain visibility leader FourKites to offer real-time inventory tracking for its customers. This collaboration aims to enhance supply chain efficiency, reduce lead times, and improve the delivery experience for end consumers by providing complete visibility throughout the network. The partnership supports Quiet Platforms' goal of becoming an "anti-Amazon" logistics powerhouse.

title: FTC says Bezos, Jassy must testify in probe of Amazon Prime
summary: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has ordered Amazon founder Jeff Bezos and CEO Andy Jassy to testify in an investigation into Amazon Prime's sign-up and cancellation practices. The FTC denied Amazon's request to cancel the subpoenas, setting a January 20 deadline for testimony from Bezos, Jassy, and 15 other executives. Amazon expressed disappointment but noted the FTC modified some subpoena provisions and stated it has cooperated with the investigation, providing tens of thousands of documents.

title: Home Depot Workers Want To Form The Chain’s First Store Union
summary: Workers at a Home Depot store in Northeast Philadelphia have filed a petition to unionize, aiming to form the chain's first store union. This effort is being led by five-year veteran Vince Quiles, who collected over 100 signed union cards, inspired by recent successful unionization campaigns at other major retailers like Starbucks and Amazon. The movement highlights a broader trend of increased labor organizing activity in the U.S.

title: Tractor Supply Co. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
summary: Tractor Supply Co. (TSCO) stock fell 1.50% to $185.59 on a day when the broader market, including the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average, experienced sharper declines. Despite its own losses and being down for the fourth consecutive day, TSCO outperformed the overall market. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high but showed mixed performance compared to competitors like Amazon, Home Depot, and Lowe's.

title: What Kroger, Walmart, Target learned from China's Alibaba about grocery's future
summary: Kroger, Walmart, and Target have adopted an "Omnichannel Retail" strategy, a concept pioneered by Alibaba's Jack Ma in China with his "New Retail" model of high-tech Freshippo supermarkets. This strategy integrates brick-and-mortar stores with online orders, logistics, and data analytics to enhance efficiency and customer experience. Kroger, in particular, has seen significant digital growth, aiming to double its digital revenues by the end of 2023, and investing heavily in automation and supply chain management.

title: Introducing Amazon’s Prime Early Access Sale—A New Holiday Shopping Event for Members to Save Big October 11 and October 12
summary: Amazon has announced its first-ever Prime Early Access Sale, a new 48-hour global shopping event exclusively for Prime members, taking place on October 11 and 12. This event offers early access to holiday deals, including significant discounts on major brands and Amazon's lowest prices of the year on select products. Members can also find holiday shopping inspiration through new gift guides and benefit from various Prime offers, including free trials for Amazon Music Unlimited and Grubhub+.

title: Omnicom launches online retail practice as clients seek full-funnel view
summary: Omnicom Group has launched Transact, a new online retail practice aimed at providing clients with connected-commerce consulting and a full-funnel view of the marketing pipeline. This initiative comes as advertising revenue for retail media networks is rapidly expanding, with GroupM forecasting $101 billion in ad spend this year. Transact leverages Omnicom's partnerships with major platforms like Amazon, Instacart, Kroger, and Walmart to offer comprehensive data and strategy insights.

title: Pennsylvania Court Holds for Non-Resident Amazon Merchants in Interesting Nexus Case
summary: The Commonwealth Court of Pennsylvania ruled that storing inventory in the state through Amazon's Fulfillment By Amazon program does not automatically establish sales or income tax nexus for non-resident merchants. The court found that these merchants lack sufficient "minimum contacts" because they have no control over inventory location and no direct interaction with customers in Pennsylvania. This decision provides significant guidance in the nexus arena, especially for income tax purposes, as marketplace facilitator rules address most sales tax issues.

title: Cloudification of Test Software
summary: The article discusses the increasing movement of major software tools, particularly in the wireless communications sector, to the cloud. It highlights how cloud-native RAN (Radio Access Network) and Open RAN (O-RAN) architectures leverage cloud benefits like reduced IT overhead, scalability, and resource optimization for modern 5G deployments. The author also emphasizes how testing methodologies are evolving with this shift, requiring test software to adopt cloud capabilities and move towards a "test-as-a-service" model.

title: Amazon will rebrand Epix as MGM+
summary: Amazon plans to rebrand its Epix streaming service as MGM+. This change is part of Amazon's strategy after acquiring MGM. The rebranding will align the service more closely with the iconic MGM brand.

title: Amazon’s Latest Devices Push It Deeper Into Wellness, Cars
summary: Amazon's annual device event introduced new products that expand its presence in wellness, security, and the automotive sector. Key announcements included the Halo Rise, a bedside sleep tracker, updated Echo smart speakers, a new Kindle, and advancements in Ring security technology. These offerings highlight Amazon's strategy to integrate its technology seamlessly into consumers' daily routines.

title: How Wolverine Worldwide Uses 3D Models To Do Business Better in the 21st Century
summary: Wolverine Worldwide is leveraging 3D models and digital assets to enhance its connection with retailers and adapt to the evolving retail landscape. The company, in partnership with Envoy B2B, Checkmate, and Ocavu, is creating immersive virtual showrooms and enabling augmented reality for wholesale buyers. This strategic shift is driven by increasing consumer expectations for 3D experiences, as seen with Amazon's virtual try-on services, and aims to future-proof wholesale channels and empower various aspects of the business, from design to social selling.

title: Peloton Strikes First Brick-and-Mortar Deal With Dick’s
summary: Peloton has partnered with Dick's Sporting Goods, the largest sports goods retailer in the U.S., to sell its equipment in over 100 stores. This marks Peloton's first brick-and-mortar retail deal outside its own stores, following a recent partnership with Amazon. The move is part of Peloton's strategy to rebound after a pandemic-driven surge in demand cooled, leading to job cuts, reduced retail presence, and price increases for some products.

title: 3 Warren Buffett Stocks With 105% to 137% Upside, According to Wall Street
summary: This article highlights three Warren Buffett stocks that Wall Street analysts believe have significant upside potential, ranging from 105% to 137% over the next year. These include Amazon, expected to double due to its high-margin segments like AWS and Prime; StoneCo, benefiting from digital payment adoption in Brazil; and Paramount Global, buoyed by the growth of its streaming division and film entertainment resurgence. The article also discusses potential headwinds for StoneCo and Paramount Global, but maintains a bullish outlook based on analyst price targets.

title: Best Christmas Stocks for 2026
summary: This article identifies top retail and e-commerce stocks positioned for a holiday season boost, advising investors to consider purchases before Black Friday given market forward-looking nature. It features Amazon, Best Buy, Target, eBay, and Etsy, highlighting their individual market positions, financials, and potential for growth. Additionally, it suggests looking into shipping, railroad, and food and beverage sectors for seasonal investment opportunities, emphasizing the predictable sales increase during Christmas.

title: India: Amazon - Future Group Dispute: Revisiting the Standard of Disclosure in M&A Filings
summary: This article examines the India: Amazon - Future Group dispute, focusing on the standard of disclosure required in M&A filings. It analyzes the Competition Commission of India's (CCI) decision to revoke approval for Amazon's acquisition of a 49% stake in Future Coupons Private Limited (FCPL) due to non-disclosure and suppression of material information, specifically Amazon's strategic intent regarding Future Retail Limited. The piece also suggests regulatory reforms to enhance the transparency and effectiveness of M&A assessments in India, drawing comparisons with disclosure requirements in the US and EU.

title: Firefly Aerospace's Alpha rocket reaches orbit for 1st time
summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully launched its Alpha rocket into orbit for the first time on its second attempt. The rocket deployed three tiny satellites, including one for educational purposes and another from NASA designed for satellite deorbiting. This achievement marks a significant step for Firefly in the competitive small-satellite launch industry.

title: Amazon plans to close several US call centres
summary: Amazon plans to close several call centers in the United States as it encourages customer service employees to work from home. This move, accelerated by the COVID-19 pandemic, aims to save money on real estate costs. While transitioning to remote work, Amazon's cloud computing division also offers software for remote customer service.

title: Super Mario Movie toy listings from Jakks Pacific pop up on Amazon
summary: Jakks Pacific is producing an array of Super Mario Movie figures, with listings for Mario, Luigi, Toad, and Bowser already appearing on Amazon. Although no images are available yet, this merchandise will accompany the film's release on April 7, 2023. More toy announcements are expected from various manufacturers as the release date approaches.

title: Jack in the Box seeks full-time Twitch streamer
summary: Jack in the Box is hiring its first-ever full-time Twitch streamer, dubbed "Gamer Jack," to bolster its presence on the Amazon-owned platform. This content creator will host weekly livestreams, develop social media strategies, and collaborate with other streamers to engage with the gaming community. The initiative highlights brands' increasing interest in leveraging creators to reach younger audiences on platforms like Twitch, following similar efforts by competitors such as Hot Pockets and Kellogg's Frosted Flakes.

title: Peloton Bike coming to all Hilton-branded hotels in US by end of 2022
summary: By the end of 2022, all 5,400 Hilton-branded hotels across the U.S. will feature at least one Peloton Bike in their fitness centers, making Hilton the first hospitality brand to offer this widespread access. This partnership also provides Hilton Honors members who are new Peloton users with a free 90-day trial to the Peloton app and preferred pricing on select products. This initiative aligns with Peloton's broader restructuring efforts and recent partnerships to expand its market reach.

title: Tupperware selling at Target stores nationwide
summary: Tupperware Brands Corp. announced that its products are now available at Target stores nationwide and on Target's website. This move is part of Tupperware's strategy to "reinvent" itself and reach new customers, particularly younger demographics who may be less familiar with direct sales. The company has also made previous small-scale retail forays with Amazon, HomeGoods, and Bed Bath & Beyond.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Discontinues Glow Video-Calling Device for Kids
summary: Amazon has discontinued its Glow video-calling device for children, launched during the pandemic, due to disappointing sales and changing consumer behavior. The device, which combined a video screen and projector for interactive activities with distant relatives, came from Amazon's Grand Challenge laboratory. This move reflects Amazon's history of experimenting with and sometimes retiring hardware products.

title: Seattle hacker gets probation for $250M Capital One data breach
summary: Paige Thompson, a former Amazon employee, received probation and time served for the 2019 Capital One data breach, which affected over 100 million people and cost the bank more than $250 million. Despite being found guilty of computer and wire fraud, the judge considered mitigating factors including Thompson's transgender identity and mental health issues, leading to a lighter sentence. The Department of Justice expressed disappointment, arguing that the sentence did not reflect justice, especially given that Thompson did not appear to profit from the data or sell it.

title: Management Team
summary: This article details the executive management team of Plug Power Inc., including their roles, career histories, and significant achievements. It highlights the leadership's contributions to the company's growth, strategic direction in the hydrogen economy, and operational successes across various departments.

title: Amazon ends testing of Scout delivery robots
summary: Amazon has ceased testing its Scout home delivery robots, citing that the program was not meeting customer needs despite efforts to create a unique delivery experience. Approximately 400 employees involved in the project will be transitioned to other roles within the company. This decision comes as Amazon continues to invest in other robotics initiatives, including recent acquisitions of iRobot and Cloostermans.

title: Amazon Abandons Home Delivery Robot Tests in Latest Cost Cuts
summary: Amazon is discontinuing its Scout home delivery robot program, a move that signals the e-commerce giant is scaling back experimental projects due to slower sales growth. The autonomous machine, which had been in testing since 2019, will see its 400-person team disbanded, with employees offered new roles within the company. This decision reflects a shift in focus as Amazon adjusts to current economic conditions.

title: CVS Health selling benefits technology provider bswift to Francisco Partners
summary: CVS Health is selling its benefits technology provider bswift to Francisco Partners, a global investment firm specializing in technology businesses. While financial terms were not disclosed, bswift will continue its partnership with CVS Health and Aetna. This divestiture comes as CVS Health also engages in acquisitions, such as the recent $8 billion deal for Signify Health.

title: General Electric cuts hundreds of jobs in the U.S.
summary: General Electric is laying off 20% of its onshore wind workforce in the United States, amounting to hundreds of jobs, in anticipation of an economic recession. This move is part of a broader trend among major American companies, including DocuSign, Gap Inc., Meta, Google, Twilio, Patreon, Snap Inc., Apple, Calm.com, Sweetgreen, Groupon, Fender, and Robinhood, which are also reducing their workforces. Companies are citing market realities and the need to streamline operations and improve profitability as reasons for the job cuts.

title: Amazon.com plans to invest over €1 billion in European electric van, truck fleet
summary: Amazon.com announced plans to invest over €1 billion ($974.8 million) in electric vans, trucks, and low-emission package hubs across Europe over the next five years. This investment aims to more than triple its European electric van fleet to over 10,000 by 2025 and acquire more than 1,500 electric heavy goods vehicles. The initiative is part of Amazon's broader commitment to achieve net-zero carbon emissions by 2040 and to spur innovation in the transportation industry.

title: Amazon.com to invest over 1 billion euros in European electric van, truck fleet
summary: Amazon announced it will invest over 1 billion euros ($974.8 million) in increasing its European electric van and truck fleet and low-emission package hubs over the next five years. This investment aims to triple its electric van fleet in Europe to over 10,000 by 2025 and purchase 1,500 electric heavy goods vehicles. The initiative supports Amazon's goal of achieving net-zero carbon by 2040 and encourages innovation in the transportation industry by expanding charging infrastructure and micro-mobility hubs.

title: Amazon's Prime Day-like event kick-starts holiday discount frenzy amid high inflation
summary: Amazon launched its second major sales event of the year, "Prime Early Access Sale," similar to Prime Day, to kick-start the holiday shopping season amidst high inflation. Rival retailers like Walmart, Kohl's, Best Buy, and Target are also offering early discounts to attract customers struggling with squeezed budgets. This early discount frenzy is expected to lead to the slowest pace of U.S. online holiday sales growth since at least 2015.

title: After multiple teen suicides, East Bay company sued for selling deadly chemicals on Amazon
summary: Amazon and an East Bay chemical company, Loudwolf, are facing lawsuits from the families of teenagers who died by suicide after purchasing sodium nitrite from Amazon. The lawsuits allege product liability, negligence, and infliction of emotional distress, claiming the companies facilitated "corporate-assisted suicide" by selling the deadly chemical. Plaintiffs argue that Amazon's platform lacked safeguards to prevent minors from purchasing such substances and even recommended related items for assisted suicide.

title: New Convenience-Store Roundup for September 2022
summary: This article provides a roundup of new, and some closed, convenience stores across the U.S. for September 2022. Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Wisconsin each added five new stores, with Sheetz and Kwik Trip opening the most locations overall. In total, 37 new stores opened and one closed, resulting in a net gain of 36 convenience stores for the month.

title: Microsoft uses strategically located subsidiaries to lower taxes more than previously known, new analysis finds
summary: A new report by the Centre for International Corporate Tax Accountability and Research (CICTAR) alleges that Microsoft uses a network of subsidiaries, particularly in Ireland and Bermuda, to significantly minimize its tax liabilities globally. While not suggesting illegal activity, the analysis raises concerns about profit shifting and its impact on tax revenues in countries where Microsoft conducts substantial business. The report points out that despite high profit margins, some Microsoft subsidiaries report much lower margins, reducing taxable income.

title: Netflix to get Nielsen ratings as streaming giant rolls out ad-supported plan
summary: Netflix will begin using Nielsen ratings for its ad-supported plan in the U.S. starting in 2023, offering advertisers better insights into audience reach. This move coincides with the launch of Netflix's $6.99 monthly ad-supported tier in November, aimed at boosting revenue after recent subscriber losses. Other streaming services like Amazon Prime and Disney+ also offer ad-supported plans, with varying price points and advertisement loads.

title: Kroger To Buy Albertsons, Creating Grocery Giant
summary: Kroger and Albertsons are reportedly in merger discussions that would unite the two largest supermarket chains in the U.S. Kroger announced on Oct. 14 that it is acquiring Albertsons for $24.6B, assuming $4.7B of Albertsons’ debt, in a deal that would create a grocery giant but likely face antitrust review. The companies plan to divest stores in overlapping markets to facilitate regulatory approval.

title: EXEC: Columbia Sportswear Aims To “Double Down” On Wholesale Growth
summary: Columbia Sportswear plans to intensify its focus on wholesale growth, contrasting with many other brands shifting to direct-to-consumer models. The brand's strategy emphasizes expanding partnerships with key retailers like Dick's Sporting Goods and Amazon, while also leveraging its digital platform, Columbia.com, for brand storytelling and consumer engagement. Columbia aims for significant growth across various regions, targeting a 7 to 9 percent compound annual growth rate over the next three years, with particular focus on Canada, Europe, and China.

title: Kroger and Albertsons Plan $25 Billion Supermarket Merger That May Face Hurdles (Published 2022)
summary: Kroger announced its plan to acquire Albertsons for $24.6 billion, creating a supermarket giant with over $209 billion in annual revenue and approximately 5,000 stores. The merger aims to compete more effectively with big-box retailers like Walmart and Amazon, potentially passing on up to $500 million in savings to customers. However, the deal quickly drew criticism from consumer advocates and politicians concerned about reduced consumer choice, higher prices, and increased corporate concentration in the grocery market.

title: How Will Kroger’s $25 Billion Buyout of Vons Parent Company Affect Pasadena?
summary: Kroger's proposed $25 billion acquisition of Albertsons, the parent company of Vons, is currently under antitrust review. This merger would create a massive grocery chain with nearly 5,000 stores nationwide. While the deal could lead to lower costs for consumers and increased competitiveness against Walmart, it raises concerns among workers and analysts about potential monopolies and store closures, especially in areas like Pasadena where both companies already have a significant presence.

title: Amazfit、最新スマートウォッチ「Amazfit GTR 4＆GTS 4」発売
summary: Amazfitは、最新スマートウォッチ「Amazfit GTR 4」と「Amazfit GTS 4」を発売しました。これらのモデルは、業界初のデュアルバンド円偏波GPSアンテナテクノロジーを搭載し、高精度な測位を実現しています。また、150種類のスポーツモードやBioTracker 4.0 PPG生体光学センサーによる健康モニタリング機能、およびスマートウォッチ本体に音楽を保存し再生できる機能を備えています。

title: How one decision, made last year, saved Sea Ltd. from a brush with bankruptcy
summary: Sea Ltd.'s 2021 decision to raise US$6.3 billion in funding, despite having significant cash reserves, proved crucial for its survival. This preemptive move provided a financial runway that allowed the company to weather substantial losses from Shopee's operations and a subsequent market downturn. The article credits CEO Forrest Li's foresight for making a decision that saved the company from potential bankruptcy, highlighting the importance of leadership in navigating unpredictable market conditions.

title: Pardon the Disruption: The digital forces driving the Kroger-Albertsons merger
summary: The proposed $24.6 billion merger between Kroger and Albertsons aims to create a new "Big 3" in the grocery industry, competing more effectively with Walmart and Amazon. Beyond increasing store footprint and negotiating power, the deal is largely driven by the need for scale in data, e-commerce, and retail media, crucial for future profitability and innovation. The merger faces significant regulatory scrutiny regarding potential impacts on competition and prices, with Kroger preparing to divest numerous stores to gain approval.

title: JBG Smith Plans Mixed-Use DC Asset Near Amazon HQ
summary: JBG Smith has revealed plans for the RiverHouse neighborhood, a 36-acre mixed-use project in Arlington, Va., near Amazon's HQ2. The development will include thousands of residential units (apartments, senior living, townhomes, condos), 30,000 square feet of retail, and over 7 acres of public parks. The project plans to redevelop existing parking lots with below-grade garages and enhance connectivity with public transport access and a protected cycle track.

title: What the Kroger-Albertsons merger means for the retail media landscape
summary: The proposed merger between Kroger and Albertsons, valued at $24.6 billion, poses a significant shift in the retail media landscape by combining their first-party data and expanding their retail footprint. This consolidation could allow the new entity to challenge major players like Walmart in the retail media space, offering brands a more compelling advertising platform with a broader reach and richer data for ad targeting. Experts suggest this merger will highlight the growing importance of physical stores and data scale in the future of retail media, potentially benefiting the combined company in a challenging economic environment.

title: Amazon Helps Ukrainians Gain New Skills and Launch Tech Careers
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) launched "IT Skills 4U," a free workforce-development program aimed at helping Ukrainians pursue careers in cloud computing. The program, available virtually and in Poland, addresses the challenges faced by over 7.5 million Ukrainians who have fled their homes. More than 30 AWS customers and partners, including EPAM Systems, have committed to offering jobs, internships, and traineeships to program participants.

title: Despite late entry, analysts bullish on Spotify's audiobook play
summary: Analysts are optimistic about Spotify's recent entry into the audiobook market, despite its late start. Although Spotify's audiobook store, launched after acquiring Findaway World LLC, currently has limitations like web-only purchases and U.S.-only availability, experts believe the company can leverage its success in podcasts and the overall growth of audiobooks to compete with established players like Audible. However, challenges remain, including complex licensing for audiobooks compared to podcasts and a flattening growth curve after the pandemic boom, along with Spotify's continued unprofitability despite revenue growth.

title: Configure Hybrid Cloud with FSx for NetApp ONTAP and VMware Cloud on AWS SDDC using VMware HCX
summary: This article details how to set up a hybrid cloud environment using FSx for NetApp ONTAP as an NFS datastore for VMware Cloud on AWS (VMC) and how to migrate virtual machines from on-premises to VMC SDDC using VMware HCX. It provides a seven-step guide for deploying and configuring HCX, including network and compute profiles, service mesh creation, and demonstrating bulk migration. The integration aims to simplify hybrid cloud deployments, reduce TCO, and enable various migration mechanisms.

title: Battle with Bots Prompts Mass Purge of Amazon, Apple Employee Accounts on LinkedIn
summary: LinkedIn experienced a significant purge of fake employee accounts for companies like Amazon and Apple, with some firms seeing a 33% to 50% drop in listed employees overnight. The purges are believed to be LinkedIn's attempt to combat the proliferation of AI-generated profiles and fraudulent activity, including "pig butchering" scams and identity theft. This event highlights a larger problem of bot accounts and raises questions about the accuracy of LinkedIn's employee statistics and its broader impact on the platform's reliability for hiring and networking.

title: Hawaiian Airlines Announces Agreement with Amazon to Operate Freighter Aircraft
summary: Hawaiian Airlines (NASDAQ: HA) has announced an agreement with Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) to operate an initial fleet of 10 Airbus A330-300 freighters starting in fall 2023. Hawaiian will maintain and fly these aircraft under its FAA certificate to transport cargo for Amazon, with the fleet expanding through 2024 and potentially beyond. This partnership will lead to the establishment of a pilot base on the continental U.S., growth of maintenance bases, and the hiring of additional staff, while Amazon receives warrants to acquire up to 15% of Hawaiian's common shares.

title: Chinese internet giants gear up for global e-commerce push
summary: Chinese e-commerce companies are increasingly looking to overseas markets for growth due to tightening domestic regulations, slowing revenue, and a plateauing user base. Pinduoduo's new cross-border platform, Temu, targets the US market with a low-price strategy, while established players like Alibaba and JD.com are expanding their international retail and logistics operations. Despite geopolitical and economic uncertainties, these tech giants are investing heavily in global expansion, adapting strategies to navigate new challenges like increased compliance demands and supply chain disruptions.

title: UPS hikes 2023 GRI by 6.9%, matching FedEx
summary: UPS announced a 6.9% increase to its 2023 general rate increase (GRI), matching the earlier announcement by FedEx. This increase applies to its U.S. air, ground, and international delivery services, taking effect on December 27, 2022. The move reflects efforts by both carriers to offset elevated cost inflation, with GRIs now serving as a key indicator for contract rates and discounts.

title: Sally Beauty Introduces bondbar: Bond Repair for All Hair Types
