summary: Sally Beauty has launched "bondbar," a new line of pro-quality, accessibly priced bond repair products designed for all hair types. The four-step system, born from Sally Beauty's expertise in hair care, aims to address hair damage from styling and coloring, offering clinically proven performance with up to 70% reduced breakage. The products are cruelty-free, vegan, and free of SLS/SLES, parabens, and phthalates, with recyclable packaging.

title: Apple (AAPL) Hikes Music and TV+ Prices in First Increases Since Launch
summary: Apple Inc. has raised the prices for its Apple Music and TV+ services for the first time since their launch, citing increased licensing costs. Apple Music for individuals will now cost $10.99 per month, while Apple TV+ increased by $2 to $6.99 monthly. This price hike makes Apple Music more expensive than rival services from Spotify and Amazon, potentially giving competitors an advantage.

title: JPMorgan Chase teams with Vera Whole Health to open primary care clinics for employees
summary: JPMorgan Chase is launching primary care clinics for its employees in the Columbus, Ohio area, partnering with Vera Whole Health and Central Ohio Primary Care. This initiative will offer comprehensive in-person and virtual health services to over 20,000 employees and their families, with Morgan Health, JPMorgan Chase's healthcare arm, playing a key role. The move aims to provide high-quality and integrated healthcare, building on Morgan Health's prior investment in Vera Whole Health.

title: Opinion: This one stock stands out when looking at key data from the FAANG+ group
summary: This article discusses the current landscape for tech-oriented stocks, noting that 2022 has been difficult for the sector. It suggests that smart long-term investors should look for bargains among quality merchandise, particularly within the FAANG group. The piece aims to identify one stock from this group that offers a good combination of low valuation and high growth, despite the challenging market conditions.

title: Clorox recalls cleaning products that could contain bacteria
summary: The Clorox Company has recalled scented multi-surface cleaners and all-purpose cleaners due to potential bacterial contamination, including Pseudomonas aeruginosa, which can cause serious illness in individuals with compromised immune systems. The recalled products were sold nationwide at various major retailers and online, with specific date codes indicating affected batches produced between January 2021 and September 2022. Consumers are advised to dispose of the products and contact Clorox for a refund.

title: Alamo Drafthouse Cinema opens in Crystal City
summary: Alamo Drafthouse Cinema has officially opened its nine-screen, 915-seat location in Crystal City, near Amazon's second headquarters. This opening comes over five years after the chain initially signed on to anchor JBG Smith's Central District retail development. The article notes the significant changes that have occurred in the area since the initial agreement.

title: Amazon predicts sales growth slowdown for holidays, crushing shares
summary: Amazon anticipates a significant slowdown in sales growth for the upcoming holiday season, prompting investor concern and an approximately $140 billion drop in its market capitalization. The company attributes this outlook to inflation-wary consumers and businesses tightening their spending, leading to lower-than-expected third-quarter net sales and a cautious approach to future growth. This mirrors a broader trend in the retail and tech sectors, with cloud-computing rivals also facing challenges amidst slowing economic conditions.

title: Brief: Trigo nabs $100m, plans expansion of its Amazon Go-like cashierless checkout tech
summary: Israeli startup Trigo has secured $100 million in equity financing, led by Temasek and 83North, to expand its cashierless checkout technology across Europe and the US. The "EasyOut" system, similar to Amazon Go, uses AI and computer vision to enable customers to shop and leave without traditional checkout, while its StoreOS software manages inventory. Trigo plans to deepen its relationships with existing partners like Tesco, ALDI Nord, and REWE Group, and develop new offerings.

title: Plug Power adding jobs locally to deliver green hydro energy worldwide by 2028
summary: Plug Power's Henrietta gigafactory is now at full production, creating new jobs to support the company's goal of supplying 1,000 tons of green hydrogen daily worldwide by 2028. The facility, which manufactures fuel cell stacks and electrolyzers, also serves as a model for future global expansion. Despite supply chain challenges impacting 2022 revenue projections, Plug Power remains committed to its growth in New York and its mission to build the green hydrogen economy.

title: How to Survive a Shipping ‘A-Parcel-Lypse’? Unchain the Supply Chain
summary: Shekar Natarajan, chief supply chain officer at American Eagle Outfitters, warns of an impending "a-parcel-lypse" due to distribution and fulfillment inefficiencies. He advocates for companies to collaborate and share resources to create a more elastic supply chain, rather than each building individual, costly networks that lack the scale of giants like Amazon or Walmart. American Eagle's Quiet Platforms is an example of this approach, offering shared logistics services to over 60 customers to improve efficiency and reduce costs.

title: How did the surprise Amazon Prime Day in October 2022 compare to similar sales events?
summary: Amazon's second Prime Day event in October 2022 saw lower sales than the July 2022 Prime Day, but sales were still higher than Prime Day events in 2020 and 2021. The sales growth during Amazon's October event outpaced Walmart and Target's competing sales events. Despite the success of Prime Day, Amazon's weekly sales during these events remain lower than holiday shopping sales in December.

title: Ansys launches latest simulation gateway product powered by AWS
summary: Ansys has partnered with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to introduce its new Ansys Gateway powered by AWS product. This collaboration brings together Ansys's simulation technology with AWS's on-demand cloud computing platforms. The launch is a significant step for Ansys in expanding its product offerings and leveraging cloud infrastructure.

title: Blackstone Lends $700M on Onni Group’s Luxury Rental Project
summary: Blackstone has provided a $700 million whole loan to the Onni Group for its newly completed luxury rental project in Seattle's South Lake Union neighborhood. The project, Onni South Lake Union, consists of 1,097 units across three buildings and 23,000 square feet of retail space, boasting resort-style amenities. This financing highlights continued capital availability for top sponsors even amidst market volatility, especially for projects in thriving life science and technology hubs like South Lake Union.

title: Amazon Closes Below $1 Trillion in Market Value for First Time Since 2020
summary: Amazon's market value closed below $1 trillion for the first time since early 2020, with its shares falling 5.5% to $96.79. This decline is attributed to a weak forecast and broader tech industry slump, marking a 42% drop in the stock this year from its peak of nearly $1.9 trillion.

title: Best Buy Co. Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors
summary: Best Buy Co. Inc. (BBY) stock fell 4.17% on Wednesday, closing at $66.01, underperforming the broader market. This drop ended a three-day winning streak and left the stock $75.96 below its 52-week high. Competitors like Apple Inc. (AAPL) and Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) also saw declines, while Walmart Inc. (WMT) experienced a lesser fall.

title: Apple now valued at more than Amazon, Alphabet and Meta — combined
summary: Apple's market capitalization has surpassed the combined value of Alphabet, Amazon, and Meta Platforms, demonstrating its strong performance relative to other major technology companies this year. This achievement highlights Apple's dominant position in the tech industry, especially given that its stock has fared better than its peers.

title: Amazon commits $53 million to fast-track innovations by female climate tech entrepreneurs
summary: Amazon is investing $53 million to support female climate tech entrepreneurs and address gender inequities in climate finance. This includes $3 million for USAID's Climate Gender Equity Fund, which will provide grants and support to women-led climate solutions globally, and an additional $50 million from Amazon's Climate Pledge Fund to invest directly in women-founded and women-led climate tech companies. The initiative aims to accelerate climate innovations, advance gender equity, and enhance access to funding and technical support for women in the climate sector.

title: Sprouts partners with DoorDash to expand online options
summary: Sprouts Farmers Market has partnered with DoorDash to introduce on-demand delivery services across its approximately 380 stores, starting in Phoenix and rolling out phased across other cities in November. This collaboration enhances Sprouts' existing delivery and pickup options with Instacart, making over 20,000 Sprouts products available through DoorDash's platform. Customers can choose same-day or future deliveries, and DashPass members will receive free delivery and reduced service fees.

title: Lambda Ephemeral Storage: Why the 10 GB Increase Is Important
summary: AWS has increased the ephemeral storage limit for Lambda functions from 512 MB to 10 GB, significantly enhancing its capability for data-intensive applications. This expansion addresses previous limitations that forced developers to use less performant workarounds like S3 or EFS for large datasets. The article discusses various use cases benefiting from this increase, such as ETL jobs, machine learning tasks, and large file processing, while also clarifying when ephemeral storage is not suitable due to its temporary nature.

title: Homeland Grocery Stores name Walmart, Amazon veteran as new president and CEO
summary: HAC, Inc., parent company of Homeland Grocery Stores, has appointed David Criscione, a veteran of Walmart International, Amazon, and Delhaize Group, as its new president and CEO. Criscione will oversee 78 grocery stores in four states and replaces Marc Jones. His background includes executive roles in finance, strategy, and technology, and he was involved in Amazon's launch of physical grocery stores and the potential acquisition of Whole Foods Market.

title: An advent calendar about the hit-show "Friends" will be there for you to count down the days in December
summary: This article highlights various Advent calendars available for the 2022 holiday season, ranging from toys and tech to beauty products and gourmet treats. It emphasizes options under $50, making them budget-friendly gift ideas. Featured calendars include themes like "Friends," Disney princesses, Star Wars, Build-A-Bear, Sephora beauty, and even options for pets and escape room enthusiasts, catering to a wide audience.

title: EBay’s focus on luxury to refurbished goods drives results beat
summary: EBay Inc. surpassed Wall Street's quarterly targets as inflation-conscious shoppers increasingly bought refurbished goods and the company expanded its luxury offerings. Despite forecasting holiday-quarter revenue slightly below estimates, investors drove shares up nearly 8% in extended trading, recognizing eBay's appeal for value in a high-inflation environment. The company acknowledged risks from a strong U.S. dollar, Europe's energy crisis, and consumer confidence but noted increased demand for home energy products in Germany.

title: Amazon Continues its Cloud Expansion in Northern Virginia
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is significantly expanding its data center presence in Northern Virginia by leasing large amounts of new space and increasing the size of its data centers. This expansion, primarily through its partnership with Corporate Office Properties Trust (COPT), signifies AWS's anticipation of strong future demand and its continued dominance in the cloud computing market. The article details COPT's role in building "powered shells" for Vadata (Amazon's data center operating unit) and highlights a shift towards larger data facility templates for AWS, ensuring continuous capital for COPT through joint ventures.

title: Matter is just the first step towards the ideal smart home
summary: The Matter smart home protocol officially launched, representing a significant collaborative effort by 300 technology companies to standardize smart home device interoperability. While it aims for a seamless user experience and a revolution in product-buying, initial adoption will involve a gradual transition focusing on specific device categories like lighting, HVAC, and door locks. Future stages will incorporate more device types, but the protocol still needs to address broader issues like user data security and privacy beyond just device interoperability.

title: Resale Shoppers to Buy More in 2023, But What About the Seller Side?
summary: A new survey by Morning Consult indicates that the robust resale market is set to grow further in 2023, with 62% of consumers having engaged in purchase-driven resale shopping and 37% expecting to increase their secondhand purchases. While buyer activity is strong across demographics, attracting sellers remains a challenge for companies like TheRealReal. The article highlights the critical imbalance between buyers and sellers, which is driving consolidation in the secondary market.

title: Gap Sells Greater China Business to Chinese E-commerce Firm for Up to $50 Million
summary: Gap is selling its Greater China business, including mainland China and Taiwan operations, to Chinese e-commerce operator Baozun for an acquisition amount of $40 million, subject to adjustments up to $50 million. The deal, expected to close in the first half of 2023, grants Baozun exclusive rights for 10 years to manage, design, manufacture, promote, and distribute Gap products in the region. This strategic move follows years of slumping sales for Gap, its exit from China with its Old Navy brand, and an intense boycott from Chinese customers over the Xinjiang cotton controversy.

title: The Quiet Invasion of 'Big Information'
summary: This article highlights how companies like RELX (Reed Elsevier LexisNexis) and Thomson Reuters operate largely unnoticed, despite collecting vast amounts of personal data and influencing critical life decisions. Unlike the Big Five tech companies, these "information giants" avoid public scrutiny while selling data to various institutions and privatizing public information. The author argues for robust data privacy laws, antitrust enforcement, and public funding for information access to curb their monopolistic power and prevent a digital dystopia.

title: Amazon Becomes World’s First Public Company to Lose $1 Trillion in Market Value
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has become the world's first public company to lose $1 trillion in market value, with its stock falling 4.3% on Wednesday. The e-commerce and cloud giant's value dropped to approximately $879 billion from a peak of $1.88 trillion in July 2021, attributed to rising inflation, tightening monetary policies, and disappointing earnings. Microsoft Corp. is also close to this milestone, having lost $889 billion from its November 2021 peak.

title: Don't Sleep on e.l.f. Cosmetics' Magical Cyber Monday Sale
summary: e.l.f. Cosmetics is holding a Cyber Monday sale from November 29 to November 30, offering 40% off orders over $30 and a free gift for orders over $40. The article highlights several popular and affordable e.l.f. products, including the Poreless Putty Primer and Hydrating Camo Concealer. It encourages shoppers to take advantage of the sale to stock up on their favorite budget-friendly beauty items.

title: Gap Launches Collection of Family Essentials with Amazon Fashion in U.S. and Canada
summary: Gap has launched a new collection of family essential apparel, accessories, and baby gear on Amazon Fashion in the U.S. and Canada. This collaboration aims to make Gap's modern American essentials more accessible to customers through Amazon's extensive reach and fast, free Prime delivery services. The collection includes thousands of items for adults, teens, kids, and babies, along with babyGap-branded nursery furniture.

title: Predictions 2023: Retail Health, Decentralized Trials, And Wellness Are On The Front Lines
summary: The article discusses healthcare trends for 2023, highlighting challenges like hospital bankruptcies due to economic downturns and changing consumer behaviors. It predicts a significant rise in retail health clinics' market share for primary care and increased adoption of remote patient monitoring (RPM) tools for chronic conditions. The authors emphasize the need for healthcare organizations to transform and invest in workforce talent to remain competitive.

title: DoorDash is starting to look like a tech platform
summary: DoorDash is expanding beyond its core on-demand delivery service, acquiring Wolt Enterprises Oy and Bbot, and launching "DashMart" to offer household essentials. The company is evolving into a broader tech platform by introducing self-serve ad solutions for CPG partners and a "Merchant Benefits" program that provides discounted services like healthcare and mental health benefits to its retail partners. These moves suggest DoorDash is following a path similar to Amazon or Google, differentiating itself from rivals by offering more comprehensive tech services.

title: Alibaba, JD.com keep mum on this year’s Singles’ Day transaction volume
summary: For the first time, Chinese e-commerce giants Alibaba and JD.com did not disclose their final gross merchandise volume (GMV) for the Singles' Day shopping festival, amid economic headwinds and China's zero-Covid policy. While Alibaba stated its results were in line with last year's GMV and JD.com claimed "record-breaking" results, analysts speculate the actual figures might have been underwhelming, leading to their decision to withhold specific numbers. This transparency shift reflects the challenging economic environment affecting consumer spending in China.

title: Dow pops 1,200 points, S&P 500 jumps 5% in biggest rally in two years after light inflation report
summary: Stocks experienced their largest rally since 2020 following an October consumer price index report that suggested inflation might have peaked. The Dow Jones Industrial Average surged 1,201.43 points (3.7%), the S&P 500 rose 5.54%, and the Nasdaq Composite jumped 7.35%. This market reaction was driven by a lower-than-expected CPI reading, leading traders to anticipate a less aggressive stance from the Federal Reserve regarding interest rate hikes.

title: Amazon to lay off 10,000 people: Report
summary: Amazon is reportedly planning to lay off approximately 10,000 corporate and technology employees, focusing on its devices unit (including Alexa), retail division, and human resources. This move comes as the company, which recently froze corporate hiring, prepares for a potential economic downturn and slowing consumer demand, following similar job cuts by other major tech companies like Meta and Twitter.

title: Bob Greenblatt, Allison Janney Partner to Adapt ‘Confident Women’ Into Anthology Series for Amazon (EXCLUSIVE)
summary: Bob Greenblatt and Allison Janney are collaborating with writer Alexa Junge to develop an anthology series called "Confident Women" for Amazon Prime Video. The series will be based on Tori Telfer's book, which explores the stories of female con women throughout history. Janney is attached as a non-writing executive producer, and each season will focus on a different woman's story, starting with Sante Kimes.

title: Amazon to lay off thousands of employees -source
summary: Amazon plans to lay off approximately 10,000 employees in corporate and technology roles, starting this week. This reduction, affecting about 3% of its corporate staff, includes positions in its devices organization, human resources, and retail divisions. The move is attributed to the uncertain macroeconomic environment and follows similar layoffs across the tech sector.

title: Amazon Starts Virtual Health Referral Service Linking Patients to Doctors
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has launched "Amazon Clinic," a new virtual health referral service designed to connect patients with healthcare providers for various conditions, including acne, hair loss, and allergies. This initiative marks Amazon's continued expansion into the healthcare sector, complementing its existing online pharmacy and its acquisition of One Medical. The service aims to streamline access to virtual medical consultations.

title: Mass layoffs at Amazon hit Vancouver offices
summary: Amazon's mass layoffs have extended to its Vancouver offices, as confirmed by a Vancouver-based software engineer who was among those let go. While Amazon Canada declined to provide specific numbers for Vancouver, the company indicated that general macroeconomic conditions and rapid hiring necessitated role adjustments across its global operations. This news follows a trend of significant layoffs in Vancouver's innovation sector throughout 2022.

title: Amazon lays off some devices unit staff as it targets 10,000 cuts
summary: Amazon has begun laying off employees in its devices group as part of a broader plan to cut approximately 10,000 jobs across the company, including in retail and human resources. This move comes as Amazon faces an "unusual and uncertain macroeconomic environment" and marks a significant shift for the tech giant, which has historically been known for its rapid job creation. The layoffs reflect a wider trend of dismissals within the technology sector, with other major companies like Meta Platforms and Twitter also announcing substantial job cuts.

title: eBay Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: On Thursday, November 17, 2022, eBay Inc. stock (EBAY) fell by 0.04% to $45.47, underperforming the broader market indices like the S&P 500 and Dow Jones. Despite this daily loss, eBay outperformed competitors such as Amazon.com Inc., Walmart Inc., and Costco Wholesale Corp., which experienced larger declines. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high, with trading volume also below its 50-day average.

title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy says layoffs have already begun, and will extend into next year
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy has publicly confirmed that layoffs have begun at the company and will continue into early 2023. These job cuts, potentially affecting over 10,000 workers, are primarily targeting corporate and technology roles in response to economic pressures and a period of slower growth. Jassy described the decision as difficult but expressed optimism for Amazon's future, focusing on customer obsession and innovation.

title: Gap Inc. recovers in Q3 despite Yeezy debacle and heavy discounting
summary: Gap Inc. reported a Q3 recovery with net sales up 2.4% to $4.04 billion, driven by online sales and strong performance from Banana Republic. The company swung to a net income of $282 million despite writing off $53 million from the terminated Yeezy Gap collaboration and significant discounting. Analysts noted improvements but cautioned about high inventory, a late-quarter slowdown, and a downgraded holiday outlook due to market pressures.

title: Netflix Still on Top in Terms of Original Content, with 37% of All Movies and Shows
summary: A study by BB Media, commissioned by Media Play News, found that Netflix leads all streaming services in original content, offering 3,260 titles, which accounts for nearly 37% of the total original content across 41 platforms in the U.S. HBO Max and Yupp TV follow, with Amazon Prime Video ranking among the top five. When considering hours of original content, Prime Video displaces IQIYI for fourth place.

title: Is DigitalOcean Stock a Buy Now?
summary: DigitalOcean is presented as a cloud infrastructure company targeting small businesses, developers, and startups, distinguishing itself from giants like AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud with competitive pricing. The article highlights DigitalOcean's strong financial performance in Q3, including significant revenue growth and ambitious targets for 2024, despite considerations about stock-based compensation affecting free cash flow. The author concludes that DigitalOcean is a strong buy due to its rapid growth, reasonable stock valuation, and focused niche market.

title: Costco could put delivery warehouse in Cedar Park
summary: Costco is reportedly looking to establish a new delivery warehouse in Cedar Park, a northern suburb of Austin, Texas. This move is part of the expansion of Costco Logistics, the company's division responsible for home delivery of large items, aiming to operate more like Amazon. The proposed facility would enhance Costco's national network of logistics hubs.

title: Tech forward: Beauty A.I. has inherent racial bias—but it doesn't have to
summary: Joy Buolamwini's experience at MIT revealed inherent racial bias in AI facial recognition, performing poorly on darker skin. Her research, echoed by Dr. Ellis Monk, links this to non-diverse training data and colorism in society. Google's partnership with Dr. Monk to implement the inclusive Monk Skin Tone Scale and Perfect Corp.'s proactive approach show how the industry is addressing and working to correct these biases for more inclusive AI.

title: These 2 ETFs show why owning small-cap stocks is key to beating the market at times like this
summary: Small-cap stocks within the S&P 500 have significantly outperformed larger-cap stocks this year, with the eight largest market cap stocks experiencing an average loss of 40.4% compared to a 9.5% average loss for the remaining stocks. This highlights how an equal-weight strategy can be more beneficial for S&P 500 investors when the biggest names are struggling. The article suggests that owning small-cap stocks is crucial for beating the market in such conditions.

title: Amazon Plans to Invest $1 Billion a Year in Movies for Theaters
summary: Amazon plans to invest over $1 billion annually to produce 12 to 15 movies for theatrical release, marking its largest commitment to cinemas. This strategy aims to compete with major studios like Paramount Pictures, with a gradual increase in film output starting next year. The move signifies a significant expansion of Amazon's presence in the film distribution market.

title: Amazon acquires two data centers from State Street Corp in Massachusetts
summary: Amazon has acquired two data centers in Massachusetts from State Street Corp for a total of $26.7 million. The properties, located in Grafton and Westborough, include existing data center facilities and greenfield land. This acquisition suggests Amazon might demolish and rebuild the sites as part of its data center expansion, despite not having an AWS cloud region in Massachusetts.

title: Black Friday 2022: From Target and Walmart to Best Buy and Apple - deals for holiday shopping
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to Black Friday 2022 deals from numerous major retailers including Target, Walmart, Amazon, Best Buy, and Apple, covering various categories such as tech, home goods, clothing, and cosmetics. It highlights specific offers and sale dates for each retailer. The piece also includes expert tips on how to effectively navigate Black Friday sales to ensure shoppers get the best value for their money.

title: How to Back Up S3 Data using AWS Backup
summary: This article explains how to back up S3 data using AWS Backup, highlighting its features such as automated scheduling, incremental backups, and encryption. It provides a step-by-step guide for creating an on-demand backup of an S3 bucket via the AWS console and discusses the current limitations of AWS Backup for S3. The author also briefly mentions NetApp BlueXP as an alternative solution for NetApp Cloud Volumes deployments.

title: Employers Rethink Need for College Degrees in Tight Labor Market
summary: A tight labor market is leading more employers, including Google, Delta Air Lines, and IBM, to reduce college degree requirements for certain positions, focusing instead on skills and experience. This trend has resulted in a decline in job postings requiring bachelor's degrees compared to pre-pandemic levels. The shift is influenced by high demand for workers, low unemployment, and efforts to address racial disparities in the workplace.

title: JCPenney was once a shopping giant. Can it make a comeback?
summary: JCPenney's new CEO, Marc Rosen, is attempting to revitalize the 120-year-old brand by appealing to "America's diverse working families" with an overhauled beauty strategy, remodeled stores, and improved online experience. Despite a strong start to the holiday season, the company faces challenges from inflation, intense competition, and a need to attract new, younger customers. JCPenney, which filed for bankruptcy in 2020 and is now owned by mall landlords, aims to leverage its reduced debt and strategic focus on its core customer base to secure its future in a challenging retail landscape.

title: House Republicans target U.S. Chamber of Commerce as GOP battle against ‘woke capitalism’ revs up
summary: House Republicans are intensifying their campaign against "woke capitalism," targeting corporations that have embraced environmental, social, and governance (ESG) goals or supported Democratic candidates. This has put the U.S. Chamber of Commerce in the crosshairs, with House Speaker Kevin McCarthy reportedly pushing for a leadership change after the Chamber endorsed some Democrats. The growing rift between the GOP and big business stems from differing views on social issues, immigration, and climate change, threatening legislative gridlock and increasing scrutiny on corporate practices.

title: "Twelve Days of Christmas" - 12 companies for the Santa Claus Rally
summary: This article discusses the "Santa Claus Rally," a stock market phenomenon typically occurring in late December and early January, where the S&P 500 historically sees an average rise of 1.3%. It examines past rallies, their occurrence during bear markets, and potential reasons for the phenomenon, such as tax optimization and investor sentiment. The article then highlights 12 companies, including Amazon, Alphabet, and NVIDIA, that could benefit from this rally and provide investment opportunities based on their current valuations and market positions.

title: Where Does All the Cardboard Come From? I Had to Know. (Published 2022)
summary: This article delves into the massive and rapidly growing cardboard industry, tracing the journey of a cardboard box from a loblolly pine tree to a finished product. It highlights the dominance of major companies like International Paper, the environmental benefits of cardboard's recyclability compared to other materials, and how e-commerce has fueled an unprecedented demand for packaging. The piece also touches on innovations in packaging and global market dynamics, including China's role as a major consumer and the industry's significant impact on regional economies.

title: Amazon Launches Supply-Chain Software Service
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has introduced "AWS Supply Chain," a new cloud-based software service designed to help businesses manage and track cargo flows. This move positions Amazon as a competitor to existing supply-chain software providers such as Manhattan Associates and Blue Yonder, and follows a similar launch by Microsoft Corp. The service aims to assist businesses, especially the numerous small and medium-sized businesses in Amazon's marketplace, in gaining better control over their complex supply chains.

title: Amazon pays $26.7 million for properties in Grafton, Shrewsbury and Westborough
summary: Amazon Data Services Inc. has purchased properties in Grafton, Shrewsbury, and Westborough, Massachusetts, for a total of $26.7 million from State Street Corp. These acquisitions include a 1.8-acre property in Grafton for $15.1 million and properties at 600 and 800 Friberg Parkway in Westborough for $11.6 million. Amazon has not yet disclosed its development plans for these newly acquired sites to the respective towns, though it recently bought a 93-acre lot in Westborough for a delivery station.

title: Exclusive Q&A: MongoDB CEO Dev Ittycheria on empowering developers and how AWS is changing
summary: MongoDB CEO Dev Ittycheria discusses the company's strong partnership with AWS and its unique document-based data structure that empowers developers. He highlights how MongoDB's platform provides a unified and elegant user experience, enabling rapid innovation for a diverse customer base. Ittycheria also shares his insights on the evolving cloud landscape, the importance of developer productivity, and the shift towards smart applications leveraging AI and machine learning.

title: Amazon Launches Supply-Chain Software Service
summary: Amazon Web Services has launched a new supply-chain software service, entering the burgeoning tech sector that aims to bring more efficiency and visibility to goods management. This move positions Amazon alongside established players like Blue Yonder and SAP, as well as other big operators such as Microsoft, in helping businesses navigate supply chain disruptions. The service is expected to find a ready audience among the thousands of small- and medium-sized businesses on Amazon's third-party marketplace.

title: American Family Insurance and AWS Team Up to Drive Innovation in the Insurance Industry
summary: American Family Insurance has selected AWS as its preferred cloud provider to accelerate its IT transformation, drive innovation, and improve customer experience. This multiyear agreement includes a co-innovation fund and initiatives to provide free cloud training in local communities and reduce environmental impact through cloud migration. The partnership aims to centralize IT infrastructure, reduce operating costs, and enhance responsiveness for American Family Insurance's 13.1 million policyholders.

title: Fubo Sports Network Now Available on Amazon Freevee
summary: Fubo Sports Network, the free-to-consumer TV network from FuboTV Inc., has expanded its reach by launching on Amazon Freevee. This move significantly increases its distribution to over 155 million devices, allowing fans to access live sports, original programming, and partner content. The network has seen substantial viewership growth and continues to add new series and content, aiming to connect with sports fans across various platforms at no additional cost.

title: Amazon won't take down antisemitic film tweeted by Kyrie Irving, CEO says
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced that the company will not remove an antisemitic film from its platform, despite pressure to do so after Kyrie Irving tweeted a link to it. Jassy stated that Amazon must allow access to various viewpoints, even objectionable ones, citing a "slippery slope" in content moderation, though he personally finds parts of the film objectionable and is concerned about antisemitism. The film, "Hebrews to Negroes: Wake Up Black America!", contains false claims about Jewish control of media and Holocaust denial.

title: Hush appoints Sarah Miles as its new CEO
summary: Hush, a women's fashion and lifestyle brand, has appointed Sarah Miles as its new chief executive, effective January 16, 2023. Miles brings extensive experience from roles at Amazon, Diageo, and Sephora, and will lead Hush through its next phase of strategic growth. She replaces Kate Bartman, who will return to her previous role as chief trading officer after overseeing significant transformation and customer base growth.

title: Big Tech Antitrust Investigations: Amazon, Apple, Google, Meta/Facebook and Microsoft Updates
summary: This article provides an exhaustive overview of the ongoing antitrust investigations and regulatory scrutiny faced by major technology companies including Amazon, Apple, Google, Meta/Facebook, and Microsoft. It details numerous legal actions, fines, and inquiries across various countries and regions such as the EU, US, UK, Australia, India, and others, highlighting concerns over competition, data privacy, market dominance, and anti-competitive practices. The comprehensive list of updates serves as a running log of the global regulatory challenges confronting Big Tech since July 2020.

title: Business Matters: Central Mass. by the Numbers
summary: This article highlights several business developments in Central Massachusetts "by the numbers." It covers the reopening of Central Pizza in Southbridge after a two-year closure and $1 million investment, Amazon Data Services' $26.7 million property acquisitions in Grafton, Shrewsbury, and Westborough, and the reopening of The Red Baron Diner as Josh's Place at the Southbridge Municipal Airport. Additionally, it details a lawsuit against the Town of Westborough over the former Regal Cinemas property and the City of Worcester's $3 million minimum bid RFP for the Denholm Building.

title: Executives from eBay and Golden State Foods Join GS1 US Board of Governors
summary: GS1 US has elected Scott Gaffney from eBay and Brian Dick from Golden State Foods to its Board of Governors. These industry leaders will help guide GS1 US's strategy to enhance supply chain efficiency and visibility across various sectors, supporting members with digital transformation and demand planning. Their expertise is expected to be instrumental in optimizing supply chains to meet consumer needs.

title: Disney Advertising enhances clean room capabilities with VideoAmp partnership
summary: Disney Advertising is upgrading its data clean room technology through a partnership with VideoAmp, integrating measurement, planning, and optimization tools to help advertisers with audience-based matches across platforms. This move comes ahead of the Disney+ ad-supported tier launch and addresses the growing need for meaningful advertising measurements in connected TV and the deprecation of third-party cookies. The collaboration aims to provide advertisers with better insights into audience reach and frequency across Disney's properties, with initial brand tests already underway with Omnicom Media Group.

title: Warner Bros. Discovery Returns HBO Max to Amazon's Prime Video Channels | Next TV | Broadcasting+Cable
summary: Warner Bros. Discovery has returned HBO Max to Amazon's Prime Video Channels in the United States, reversing a previous decision made under AT&T ownership that resulted in a loss of 5 million subscribers. This move aligns with WBD CEO David Zaslav's strategy to make HBO Max as widely available as possible. The company is also working to combine HBO Max and Discovery Plus into a single streaming service, potentially named Max.

title: Dollar General Corp. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Dollar General Corp. (DG) shares declined 0.20% on a generally poor trading day for the stock market, though the company still outperformed several key competitors like Amazon, Walmart, and Costco. Despite the daily loss, the stock broke a two-day winning streak and closed below its 52-week high. Trading volume was higher than its 50-day average.

title: Amazon.com Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) shares rose by 0.24% to $88.46 on a day when the broader market saw declines, ending a four-day losing streak. The stock's performance outpaced major competitors like Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet. Amazon closed significantly below its 52-week high, with trading volume also below its 50-day average.

title: Washington DC sues Amazon over withheld delivery driver tips
summary: The District of Columbia is suing Amazon.com Inc and its subsidiary, Amazon Logistics, alleging they withheld tips from delivery drivers using the Amazon Flex service. Attorney General Karl Racine stated that Amazon diverted tips to reduce its own labor costs and increase profits, tricking consumers into thinking they were compensating drivers. This lawsuit follows a 2021 settlement where Amazon paid $61.7 million to drivers after a similar investigation by the U.S. Federal Trade Commission.

title: Amazon seeks dismissal of class action over penalizing workers who take time off for military service
summary: Amazon has requested the dismissal of a class-action lawsuit alleging they penalized employees who took time off for military service, violating the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act (USERRA). The company claims one plaintiff was inadvertently fired and another was unqualified for promotion, while highlighting its status as a major employer of military service members. This case is part of a broader legal trend, as a similar lawsuit against American Airlines also addresses USERRA violations regarding military leave compensation.

title: Pentagon awards multibillion-dollar cloud contract to Amazon, Google, Microsoft and Oracle
summary: The Pentagon has awarded a multibillion-dollar cloud computing contract, Joint Warfighting Cloud Capability (JWCC), to Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Oracle. This move diverges from a previous single-vendor approach (JEDI contract) that faced legal challenges from Amazon alleging interference by former President Trump. The new multi-vendor contract, worth up to $9 billion, aims to provide resilient and cost-effective cloud services for the military, enabling access to sensitive data globally.

title: Is Amazon employing a deceptive strategy to keep its Prime members subscribed?
summary: Amazon is accused of using deceptive tactics to make it difficult for Prime members to cancel their subscriptions, a process code-named "Iliad" which significantly reduced cancellations. The cancellation process involves navigating multiple pages, trick questions, and attractive offers. Both the Federal Trade Commission and the Norwegian Consumer Council have received complaints and launched investigations into Amazon's methods.

title: Amazon pilots returns at Staples stores
summary: Amazon is piloting a program that allows customers to return products at select Staples stores, expanding its physical touch points for returns. This initiative builds on Amazon's existing partnerships with retailers like Kohl's and Whole Foods for return drop-offs, addressing the high rate of e-commerce returns. This expansion could add about 1,000 more return locations for Amazon across 45 states if fully implemented.

title: Will TNT Walk Away From The NBA?
summary: Warner Bros. Discovery CEO David Zaslav's recent comments questioning the necessity of the NBA for TNT have sparked industry speculation about the future of their 33-year partnership. As the NBA seeks significantly increased media rights—potentially $50 billion to $75 billion—from incumbents TNT and ESPN, the possibility of tech giants like Amazon entering the bidding process looms. While an NBA-TNT divorce seems unlikely due to their deep integration and the network's commitment to "Inside the NBA," the evolving media landscape and WBD's cost-cutting measures could lead to a reevaluation of the relationship, possibly involving a split package with streaming services.

title: New Technique Discovered To Bypass Web Application Firewalls (WAF) Of Several Vendors
summary: Cybersecurity experts at Claroty have developed a new generic method to bypass Web Application Firewalls (WAFs) from multiple leading manufacturers. This technique was discovered while researching a SQL injection flaw in Cambium Networks' wireless device management platform. The bypass abuses the default JSON syntax support in SQL engines to craft new, less common SQLi payloads that can evade WAF detection.

title: Popular WAFs Subverted by JSON Bypass
summary: Web application firewalls (WAFs) from major vendors like AWS, Cloudflare, F5, Imperva, and Palo Alto Networks are vulnerable to a JSON bypass technique, allowing attackers to obfuscate malicious SQL commands. Claroty researchers discovered that these WAFs fail to detect SQL commands embedded in the JSON format, enabling attackers to access and modify database data. The affected vendors have been notified and have updated their products to include JSON syntax support, but experts advise additional security layers beyond WAFs.

title: As Market Dynamics Pivot, Alpha Opportunities Are Set to Improve
summary: The U.S. equity market is shifting from an environment favoring passive strategies to one that is more conducive for active managers. Historically, during periods of extreme market concentration, low volatility, and low dispersion of returns, active managers often underperformed. However, as these extremes unwind—driven by factors like slowing economic growth, inflation, and rising interest rates—opportunities for active stock picking and alpha generation are expected to improve significantly.

title: The Honest Company Names CPG Veteran Carla Vernón as CEO
summary: The Honest Company has appointed Carla Vernón, a former Amazon and General Mills executive, as its new CEO, effective January 9, 2023. Vernón will become one of the few Afro-Latina chief executive officers at a US publicly traded company, focusing on category growth and profitability while continuing the company's mission of conscious living. This leadership transition follows current CEO Nick Vlahos's nearly six-year tenure, during which he led the company's IPO and expanded its brand.

title: A New Amazon Returns Location: Staples
summary: Amazon has launched a pilot program allowing customers to return items at select Staples stores, similar to its existing partnership with Kohl's. This initiative provides customers with another convenient, often free, option for returns, potentially adding 1,035 new drop-off locations if the program expands nationally. For Staples, this partnership could drive foot traffic and increase potential sales as customers enter stores for returns.

title: What is AWS Security?
summary: This article defines AWS Security, detailing the shared responsibility model between AWS and its users. It highlights the benefits of comprehensive AWS security, including compliance, threat remediation, and centralized management. The article then introduces Check Point CloudGuard as a solution to augment AWS's built-in security, offering advanced threat prevention, visibility, and control tailored for AWS environments.

title: 5 Charts on Big Tech Stocks’ Collapse
summary: In 2022, Big Tech stocks collectively lost nearly $4 trillion in market value, with the Morningstar US Technology Index down 28.7%. This collapse significantly impacted overall market returns, contributing 7 percentage points to the U.S. market index's 18.7% loss. Factors like recession fears, advertising slowdowns, and changes in consumer spending led to substantial declines for companies such as Meta Platforms, Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Nvidia.

title: Amazon struggling to hit 2-day delivery commitments, consultant says
summary: Amazon is struggling to meet its two-day delivery commitments, leading it to hand over about 1 million parcels daily to UPS and the U.S. Postal Service, according to consultant Satish Jindel. The issue primarily affects Amazon's "middle-mile" network, not its last-mile drivers. Despite these challenges, Amazon states that no customers have lost their two-day shipping benefits.

title: Amazon Games and Crystal Dynamics Strike Deal to Develop and Publish Next Major Entry in Iconic Tomb Raider Series
summary: Amazon Games and Crystal Dynamics have partnered to develop and publish the next major Tomb Raider game. This new multiplatform title will be a single-player, narrative-driven adventure continuing Lara Croft's story, utilizing Unreal Engine 5. The game is currently in early development, with Amazon Games providing global publishing support.

title: Ramsey County to move on $800 million St. Paul development on Mississippi River
summary: Ramsey County is preparing to advance an $800 million housing, office, and commercial development along the Mississippi River in downtown St. Paul, including plans for a nine-acre public park. This development aims to activate the riverfront, similar to other major cities, by creating additional terraced acreage over existing infrastructure. The article also touches on other Minnesota news, such as Amazon workers unionizing efforts, declining Twin Cities housing sales but rising prices, and a winter storm affecting Minnesota.

title: Meet the high-design startups taking on the $30 billion baby furniture industry
summary: The baby furniture industry, traditionally dominated by brands offering inexpensive, colorful plastic items, is experiencing a shift. A new generation of design-forward, direct-to-consumer startups like Lalo, Nestig, and Babyletto are emerging, focusing on aesthetics that appeal to millennial parents who prioritize stylish home designs. These companies create baby products with clean lines and muted colors, aiming to seamlessly integrate them into modern family homes rather than disrupt the overall decor.

title: ALS patients contend with $158K price tag on new drug
summary: ALS patients and physicians are facing significant obstacles due to the $158,000 annual price tag for the new drug Relyvrio, despite its recent FDA approval. Many patients report insurance delays, denials, and high out-of-pocket costs, highlighting broader issues with specialty drug pricing and insurer policies. Critics argue the drug's price is too high, especially given that its active ingredients are older, less expensive compounds, prompting some patients to consider alternative, cheaper ways to obtain the components.

title: Clean Energy Opens New Renewable Natural Gas Station for Amazon
summary: Clean Energy Fuels Corp. has opened a new renewable natural gas (RNG) fueling station in Romeoville, IL, specifically for Amazon's fleet. This station is part of a nationwide agreement to build 19 stations and is expected to provide 1.4 million gallons of RNG annually, significantly reducing carbon emissions in the Chicago area. The RNG is produced from organic waste and supports Amazon's sustainability goals by providing a low-carbon fuel alternative to diesel.

title: 7 Long-Term Stocks to Buy to Bet on Nuclear Fusion
summary: This article highlights seven long-term stocks that could benefit from advancements in nuclear fusion technology following a major breakthrough announced by scientists. The selected companies, including tech giants, energy firms, and even a food manufacturer, are either directly investing in fusion startups or stand to gain indirectly from a future with abundant, clean energy. The author emphasizes that while the technology is still in its early stages, these companies offer interesting investment opportunities for patient investors.

title: Amazon Will Open 19 Renewable Gas Stations
summary: Amazon has partnered with Clean Energy Fuels Corp. to establish 19 renewable natural gas (RNG) fueling stations across the U.S. The latest station in Romeoville, Illinois, is expected to supply 1.4 million gallons of sustainable fuel annually from organic waste, significantly reducing carbon emissions. This initiative supports Amazon's use of RNG in its heavy-duty truck fleet, with 17 more stations planned to open.

title: Clean Energy Opens New Renewable Natural Gas Station for Amazon
summary: Clean Energy Fuels has opened a new renewable natural gas (RNG) fueling station in Romeoville, IL, specifically for Amazon's fleet and other truck fleets in the Chicago area. This station is part of a larger agreement to build 19 stations nationwide for Amazon, aiming to provide 1.4 million gallons of low-carbon fuel annually. The initiative is set to significantly reduce carbon emissions and lessen the impact of climate change in the region.

title: FedEx Introduces AI-powered Robotic Sorting Arm to Drive Operational Efficiency at its Singapore Hub
summary: FedEx Express has deployed an AI-powered intelligent sorting robot at its South Pacific (SPAC) Regional Hub in Singapore to enhance operational efficiency and digitize its logistics network. This initiative, supported by the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS), reflects a growing trend in warehouse automation within the logistics industry. The robotic arm can sort up to 1,000 packages per hour with high accuracy, aiming for round-the-clock sorting operations.

title: Purpose pushes through with yield-focused single-stock ETFs
summary: Purpose Investments has launched its initial suite of Yield Shares ETFs, which are single-stock, yield-focused ETFs. These world-first ETFs allow Canadian investors to gain exposure to individual stocks like Tesla and Apple while receiving enhanced monthly distributions through a covered-call options strategy and moderate leverage. The ETFs also address an advisor pain point by simplifying the process of generating augmented income from individual stocks and offer Canadian dollar-denominated exposure to U.S. stocks with currency hedging.

title: Michael Bloomberg reportedly eyes buying Dow Jones, Washington Post
summary: Michael Bloomberg is reportedly interested in expanding his media empire by acquiring either Dow Jones, the parent company of the Wall Street Journal, or the Washington Post. While Bloomberg favors Dow Jones as an "ideal" fit to create a business news juggernaut, he would also consider the Washington Post if Jeff Bezos were to sell, aiming to compete with The New York Times. Despite the speculation, representatives for Bloomberg and the Washington Post have dismissed the rumors, with News Corp declining to comment.

title: Media mogul Mike Bloomberg looks to buy WSJ or WaPo: Report
summary: Billionaire Michael Bloomberg is reportedly interested in acquiring either Dow Jones, parent company of The Wall Street Journal, or The Washington Post. Such a merger would significantly expand his financial data and news empire, potentially boosting sales of Bloomberg Terminals. While Bloomberg sees Dow Jones as an ideal fit, The Washington Post is not currently for sale, and News Corp has not been approached.

title: iShares MSCI ACWI Index Fund (ACWI) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the iShares MSCI ACWI Index Fund (ACWI), including its stock price, key statistics, top holdings, and dividend information. It details the fund's portfolio as of July 31, 2025, highlighting major tech companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple among its top constituents. The report also includes performance metrics and historical data for ACWI.

title: eBay Business Model
summary: The eBay business model centers around an online marketplace for buyers and sellers, generating revenue primarily through seller fees, advertising, and subsidiary contributions. Founded in 1995, eBay has grown into a major e-commerce platform by offering a low-fee, accessible auction and direct sales environment. The article details eBay's customer segments, value propositions, revenue streams, key resources, activities, partners, cost structure, and a SWOT analysis, while also comparing it to competitors like Amazon and Alibaba.

title: Automotive Grade Linux Showcases Open Source Technology and Software Defined Vehicle at CES 2023
summary: Automotive Grade Linux (AGL) will showcase its open source platform for connected car technologies at CES 2023 from January 5-8. The demonstrations will feature infotainment and instrument cluster technology developed by the AGL community, alongside contributions from members such as Amazon Web Services (AWS), EPAM, Igalia, Panasonic, Renesas, and VNC Automotive. EPAM will specifically highlight its building blocks for the Software Defined Vehicle, including virtualization for digital cockpits and central compute units, cloud-to-vehicle services, and AAOS integration.

title: What Is A Snowflake Database? Introduction To Modern Data Platform
summary: This article provides an in-depth introduction to Snowflake, a modern cloud-independent data warehousing platform. It explains Snowflake's architecture, including its cloud services, query processing, and database storage layers, and highlights its numerous benefits like scalability, security, and seamless data sharing. The article also differentiates Snowflake from other data platforms based on architecture, maintenance, scalability, and cost.

title: Better Buy: Rivian Stock or Lucid Stock?
summary: This article compares Rivian Automotive (RIVN) and Lucid Group (LCID) as long-term investment opportunities in the electric vehicle (EV) market. While both companies have seen significant stock price drops since their IPOs, the analysis concludes that Rivian appears to be the better buy due to higher production, increasing reservations, a stronger cash position, and a lower price-to-sales ratio compared to Lucid. However, the author cautions that the entire EV industry remains volatile due to rising material costs, supply chain issues, and potential recession impacts.

title: Should John Hancock Multifactor Large Cap ETF (JHML) Be on Your Investing Radar?
summary: The John Hancock Multifactor Large Cap ETF (JHML) offers broad exposure to the Large Cap Blend segment of the US equity market, launched in 2015 with over $722 million in assets. It has an expense ratio of 0.29% and a 1.46% dividend yield. The ETF's portfolio is heavily allocated to Information Technology, with top holdings including Microsoft, Apple, and Amazon.

title: Amazon starts rolling out its Prime Air drone delivery service
summary: Amazon has launched its Prime Air drone delivery service in Lockeford, California, and College Station, Texas, aiming to deliver packages under five pounds within an hour. The company is using its MK27-2 drones for deliveries and plans to introduce an improved MK30 drone in 2024 for broader expansion. This move positions Amazon among other companies like Alphabet's Wing, which are also developing drone delivery solutions.

title: Washington Housing Conservancy Buys D.C. Development
summary: The Washington Housing Conservancy (WHC) has acquired the 212-unit Loree Grand property in Washington, D.C., to preserve affordable housing for moderate- and low-income families. This $71.5 million acquisition, supported by Amazon's Housing Equity Fund and the Washington Housing Initiative Impact Pool, expands WHC's portfolio to over 1,600 units. The initiative aims to stabilize rents, prevent displacement, and create more affordable units in a high-opportunity neighborhood.

title: Warren Buffett Surpassed Jeff Bezos in Wealth, May Overtake Bill Gates
summary: Warren Buffett has surpassed Jeff Bezos in wealth this year and is nearing Bill Gates' net worth, potentially making him the second-richest American after Elon Musk. This shift is attributed to the strong performance of Berkshire Hathaway stock compared to the declines seen in tech giants like Amazon, Microsoft, and Tesla. Despite significant charitable donations, Buffett's conservative investment strategy has proven resilient in the current economic climate, attracting investors away from growth stocks.

title: Does This Acquisition Make Microsoft a Bear Market Buy?
summary: Microsoft's recent acquisition of a 4% stake in the London Stock Exchange Group and a 10-year strategic partnership to create a centralized financial data platform could make it an attractive investment. This move aims to strengthen Microsoft's competitive position in the cloud and financial data markets, projecting $5 billion in revenue from the partnership. The acquisition could broaden and diversify Microsoft's income streams amidst a rapidly growing global finance cloud market, helping it maintain its lead against competitors like Amazon and Google Cloud.

title: What time does Target close today on New Year’s Eve 2022?
summary: This article provides details on Target's closing time for New Year's Eve 2022, which is 9 p.m., with an opening time of 8 a.m. It also lists various other retailers offering New Year's Eve deals, including Walmart, Amazon, Samsung, Wayfair, Nordstrom, and Tory Burch.

title: What is TSMC?
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) is the world's largest semiconductor manufacturer, producing chips for major tech companies like Apple, Amazon, and Google. It focuses solely on manufacturing for third parties, unlike competitors, and boasts significant market dominance and advanced process technology. The article also discusses the potential impact of a Chinese invasion of Taiwan on TSMC and its global operations, as well as the company's expansion plans in the US.

title: Apple’s market value drops below $2 trillion
summary: Apple Inc.'s market capitalization fell below $2 trillion for the first time since March 2021, driven by concerns over high inflation and a slowing global economy impacting demand for its products. This significant drop follows a year after the company briefly reached a $3 trillion valuation. Analysts have downgraded Apple due to anticipated supply chain issues and reduced consumer spending on high-end phones, forecasting a potential revenue decline.

title: Amazon's AWS Growth Strategy Probably More Sustainable Than Oracle's (AMZN)
summary: The article compares the sustainability of growth strategies between Amazon's AWS and Oracle. While Oracle has seen growth due to acquisitions and price cutting, AWS's growth is attributed to its vertically integrated cloud infrastructure and product strength, accounting for a significant portion of Amazon's profits. The author suggests Oracle's strategy may not be sustainable given its debt-to-sales ratio and profitability, and advises that Oracle needs a clearer plan for profitable growth.

title: Amazon to slash more than 18,000 jobs in an escalation of cuts
summary: Amazon is laying off over 18,000 employees, a significant increase from its previously announced cuts, as the technology sector experiences a deepening slump. The reductions are primarily within Amazon's corporate retail division and human resources, reflecting a shift to a stronger cost structure amid uncertain economic conditions. This move follows similar workforce reductions by other tech giants like Salesforce, and investors reacted positively to Amazon's decision.

title: Distributed Cloud Global Market Report 2022: Ukraine-Russia War Impact
summary: The global distributed cloud market is projected to grow significantly, from $2.84 billion in 2021 to $7.42 billion in 2026, despite disruptions caused by the Russia-Ukraine war and the COVID-19 pandemic. This growth is driven by the increasing adoption of distributed cloud architectures to meet performance, compliance, and edge computing demands. Technological innovation, such as F5's Distributed Cloud WAAP, is a key trend, with companies focusing on advanced solutions to secure and manage applications across multi-cloud environments.

title: Opendoor: The Future 'Amazon Of Real Estate' At A Compelling Valuation
summary: Opendoor's stock, like many SPACs, has plummeted, trading significantly below its original SPAC price and previous highs. Despite outperforming initial SPAC estimates before this year, the company now faces heightened risks due to the challenging macro environment and rising interest rates impacting the real estate market. However, with its new third-party Marketplace offering in select cities, Opendoor still possesses a substantial long-term growth opportunity, with current investor sentiment potentially offering a compelling valuation.

title: Amazon to cut 18,000 jobs as layoffs expand in tech sector
summary: Amazon is cutting over 18,000 jobs, more than initially planned, affecting primarily its e-commerce and human resources divisions. CEO Andy Jassy cited the uncertain economy and rapid hiring as reasons for the significant workforce reduction, which follows similar layoffs in the tech sector. This move highlights a shift for Amazon from a pandemic-fueled growth period to a more cautious outlook.

title: Software company Pegasystems to lay off 4% workforce amid recession fears
summary: Software company Pegasystems is set to lay off 4% of its workforce, totaling over 6,000 employees, due to looming recession fears in 2023. This move is part of the company's strategy to improve its go-to-market operating model and is expected to incur a charge of $18.9 million in Q4 2022 for severance and benefit costs. Pegasystems joins other major tech firms like Amazon and Salesforce in announcing significant job cuts amid economic uncertainties.

title: 8 Secrets About Shopping on QVC You Need to Know, According to Experts
summary: This article reveals eight insider secrets for shopping on QVC, according to retail experts. It covers how QVC has evolved beyond traditional TV broadcasts with streaming options and a strong online presence, highlighting the mix of proprietary and well-known brands available. The piece also delves into QVC's strategic marketing, focusing on generic products for a broad demographic, discounts on "as-is" items, and the psychological influence of its hosts, while cautioning against impulse purchases, especially on April Fool's Day.

title: You might actually want a TiVo TV someday
summary: Xperi, the company that owns the TiVo brand, is launching its own TiVo OS smart TV platform in Europe in the first half of 2023, with U.S. availability planned for the future. The platform focuses on content discovery over apps, offering recommendations across various streaming services and an intuitive voice search. While it currently lacks a robust app store compared to competitors, its neutral approach and potential for ad revenue sharing aim to attract TV manufacturers.

title: CVS eyes next-generation health clinics after Amazon’s move for One Medical
summary: CVS Health Corp. is expanding its healthcare services by investing $100 million in Carbon Health Inc., a chain of urgent- and primary-care clinics. Additionally, CVS is reportedly in negotiations to acquire Oak Street Health Inc., a chain specializing in Medicare recipients, for over $10 billion. These moves signal CVS's strategy to evolve from a drugstore chain into a full-service healthcare company in response to trends like Amazon's acquisition of One Medical.

title: 10 Data Center Companies You Need To Watch In 2023
summary: The data center market is experiencing significant growth driven by digital transformation, IoT, 5G, and streaming content, with hyperscalers accounting for a large portion of this expansion. This article highlights ten key data center companies—including major players like AWS and Equinix, and rapidly growing firms like Aligned Data Centers and Evocative—that are making significant investments in new construction, acquisitions, and technological advancements to meet the escalating global demand for capacity and enhance their service offerings. These companies are adapting to market changes, such as leadership transitions and strategic partnerships, to expand their footprints and provide advanced infrastructure solutions worldwide.

title: Amazon Expands Prime Delivery Service to More Web Stores
summary: Amazon is broadening its "Buy With Prime" program, allowing Prime subscribers to utilize the company's fast delivery services on participating third-party websites. Initially launched via invitation, the service will be widely accessible across the US by the end of January. Amazon reports that the program has significantly boosted conversions, with a 25% increase in purchases on sites integrating the service.

title: Helping Construction Firms Gain Useful Insights from Their Data
summary: Autodesk and Amazon have partnered to help construction firms leverage their abundant but often unused data. They are integrating Autodesk Construction Cloud with Amazon S3 and Redshift, allowing construction teams to synchronize project data and use advanced Amazon analytics, AI, and Machine Learning tools like Amazon Rekognition, Amazon Forecast, and Amazon QuickSight to gain actionable insights, improve efficiency, and enhance safety on job sites. This collaboration aims to make data analysis more accessible and powerful for construction professionals.

title: Stocks waver as traders eye US inflation and interest rates
summary: Global stock markets experienced fluctuations following a pessimistic economic forecast from the World Bank. Investors are awaiting critical inflation data and corporate earnings reports, with a particular focus on the upcoming US consumer price index. The Federal Reserve's future policy decisions regarding interest rate hikes to combat inflation remain a key concern, though some officials express hope for a "soft landing" despite the ongoing need for restrictive monetary policy.

title: Amazon workers' union victory upheld by U.S. labor board director
summary: A U.S. labor board director has upheld the Amazon workers' union victory at a Staten Island warehouse, moving the company closer to bargaining with staff. Amazon plans to appeal the decision, arguing the election process was unfair. This landmark unionization marks the first time U.S. Amazon staff have unionized and is a significant moment for organized labor amid Amazon's recent layoffs and criticisms of its employee practices.

title: Amazon Fashion Launches Collaboration With Rent the Runway
summary: Amazon Fashion is launching an online storefront with Rent the Runway, expanding the rental company's reach beyond its subscription model. The collaboration will feature both previously worn and new designer styles, and Rent the Runway recognized approximately $4.6 million in revenue from this partnership in the quarter ending October 31st. This move aims to boost Amazon's existing apparel resale offerings.

title: Macy's CEO is 'cautious' about consumer spending after seeing dip in self purchases
summary: Macy's CEO Jeff Gennette expressed caution about consumer spending despite a strong holiday season, specifically noting a decline in "self purchases" during the lull periods in November and December. He also pointed to rising credit card balances and delinquencies among Macy's customers as signs of consumer pressure due to stubbornly high inflation. While acknowledging the strength of luxury brands like Bluemercury and Bloomingdale's, Gennette emphasized a need to be cautious but ready for market opportunities.

title: From Apple To Amazon And Toyota, Here Are The Top 10 Biggest Companies In The World
summary: This article lists the top 10 biggest companies in the world based on revenue. Walmart, State Grid Corporation Of China, and Amazon lead the list, primarily highlighting a competition between US and Chinese firms. The article also includes companies from Japan and Germany, detailing their revenue, founding year, and number of employees.

title: SoundHound axes nearly 50% of staff less than a year after IPO
summary: SoundHound, a speech and music recognition company, laid off nearly half of its remaining staff less than a year after its IPO. The company cited changing economic conditions and continued to book losses in Q3 2022 despite increased revenue. This move follows an earlier 10% staff reduction and comes amid widespread job cuts in the tech industry.

title: Costco Wholesale Corp. stock rises Tuesday, outperforms market
summary: Costco Wholesale Corp. (COST) shares rose 0.31% on Tuesday, closing at $486.77, despite a general market decline for the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock outperformed competitors like Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) and Walmart Inc. (WMT), which both saw declines. Costco's trading volume was below its 50-day average.

title: Microsoft to shed 10,000 jobs, adding to glut of tech layoffs
summary: Microsoft announced it would eliminate 10,000 jobs and take a $1.2 billion charge to earnings as cloud-computing customers reduce spending and the company prepares for a potential recession. Despite this, Microsoft plans to increase investment in generative artificial intelligence, seen as a new growth area. The layoffs, affecting less than 5% of its workforce, are expected to conclude by the end of March, though strategic hiring will continue.

title: Temu Review: What Is Temu and Why Is It Suddenly America’s Most Popular App?
summary: Temu, the American sister brand of China's Pinduoduo, has rapidly become America's most popular shopping app since its U.S. launch last fall. Its success is primarily attributed to extremely low prices, free shipping, and aggressive advertising. While the author found good deals on certain electronics and kitchen items, he advises caution on other categories and notes the app's business model involves cutting out middlemen for direct sales.

title: Walmart Plays Catch-Up As Amazon Pulls Further Ahead Of Its E-Commerce Competitors
summary: Amazon continues to lead the e-commerce sector due to its ongoing innovation and expanding scale, maintaining a widening competitive moat. While Walmart dominates traditional retail, it is aggressively investing to close the e-commerce gap by leveraging its assets and emulating Amazon's strategies. Although Amazon's stock has recently pulled back, the author believes it still offers significant long-term growth potential, whereas Walmart's future growth hinges critically on the flawless execution of its e-commerce strategy.

title: Amazon ends its charity donation program following other cost-cutting efforts
summary: Amazon is discontinuing its AmazonSmile charity donation program by February 20, 2023, as part of broader cost-cutting measures. Since 2013, the program has donated over $400 million to U.S. charities by giving 0.5% of eligible purchases. While Amazon states the impact was spread too thin, many small charities and shoppers have expressed dismay, noting the program's significant benefit to their operations.

title: Amazon, Microsoft Job Cuts Deal Another Blow to Ailing Seattle
summary: Job cuts at Amazon and Microsoft are impacting Seattle, America's second-largest tech hub, which is already struggling to recover from the pandemic's blow to its commuter economy. While the 28,000 global job losses might seem small for the region's 2.1 million workforce, this development is a significant psychological setback, making investors and businesses hesitant about new projects and expansion. This further exacerbates the economic challenges faced by the city.

title: Amazon to Plow Extra $35 Billion Into Virginia Data Centers in Cloud Race
summary: Amazon's cloud unit, Amazon Web Services (AWS), plans to invest an additional $35 billion in new data centers in Virginia by 2040. This move aims to reinforce its leading position against competitors like Microsoft and Alphabet. The expansion is expected to create 1,000 jobs in the state, which is a crucial hub for AWS operations.

title: 予算3万円台の「スマートウォッチ」おすすめ5選　人気の「Apple Watch SE」から、コスパが良い高機能モデルまで【2023年1月版】 - Fav-Log by ITmedia
summary: This article recommends five smartwatches under 30,000 yen, including popular models like the Apple Watch SE and high-performance, cost-effective alternatives. It caters to users looking for smartwatches equipped with functions like health tracking, mobile payments, and sports activity monitoring. The selection includes devices suitable for various needs, from outdoor enthusiasts to those prioritizing design.

title: Stripe becomes a strategic payments partner for Amazon
summary: Amazon is significantly broadening its use of Stripe's payment processing platform, making Stripe a strategic payments partner across various Amazon business units in the US, Europe, and Canada. In return, Stripe will expand its utilization of Amazon Web Services (AWS), focusing on AWS Graviton chips and Nitro Enclaves for enhanced performance and security. This expanded partnership builds on an existing relationship, with Stripe set to handle a substantial portion of Amazon's transaction volume.

title: Building blocks for the future: A look inside General Mills’ experience at the 2023 Consumer Electronics Show
summary: General Mills attended the 2023 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) to explore emerging technologies and their impact on consumers' eating habits and the food industry. The company focused on three innovation pillars: Connected Home, 360 Shoppability, and Agile Fulfillment, noting trends like AI integration, generative AI, faster hardware, and sustainability. General Mills plans to translate this inspiration into action by exploring partnerships, such as with Smarter, to enhance kitchen experiences and gather consumer data.

title: Amazon Pharmacy Introduces RxPass: Unlimited Prescription Medications for Only $5 a Month, Delivered Free to your Door—Available Exclusively for Prime Members
summary: Amazon Pharmacy has launched RxPass, a new Prime membership benefit offering unlimited access to common generic medications for $5 a month, with free delivery. This service aims to provide affordable and convenient access to prescriptions for over 80 common health conditions, allowing Prime members to save significantly, especially those taking multiple medications monthly. It complements existing Prime prescription savings and other Amazon Pharmacy services, including options for those with and without insurance.

title: The Best Leggings for Literally Everything
summary: SELF's 2023 Activewear Awards present a curated list of the best leggings for various activities, from intense workouts to everyday lounging. The selection process involved extensive testing by 45 individuals and focused on criteria like waistband fit, fabric quality, and activity-specific features, with a strong emphasis on size inclusivity. The article highlights top picks across categories like compression, lounging, everyday wear, yoga, strength training, and cold weather, including "All-Time Bests" that testers consistently love.

title: IMAA welcomes seven new media partners for 2023
summary: The Independent Media Agencies of Australia (IMAA) has announced seven new media partners for 2023, bringing their total to 44. The new partners include Amazon Ads, BrandSpace, Integral Ad Science (IAS), Reddit, Sports Entertainment Network (SEN), StackAdapt, and Taboola. IMAA's chair, Jacquie Alley, highlighted the association's continued growth and the significance of these partnerships for independent agencies in Australia.

title: Big Tech to Congress: Listen to Taylor Swift and Go After Ticketmaster, Not Us
summary: Lobbyists for major technology companies capitalized on public outrage over Ticketmaster's handling of Taylor Swift concert tickets to deflect congressional antitrust scrutiny away from Big Tech. They argued that Congress should focus on clear monopolies like Ticketmaster, implying that existing antitrust laws are sufficient and that efforts to reform them to target tech giants are misguided. This strategy led to a Senate Judiciary Committee hearing focusing on Live Nation/Ticketmaster's monopolistic practices instead of Big Tech.

title: Intel shares drop on weak quarterly results and expectations for another quarter of losses
summary: Intel's shares fell after the company reported weaker-than-expected fourth-quarter results and provided a soft forecast for the first quarter of 2023. The chipmaker experienced a significant decline in revenue for the fourth consecutive quarter due to a downturn in PC chip sales and broader economic headwinds.
