The company also announced a net loss for Q4 and anticipates further losses in Q1, leading to concerns about its financial performance in the near future.

title: Lowe's Cos. stock falls Thursday, underperforms market
summary: Lowe's Cos. (LOW) stock fell 2.09% on Thursday, closing at $201.81, despite a positive trading session for the broader market with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both rising. The stock underperformed competitors like Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) but fared better than Home Depot Inc. (HD) and Walmart Inc. (WMT), which also saw slight declines. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for Lowe's, with its trading volume exceeding its 50-day average.

title: Licking County becomes the 'bullseye' for development one year after Intel
summary: One year after Intel announced its plans for a $20 billion computer chip manufacturing facility in Licking County, Ohio, the area has become a hub for development. This growth has led to escalating property values and changes in the rural landscape, causing some residents to relocate. While the Intel project promises significant economic benefits and job creation, local officials are also grappling with challenges like funding disparities and utility expansion conflicts.

title: News | Amazon Lands Buyer for Bay Area Office Campus Amid Company’s Real Estate Contraction
summary: Amazon is selling its four-building Metro Corporate Center office campus in Milpitas, California, to Dermody Properties for conversion into warehouse space. This disposition is part of Amazon's larger real estate contraction and efforts to cut rising expenses following a period of rapid pandemic-era expansion. The company originally purchased the property for $123 million in late 2021 and is also undergoing significant job cuts.

title: S3 Intelligent-Tiering's Archive Instant Access Tier
summary: Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering now includes an Archive Instant Access (AIA) tier, which automatically moves infrequently accessed data to a more cost-effective storage class while still allowing sub-millisecond retrieval. This tier offers benefits such as no retrieval charges, no minimum storage duration, and zero operational overhead, making it ideal for data with unpredictable access patterns, backups, and log files. NetApp BlueXP Cloud Volumes ONTAP can further extend these benefits by enabling automatic tiering between EBS and S3 Intelligent-Tiering, along with other data management features.

title: Internet Records Historic $1 Trillion Consumer Milestone
summary: Comscore announced that the internet surpassed $1 trillion in U.S. online retail consumer spending in 2022, marking a 21% year-over-year increase. Mobile devices played a crucial role, accounting for almost 40% of the total digital commerce and growing faster than desktop spending. This milestone highlights the accelerated adoption of online purchasing, with significant growth in categories like event tickets, digital content, and apparel.

title: Amgen lays off 300, does not disclose whether any are in Thousand Oaks
summary: Amgen, a Thousand Oaks-based biotech company, announced workforce restructuring resulting in approximately 300 layoffs, about 1.3% of its global workforce. The company stated the job cuts are mainly in the commercial team in the United States, but did not specify if any were in its Thousand Oaks headquarters. This follows other major tech companies like Microsoft, Google, and Amazon also announcing significant layoffs, and Amgen released Q4 2022 financial results showing flat revenue and a 15% decrease in net income compared to the previous year.

title: Amazon’s holiday sales growth outpaced superstore competitors in 2022
summary: Amazon's U.S. consumer sales during the 2022 holiday season grew by 9 percent year-over-year, surpassing its performance in the previous three years. This growth rate outpaced several major superstore competitors like Target and Costco, matching only Walmart. Other retailers such as Best Buy and Bed Bath & Beyond experienced declines in holiday sales.

title: AerCap celebrates the delivery of its 50th 737-800 Boeing Converted Freighter
summary: AerCap Holdings N.V. has reached a significant milestone by delivering its 50th 737-800 Boeing Converted Freighter (BCF). This converted aircraft was redelivered to GOL Linhas Aéreas to support a Latin-American e-commerce company's logistics, highlighting the growing demand for express air cargo. AerCap Cargo, a launch customer for the 737-800BCF program, emphasized the aircraft's versatility and reliability in meeting e-commerce logistics needs.

title: 10 Companies Who Inspire Creative Team Collaboration
summary: This article highlights ten companies that excel at fostering creative team collaboration. It explains how these businesses, ranging from tech giants like Google and Amazon to entertainment powerhouses like Disney and Blizzard Entertainment, prioritize open communication, diverse perspectives, and inclusive environments to drive innovation and achieve shared goals. The article emphasizes that creative collaboration is crucial for developing new ideas and products, leading to a sense of shared ownership and motivation among team members.

title: AMZN Earnings: All you need to know about Amazon’s Q4 2022 earnings results
summary: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) reported its Q4 2022 earnings, with net sales increasing 9% year-over-year to $149.2 billion, beating expectations. However, net income significantly dropped to $0.3 billion, or $0.03 per share, from $14.3 billion in the prior year, causing the stock to fall 5% in after-market hours. For Q1 2023, Amazon projects net sales between $121-126 billion.

title: Dollar General Corp. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
summary: Dollar General Corp. (DG) stock fell 1.83% on Thursday, closing at $231.31, despite a mixed trading session with the S&P 500 rising. The stock underperformed several competitors, including Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) which rose, and Walmart Inc. (WMT) and Costco Wholesale Corp. (COST) which had varied performances. This decline ended a two-day winning streak for Dollar General, leaving it $30.90 below its 52-week high.

title: Gap Inc. Wants to Outsource Warehousing for SMBs
summary: Gap Inc., through its GPS Platform Services, is partnering with UPS-owned Ware2Go to provide outsourced warehousing and fulfillment solutions for small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs). This collaboration aims to offer SMBs access to enterprise-level supply chain capabilities, including advanced technology, automation, and a network of over 35 warehouses, enabling fast fulfillment and allowing businesses to focus on marketing and product development. The initiative builds on a growing trend of co-warehousing, where SMBs share resources to achieve cost savings, operational efficiency, and better technology access, following similar models adopted by companies like Amazon and American Eagle Outfitters.

title: Sysco Corp. stock falls Friday, still outperforms market
summary: Sysco Corp. (SYY) stock fell 0.53% to $76.72 on Friday, February 3, 2023, while still outperforming the broader market which saw the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also decline. Despite its losses, Sysco's stock performed better than competitors like Amazon.com Inc., Walmart Inc., and Costco Wholesale Corp. Trading volume for Sysco was 3.3 million, exceeding its 50-day average.

title: State Street Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLY) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY), including its stock price, key statistics, top holdings, and dividend information. It highlights XLY's portfolio value, turnover rate, and its largest positions in companies like Amazon, Tesla, and Home Depot, based on its latest N-PORT filing. The report also includes historical performance data, fundamental charts, and recent trading actions within the ETF's portfolio.

title: What ‘Digital First’ Means for Church & Dwight
summary: Church & Dwight's "digital-first" strategy, highlighted by the successful acquisition of the Hero brand and a significant increase in e-commerce sales, is driving its growth. The company is investing heavily in digital marketing (70% of its spend) and comprehensive employee training to elevate its "digital IQ" across all departments. This approach aims to make digital a business builder rather than just a capability, leveraging sustained post-pandemic consumer behavior towards online shopping.

title: News | Amazon’s HQ2 Set To Open This Year in Northern Virginia Even As Tech Giants Scale Back
summary: Amazon's HQ2 in Northern Virginia is on track to open its initial phase this year, despite other tech giants scaling back operations and laying off employees. The first phase of the megadevelopment is scheduled to debut in summer 2023, with Amazon already having hired over 5,000 employees for the location. This progress contrasts with other tech companies like Google and Microsoft, which are reducing their office footprints.

title: CEBA: Energy Customers Announce Record-High of Nearly 17 GW of Clean Energy in 2022, Despite Policy and Market Challenges
summary: In 2022, commercial and industrial energy customers procured a record-breaking 16.9 gigawatts of new clean energy deals, as announced by CEBA. Amazon led the list for the third consecutive year, with General Motors achieving its second-highest annual deal volume. The report highlights a significant commitment to decarbonization, with 54% of participating customers being new to the market, and CEBA's community having catalyzed 64.5 gigawatts of clean energy since 2014, surpassing their 2025 goal.

title: Sonos Is Having a Secret Sale Ahead of President's Day
summary: Sonos is holding a "secret sale" on its popular speakers and soundbars ahead of President's Day and the Super Bowl, offering significant discounts. Highlighting the rarity of Sonos sales and their premium status, the article details deals on products like the Sonos Arc and Sonos Beam Gen 2, recommending them for home theater upgrades. The sale includes individual speakers and immersive sets, but the duration and inventory are limited.

title: NetApp adds storage buying options, arrays
summary: NetApp is expanding its storage offerings with new buying options, hybrid cloud capabilities for VMware workloads on Google Cloud, and upcoming QLC all-flash arrays. The NetApp Advance portfolio introduces programs like the Storage Lifecycle Program and Cloud Advantage Program to provide flexibility for on-premises hardware refreshes and cloud storage migrations, positioning NetApp in closer competition with Pure Storage. A new line of all-flash QLC-based arrays, the AFF C-Series, and an AFF A-Series model for remote offices are set to debut in March.

title: NFL extends Zebra next gen data partnership until 2025
summary: The NFL has extended its data partnership with Zebra Technologies until the end of the 2025 season. Zebra will continue to supply live insights from on-field tracking devices which feed into the NFL's Next Gen Stats, enhancing broadcasts and digital experiences for fans. This partnership, ongoing since 2015, also provides valuable data for coaches and league rule evaluations.

title: Cure imaging storage challenges in the medical cloud
summary: This article discusses the challenges healthcare organizations face with storing and managing vast amounts of medical imaging data, which constitutes 90% of all medical data worldwide. It proposes a solution using NetApp ONTAP and Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP to move medical imaging data to the cloud, offering benefits like cost-effectiveness, enhanced data protection, and improved accessibility for AI/ML analytics and research. The solution also aids in sustainability efforts and ensures compliance, enabling a seamless transition from on-premises storage to the cloud.

title: CVS buys primary care provider Oak Street for $10.6 billion
summary: CVS Health is acquiring primary care provider Oak Street Health for approximately $10.6 billion, paying $39 per share in cash. This acquisition deepens CVS's presence in primary care, particularly in value-based care arrangements for Medicare Advantage patients, aligning with federal efforts to cut costs and improve health outcomes. The deal follows other major investments by CVS and rivals like UnitedHealth Group and Walgreens into primary care services.

title: Here’s Some Wisdom: Expect the Unexpected
summary: The article discusses tough investing lessons from 2022, a year marked by high inflation, aggressive monetary policy, and geopolitical conflict, which challenged conventional investment wisdom. It covers five key lessons: the failure of traditional diversification, the unpredictable impact of rising rates on stocks, the vulnerability of sustainable investments to market cycles, the risks of thematic investing, and the fact that private markets do not always go up. The author emphasizes the importance of accepting uncertainty in investing, preparing for the unexpected, and focusing on fundamental factors over macro timing.

title: A new era for discoveries at Admera Health
summary: Admera Health, a lab providing genomic and bioinformatic services, is expanding its next-generation sequencing capabilities through strategic investments in advanced technology. With CEO Yun Zhao at the helm, the company works with various clients globally, from biotech firms to universities, focusing on human, animal, and plant health. Admera's adoption of Illumina NovaSeq 6000 and the upcoming NovaSeq X Plus enables faster turnaround times, increased sequencing capacity, and higher quality data, marking a new phase of growth and discovery for the company.

title: Rhode Island Leverages Blockchain Technology for Easier Business
summary: Rhode Island has implemented blockchain technology to streamline the credentialing process for Certified Public Accountants (CPAs), making professional license renewal faster and more efficient. Developed by Infosys Public Services using a distributed ledger on AWS, this solution provides secure digital identities and allows CPAs to store credentials on their phones. The state plans to expand this technology to enable businesses to form LLCs and corporations through a single data entry website, simplifying interactions with government agencies.

title: Ring Floodlight Cam Wired Pro vs. Arlo Pro 3 Floodlight Camera: Which is right for you?
summary: This article compares the Ring Floodlight Cam Wired Pro and the Arlo Pro 3 Floodlight Camera, highlighting their features, pros, and cons to help consumers decide which security camera best fits their needs. Both cameras are priced at $250 and offer robust security features, but differ in video resolution, integration with smart home ecosystems, installation methods, and subscription service benefits. The Ring camera is ideal for Alexa users seeking a hardwired solution with extensive motion detection, while the Arlo excels with 2K HDR video, wireless design, and broader smart assistant compatibility.

title: Diverse Brokers in Bond Issuances
summary: Morgan Stanley is actively working to increase the involvement of minority-owned broker dealers in corporate bond issuances by acting as a diversity and inclusion coordinator. The firm aims to elevate their roles from passive to active co-managers, providing access to broader investor networks and unique insights. This initiative has led to increased partnerships and improved economic payouts for these diversity firms, demonstrating their value beyond just fulfilling DEI objectives.

title: Uber goes multicloud, announcing big deals with Oracle and Google
summary: Uber has announced a multicloud strategy, signing a seven-year partnership with Oracle to move critical workloads to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, a deal estimated to be worth over a billion dollars. Concurrently, Uber is expanding its existing partnership with Google Cloud to modernize its infrastructure and enhance the customer experience. This move aims to accelerate Uber's path to profitability by closing its on-premises data centers and focusing resources on core strengths, while leveraging the price, performance, and flexibility offered by Oracle and Google.

title: KB Securities launches AI-based RoboNews on US stocks
summary: KB Securities has launched KB RoboNews, an AI-based service offering in-depth analysis of public disclosure information for US-listed companies. Developed in collaboration with Whisper, the service analyzes around 3,000 major US stocks, providing insights on investor movements, trading trends, and virtual investment reports. Clients can access this data via KB Securities' M-able Mini mobile trading system.

title: Hedge Fund and Insider Trading News: George Soros, Seth Klarman, D.E. Shaw, Millennium Management, Element Capital, Coliseum Capital Management, Renaissance Technologies, Clearfield Inc (CLFD), Alset Inc. (AEI), and More
summary: This article provides an overview of recent activities in the hedge fund world, including D.E. Shaw closing its first private equity fund, Seth Klarman increasing stakes in Amazon, Alphabet, and Meta, and George Soros' fund investments. It also covers Millennium Management's hiring of a star macro manager, Element Capital's rule relaxation after a significant loss, and a proxy battle involving Coliseum Capital Management and Purple Innovation. The report highlights a strong month for Asia-focused hedge funds, increased scrutiny for crypto's hedge fund ties, Renaissance Technologies' substantial increase in Tesla shares, and insider buying in Clearfield Inc. and Alset Inc.

title: Akamai Stock Tumbles on New Cloud Computing Push
summary: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) stock tumbled after its fourth-quarter earnings report, not primarily due to the results themselves, but because of a major shift in strategy towards aggressive expansion in the highly competitive cloud computing market. The company plans substantial capital spending to build out its new Akamai Connected Cloud, aiming for cloud computing to eventually account for most of its revenue. Analysts expressed concern over the increased risk and competition with hyperscale cloud providers, leading to ratings downgrades and a significant drop in stock price.

title: SaaS company Blackbaud closes four data centers as it moves to the cloud
summary: Blackbaud, a Software-as-a-Service company specializing in CRM and analytics for non-profits, has closed four colocation data centers as part of its migration to cloud platforms like AWS and Azure. The move aims to reduce complexity and costs, despite a recent 10.8 percent revenue growth. The company plans further data center closures and network shutdowns, following a trend seen with other tech giants like Uber.

title: The 7 Best ChatGPT Stocks to Buy Now
summary: This article identifies seven top stocks poised to benefit from the rise of ChatGPT and AI, focusing on companies with established AI development rather than speculative firms. It highlights Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Alphabet (GOOG), Amazon (AMZN), Meta Platforms (META), Microsoft (MSFT), NVIDIA (NVDA), and Salesforce (CRM) as strong investment opportunities. The author emphasizes scrutinizing a company's long-term AI commitment and business model integration to avoid hype-driven investments.

title: WPP partners with Stripe to expand commerce and payments solutions for brands
summary: WPP has partnered with Stripe to expand its commerce and payments solutions, becoming a Consulting Partner within the Stripe Partner Ecosystem. This collaboration aims to help clients with digital transformation, e-commerce, and mobile applications, leveraging WPP's 13,500 commerce specialists and Stripe's financial infrastructure. The partnership addresses the growing trend of online spending and the increasing demand for integrated brand and commerce strategies to enhance customer experience.

title: Amazon asks employees to be in office at least three days a week
summary: Amazon announced that effective May 1, employees would be required to work from the office at least three days a week, a shift from previous, more flexible remote work policies implemented during the pandemic. CEO Andy Jassy stated this decision would enhance learning and collaboration and boost businesses near Amazon's urban headquarters. Some exceptions will be made for specific roles like customer support and sales.

title: Funding Roundup: Crypto Companies Landed $185M This Week
summary: Crypto and Web3 companies raised a cumulative $185 million this week, a decrease from the previous week's total of $213 million. The majority of this funding went to pre-seed or seed rounds for startups focusing on building Web3 infrastructure. Notable funding rounds included Taurus securing $65 million, Superplastic raising $20 million, and Monad Labs receiving $19 million.

title: Kroger workers who quit are getting texts and emails from the company asking them to come back
summary: Kroger, the largest supermarket operator in the U.S. by sales, is actively reaching out to former employees via texts and emails, encouraging them to return. This unusual recruitment strategy is a response to the current low unemployment rate and the competitive job market, with Kroger's chief people officer stating that "alumni are also a talent source." The company has found that returning workers are often more productive than new hires and has seen success in re-recruiting individuals, sometimes offering higher salaries.

title: Veritone Q4 Earnings Preview: Going Nowhere (NASDAQ:VERI)
summary: Veritone's shares have seen a significant rebound this year, largely due to a new cost-cutting plan. However, the company faces stagnation in its core business, exhibiting only single-digit growth when acquisitions are excluded. Furthermore, Veritone lags behind better-branded AI competitors like C3.ai and carries a substantial revenue risk due to its over 30% concentration with Amazon, which is actively seeking to reduce costs.

title: FTC Alleges Supplement Company ‘Hijacked’ Amazon Reviews to Boost Sales
summary: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has taken its first enforcement action against "review hijacking," fining Bountiful Co. $600,000. The nutritional supplement company is accused of manipulating Amazon reviews to make some of its products appear to have more reviews and higher ratings than they actually did. Bountiful Co. stated it used Amazon’s “variation” feature to highlight similar products and denied deceiving shoppers.

title: Why Missouri has seen a wave of distribution center openings
summary: Missouri has become a popular location for distribution and fulfillment centers due to its central geographic location, offering reduced transportation costs and faster delivery times across the U.S. Companies like Urban Outfitters and Chewy have capitalized on the state's strategic position and strong labor market. While warehouse rents have risen, the benefits of lower transportation costs and quicker transit times often outweigh increased lease agreements, although sustaining this growth might face challenges due to a tightening local real estate market.

title: One Medical joins Amazon to make it easier for people to get and stay healthier
summary: Amazon has completed its acquisition of One Medical, aiming to enhance access to quality, convenient, and affordable primary care. Following the acquisition, Amazon and One Medical plan to expand their human-centered and technology-powered healthcare model, offering services from virtual care to in-office visits and specialized programs. For a limited time, One Medical is offering new members a discounted annual membership at $144 for the first year.

title: Amazon completes One Medical takeover after FTC nod, discounts membership
summary: Amazon announced the completion of its $3.5 billion acquisition of primary care provider One Medical, following the FTC's decision not to challenge the deal. This move marks Amazon's entry into in-person medical services and expands its virtual health offerings, despite previous challenges in the healthcare sector. Amazon is also offering a discounted first-year membership for One Medical to attract new customers.

title: Roku, Best Buy Partner for SXSW Confab Exhibit March 11-12
summary: Roku is partnering with Best Buy to host its first-ever South by Southwest (SXSW) conference pop-up display called “Roku City featuring the Best Buy Home Theater Experience.” The activation, open to the public March 11-12 in Austin, aims to bring the "Roku City" screensaver to life as an interactive experience, including a style shop, a rooftop diner, and a Best Buy Home Theater Experience. This collaboration seeks to spark conversations about brand activation based on audience feedback and the value of in-person experiences in a digital world.

title: Netflix plunges after the streaming giant launches a wave of price cuts in 100 countries in the face of stiff competition
summary: Netflix's stock dropped 5% on Thursday following reports that the company is implementing significant price cuts across over 100 countries, affecting more than 10 million subscribers. These price reductions, some as high as 50%, mark a shift from Netflix's previous strategy of raising prices and come amid increased competition in the streaming market and efforts to curb password sharing. The company aims to improve member experience and offer more affordable options, as evidenced by its recent ad-supported subscription tier.

title: Amazon Launches OEM Auto Parts Store
summary: Amazon has launched a new OEM Parts Shop, building on its existing Amazon Garage, to offer aftermarket replacement and custom parts from various automotive brands including GM, Stellantis, BMW, and Yamaha. This move is seen as a response to the growth of eBay Motors and aims to simplify the automotive parts shopping experience for customers. The new shop provides detailed product information, transparent pricing, and multiple delivery options, allowing customers to easily find parts using vehicle specifications.

title: 18 Ways to Make Money Online and Offline in Canada
summary: This article from NerdWallet Canada outlines 18 legitimate ways for Canadians to earn money, both online and offline. It covers opportunities ranging from selling collectibles and offering freelance services to house-sitting and dog walking, providing details on setup, costs, and earning potential for each method. The guide also includes specific sections for teens and advice on making money in other countries.

title: Amazon Takes Over One Medical Primary Care Service, Which Includes San Diego Market
summary: Amazon has completed its $3.5 billion acquisition of primary care provider One Medical, after the FTC decided not to challenge the deal. This move allows Amazon to enter the virtual and in-person healthcare services market, aiming to simplify healthcare navigation. One Medical, which had 836,000 members and $1 billion in net revenue in 2022, will now offer discounted memberships to new customers.

title: Amazon's Lagging Performance Vs. eBay Since 2020 Should Continue
summary: The author reiterates a successful spread trade strategy involving going long on eBay (EBAY) and short on Amazon (AMZN), which has performed well since late 2020 and is suggested to continue in 2023. This recommendation is based on the belief that eBay is undervalued despite slow growth, while Amazon remains overvalued, particularly if a recession occurs, due to its slim margins. The article emphasizes that eBay's future value is often misunderstood, while Amazon's valuation is seen as unsustainable under potential economic pressures.

title: GSMA debuts open API toolkit as AWS and Microsoft roll out new carrier offerings
summary: The GSMA Association introduced its Open Gateway toolkit at MWC Barcelona 2023, designed to allow carriers to make their networks accessible through interoperable APIs, fostering development across multiple carrier networks. Simultaneously, Amazon Web Services Inc. and Microsoft Corp. unveiled new offerings aimed at simplifying cloud adoption for carriers, including collaborations with Nokia Corp. and Juniper Networks Inc. The Open Gateway, backed by 21 carriers, is based on the open-source CAMARA toolkit and seeks to integrate mobile operators and cloud services.

title: How Have the Top U.S. Asset Managers Voted in 2022?
summary: This article analyzes how the top U.S. asset managers voted on key environmental and social shareholder resolutions in 2022, comparing trends across 2020-2022. It highlights a significant increase in social and environmental resolutions and varying support levels among major asset managers, especially the "Big Three" (Vanguard, BlackRock, State Street). The report also examines how sustainable funds within these managers voted compared to their wider fund ranges.

title: Amazon closes, cancels more warehouses as cost-cutting persists
summary: Amazon continues to reduce its fulfillment network as part of a cost-cutting initiative, canceling, closing, or delaying 99 facilities, impacting nearly 32.3 million square feet of space. While Amazon disputes the exact figures from consulting firm MWPVL International, CEO Andy Jassy has emphasized that reducing operational costs is a top priority for 2023. Despite these closures, Amazon still plans to open 231 new facilities, though some of these openings may be at risk due to ongoing cost-cutting efforts.

title: Better Launches Newest Mortgage Innovation ‘Equity Unlocker’ to Help Tech Employees Purchase Homes
summary: Better Mortgage has introduced "Equity Unlocker," a new program for tech employees, starting with Amazon, that allows them to use vested equity as collateral for a home down payment instead of selling shares. This innovation aims to address the challenges millennials face in homeownership due to student loans and an affordability crisis. The program is currently available in Florida, New York, and Washington, with plans for nationwide expansion and inclusion of other companies' employees.

title: Banneker Ventures Secures $20 Million Loan from Amazon
summary: Banneker Ventures, a Black-led construction firm, has obtained a $20 million loan from Amazon's Housing Equity Fund to construct an affordable housing project in Prince George’s County. The $45 million project will include 193 affordable housing units in Capitol Heights, with completion expected by 2025. This loan is crucial for the project, as stated by Banneker Ventures' founder Omar A. Karim, and the units will remain affordable for 99 years, targeting households earning 40% to 60% of the median regional income.

title: 5G Future Forum accelerating 5G and Edge Compute solutions around the world
summary: The 5G Future Forum (5GFF) demonstrated a first-of-its-kind music collaboration using its Edge Discovery Service (EDS) API, allowing guitarists in New York, Toronto, and London to sync their performance. This showcase, powered by Amazon Web Services, Rogers, Verizon, and Vodafone, highlights the potential of 5G and mobile edge compute technology for transforming global communication and collaboration. Rogers, a founding member of 5GFF, expressed pride in accelerating the delivery of these technologies.

title: Amazon Business Launches 3-Way Match for Mobile App
summary: Amazon Business has launched a 3-Way Match feature on its mobile app, allowing users to scan package barcodes to automatically mark items as received. This new tool, available in the U.S., helps businesses improve procurement, automate reporting, and ensure accuracy in purchase payments by matching purchase orders, invoices, and receipts from anywhere at no additional cost. The feature aims to streamline processes, save time, and reduce the risk of incorrect or fraudulent payments.

title: 8 in-demand creative skills for freelancers―many can pay more than $100 per hour
summary: Freelancing remains a popular career choice in the U.S., with many workers planning to continue or start freelancing in 2023. While tech and marketing skills are in demand, creative skills also offer significant opportunities, with some paying over $100 per hour. Upwork identified eight top in-demand creative skills for freelancers this year, including graphic design, video editing, 3D animation, and product design.

title: Amazon delays construction on HQ2 in Northern Virginia
summary: Amazon has announced delays in the construction of its HQ2 in Northern Virginia, specifically impacting the PenPlace phase. Despite the construction delay, the company still intends to hire 25,000 employees for the HQ2 project. The updated timeline for PenPlace remains uncertain.

title: Amazon delays HQ2's second phase in Pentagon City, updates hiring count
summary: Amazon has delayed the second phase of its HQ2 buildout, known as PenPlace, in Pentagon City. Despite this delay, the company reaffirms its commitment to hire 25,000 employees at HQ2. This update comes amidst previous decisions by Amazon to vacate office spaces and adjust return-to-office plans.

title: The Victoria’s Secret Fashion Show Returns to TV in September
summary: The Victoria's Secret Fashion Show is returning in September after a four-year hiatus, rebranded as "The Tour." This new iteration aims to be a fashion event and documentary celebrating women and global creatives, streaming on Prime Video. The show seeks to address past controversies by emphasizing inclusivity, featuring a mix of returning "Angels" and newcomers, and allowing viewers to shop pieces inspired by the event.

title: Sprouts' latest real estate strategy means WA grocery store closure
summary: Sprouts Farmers Market announced it would be closing grocery stores in Washington, Texas, California, Georgia, and Florida, as part of its latest real estate strategy. The Phoenix-based specialty grocery chain is implementing these closures across multiple states. This move reflects a broader strategic shift for the company.

title: Amazon worker loses bid for California class action over remote work expenses
summary: A U.S. District Judge in San Francisco denied a class action lawsuit against Amazon seeking reimbursement for remote work expenses for nearly 7,000 California workers. The judge stated that the plaintiff failed to prove a company-wide policy of non-reimbursement, noting that many workers had received some reimbursement. The denial was without prejudice, allowing the plaintiff to refile with a revised class.

title: What Are Optimization Problems? How QCI’s Unique Quantum Approach Can Help Organizations Solve Complex Problems Across Industries
summary: This article explains optimization problems, which involve finding the best solution given various constraints, and how businesses can benefit from solving them. It highlights why quantum computers, specifically QCI's Dirac devices with their qudit approach, are well-suited to tackle these complex problems that often exceed the capabilities of classical computers. The piece details QCI's successful application in real-world scenarios, such as optimizing sensor placement for BMW and supply chain logistics, and discusses the future of quantum solutions in achieving quantum advantage for difficult business challenges.

title: Why Doesn’t Airbnb Have a Loyalty Program?
summary: Despite users expressing interest and a past announcement of a "Superguest" program in 2018, Airbnb has not implemented a loyalty program. The company's significant success and strong market position, coupled with a lack of reliance on business travelers who typically drive loyalty programs, are cited as key reasons for this absence. For frequent travelers seeking rewards, hotel loyalty programs remain a viable alternative.

title: Southwest Airlines Partners With Amazon After the Colossal Mess That Was Late 2022
summary: After its significant operational meltdown in late 2022, Southwest Airlines has announced a partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS). This collaboration aims to enhance Southwest's reliability by using AWS for ticket booking, flight operations optimization, customer interactions, and crew scheduling. The airline hopes this technological upgrade will prevent future travel disruptions and improve overall efficiency.

title: Zero trust illustrated: Secure cloud migration
summary: This article focuses on securing cloud migrations using a zero-trust architecture (ZTA), emphasizing posture control and secure workload communications. It highlights the importance of protecting against misconfigurations, vulnerabilities, and threats in cloud-native applications, advocating for early security integration in the software development lifecycle. The piece stresses that robust zero-trust protection must extend beyond user access to encompass the security of applications and workloads themselves.

title: Niu's KQi3 Max Escooter Is a Heavy but Reliable Ride
summary: The Niu KQi3 Max escooter is reviewed as a reliable and well-balanced ride, despite being a heavy but minor upgrade over its predecessor, the KQi3 Pro. It offers a powerful 450-watt motor, solid range, comfortable handling for taller riders, and a unique folding mechanism. While it has some drawbacks like lack of turn signals and app-dependent speed settings, its overall performance and sturdy build make it a dependable choice for daily commutes, especially in hilly areas or for heavier riders.

title: The 10 highest paid people in cloud networking
summary: The article lists the top 10 highest-paid executives in the cloud networking industry, detailing their salaries, stock awards, and contributions to their respective companies. It highlights that while base salaries have remained steady for most, significant bonuses and stock awards have led to multi-million dollar paydays, especially for those instrumental in growing cloud and AI divisions. Andrew Jassy of Amazon tops the list with $212 million, largely from stock awards.

title: Four Ways to Cluster based on Correlation
summary: This article explores four methods to cluster 20 companies based on the correlation of their stock prices. It aims to see if these methods can accurately classify companies into categories like tech or oil and gas, as an alternative to clustering based on raw stock prices. The objective is to validate whether correlation-based clustering reveals meaningful industry groupings.

title: Southwest Airlines moves to AWS
summary: Southwest Airlines is migrating its applications to Amazon Web Services (AWS) as part of a digital transformation initiative. This move aims to enhance passenger experience, improve operational efficiency, and optimize IT infrastructure resource allocation, covering everything from ticket purchases to flight operations. The decision follows a major technological breakdown in December 2022 that disrupted the airline's operations.

title: Bakkt Theater to replace Zappos Theater name at Planet Hollywood
summary: The showroom at Planet Hollywood, formerly known as Zappos Theater, will be renamed Bakkt Theater. This change comes as Bakkt Holdings, Inc., a technology platform for crypto and loyalty, solidifies its relationship with Caesars Entertainment. The partnership aims to integrate Bakkt Crypto Rewards into the Caesars Rewards program, offering new crypto options to customers.

title: Inside Best Buy’s big bet on the Home
summary: This article examines Best Buy's growing involvement in the healthcare sector, particularly its strategy to enable rather than directly provide care in the home, leveraging its Geek Squad and Current Health acquisition. The company is partnering with major health systems on hospital-at-home initiatives, aiming for future growth in its Health division. The piece also briefly touches on the Silicon Valley Bank crisis and other health tech, finance, and policy updates.

title: Amazon to Test Satellite Internet Service Starting Next Year
summary: Amazon plans to begin beta testing its Project Kuiper satellite internet service with customers in 2024, following the launch of its first production satellites. Dave Limp, Amazon's senior vice president of devices and services, announced this timeline and showcased prototype user terminals, including a compact seven-inch antenna, at a recent satellite industry trade show. The company is investing over $10 billion in this ambitious internet-from-space venture.

title: 7 Cloud Computing Stocks That Can Make You a Millionaire as Data Explodes
summary: The article highlights seven cloud computing stocks—Salesforce, Microsoft, Snowflake, Amazon, IBM, Veeva Systems, and Alphabet—that are poised for growth as the demand for data storage and processing continues to explode. Despite a challenging 2022 for tech stocks, the public cloud sector demonstrated significant growth, indicating a strong migration of data to the cloud. Each company is detailed with its market position, recent financial performance, and reasons for its potential as a millionaire-maker investment.

title: 7 Kinds of Telehealth Care You Can Get Through Alexa
summary: Amazon's Alexa has evolved beyond simple requests to offer a range of telehealth services, partnering with organizations like Teladoc and One Medical for virtual doctor visits and primary care. Users can manage prescriptions via Amazon Pharmacy, utilize Alexa Together for remote caregiving, and access medical information and reminders through Mayo Clinic First Aid and WebMD skills. The integration of telemedicine with voice assistants aims to make healthcare more accessible and convenient for minor illnesses and ongoing health management.

title: The Honest Company reports near-flat sales growth, indicating slowing demand
summary: The Honest Company reported near-flat sales growth in Q4 2022, with revenue increasing by only 2% to $81.9 million, and net losses rising. This performance, along with similar struggles at Grove Collaborative, highlights challenges for sustainable CPG startups in a market impacted by inflation and shifting consumer behaviors. New CEO Carla Vernón plans to improve profitability by focusing on margin improvement, bottom-of-the-funnel marketing, and expanding brick-and-mortar presence.

title: Kohl’s marketplace gives Lands’ End a much-needed boost
summary: Lands’ End reported a 4.6% year-over-year decline in Q4 net revenue, with global e-commerce down 6.1%. However, its third-party net revenue, which includes sales on marketplaces like Kohl's, rose 8%, significantly boosting the brand. Lands’ End plans to expand its presence on other marketplaces, including Macy's, acknowledging the potential for customer acquisition among lapsed and new customers.

title: IRS to crack down on income earned by third-party sellers on eBay, Amazon and other sites
summary: The IRS is changing its reporting rules for third-party sellers on platforms like eBay and Amazon, requiring companies to issue 1099 forms for transactions exceeding $600, eliminating the previous $20,000 sales threshold. This move aims to ensure that income from online sales is properly taxed. The article highlights a case where an individual was wrongly accused of undeclared sales, demonstrating potential identity theft issues that sellers might face.

title: Quantum Bits: IBM-Cleveland Clinic Launch; D-Wave Adds Solver; DOE/AWS Offer QICK
summary: IBM has launched its first Quantum System One at the Cleveland Clinic for medical research, aiming to integrate quantum with classical computing and AI to accelerate biomedical discoveries. Concurrently, D-Wave has released a new hybrid plug-in solver to simplify quantum functionality for machine learning workflows. Separately, AWS has made available the Quantum Instrumentation Control Kit (QICK), an open-source tool developed with DOE, Princeton, and the University of Chicago, designed to provide cloud-based access to experimental quantum hardware, fostering global collaboration in quantum research.

title: Amazon to Cut 9,000 More Jobs, Deepening Biggest Pullback Ever
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced an additional 9,000 job cuts, primarily impacting Amazon Web Services, human resources, advertising, and Twitch. These new layoffs deepen the company's largest-ever reduction in workforce, following an initial round of 18,000 workers laid off starting in November. The announcement was made internally, with the cuts expected to occur in the coming weeks.

title: For Broad-Based Large-Cap ESG Exposure, See CVLC
summary: The article highlights the Calvert US Large-Cap Core Responsible Index ETF (CVLC) as a significant option for investors seeking broad-based large-cap ESG exposure, particularly focusing on climate change and net-zero goals. It emphasizes CVLC's ability to identify companies taking actual steps towards net-zero objectives, citing examples like Apple, Amazon, and Tesla. The ETF offers a tangible way for investors to align their portfolios with values related to environmental sustainability and responsible investing.

title: Gap pumps brakes on retail media unit
summary: Gap has decided to pause its retail media unit, GPS Media, after just over a year of operation, shifting resources to logistics and fulfillment instead. This move highlights the challenges faced by retailers in the rapidly growing but highly competitive retail media market, dominated by larger players like Amazon and Walmart. Despite the overall growth in retail media, Gap's struggles suggest that an established e-commerce presence alone does not guarantee success in this specialized advertising sector.

title: CVS Health Corp. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
summary: CVS Health Corp. (CVS) stock decreased by 2.74% on Thursday, closing at $72.37, despite an overall positive trading day for the stock market. This marks the second consecutive day of losses for CVS, and its stock is now significantly below its 52-week high. The company's performance was mixed compared to competitors, with Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) rising, while UnitedHealth Group Inc. (UNH) and Elevance Health Inc. (ELV) also experienced declines.

title: Diversity in Business Awards honoree: Brian Kenner - Washington Business Journal
summary: Brian Kenner, the head of HQ2 Policy for Amazon.com Inc., has been recognized as a Diversity in Business Awards honoree by the Washington Business Journal. Kenner previously served as the D.C. deputy mayor for planning and economic development. This article highlights his current role at Amazon.

title: Focus: Amazon, other retailers revamp 'free' shipping as costs soar
summary: Online retailers, including Amazon, are revamping their "free" shipping policies as rising costs and a contracting e-commerce market drain profits. They are introducing new fees, raising minimum purchase requirements, and shifting more expenses to consumers. This shift reflects a broader trend where retailers are starting to resemble airlines, charging for previously free services to maintain profitability in a challenging economic environment.

title: Cryptocurrencies add nothing useful to society, says chip-maker Nvidia
summary: Nvidia's chief technology officer, Michael Kagan, stated that cryptocurrencies bring nothing useful to society, despite the high demand for Nvidia's processors for crypto mining. He contrasted this with the value of AI applications like ChatGPT, which he believes represent a more worthwhile use of processing power. Nvidia had previously taken steps to limit the use of its graphics cards for crypto mining to prioritize supply for AI researchers and gamers.

title: WPP acquires social influencer marketing agency Obviously, expanding tech-powered creator content capabilities
summary: WPP has acquired Obviously, a New York-based social influencer marketing agency with operations in San Francisco and Paris, founded by Mae Karwowski and Maxime Domain in 2014. This acquisition expands WPP's tech-powered creator content capabilities, integrating Obviously's proprietary platform and nearly 100-person team into VMLY&R's network. Obviously's advanced technology includes AI-driven predictive analytics and streamlined campaign management, positioning WPP to strengthen its influencer marketing offerings for clients in a rapidly growing market.

title: U.S. parcel volume dipped 2% in 2022 after a 6% rise in 2021
summary: U.S. parcel volume saw a 2% dip in 2022, following a significant 33% surge in 2020 and a 6% rise in 2021, according to Pitney Bowes. Despite this decline in volume, parcel carriers achieved record revenues totaling $198 billion. Pitney Bowes forecasts a rebound, projecting parcel volume to reach 28 billion by 2028.

title: FedEx slumps, Amazon slows and more takeaways from a top shipping index
summary: A new report from the Pitney Bowes Parcel Shipping Index reveals a 2.2% decline in U.S. parcel volumes in 2022, marking a shift after years of growth. Despite this, parcel revenues increased by 6.5% year-over-year due to inflation and higher surcharges. FedEx saw the steepest drop in volume, while Amazon Logistics' growth flattened, prompting both companies to adjust strategies, including cost-cutting and capacity adjustments.

title: Synchrony mulls creating a digital wallet
summary: Synchrony Financial, a major provider of private label credit cards for retailers, is exploring the development of its own digital wallet while actively focusing on enhancing digital wallet services for its existing clients. The company aims to transition consumers from physical cards to more digital experiences, recognizing changing customer expectations and potential future revenue streams. Synchrony is currently integrating its digital cards into third-party wallets but is contemplating creating its own, although this move would require careful consideration of its partnerships and potential regulatory impacts.

title: Amazon targets multiple bad actors in novel lawsuit, further protecting selling partners from fraud
summary: Amazon has filed a novel lawsuit against multiple bad actors who submitted thousands of false copyright infringement claims to remove products and sellers from its store. This action by Amazon's Counterfeit Crimes Unit (CCU) aims to protect selling partners, customers, and brands, reinforcing the integrity of the Amazon store. The lawsuit builds on the existing Amazon Brand Registry program, which has already seen a 99% reduction in suspected infringement reports from enrolled brands.

title: CVS Health Finalizes $8B Acquisition of Signify Health
summary: CVS Health has completed its $8 billion acquisition of home healthcare company Signify Health, accelerating its value-based care strategy. The merger aims to improve care delivery, lower costs, and enhance member engagement, particularly for Medicare Advantage beneficiaries. Signify Health's CEO, Kyle Armbrester, will continue to lead the company, which also includes Caravan Health.

title: Top and Flop ETFs of March
summary: The U.S. stock market experienced extreme volatility in March, characterized by bank failures and a tech-driven rally. This article highlights the top and flop ETFs of the month, with technology and gold-related funds performing well, while regional bank and natural gas ETFs saw declines.

title: Walmart Inc. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: Walmart Inc. stock (WMT) saw a 1.22% increase on Friday, closing at $147.45. Despite this rise and a positive trading day for the broader market, Walmart's stock underperformed the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock closed below its 52-week high and rose less than competitor Amazon.com Inc.

title: ProShares Ultra QQQ 2x Shares (QLD) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of ProShares Ultra QQQ 2x Shares (QLD), including its stock price, key statistics, top holdings, and dividend information. It highlights QLD's current portfolio value of $6.70 billion with top holdings like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple, and details its performance and historical data. The article also lists recent trades and key fundamental data.

title: Schwab 1000 Index ETF (SCHK) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the Schwab 1000 Index ETF (SCHK), detailing its stock price, holdings, dividend yield, and key financial statistics. As of November 30, 2025, SCHK held 994 stocks with a total value of $4.83 billion, with top holdings including NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft. The ETF also shows various performance metrics, key statistics, and historical data like PE and PB ratios, and dividend information.

title: OMNI Systems acquires ITW Labels
summary: OMNI Systems, the largest privately-owned label converter in the United States, has acquired ITW Labels, a company with a 110-year history. This acquisition significantly expands OMNI Systems' geographical footprint and capabilities, aiming for substantial long-term growth as a full product line provider. The merge creates powerful synergies, allowing both companies to benefit from increased capacity, expertise, and a broader market reach without significant customer overlap.

title: FedEx plans to consolidate operating companies into one organization
summary: FedEx announced plans to consolidate its various operating companies, including FedEx Express, FedEx Ground, and FedEx Services, into a single organization by June 2024. This restructuring aims to streamline operations, enhance efficiency, and achieve $4 billion in permanent cost reductions by fiscal year 2025 as part of its "DRIVE" transformation strategy. Raj Subramaniam will lead the combined entity, while FedEx Freight will remain a separate company.

title: Sonos Era 100 Review (& Comparison to the Sonos One)
summary: The Sonos Era 100 is a new compact wireless speaker replacing the Sonos One, offering enhanced connectivity, a faster processor, and improved audio with a larger woofer and dual tweeters. This review details its features, setup, and sound performance, while also providing a direct comparison to its predecessor, highlighting significant upgrades like Bluetooth, line-in, and a capacitive volume slider. The article concludes that the Era 100 is a worthwhile upgrade for those entering the Sonos ecosystem, offering great value for its price and superior sound quality over the Sonos One.

title: iShares Global 100 ETF (IOO) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the iShares Global 100 ETF (IOO), detailing its stock price, current holdings, and dividend yield. As of its latest N-PORT filing on September 30, 2025, IOO primarily invests in major tech companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, and Broadcom. The ETF manages $7.30 billion in common equities with a 0% turnover rate, offering key statistics and historical performance data.

title: ByteDance’s Profit Surges 79%, Exceeding Alibaba and Tencent
summary: ByteDance's profit (Ebitda) surged 79% to $25 billion in 2022, surpassing Chinese tech giants Tencent and Alibaba for the first time. This growth was driven by its video platforms TikTok and Douyin, even as TikTok faced mounting losses and scrutiny in the US. The company's revenue also exceeded $80 billion, matching Tencent's figures.

title: The 3 Best Retail ETFs to Buy in April 2023
summary: This article identifies the three best retail ETFs for April 2023, offering investors a way to capitalize on an anticipated rebound in the U.S. economy with relatively low risk. The recommended ETFs are SPDR S&P Retail ETF (XRT), iShares US Consumer Staples ETF (IYK), and ProShares Online Retail ETF (ONLN), each providing diversification and exposure to different segments of the retail sector. The article highlights their specific benefits, such as low expense ratios, investment in defensive stocks, and exposure to the online retail trend.

title: FTC orders supplement maker to pay $600K in first case involving hijacked Amazon reviews
summary: The FTC has ordered The Bountiful Company, makers of Nature's Bounty vitamins, to pay $600,000 for deceptively combining product reviews on Amazon. The company exploited an Amazon feature meant for product variations, merging newer, less-popular supplements with older, well-reviewed ones to artificially boost ratings and sales. This marks the FTC's first enforcement action against "review hijacking."

title: Fabuloso recall 2023: Full list of products that have been recalled
summary: Colgate-Palmolive issued a voluntary recall in February 2023 for millions of bottles of Fabuloso multi-purpose cleaners in the US and Canada due to potential bacterial contamination. The recall affects specific scents and sizes of products manufactured between December 14, 2022, and January 23, 2023, which may contain Pseudomonas bacteria due to insufficient preservative levels. Customers are advised to stop using and dispose of recalled products and can claim a refund, though no infections have been reported to date.

title: Ibex Global Solutions closing San Antonio call center, affecting 227 employees
summary: Ibex Global Solutions is closing its San Antonio call center in June, impacting 227 employees. Most workers will be offered positions at the company's New Braunfels call center or the option to work from home. This closure is part of a larger trend of layoffs in the region, with Ibex shifting operations to offshore and nearshore locations.

title: The leading IoT software companies 2023
summary: This article identifies the leading IoT software companies for 2023, noting that IoT software spending reached $53 billion in 2022 and is continuing significant growth. Based on a survey of IoT users, key vendors include Microsoft, AWS, Siemens, IBM, Cisco, Oracle, PTC, and MongoDB, with Microsoft holding the largest market share. The report emphasizes the critical role of software in IoT solutions, analyzes the market's fragmentation despite some consolidation, and outlines future growth opportunities for vendors.

title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy highlights efficiency drive, AI opportunities in shareholder letter
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's second annual shareholder letter emphasizes efficiency and artificial intelligence. Jassy detailed how Amazon is optimizing its fulfillment network and AWS is focusing on AI-related offerings and optimizing infrastructure costs for customers. The letter also highlighted growth opportunities in advertising, healthcare, and grocery retail, with significant investments in generative AI.

title: Amazon Launches AI Platform Aimed At Corporate Customers—Joining Google And Microsoft In AI Race
summary: Amazon has launched a new generative AI service called Bedrock, targeting corporate customers through its Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud platform. This move distinguishes Amazon from competitors like Microsoft and Google, which have focused more on consumer-facing AI products. Bedrock offers tools for businesses to build and scale their own AI applications, including Amazon's Titan large language models and integrations with other AI startups, aiming to democratize AI development securely and efficiently for enterprises.

title: JD.com Identifies Five Consumption Trends Driving Significant Growth in China's Cross-Border E-Commerce Market
summary: JD.com released its "2023 Cross-Border Imported Products Consumption Report," highlighting a 64% sales increase and nearly tripling user numbers for cross-border products on JD Worldwide between 2020 and 2022. The report identifies five key consumption trends: prioritizing scalp care, embracing harm-free skincare, choosing sustainable makeup, opting for premium low-lactose infant formula, and savoring relaxing beverages, offering insights for international brands targeting the Chinese market. These trends indicate a growing demand for premium, niche, and health-conscious products among Chinese consumers, particularly younger demographics and female users.

title: PayPal's Outgoing CEO Dan Schulman Got A Significant Pay Cut for FY22, Down 32% To $22M
summary: PayPal's outgoing CEO, Dan Schulman, experienced a 32% pay cut in FY22, receiving $22 million compared to $32.1 million the previous year. This reduction comes as Schulman plans to retire at the year-end, and the company acknowledges his leadership through challenging economic times, despite failing to meet several key performance targets. PayPal also faces upcoming shareholder proposals concerning its practices related to law enforcement requests for abortion-related data and account suspensions of legal sex workers.

title: Ben & Jerry's ice cream 'scoopers' launch union drive
summary: Workers at the flagship Ben & Jerry's scoop shop in Burlington, Vermont, have initiated a union drive over concerns regarding pay, health benefits, and having a voice in staffing decisions. They are organizing with Workers United, the same union involved in the Starbucks organizing campaign. This move comes as the ice cream maker, known for its social justice values, faces increased unionization efforts across various companies.

title: Chinese e-commerce giant makes successful entry into Canadian market
summary: PDD Holdings, owner of China's e-commerce platform Pinduoduo, has successfully entered the Canadian market with its North American online shopping site, Temu. Temu, now the No. 1 app in Canada, offers competitive pricing and free shipping, putting pressure on local B.C. businesses. While disruptive for some retailers, experts note it benefits consumers through increased competition and lower prices.

title: Video Game Engine market Future Demand Competitive Situation and Emerging Trends with Historic Forecast 2023-2029-Unity Technologies, Epic Games, Chukong Tech
summary: A new report by Xherald mir provides a comprehensive analysis of the Video Game Engine Market, forecasting its growth from USD 2.14 billion in 2023 to expand at a CAGR of 26.76% by 2029. The report covers market segments, competitive landscape including key players like Unity Technologies and Epic Games, and factors driving and restraining market growth such as increased app store spending and the adoption of AR/VR technologies. It also provides a regional outlook and strategic insights for market stakeholders.

title: Gartner Magic Quadrant for Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms 2023
summary: This article summarizes the 2023 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms, categorizing vendors into Leaders, Challengers, Visionaries, and Niche Players. It details the strengths and cautions for each vendor based on Gartner's evaluation of their ABI platforms. The report highlights Microsoft, Salesforce, and Qlik as leaders, and notes movements of several companies since the 2022 report, particularly Pyramid Analytics' jump to visionary status.

title: Aterian Announces Updated First Quarter 2023 Net Revenue and Adjusted EBITDA Ranges
summary: Aterian, Inc. announced updated first-quarter 2023 financial ranges, expecting net revenue between $34.0 million to $35.0 million and an improved adjusted EBITDA loss range of $(3.9) million to $(4.5) million. The company aims for adjusted EBITDA profitability in the second half of 2023 by liquidating high-cost inventory and reducing supply chain costs. These figures are preliminary and subject to final review.

title: Pitney Bowes PitneyShip Cube Now Available for Purchase on Amazon
summary: Pitney Bowes has announced that its award-winning PitneyShip™ Cube is now available for purchase on Amazon, marking the first time a Pitney Bowes product is sold directly to customers through an independent third-party retailer. This all-in-one Wi-Fi-enabled thermal shipping label printer with an integrated scale and direct mobile printing aims to simplify shipping for e-commerce and office users by saving time, space, and costs. The device, coupled with PitneyShip software, allows users to process orders, compare shipping rates, print labels, and track packages from a compact unit.

title: Stanley Black & Decker recalls about 2.2 million sledgehammers due to injury risk
summary: Stanley Black & Decker has issued a recall for approximately 2.2 million DeWALT, Stanley FATMAX, and Craftsman Fiberglass Sledgehammers due to a risk of the hammer heads detaching unexpectedly. This defect has led to 192 reported incidents, including two resulting in face and head injuries. Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled sledgehammers and contact Stanley Black & Decker for a refund.

title: Amazon defeats consumer antitrust lawsuit over fulfillment centers
summary: Amazon has successfully defended against a consumer antitrust lawsuit in Seattle federal court. The lawsuit, filed by Amazon Prime members, alleged that Amazon unlawfully tied the online sale of third-party products to its "Fulfillment by Amazon" program, leading to higher consumer costs. U.S. District Judge Ricardo Martinez ruled that the plaintiffs lacked standing as they were not direct buyers of the logistics services in question.

title: 3M to partner with AWS to leverage AI for clinical documentation
summary: 3M Health Information Systems is collaborating with Amazon Web Services to enhance its M*Modal ambient intelligence machine learning and generative AI services. This partnership aims to expedite and scale real-time speech recognition for clinical documentation, helping clinicians focus more on patient care by automating structured notes in EHRs. The initiative also seeks to reduce the documentation burden on clinicians, a significant factor in physician and nurse burnout.

title: Bed Bath & Beyond files for bankruptcy. Here's what it means for Wilmington shoppers.
summary: Bed Bath & Beyond has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after over 50 years in business, leading to the closure of hundreds of stores nationwide, including one in Wilmington. While stores will temporarily remain open, customers should note important dates for returns, gift card acceptance, and coupon expiration. The company's financial struggles and declining sales prompted the bankruptcy filing.

title: Amazon Contract Drivers in California Join Teamsters Union
summary: More than 80 contract drivers and dispatchers for Amazon in Palmdale, California, have joined the International Brotherhood of Teamsters, aligning with Teamsters Local 396. These drivers work for Battle-Tested Strategies, one of Amazon's Delivery Service Partners, and have a tentative agreement that includes pay raises and a grievance procedure. This move highlights ongoing efforts by unions to organize Amazon workers across the U.S. for better wages and working conditions.

title: IBM acquires Ahana, steward of open source PrestoDB
summary: IBM has acquired Ahana Cloud Inc., a Presto database SaaS vendor, to expand its open-source presence and offer a data lake product to enterprise customers. This acquisition gains IBM membership in the Presto Foundation and provides a managed SaaS and AWS marketplace version of the Presto database. Analysts view this move as a way for IBM to reassure customers with open-source solutions and deepen its partnership with AWS.

title: 3 reasons why the Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF (VGS) could be an excellent investment for beginners
summary: The Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF (VGS) is presented as an excellent investment option for beginners due to its significant diversification across major developed markets and sectors, its strong long-term performance, and its low management fees. The ETF offers exposure to over 1,400 businesses globally, including tech giants, and has historically delivered solid returns while keeping costs down.

title: 3M laying off 10 percent of workforce amid slowing demand
summary: 3M announced it will cut 6,000 jobs, in addition to 2,500 previously announced, reducing its workforce by approximately 10 percent due to slowing global demand for goods. CEO Mike Roman stated the layoffs will span all functions, businesses, and geographies, aimed at reducing costs by up to $900 million annually. The decision reflects a shift in consumer spending from goods to services post-pandemic and broader economic slowdown.

title: Inflation, innovation and insight: carving out growth for retailers in 2023
summary: This report analyzes global retail trends in Consumer Technology and Durables for 2023, highlighting a shift from slowdown to stabilization. It provides strategies for retailers to navigate challenges like inflation and changing consumer behavior, focusing on optimizing value, promotions, innovation, and sustainability to drive growth.

title: Grubhub, Postmates, Uber Eats fight for arbitration in price-fixing class action
summary: Food delivery giants Grubhub, Uber Eats, Postmates, and DoorDash are appealing a federal judge's ruling that an antitrust class action lawsuit against them cannot be settled through arbitration. Consumers allege that these companies engage in price-fixing through "No Price Competition Clauses" in their merchant contracts, which restrict competition and inflate prices for both delivery and dine-in customers, creating an unlawful monopoly. The lawsuit seeks to represent two classes: those who purchased meals directly from contracted restaurants and those who purchased dine-in meals from contracted restaurants since April 14, 2016.

title: Pinterest and Amazon team up for multi-year ads partnership
summary: Pinterest and Amazon have announced a multi-year advertising partnership where Amazon ads will be integrated into Pinterest, directing users to Amazon's website for purchases. This collaboration, which will roll out over several quarters starting later in 2023, positions Amazon as Pinterest's first third-party advertising partner and aims to enhance the shopping experience for Pinterest users. Pinterest anticipates a significant impact on its revenue starting next year.

title: U.S. government has 'serious concerns' that some H-1B visa applicants are trying to game the system
summary: The U.S. government has expressed "serious concerns" that some H-1B visa applicants are manipulating the lottery system, as application numbers soared by 61% this year. Authorities suspect some applicants are submitting multiple bids through different company names without genuine job offers to gain an unfair advantage. U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services is conducting fraud investigations and referring some cases to federal prosecutors, noting a significant increase in registrations tied to multiple applications for the same individual.

title: AWS Data Transfer Pricing: Solving Hidden Network Transfer Costs
summary: This article explains the complexities of AWS data transfer pricing, covering costs for data moving between AWS and the internet, as well as within the AWS cloud across regions and Availability Zones. It provides tips for reducing these costs, including optimizing infrastructure design and utilizing tools like NetApp Cloud Volumes ONTAP, which helps minimize data transfer through storage efficiencies. The aim is to help users avoid unexpected spikes in their monthly AWS bills by understanding and controlling hidden network transfer expenses.

title: Forerunner 965, 265, 265S: Everything to know about Garmin’s first AMOLED display-toting running watches in India
summary: Garmin has launched its first AMOLED display running watches in India, including the Forerunner 965, 265, and 265S. These watches offer extensive tracking features for runners and triathletes, with the 965 boasting advanced mapping and longer battery life. The prices are Rs 67,490 for the Forerunner 965 and Rs 50,490 for the 265 series, and they are available both online and offline.

title: Restaurant Brands earnings beats estimates, fueled by Tim Hortons' and Burger King's strong sales
summary: Restaurant Brands International exceeded Q1 earnings and revenue estimates, driven by double-digit same-store sales growth at Tim Hortons and Burger King. Burger King's U.S. same-store sales climbed 8.7%, indicating a successful turnaround, while Tim Hortons saw a 13.8% increase, especially strong in Canada. Popeyes also contributed with 5.6% same-store sales growth, and Firehouse Subs grew by 6.1%.

title: 3M laying off 1,100 at Maplewood headquarters
summary: 3M is laying off 1,100 employees at its Maplewood headquarters, which represents 10% of its local workforce and is part of a larger global cost-cutting initiative. This follows an earlier announcement of 6,000 global job cuts and an additional 2,500 manufacturing job reductions, bringing the total workforce reduction to 10% worldwide. Economists suggest these layoffs signal broader economic impacts from the Fed's interest rate hikes and changes in fiscal stimulus.

title: Keysight Highlights Challenges, Opportunities in Satellite Industry
summary: Keysight Technologies commissioned a survey revealing software test automation as a top challenge in the satellite industry, alongside the shift from large GEO to smaller LEO satellites due to reduced launch costs. The article discusses how automation in design-to-manufacturing and operational control systems can address these challenges, driven by a "gold rush" mentality and shortened development cycles. Keysight aims to support customers throughout the satellite lifecycle, from design to operations, through various testing, emulation, and simulation tools.

title: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna warns employees who work remotely: 'Your career does suffer'
summary: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna stated that while he is not forcing employees back to the office, those who work remotely may find their careers suffering, particularly regarding promotions into managerial roles. He emphasized that in-person interaction is beneficial for career development and that remote work is best suited for specific individual contributor roles. Krishna's comments contribute to the ongoing debate about the merits of remote versus in-person work, with IBM encouraging employees to come into the office three days a week.

title: 40 Under 40: Champions of Construction 2022
summary: This article celebrates Autodesk's 40 Under 40: Champions of Construction for 2022, highlighting 40 individuals who are making significant contributions to the construction industry through innovation, leadership, and digital transformation. It details their roles, achievements, and impact on their respective companies and the broader AEC sector. The honorees are recognized for advancing digital maturity, implementing new technologies, and fostering collaborative and sustainable practices.

title: DXC Technology migrates insurance marketplace Lloyd’s to new data center
summary: DXC Technology has successfully migrated Lloyd's, the insurance marketplace, to a new, more modern data center as part of Lloyd's 'Blueprint Two' modernization strategy. This complex migration involved moving 70 billion rows of data, 700 IT assets, and over 200 mission-critical applications from a 40-year-old mainframe environment. The move, which took place over a single weekend in February, involved extensive planning and collaboration with over a dozen partners to minimize disruption.

title: Amazon Prime Subscribers Do Not Get the ‘Exclusive Savings’ They’re Promised, Class Action Alleges [DISMISSED]
summary: A class action lawsuit alleging that Amazon Prime subscribers do not receive promised "exclusive savings" has been dismissed. The judge ruled that the plaintiff failed to demonstrate how Amazon broke its promise or that he incurred damages, noting he did receive a discount on a television. The lawsuit claimed Amazon used "fictitious" strike-through prices by artificially raising product prices before applying "discounts" for Prime members.

title: Amazon class action alleges Prime subscribers pay false discount prices
summary: Amazon is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that its advertised Amazon Prime discounts are not genuine. The plaintiff claims that Amazon inflates original prices to create the appearance of a discount, thereby misleading consumers. The lawsuit seeks damages for affected customers and alleges violations of FTC guidelines regarding price comparisons.

title: Hanumakonda: KTR inaugurates 4 new companies; IT sector hired 1.5L people last year
summary: Telangana's IT and Industries Minister K.T. Rama Rao inaugurated four new IT companies in Hanamkonda (LTI Mindtree, Genpact, HRH Next, and Hexad Solutions Pvt. Ltd.), encouraging local youth for employment and entrepreneurship. Hyderabad continues to be a major IT hub, attracting global companies and adding over 1.5 lakh jobs in 2021-22, with plans to expand IT growth to other districts like Mahabubnagar, Siddipet, Nizamabad, and Nalgonda. The state government's policy framework, infrastructure, and human capital access have made Hyderabad a preferred destination for IT companies.

title: Is the Market Reacting Too Negatively to Pinterest Earnings?
summary: Pinterest's stock tumbled 25% despite beating Q1 2023 top and bottom-line estimates and growing monthly active users (MAUs). The sell-off was primarily driven by concerns over slowing growth, rising operating expenses (opex), and a GAAP net loss of $209 million due to restructuring charges. While analysts remain cautiously optimistic with reiterations, several lowered price targets, reflecting market uncertainty regarding the company's profitability amidst its strategic restructuring efforts.

title: CEO pay continues to soar – new study
summary: A new study by Equilar reveals that median CEO pay at top US public companies increased by 7.7% to a record $22.3 million in 2022, despite negative S&P 500 returns. This rise was primarily driven by stock awards, leading to a median "pay ratio" of 288 times the median employee's compensation, significantly outpacing the 3.6% increase in average US private sector employee earnings. The study highlights a trend of incentivizing executives through substantial stock awards during uncertain economic times.

title: IonQ Appoints Pat Tang as VP of Research & Development
summary: IonQ, a quantum computing leader, announced the appointment of Pat Tang as Vice President of Research and Development. Tang, previously an Engineering Vice President at Amazon Lab126 and a team lead at Apple, will lead the company's technical exploration with a focus on developing impactful technologies for IonQ's systems. His extensive experience in building and scaling innovative technologies is expected to be invaluable as IonQ continues to advance quantum computing.

title: GlobalFoundries taps former Amazon exec for CFO seat
summary: GlobalFoundries has appointed former Amazon executive Tim Stone as its new Chief Financial Officer, replacing David Reeder. Stone brings extensive experience from Amazon and previous CFO roles at Snap, Ford Motor, and ASAPP. This leadership change comes as GlobalFoundries reported a 5% year-over-year revenue decline in its first quarter, though the company sees continued growth in markets like automotive and industrial IoT despite broader semiconductor industry challenges.

title: Dillard’s Shoppers Bought Shoes, Cosmetics and Women’s Clothing in Q1
summary: Dillard's Inc. surpassed Wall Street's expectations in Q1 despite a decline in sales and net income, driven by strong performance in cosmetics, shoes, and women's apparel. The company's retail gross margin was 45.6 percent, and operating expenses rose slightly.
