Analysts suggest that while Dillard's performed well given tough comparisons, the broader retail sector faces challenges due to a pressured middle class and increased spending on experiences.

title: One Giant Leap for Brand Kind
summary: Companies are increasingly engaging in space marketing by placing product logos on spacecraft and conducting stunts in space to promote their brands. This trend is driven by the era of space capitalism and regulatory changes allowing private companies to participate in space missions, offering new revenue streams for financially risky ventures. While some express concern that this commercialization might diminish the sense of wonder surrounding space, brands like Columbia Sportswear believe it can highlight their innovative capabilities and garner publicity, even for failed missions.

title: George Soros bet on railroads, fitness, gig economy and lightened load on Disney, Amazon, dumped Tesla
summary: Soros Fund Management made new investments in railroad operators CSX Corp. and Norfolk Southern Corp., and the gig economy during the first quarter. The fund also adjusted its holdings in the media sector and divested from electric-vehicle maker Tesla Inc. These railroad investments occurred amidst ongoing labor tensions and increased scrutiny over safety in the industry.

title: Layoffs may cause organizational drag: Gartner
summary: According to Gartner research, layoffs can negatively impact shareholder returns within three years by leading to reduced employee morale, increased turnover, and loss of customers, despite initial cost savings. The report highlights that businesses often face immediate costs like severance payments and long-term issues such as higher contractor costs and demands for increased compensation from remaining staff. Gartner suggests alternative cost-cutting measures like voluntary reduced hours, executive pay cuts, hiring freezes, and internal redeployment to avoid "organizational drag."

title: Opal Fuels Sees Heavy-Duty Trucking Bulking RNG Demand
summary: Opal Fuels Inc., a major renewable natural gas (RNG) producer and distributor, is expanding its production projects to meet growing demand for RNG as a transportation fuel, particularly from the heavy-duty trucking sector. The company has secured long-term contracts with major corporations like Amazon.com Inc. and UPS, and is developing new landfill-based RNG facilities. Despite a decrease in first-quarter revenue due to holding back renewable identification number (RIN) sales following a drop in RIN prices, Opal Fuels remains optimistic about the future of RNG in the heavy-duty fuel market, projecting significant growth by 2030 and beyond.

title: Cable One® Teams with eero to Launch Advanced Whole-Home Connectivity Solution
summary: Cable One® announced the launch of Wall-To-Wall WiFi, an advanced whole-home connectivity solution, in collaboration with eero, an Amazon company. This service utilizes eero Pro6E and Wi-Fi 6 technology with patented TrueMesh to provide fast, reliable, and secure internet coverage throughout the home, eliminating dead zones and supporting over 100 connected devices simultaneously. The offering includes advanced online security features, parental controls, and an eero Plus option for additional protection.

title: Galileo Financial Technologies Joins AWS Marketplace
summary: Galileo Financial Technologies, a leading fintech company owned by SoFi Technologies, announced its solutions are now available on AWS Marketplace. This move aims to provide clients with innovative and accessible financial technology solutions, streamlining procurement and billing processes while offering Galileo's powerful API platform for card issuing and payment processing. The offerings include transaction processing, a payment risk platform, and conversational AI technology Cyberbank Konecta.

title: Cognizant Strengthens its Global Delivery Network with New Innovation Hub in Malaysia
summary: Cognizant has opened a new regional innovation hub in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, aiming to strengthen its global delivery network and expand its talent pool to 5,000 employees by 2026. This hub features Cognizant’s first ASEAN Innovation Experience Lab and Centres of Excellence, including a Global Quality Engineering and Assurance Centre and an AWS Centre of Excellence, to provide innovative digital solutions to clients. Malaysian officials highlighted the investment as a testament to Malaysia's business environment and its role as a digital hub for ASEAN.

title: Amazon Bulls, Take Note: The Company's Improving Its Operations
summary: Amazon's stock is up 29% for the year due to improvements in its operations, particularly its delivery network, which has cut costs and shipment times. The company plans to expand same-day delivery centers to further enhance efficiency and profitability. Traders can leverage Amazon's performance using specialized ETFs like AMZU for bullish bets and AMZD for bearish positions.

title: Aramark Sports + Entertainment Announces Latest Innovation with Launch of New Amazon One Age Verification Capability at Coors Field
summary: Aramark Sports + Entertainment has announced the launch of a new Amazon One age verification capability at Coors Field. This innovation aims to streamline the purchasing process for age-restricted products, enhancing customer experience and operational efficiency. The integration signifies Aramark's commitment to leveraging technology for improved service delivery in sports and entertainment venues.

title: What you need to know about Temu, the online shopping app dominating download charts
summary: Online shopping app Temu, owned by Chinese company PDD Holdings Inc., has quickly risen to popularity in Canada with its aggressive marketing, low prices, and gamified shopping experience. While it offers bargain deals, experts caution consumers about product quality, potential environmental impact from shipping and returns, and data privacy concerns given its sister app's issues. The platform's "growth-at-all-costs" approach and referral schemes are disrupting the e-commerce market, leading to a "race to the bottom" that could hurt local businesses.

title: Is BULZ Stock a buy?
summary: The article discusses BULZ, the ticker symbol for the MicroSectors FANG & Innovation 3X Leveraged ETNs, which trades on NYSE Arca. It explains that BULZ is an unsecured debt security providing leveraged exposure to the Solactive FANG Innovation Index, tracking 15 large-cap US tech stocks, and is not suitable for "buy and hold" investments. The article also mentions Bank of Montreal as the issuer and highlights Barclays' recent decision to redeem some of its own ETNs due to past issues, noting this does not directly impact BULZ but provides context on the ETN market.

title: Japan Airlines
summary: Japan Airlines (JAL) partnered with IBM Consulting to digitally transform its financial systems using SAP S/4HANA, overcoming pandemic challenges with a 100% remote deployment. This modernization allowed JAL to enhance operational efficiency, streamline processes, and gain deeper financial insights, despite significant market disruption. The project resulted in a 60% reduction in SAP customizations and improved financial reporting and analysis capabilities.

title: Amazon Drivers Avoid Arbitration Claiming Non-delivery of Updated TOS
summary: The Ninth Circuit denied Amazon's motion to compel arbitration in a case brought by Amazon Flex drivers, ruling that the 2016 Terms of Service (TOS) applied instead of the 2019 version. Amazon failed to prove it provided adequate notice of the updated 2019 TOS and that there was mutual assent to its arbitration agreement. This decision highlights the importance for companies to ensure broad arbitration provisions and maintain records of notice for TOS updates.

title: Activist firm Jana eyes boardroom fight at Freshpet
summary: Activist investor Jana Partners, the largest shareholder in pet food company Freshpet, wants to replace four board members to improve capital expenditure supervision and position the company for a potential sale. Jana, which owns a 9.3% stake, criticized the board for failing to oversee management and allowing performance to decline. Freshpet's shares have experienced significant volatility, and Jana's push comes amidst consolidation in the pet food industry.

title: Local Bounti names new CEO in executive transition
summary: Local Bounti Corporation announced that Anna Fabrega, former Freshly CEO and Amazon executive, will become CEO of the company on June 5, 2023. Current Co-CEOs and Co-Founders, Craig Hurlbert and Travis Joyner, will transition to Senior Vice President of Strategy and Chief Technology Officer, respectively, while retaining their board seats. Fabrega brings over two decades of experience in operations, consumer goods, and food production, with a proven track record of scaling businesses, including leading Amazon Go.

title: ETF of the Week: Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP)
summary: The Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP) is highlighted as the ETF of the Week. GVIP provides exposure to top stock picks from hedge funds, focusing on a consensus of leading managers rather than an overweighting of individual stocks. The ETF diversifies holdings across large-cap, mid-cap, and international companies, offering a less volatile way for investors to participate in innovative companies.

title: US: Local Bounti appoints Anna Fabrega as CEO
summary: Local Bounti Corporation has announced the appointment of Anna Fabrega, formerly CEO of Freshly and an Amazon executive, as its new CEO, effective June 5, 2023. Current Co-CEOs and Co-Founders, Craig Hurlbert and Travis Joyner, will transition to strategic leadership roles within the company and remain on the Board of Directors. Fabrega brings extensive experience in operations, consumer goods, and food production, with a focus on scaling businesses and expanding market reach.

title: Local Bounti names new CEO
summary: Local Bounti Corp. has announced Anna Fabrega, former Freshly CEO and Amazon executive, will take over as CEO on June 5. The current co-CEOs and co-founders, Craig Hurlbert and Travis Joyner, will transition to senior leadership roles and retain their board seats. Fabrega brings over two decades of experience in operations, consumer goods, and food production to her new role at Local Bounti.

title: Local Bounti names new CEO
summary: Anna Fabrega, formerly CEO of Freshly and an Amazon executive, has been appointed as the new CEO of Local Bounti, effective June 5. The company's co-founders, Craig Hurlbert and Travis Joyner, will transition to senior vice president of strategy and chief technology officer, respectively, while retaining their board seats. Fabrega brings extensive experience in operations, consumer goods, and food production, with a focus on scaling businesses to drive Local Bounti's growth strategy.

title: Amazon Settles With FTC Over Claims Ring Doorbell Spies on Users (AMZN)
summary: Amazon has agreed to pay $30.8 million to settle two privacy cases with the Federal Trade Commission. One case involved allegations that Amazon's Ring video doorbells illegally spied on users, while the other concerned Alexa speakers collecting children's data without consent. The company will pay $5.8 million for the Ring complaint.

title: Netgear Nighthawk M6 Pro Wi-Fi Router Review
summary: The Netgear Nighthawk M6 Pro is a high-performance mobile Wi-Fi router supporting 5G mobile broadband and Wi-Fi 6E, making it suitable for business travelers and remote work. Despite its versatility and robust features like a 13-hour battery life and a 2.5 Gigabit Ethernet port, its high price and somewhat unclear initial setup documentation are notable drawbacks. This review highlights its compact design, strong performance, and ability to switch between networking modes, positioning it as a top-tier but expensive solution for connectivity on the go.

title: Apple Bets On The Emerging Markets Growth Story
summary: Apple is increasingly focusing on emerging markets, particularly India, to drive growth amidst a challenging global smartphone market. The company achieved marginal growth in iPhone sales despite overall industry decline, with India seeing significant retail and manufacturing expansion plans. While services and financial technology offer long-term opportunities, the emerging markets strategy is a key component of Apple's immediate growth narrative, alongside ongoing share repurchases and dividend increases.

title: Salesforce shares drop on slowest revenue growth in more than 10 years
summary: Salesforce Inc (CRM.N) shares dropped about 7% after the company reported its slowest quarterly revenue growth in over 10 years, attributing it to companies cutting back on cloud-based software spending amid an uncertain U.S. economy and weaker demand. Despite the slowdown, analysts remained largely positive, citing Salesforce's push into AI and improved profitability as factors for future growth, leading to several price target increases.

title: Vimeo is tuning out its streaming TV apps
summary: Vimeo is discontinuing its dedicated streaming TV apps for Apple TV, Android TV, Amazon Fire TV, and Roku starting June 27th. Users can still watch Vimeo content on their TVs by casting from the Vimeo mobile app via Apple AirPlay 2 or Google Chromecast. This move aligns with Vimeo's strategic shift in recent years from a consumer-facing streaming destination to focusing on video-streaming services for businesses.

title: Introducing Apple Vision Pro: Apple’s first spatial computer
summary: Apple has unveiled Apple Vision Pro, its first spatial computer, which blends digital content with the physical world using a three-dimensional user interface controlled by eyes, hands, and voice. Running on the new visionOS, Vision Pro features an ultra-high-resolution display system, custom Apple silicon, and offers new ways to interact with apps, enjoy entertainment, relive memories, and connect via FaceTime. The device is designed with advanced materials, focuses on privacy, and will be available early next year starting at $3,499 (U.S.).

title: Is Walmart Stock a Buy, Sell, or Fairly Valued After Earnings?
summary: Morningstar maintains that Walmart (WMT) is fairly valued after its solid first-quarter earnings, raising its fair value estimate to $152 per share. The company's wide economic moat is attributed to its scale and cost advantage, allowing it to compete effectively in the evolving retail landscape. While facing an intensely competitive environment and economic uncertainty, Walmart's omnichannel strategy and value proposition are expected to drive continued growth.

title: How Microsoft Fabric aims to beat Amazon and Google in the cloud war
summary: Microsoft has launched Fabric, a new cloud data and analytics platform designed to consolidate and simplify data management and analysis for enterprises. Analysts believe Fabric offers Microsoft a significant advantage over competitors like Amazon and Google by unifying disparate tools into a single platform with OneLake, a single data lake. This integration aims to reduce complexity, lower costs, and improve data quality, addressing key pain points for customers and potentially leapfrogging other cloud providers in enterprise analytics.

title: The 2023 Fortune 500 has been revealed: See which 9 Northeast Ohio companies made the list
summary: Nine Northeast Ohio companies made the prestigious 2023 Fortune 500 list, ranked by revenue for the 2022 fiscal year, with Mayfield Village-based Progressive Corporation being the highest-ranked local company at No. 88. The revenue threshold for making the list this year was $7.2 billion, a 13% increase from 2022. Several companies saw shifts in their rankings, with many increasing their revenue despite some dropping places on the list.

title: People News: C.H. Robinson, Port NOLA
summary: C.H. Robinson has appointed Dave Bozeman as its new CEO and a member of its Board of Directors, effective June 26, 2023. Concurrently, Port NOLA announced Matthew Wypski as its new COO, succeeding Mike Stolzman. Both new appointees bring extensive experience from industry-leading companies to their respective roles.

title: 2 Alabama-based companies make the Fortune 500
summary: Two Birmingham-based companies, Regions Financial and Vulcan Materials, have been included in Fortune's 2023 list of the nation's largest corporations. Regions Financial ranked 483rd with $7.5 billion in revenue, while Vulcan Materials placed 494th with over $7.3 billion in revenue. The Fortune 500 list, which ranks companies by their 2022 fiscal year revenue, features 52 companies led by women CEOs, an all-time high.

title: Revolutionizing cloud spending: How ProsperOps' AI solution is curbing public cloud expenses
summary: ProsperOps, a company founded by ex-Rackspace Technology Inc. employees, has developed an AI-enabled automation platform to reduce public cloud expenses. Erik Carlin, co-founder and chief product officer, explained that their platform moves beyond simple recommendations to actively optimize cloud environments, saving customers money. The company aims to tackle the significant issue of waste in public cloud spending, estimated at around $24 billion for AWS alone.

title: Apple, Amazon must face consumer lawsuit over iPhone, iPad prices, US judge rules
summary: A federal judge in Seattle has ruled that Apple and Amazon must face a consumer antitrust lawsuit accusing them of conspiring to artificially inflate the price of iPhones and iPads sold on Amazon's platform. The lawsuit alleges that a 2019 agreement between the two companies restricted the number of competitive resellers, a claim that will now proceed to evidence-gathering. Apple contends the agreement was to minimize counterfeit goods and is procompetitive.

title: A breakdown of AI ETFs as tech names burn bright
summary: This article breaks down various AI-focused ETFs available in Europe, examining their methodologies, holdings, and performance. It highlights the differences in how these ETFs define and capture the AI theme, from market-cap weighted exposure to companies involved in big data and NLP to equally-weighted exposure based on AI-related revenue or IP. The piece also compares their costs, liquidity, and correlation of returns, noting that while some are dominated by large tech names, others offer differentiated exposure to companies leveraging machine learning.

title: Additions to Fortinet FortiFlex deliver more flexible licensing options to customers
summary: Fortinet Inc. has introduced new product and service additions to its FortiFlex program, offering customers more flexible licensing options, including support for physical FortiGate next-generation firewall appliances. The program, which uses a points system for usage-based pricing, is now available via private offer in the Amazon Web Services Inc. Marketplace. FortiFlex aims to address the evolving needs of highly distributed networks by allowing organizations to pay by usage and manage licenses more efficiently across various deployment types.

title: Uber Eats to Deploy 2,000 Autonomous Robots from Serve Robotics
summary: Uber Eats is partnering with Serve Robotics to deploy 2,000 autonomous delivery robots, aiming to reduce delivery costs and increase efficiency. These robots navigate city environments with Level 4 autonomy using AI, LiDAR, ultrasonic transducers, and cameras. While the move offers significant technical advantages, it also raises societal questions about job displacement and ethical considerations in the expanding field of autonomous logistics.

title: AMD's AI chip challenge to Nvidia remains uphill fight with no big customers named
summary: AMD unveiled new details about its MI300X artificial intelligence chip, designed to challenge Nvidia's market dominance, but did not announce any major customers for the product. While the chip offers significant memory capacity aimed at cost reduction for AI services, the lack of customer commitments disappointed Wall Street, causing AMD's stock to fall. Analysts noted that Nvidia's strong market position is reinforced by its software ecosystem, which AMD is attempting to counter with its Rocm platform.

title: Labor Ruling Offers Unionization Path to Uber, Lyft Drivers
summary: A recent U.S. labor board ruling has made it easier for workers to be classified as employees rather than independent contractors, potentially opening the door for unionization efforts among gig workers at companies like Uber and Lyft. While gig companies argue this could impact their business model, the decision emboldens organizing campaigns, despite the inherent difficulties in unionizing a dispersed workforce. This ruling could lead to significant challenges for gig companies, potentially forcing them to negotiate with unions, though hurdles remain for successful organizing drives.

title: Fidelity Disruptive Technology ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FDTX)
summary: The Fidelity Disruptive Technology ETF (FDTX) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund that invests in innovative businesses with the potential for market disruption, including global disruptive technology companies in areas like machine learning and AI. The fund launched in April 2020 and is managed by Fidelity, with assets under management of $191.07 million. FDTX holdings include major tech companies such as NVIDIA, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, Microsoft, and Amazon.com.

title: Sonos Ray Review
summary: The Sonos Ray Review is an article from RTINGS.com focusing on soundbars, including various brands and models across different price points and features. The site mentions that its content, including full product reviews and pricing, is supported by insider access and affiliate commissions.

title: Alger 35 ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (ATFV)
summary: The Alger 35 ETF (ATFV) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" based on 624 analyst ratings covering 24 companies, representing 89.7% of its portfolio. The ETF's aggregate price target is $33.33, with a range between $33.33 and $33.33. This rating is derived from a detailed assessment of its holdings by Wall Street analysts over the past year.

title: JD.com Announces “35711 Vision” for the Next Twenty Years
summary: JD.com unveiled its "35711 Vision" on its 20th anniversary, outlining ambitious goals for the next two decades focused on financial growth, societal impact, and sustainability. These goals include achieving significant revenue and profit targets, ranking multiple enterprises on the Fortune Global 500, creating numerous jobs, and contributing substantially to various societal and environmental initiatives. The vision is supported by strategic focuses on lower-tier markets, technology and services, and international business expansion.

title: Sensex, Nifty Decline As RIL, ICICI Bank Drag: Market Wrap
summary: India's benchmark stock indices, Sensex and Nifty, declined on Monday despite opening higher, primarily dragged down by heavyweight stocks like ICICI Bank Ltd. and Reliance Industries Ltd. The S&P BSE Sensex fell by 216 points, and the NSE Nifty 50 closed 71 points lower. This decline mirrored a cautious global market sentiment following a strong second-quarter rally in Wall Street that lost momentum.

title: Freehold Raceway Mall Sears deal: See what happened to other Jersey Shore Sears stores
summary: Macerich, the owner of Freehold Raceway Mall, has acquired full ownership of the former Sears building, making it easier to fill the 72,000-square-foot vacant lower level with new retail, entertainment, or restaurant uses. Other former Sears locations at the Jersey Shore have been redeveloped into various businesses, including an At Home store, an Amazon delivery station, new restaurants and shops, and a ShopRite supermarket complex. The article details how different malls and developers are adapting these large retail spaces.

title: Sonos Five review: is Sonos' biggest, priciest wireless speaker its best?
summary: The Sonos Five is reviewed as a solid wireless speaker, praised for its bold, expansive sound and multi-room potential within the Sonos ecosystem. While it delivers room-filling audio, the review notes it can be overly bassy and lacks Bluetooth, suggesting that the newer Era 300 offers a more refined and expressive sound despite the Five's louder output.

title: Spotify has higher-priced subscription plan in the pipeline: report
summary: Spotify is reportedly planning to introduce a higher-priced "Supremium" subscription tier that will offer high-fidelity audio and additional audiobooks. This new plan, which was previously delayed, is expected to roll out to non-U.S. users in 2023, with American users gaining access later. The company recently increased its monthly active users to 515 million and premium subscribers to 210 million in the first quarter, and also announced an AI DJ feature.

title: Minnesota firm acquires retail brokerage in Bentonville, strengthening connections with Walmart, Sam's Club
summary: Select Sales Inc., a Minnesota-based retail sales and marketing agency, has acquired Bentonville Merchants (BMI), a sales agency that specifically serves Walmart and Sam’s Club brands. This merger, announced on June 21, 2023, creates a new company designed to enhance connections between manufacturers and leading American retailers. The combined entity aims to leverage local market expertise and robust data-driven strategies to accelerate growth for both divisions and provide a comprehensive one-stop solution for manufacturers.

title: Amazon Is Spending $100 Million to Teach Cloud Customers About AI
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is investing $100 million in a new Generative AI Innovation Center. This initiative aims to help its cloud customers develop and deploy AI products, connecting them with AI and machine learning experts. The move positions Amazon to compete with Microsoft and Google in the rapidly expanding generative AI market, with early users including Highspot, Twilio, Ryanair, and Lonely Planet.

title: Amazon.com Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) stock rose 4.26% to $130.15 on Thursday, outperforming a mixed market day where the S&P 500 Index saw a slight gain and the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a minimal decline. The company's shares closed $16.42 below its 52-week high.

title: 7.5 Million 'Baby Shark' Bath Toys Recalled Due to Serious Injuries to Kids
summary: Nearly 8 million "Baby Shark" and "Mini Baby Shark" bath toys have been recalled after reports of serious injuries to children, including impalement and lacerations. The injuries occurred when children slipped or sat on the toys' hard plastic top fin. Consumers are advised to immediately stop using the toys, disable them, and contact Zuru for a refund.

title: IBM to buy software co Apptio for $4.6 billion to bolster cloud offerings
summary: IBM announced it will acquire Apptio from Vista Equity Partners for $4.6 billion in cash to strengthen its cloud and automation capabilities. This acquisition comes as companies are trimming technology budgets, and IBM aims to integrate Apptio's software-as-a-service business to benefit its Red Hat, AI, and consulting divisions. The deal is expected to close in the latter half of 2023.

title: Amazon, Friction, and the FTC
summary: This article discusses the FTC's lawsuit against Amazon regarding Amazon Prime's sign-up and cancellation processes. The author argues that while "dark patterns" for signing up might exist, the overall value of Prime to consumers is significant. For cancellations, the author contends that Amazon's process, even as depicted by the FTC, is reasonable, and that the FTC's actions reveal an anti-business bias rather than a consumer protection one, especially when considering the fundamental Internet principle of friction reduction.

title: Are AWS Local Zones right for my low-latency app?
summary: This article explores the suitability of AWS Local Zones for low-latency applications, highlighting their benefits, use cases, and limitations. It compares Local Zones with other AWS options like CDNs, Edge Locations, Availability Zones, and Outposts, to help users determine the best architectural approach for their cloud computing needs. The author emphasizes that while Local Zones reduce network latency for high-performance applications, most applications will still need to access services in remote Availability Zones.

title: Snowflake Native App Framework Now Available to Developers on AWS
summary: Snowflake has announced the public preview of its Native App Framework on AWS, allowing developers to build and test Snowflake Native Apps directly within their accounts. This framework enables the creation of applications that run close to the data, addressing issues of data silos and offering enhanced security by eliminating the need to move sensitive data. Distribution and monetization capabilities for these apps are expected later this year on AWS, with an array of existing applications already available on Snowflake Marketplace.

title: Zoox headcount grows as Amazon's self-driving unit expands testing in Vegas
summary: Amazon's self-driving vehicle unit, Zoox, has increased its headcount by 16% to about 2,200 employees despite a challenging environment for autonomous driving companies. The unit is expanding testing of its driverless robotaxis on public roads in Las Vegas, preparing for a commercial launch. This expansion comes as other major players like Ford and Volkswagen have scaled back their autonomous driving ambitions.

title: Staples accepting Amazon returns
summary: Staples and Amazon have partnered to allow Amazon customers to return items for free at nearly 1,000 Staples U.S. retail locations. By July 31, 2023, all Staples stores will accept unpackaged, unlabeled Amazon returns, aiming to provide a convenient service and increase store traffic for Staples. This expands Amazon's return options, which already include Whole Foods and Kohl's, though Amazon recently introduced a fee for returns at UPS Stores if other options are closer.

title: All Staples locations to accept Amazon returns
summary: Staples is expanding its partnership with Amazon to accept returns at all of its nearly 1,000 locations by July 31, 2023. This collaboration increases Amazon's network of drop-off points to over 8,000 nationwide and offers customers a Staples coupon upon completing a return. While Amazon aims for hassle-free returns, it recently introduced a potential $1 fee for customers choosing a UPS Store for returns if a free option is closer.

title: QVC and HSN Launch Livestream Video Shopping Experiences on Amazon Freevee
summary: Qurate Retail Group has launched its QVC and HSN linear channels, along with its first FAST Channel, The Big Dish, on Amazon Freevee. This move provides Freevee customers with approximately 40 hours daily of live video commerce programming, expanding the reach of these shopping experiences. The launch is part of Qurate Retail Group's strategy to extend its vCommerce presence across new media and digital platforms.

title: QVC and HSN Launch on Amazon Freevee
summary: Qurate Retail Group has launched its QVC and HSN linear channels on Amazon Freevee, making them the first livestream shopping channels available on the service. Additionally, Qurate is introducing "The Big Dish," its first FAST (Free Ad-supported Streaming TV) channel, to Freevee, initially featuring culinary programming from QVC and HSN. This expansion aims to bring distinct live shopping experiences and focused culinary content to new and existing customers on Amazon Freevee.

title: Oakmark Equity and Income Fund: Second Calendar Quarter 2023
summary: The Oakmark Equity and Income Fund gained 5.3% in Q2 2023, outperforming the Lipper Balanced Fund Index, despite market concentration in large-cap tech stocks. The fund adjusted its portfolio by reducing technology exposure and initiating new positions, primarily in the healthcare sector, based on a value-driven investment philosophy. The article also reflects on the foundational principles of diversification in portfolio construction, influenced by economist Harry Markowitz, and the fund's approach to asset allocation.

title: Why India Could Be the Next Big Destination for Global Investors
summary: India is rapidly emerging as a significant global economic player and an attractive destination for foreign investors, building stronger strategic and commercial ties with the U.S. Major global corporations are increasing their investments in the country, recognizing its flourishing economy driven by a growing middle class, favorable demographics, and policy reforms. Projections suggest India could become the world's second-largest economy by 2075, offering substantial opportunities despite existing challenges.

title: A new economy hotel brand could be coming to Daytona. Here's where it would go.
summary: New York developers are planning to introduce an EchoSuites Extended Stay by Wyndham hotel in Daytona Beach, targeting an affordable rate of $50-$55 per night for guests staying seven or more nights. The proposed 124-room hotel would be built on a three-acre site under contract by Vana Partners, a company founded by Dartmouth and Harvard Business School graduates focusing on the extended-stay hotel segment. The location is strategically placed near I-4 and I-95, offering easy access and proximity to a large Amazon fulfillment center.

title: Top New Mobile Game Development Software You Should Know in 2023
summary: This article provides a guide to the top mobile game development software in 2023, including Unity, Unreal, HTML5, Godot, CryEngine, Cocos-2D, and Amazon Lumberyard. It highlights key features and popular games for each engine, aiming to help developers choose the best tools to create engaging and successful mobile games in a rapidly growing industry. The article emphasizes the importance of utilizing robust game engines and unique ideas to stay competitive.

title: Morning Blast: UPS Union Problems, More Rivian Gains, Wolfspeed Soars
summary: This article covers major market movements and corporate news from July 5, 2023, highlighting the breakdown in contract negotiations between UPS and the Teamsters union, leading to a drop in UPS stock. It also details Rivian's stock surge after Amazon announced the deployment of its custom delivery vans in Germany, and Wolfspeed's significant gains following a $2 billion wafer supply agreement with Renesas Electronics. Additionally, the article touches on overall market performance and upcoming economic reports.

title: 4 Indian American businesswomen on Forbes’ 2023 list
summary: Four Indian American businesswomen — Jayshree Ullal, Neerja Sethi, Neha Narkhede, and Indra Nooyi — have been featured on Forbes' 2023 list of America's 100 most successful women. Jayshree Ullal, CEO of Arista Networks, leads the group with a net worth of $2.2 billion. The article highlights their achievements, wealth, and contributions across various industries.

title: Amazon, AES Partner on First Utility-Scale Wind Farm in Mississippi
summary: Amazon and AES Corp. are partnering on Mississippi's first utility-scale wind farm, the 184.5-MW Delta project in Tunica County. This project is Amazon's sixth renewable energy installation in the state, aiming to power its operations with 100% renewable energy by 2025. The Delta wind farm is expected to generate significant tax revenue, create jobs, and allow for continued agricultural use of the land.

title: Worldwide Public Cloud Services Revenues Surpass $500 Billion in 2022 -
summary: Worldwide public cloud services revenue reached $545.8 billion in 2022, a 22.9% increase from the previous year, with SaaS – Applications being the largest revenue source. Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, Salesforce Inc., Google, and Oracle remain the dominant providers, collectively capturing over 41% of the global revenue. IDC predicts continued growth in foundational cloud services, especially IaaS and PaaS, driven by digital transformation and AI integration.

title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (DFAC)
summary: This article provides a review and aggregate rating for the Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC). Based on 801 analyst ratings across 25 companies (34% of the portfolio), DFAC has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and an aggregate price target of $40.95. The report details the consensus ratings for individual holdings within the ETF, including top components like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

title: Dr Reddy’s migrates SAP platform entirely on to AWS
summary: Dr Reddy’s Laboratories Ltd has fully migrated its SAP platform to Amazon Web Services (AWS) to accelerate application development and improve operations, aiming for a 30% reduction in development time. The pharma major is leveraging both AWS Asia Pacific (Hyderabad) and (Mumbai) Regions for its data center and disaster recovery, ensuring greater service continuity. This move is part of a strategic collaboration with Amazon to build innovative go-to-market models and enhance its digital platform to serve 1.5 billion patients by 2030.

title: Dr Reddy's Labs selects Amazon Web Services as preferred cloud provider
summary: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories has chosen Amazon Web Services (AWS) as its preferred cloud provider to enhance access to affordable and innovative medicines. This collaboration involves migrating the company's SAP platform entirely to AWS, aiming to accelerate the development of new healthcare applications and expand its digital platform to serve over 1.5 billion patients by 2030. The move is expected to reduce application development time by 30%, improve operations, and foster innovation in generic drug manufacturing.

title: Indian pharmaceutical company Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories migrates to AWS
summary: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, an Indian pharmaceutical company, has selected Amazon Web Services (AWS) as its primary cloud provider. The company has migrated 100 percent of its SAP platform to AWS, aiming to serve over 1.5 billion patients by 2030 and reduce application development time by 30 percent. Dr. Reddy's is also the first AWS customer to run its data center and disaster recovery workloads from both AWS cloud regions in Hyderabad and Mumbai, India.

title: Akamai expands its Connected Cloud with new data centers in US, France and India
summary: Akamai Technologies Inc. is expanding its Connected Cloud with new data centers in Paris, Washington D.C., Chicago, Seattle, and Chennai, India, to provide high performance, low latency, and global scalability for distributed applications. This expansion builds on its acquisition of Linode LLC and aims to offer a competitive cloud infrastructure alternative to giants like AWS by leveraging its extensive content delivery network backbone. Akamai is also introducing premium instance types, increased object storage capacity, and a new global load balancer to enhance its cloud service offerings.

title: Black Friday in July 2023: Best Deals from Best Buy’s Anti-Prime Day Sale
summary: Best Buy's "Black Friday in July" sale offered competitive deals during Amazon's Prime Day 2023. Wirecutter's Deals team highlighted discounts on tested products like the LG GP65NB60 External Optical Drive, Segway Ninebot KickScooter F30, EufyCam 2 Pro Wireless Security Camera, KitchenAid Shave Ice Attachment, and GE French-Door Refrigerator. The sale was expected to conclude on July 12, with some deals potentially lasting longer.

title: Sriracha bottles selling as high as $80 on eBay, Amazon amid shortage
summary: A shortage of Sriracha hot sauce, caused by a scarcity of chili peppers, has led to significantly inflated prices for the product on various online marketplaces. Unopened bottles are selling for $30 to $80, with some listings fetching up to 10 times their original value as consumers resell their stock. This shortage largely affects Huy Fong Food Inc.'s Sriracha sauces.

title: Amazon's 2023 Prime Day sets new sales record
summary: Amazon's 2023 Prime Day was the largest in company history, with Prime members purchasing over 375 million items and saving more than $2.5 billion. The event also marked the biggest Prime Day for independent sellers on Amazon, whose sales growth outpaced Amazon's retail business. Overall online spending reached $12.7 billion during the two-day period, a 6.1% year-over-year increase, with buy now, pay later services accounting for a significant portion of sales.

title: 'The Summer I Turned Pretty' Amazon store: Shop the merch
summary: Amazon has launched a new collection for 'The Summer I Turned Pretty' timed with the release of its second season. The collection features exclusive fashion from American Eagle Outfitters, P.J. Salvage loungewear, Sperry footwear, and beauty products from Supergoop! and Skylar Clean Perfume. Fans can also find an Amazon-exclusive hardcover book, listen to the official playlist on Amazon Music, and shop items seen on-screen directly through Prime Video's X-Ray feature.

title: Inside Overstock’s grand plan to reinvent itself as Bed Bath & Beyond
summary: Overstock is rebranding itself as Bed Bath & Beyond after acquiring the latter's intellectual property for $21.5 million. The company aims to shed its "overstock" reputation, attract new vendors and shoppers, and undergo a digital transformation with a significant marketing push. Early results from the Canadian website relaunch show higher conversion rates and vendor interest, indicating a potential fresh start for the legacy brand.

title: Caribbean phone companies suffering from “market failure” due to Over-The Top services, Social Media
summary: Caribbean telecommunication companies are experiencing "market failure" due to the prevalent use of Over-The-Top (OTT) services and social media, which consume the majority of data and international call traffic, leading to significant financial losses for local providers. Despite annual investments exceeding US$500 million in network upgrades since 2017, these companies face declining voice revenues and a lack of significant contribution from major tech companies that utilize their infrastructure. Industry leaders are advocating for government intervention and a unified CARICOM approach to address this imbalance and ensure a fair share from big tech, warning of dire consequences for regional connectivity and economic development if the issue remains unresolved.

title: Corsair’s K70 RGB PRO gaming keyboard is 35 percent off
summary: The Corsair K70 RGB PRO Wired Mechanical Gaming Keyboard is currently available for $110, a 35 percent discount. While the CHERRY MX RGB Blue Switches are ideal for typing, users looking for a gaming-specific keyboard might prefer models with Cherry Speed Fast or Cherry Red Linear switches, also on sale. Other gaming keyboard deals include the Logitech G915 TKL and options from HyperX and Razer, along with a Plugable USB C Dual Monitor Docking Station.

title: Foot traffic surges since Amazon return-to-office in May, company says
summary: Amazon's return-to-office policy in May led to significant increases in foot traffic and credit card transactions in Seattle's South Lake Union and Denny Regrade neighborhoods. This surge in activity, detailed in an Amazon analysis, is seen as crucial for downtown Seattle's post-pandemic recovery, though the policy itself faced employee opposition. Local leaders welcome the increased vibrancy and economic activity.

title: As Macy's stock struggles, the retailer bets on private brands with more modern looks
summary: Macy's is launching new private brands, including "On 34th," and refreshing existing ones to attract shoppers and boost struggling sales. The retailer aims to increase private brand sales to about 20% by focusing on modern, versatile looks, improved quality, and standardized sizing. This strategy comes as Macy's faces declining sales and a significant drop in its stock price, prompting a clear shift in its market approach following customer research.

title: IBM Remains America’s Worst Big Tech Company
summary: IBM continues to demonstrate mediocre financial performance, failing to achieve growth comparable to its big tech rivals for over a decade. Despite management's emphasis on hybrid cloud and AI, the company's revenue and net income figures lag significantly behind industry leaders like Amazon and Microsoft. This underperformance is also reflected in its stock, which has significantly underperformed the broader market over the past one and five years, indicating a lack of investor confidence in a turnaround.

title: Netflix falls as benefits from password-sharing crackdown to take time
summary: Netflix shares fell over 8% after its second-quarter revenue growth and forecast lagged estimates, despite adding nearly 6 million subscribers due to a password-sharing crackdown and new ad-supported tier. Executives cautioned that it would take "several quarters" to see significant returns from these new initiatives, leading to an $18 billion loss in market value. Analysts remain broadly optimistic, citing the potential for accelerated revenue growth in the latter half of 2023 and Netflix's strong content lineup amidst the Hollywood strike.

title: Is teleshopping still a viable retail channel?
summary: Teleshopping, with its origins in the 1950s, faces challenges in remaining a viable retail channel amidst the rise of online shopping and livestream platforms like TikTok. While industry leader QVC has integrated technology like QR codes, its declining net income suggests difficulties in attracting younger, tech-savvy consumers who gravitate towards more modern retail experiences. To survive, teleshopping must focus on products requiring detailed demonstrations and competitive pricing, while addressing the need to engage a younger demographic.

title: Amazon Lowers Price for iRobot Acquisition to $51.75 Per Share
summary: Amazon has lowered its acquisition price for iRobot, the maker of Roomba, to $51.75 per share, representing a 15% reduction from its initial offer. This price adjustment accounts for new financing secured by iRobot as the merger review continues under scrutiny from US and European regulators. The news led to a tumble in iRobot Corp.'s stock.

title: New YieldMax ETF Seeks Synthetic Covered Call Strategy on Amazon
summary: YieldMax has launched the YieldMax AMZN Option Income Strategy ETF (AMZY) on the NYSE, which utilizes a synthetic covered call strategy on Amazon (AMZN) to generate monthly income. This actively managed fund, advised by Tidal Financial Group and subadvised by ZEGA Financial, aims for compelling yields with capped participation in Amazon’s gains without directly investing in AMZN. AMZY follows a similar model to the previously launched YieldMax NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (NVDY), offering investors enhanced income through options.

title: Why is Netflix lagging FAANG stocks?
summary: While other FAANG stocks have seen significant rallies in 2023, Netflix has lagged, raising questions about its inclusion in the group. Despite a subscriber boost from a password crackdown, the company faces challenges from ongoing strikes and has not highlighted AI in its earnings, unlike its tech counterparts. The article discusses investment approaches for FAANG stocks through ETFs.

title: GCU graduate helps family company soar among sours on Amazon
summary: GCU graduate Edgar Diaz revitalized his family's lemon and lime juice business, Lucy's Inc., by implementing an online sales strategy on Amazon. His efforts led to the company's sales increasing significantly and being featured in an Amazon small business advertising campaign. Lucy's Inc. is now transitioning to organic products and glass bottles, aiming for high-quality, anti-inflammatory product lines.

title: You Don't Have to Pick a Winner in E-Commerce. Here's Why.
summary: The e-commerce industry, which saw significant growth during the pandemic, is projected to reach $1 trillion in U.S. revenue by the end of 2024. Investors can gain broad exposure to this growing sector without betting on individual companies by investing in exchange-traded funds (ETFs) such as the Global X E-commerce ETF (EBIZ). This ETF diversifies investments across various e-commerce segments and geographies, reducing country-specific risks and providing a hedge against the volatility of single stocks.

title: New Incheon CEO reveals vision to develop smart logistics clusters
summary: Incheon International Airport's new CEO, Hak-jae Lee, is spearheading initiatives to transform the gateway into a global logistics hub by developing smart logistics clusters. Key components of this vision include a smart cargo terminal, a Joint Distribution Center for SMEs, and enhanced global air cargo networks, alongside attracting specialist logistics companies. The airport aims to integrate Fourth Industrial Revolution technologies and offer incentives to strengthen its position in the competitive Asia-Pacific air cargo market.

title: YieldMax Launches Synthetic Covered Call ETFs for Meta, Google
summary: YieldMax has expanded its lineup of actively managed ETFs by launching two new funds that employ a synthetic covered call strategy for Meta Platforms Inc. (META) and Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL). The YieldMax META Option Income Strategy ETF (FBY) and the YieldMax GOOGL Option Income Strategy ETF (GOOY) aim to generate monthly income by selling call options on these tech giants. These new offerings follow the recent introduction of a similar ETF for Amazon (AMZN) and all have an expense ratio of 0.99%.

title: YieldMax Launches Synthetic Covered Call ETFs for Meta, Google
summary: YieldMax has introduced two new actively managed ETFs, FBY and GOOY, designed to generate monthly income through synthetic covered call strategies on Meta Platforms (META) and Alphabet (GOOGL), respectively. These ETFs do not invest directly in the underlying stocks but instead sell call options. This expansion follows the recent launch of a similar ETF for Amazon (AMZN) and reflects a growing advisor interest in options-based strategies for enhanced income.

title: AI and Data Centers: It’s About Keeping Cool
summary: A report from Citi Research highlights the critical role data centers play in the AI revolution, emphasizing that keeping servers cool will be vital. Despite historical predictions, data center energy use has been largely stable due to efficiency gains, but AI's computational demands are accelerating, making cooling and renewable energy increasingly important for sustainability. The article notes that while AI compute has outpaced Moore's Law, increased scrutiny of carbon emissions will drive higher corporate renewable PPAs.

title: Colgate Total® Teams Up with Actress and Entrepreneur Ashley Tisdale to Encourage People to Be Totally Honest with Their Dentist
summary: Colgate Total® is launching the "Totally Honest" initiative with actress Ashley Tisdale to encourage people to be more truthful with their dentists about oral care habits. The campaign highlights that 82% of people admit to lying to their dental professionals, and promotes Colgate Total® Plaque Pro-Release toothpaste as a way to proactively manage oral health and feel more confident at appointments. A pop-up event in New York City will provide a safe space for people to anonymously share their dental "lies" and receive product samples.

title: S&P 500 analysis: US stocks drop after Fitch downgrade
summary: US stocks, particularly the S&P 500, dropped following Fitch's unexpected downgrade of the US credit rating from AAA to AA+. This marks the first such downgrade in 12 years and comes amidst fiscal deterioration and high government debt. The article highlights that while the market is in an uptrend, unlike the 2011 downgrade, the potential for a sharper sell-off exists, especially with upcoming economic data like the nonfarm payrolls report and earnings from tech giants set to influence market sentiment.

title: Victoria’s Secret Fashion Show to Return as Streaming Event on Amazon Prime
summary: After a four-year break, the Victoria’s Secret fashion show will return on September 26, 2023, as "The Victoria’s Secret World Tour" on Amazon Prime Video. The reimagined event aims to celebrate and champion women globally, featuring a documentary-style presentation and collaborations with 20 global creatives. Following the streaming debut, a tour-inspired collection will be available for purchase on VictoriasSecret.com and its Amazon storefront, expanding Amazon's existing Victoria's Secret offerings.

title: Haus raises $17M Series A led by Insight Partners to build the future of growth intelligence
summary: Haus, a growth command center utilizing causal inference, experimentation, and AI, has successfully raised $17 million in Series A funding. The round was led by Insight Partners, with participation from existing investors, to further expand its team and develop integrated causal analytical tools. This funding aims to help companies measure and drive profitable growth amid challenges like cookie depreciation and increasing consumer privacy regulations.

title: Amazon set to launch credit card in Brazil with Bradesco
summary: Amazon.com is partnering with Brazilian lender Banco Bradesco SA to launch a credit card offering in Brazil. The launch is scheduled for next Tuesday, with Bradesco managing the credit risk and Mastercard powering the card. This move expands Amazon's fintech offerings in a market where interest rates are beginning to decline.

title: Exclusive: MercadoLibre Seeks to Boost Ad Revenue with New Streaming Platform
summary: MercadoLibre is launching a new ad-supported streaming service called Mercado Play, aiming to boost its ad revenue rather than compete directly with subscription-based platforms like Netflix. The service will be integrated into MercadoLibre's existing app, offering free content from partners like BBC and Sony to its 135 million active users across Latin America. MercadoLibre's Chief Marketing Officer, Sean Summers, emphasized that the goal is not content development but to leverage their growing Mercado Ads business and capitalize on the unmet need for free streaming content in the region.

title: Exploring two covered call strategies by Global X ETFs
summary: This article explores two covered call ETF strategies offered by Global X ETFs, designed to provide alternative income solutions in a potentially rising-rate environment. It details the Global X S&P 500 Covered Call UCITS ETF (XYLU LN) and the Global X Nasdaq 100 Covered Call UCITS ETF (QYLD LN), explaining how they generate income through option premiums and their potential benefits in volatile or sideways markets. The strategies aim to offer higher yields than traditional fixed income while potentially reducing volatility compared to underlying stocks.

title: Firefly Aerospace + Xtenti collaborate in support of the NRO
summary: Firefly Aerospace and Xtenti LLC are partnering on a responsive space mission, set to launch in 2024 aboard Firefly’s Alpha vehicle. The mission will demonstrate Firefly’s Elytra orbital vehicle and Xtenti’s FANTM-RiDE™ payload dispenser, supporting the National Reconnaissance Office's (NRO) study contract. This collaboration aims to showcase rapid payload reconfiguration and on-orbit deployment capabilities for both commercial and government payloads.

title: The Rise of Paper Packaging: Which Brands Are Converting?
summary: Major companies like Amazon, Chick-fil-A, and JDE Peet's are shifting towards more sustainable paper packaging to reduce plastic waste and improve their environmental footprint. This article highlights several companies and initiatives, including Amazon phasing out plastic mailers, Monterey Wine Company adopting paper bottles, and JDE Peet's launching recyclable paper packaging for instant coffee. Other companies like Graphic Packaging, Sonoco, Ranpak, DS Smith, and Holmen Iggesund are also innovating with paper-based packaging solutions across various industries to meet growing sustainability demands.

title: AWS and Five9 Become Leaders in 2023’s Gartner CCaaS Magic Quadrant
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Five9 have achieved leader status in Gartner's 2023 CCaaS Magic Quadrant, driven by their significant investments in innovation and market expansion respectively. AWS was recognized for its agile pricing and extensive partner network, while Five9's enterprise deployment growth and AI competency were key factors. Conversely, Talkdesk dropped out of the leader quadrant due to sales and service operation cautions outside of Europe and the US, despite its continued growth.

title: Pitney Bowes Parcel Shipping Index Reveals India Leading Global E-commerce Surge with 18% Parcel Volume Growth in 2022
summary: A new report from Pitney Bowes indicates that India experienced an 18% growth in parcel volumes in 2022, leading global e-commerce. This surge pushed India's per capita parcel volume from 7% in 2021 to 16% in 2022, with the country shipping 3.2 billion parcels annually. The global parcel volume only increased by 1%, largely due to lockdowns in China, positioning India as a key player in the booming e-commerce market.

title: Capital Research Global Investors Acquires Significant Stake in Carrier Global Corp
summary: Capital Research Global Investors acquired over 9 million additional shares of Carrier Global Corp. on July 31, 2023, increasing their total holdings to more than 86 million shares, representing 10.30% of Carrier Global Corp.'s total shares. This significant transaction, valued at $59.55 per share, underscores Capital Research Global Investors' continued investment in the industrial company despite Carrier Global Corp. being considered modestly overvalued by GuruFocus. The investment firm, with substantial holdings in technology and healthcare, now holds a considerable stake in the HVAC and security products manufacturer.

title: Blockchain Technology Market to grow by USD 11,047.61 million between 2022 and 2027
summary: The blockchain technology market is projected to grow by USD 11,047.61 million between 2022 and 2027, at a CAGR of 32.72%. This growth is significantly driven by blockchain's application in enhancing power grid reliability and the increasing adoption of Blockchain-as-a-Service (BaaS). The European region, particularly the UK, Germany, and Russia, is expected to contribute 38% to this market expansion.

title: Amazon cracks down on employees who stay away from office
summary: Amazon is increasing its enforcement of return-to-office policies by issuing warnings to U.S. employees who are not meeting the requirement of working three days a week in the office. This move aligns Amazon with other major companies like IBM, Zoom, Chipotle, and BlackRock, all of whom are pushing for employees to spend more time in physical offices despite many employees' preference for remote work. The company's action reflects a broader trend among corporations attempting to reduce remote work percentages, which have stabilized significantly below pre-pandemic levels.

title: TiVo's Smart TV launch: Europe first with Sharp, North America next
summary: TiVo (owned by Xperi) is launching its new Smart TV platform in Europe starting in 2024 through a multi-year deal with Sharp, which will be the second TV partner to adopt the platform. TiVo-powered Smart TVs are also slated for release in North America next year. Xperi CEO Jon Kirchner anticipates multiple partners in North America and projects at least 7 million TiVo-powered TVs within three years.

title: Amazon is removed from key list of climate-conscious companies
summary: Amazon has been removed from the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) list of companies taking action on climate goals due to its failure to set a credible target for reducing carbon emissions. This decision comes despite Amazon's 2019 pledge to eliminate or offset all carbon emissions by 2040 and a subsequent retraction of a 2030 zero-carbon delivery target. The removal raises questions about Amazon's standing among ESG investment funds, as its emissions have increased by 40% since 2019.

title: What To Consider When Using Generative AI
summary: The article discusses the considerations for using generative AI tools in a business context, focusing on security risks like data leakage and accuracy issues such as hallucinations. It highlights how consumer generative AI tools can pose dangers for sensitive corporate information and accuracy, leading many companies to restrict their use. The piece concludes by advocating for enterprise-specific generative AI solutions, like C3 Generative AI, that are designed for secure and reliable data handling to mitigate these risks.

title: How did sales growth during Walmart Plus Week compare to that of Amazon Prime Day 2023?
summary: During their respective July 2023 sales events, Amazon's Prime Day led to a 45% week-over-week increase in U.S. consumer sales for Amazon.com Inc, while Walmart Plus Week saw a 5% decrease for Walmart Inc. Interestingly, Walmart+ membership sales surged 43% the week before Walmart Plus Week, driven by a discounted annual membership and early access perks. This analysis highlights differing sales performances and membership strategies between the two retail giants.

title: Amazon Places Sponsored Products on Third-Party Sites Including Pinterest, Buzzfeed and Hearst
summary: Amazon is expanding its popular Sponsored Products advertising format to third-party websites and apps, including Pinterest, Buzzfeed, and Hearst Newspapers. This move allows advertisers to reach a broader audience without taking additional action, as ads will be automatically served based on user interest and page context. The ads will maintain familiar Amazon shopping attributes and link directly to product pages, aiming to enhance product discovery and shopping experiences beyond Amazon.com.

title: Amazon brings sponsored product ads to third-party apps, sites
summary: Amazon is extending its cost-per-click Sponsored Products ads to third-party applications and websites, including partners like Pinterest, BuzzFeed, and Hearst Newspapers. This expansion aims to provide brands with a broader reach for their ads, leveraging relevant page context and existing cost-per-click parameters without requiring additional action from advertisers. The move comes as Amazon's advertising business continues its substantial growth, with ad sales jumping 22% year-over-year in the second quarter to $10.7 billion.

title: Walmart Fulfills 50% of Online Orders From Stores
summary: Walmart now fulfills 50% of its online orders directly from its physical stores, leveraging its widespread store network to reduce delivery mileage and costs, especially for online grocery. This strategy helps the company achieve faster delivery times while addressing the expensive "last mile" challenge in e-commerce. Amazon is also focusing on reducing travel distances by regionalizing its fulfillment centers, aiming for similar efficiencies in package delivery.

title: Nine million and counting: The controversial shopping app sweeping Australia
summary: Temu, a Chinese-owned online marketplace, has rapidly become Australia's fastest-growing shopping app, amassing over 9.2 million users since its April launch. Despite its popularity driven by low prices and strong advertising, Temu faces controversy regarding alleged forced labor, counterfeit goods, and a lack of financial transparency from its parent company, PDD Holdings. The article highlights Temu's significant market presence and the ethical questions surrounding its operations.

title: Niagara Falls outlet mall loan with $87.5M balance is set to mature Oct. 1
summary: The Fashion Outlets of Niagara Falls, owned by Macerich, has an $87.5 million loan balance due to mature on October 1st. Macerich leadership has indicated they are actively working on a "transaction" regarding the mall, though specific details were not provided on a recent earnings call.

title: Technology continues to find fertile ground northeast of Austin
summary: The area northeast of Austin, including Pflugerville, Hutto, and Taylor, is experiencing significant commercial and industrial development, largely driven by large technology companies like Amazon, Samsung, and Tesla. Skybox Datacenters and Prologis are developing PowerCenter Austin, a massive 600-megawatt data center campus in Hutto. This development, alongside others, is expected to bring substantial economic benefits and further infrastructure growth to the region.

title: Silicon Labs Unveils the Series 3 IoT Platform, Pledges 100x Performance Gains for On-Device ML, AI - Hackster.io
summary: Silicon Labs has announced its new "Series 3" Internet of Things (IoT) platform, claiming a 100-fold boost in performance for on-device machine learning and artificial intelligence workloads. Built on a 22nm process, the platform promises performance, efficiency, and scalability gains, alongside multiple radio connectivity options and significant power improvements. The company also launched Simplicity Studio 6 with decoupled development tools and new chips for Amazon Sidewalk devices.

title: Amazon, Warner Bros. interested in NASCAR media rights
summary: Amazon.com Inc. and Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. are reportedly competing for a new package of NASCAR media rights, which includes six to eight summer races. This move by NASCAR aims to boost revenue by diversifying its media partners. Warner Bros. is particularly interested in filling a gap in its sports schedule after the NBA season and plans to offer races on its Max streaming service, likely with an additional subscription fee.

title: Enterprise AI for Lead Time Visibility
summary: This article details how a global retailer leveraged C3 AI's Supply Network Risk application to address challenges in predicting inbound lead times. By integrating various data sources and applying machine learning, the retailer achieved significant improvements in lead time prediction accuracy and identified substantial potential economic benefits. The solution helped reduce inventory levels, transportation costs, and labor costs by providing more accurate and continuously updated estimated delivery dates.

title: Best Back-to-School Marketing Campaigns (Plus the ‘Worst Ad Ever’)
summary: The article discusses the competitive landscape for back-to-school marketing in 2023, noting an expected 10% decrease in average spending per child. It highlights seven effective back-to-school marketing campaigns from various brands like Staples Canada, DoorDash, Dell, Amazon, Instacart, Bic South Africa, and Honda UK, showcasing diverse strategies from nostalgia to social responsibility. Additionally, it revisits top picks from previous years and comically points out an ad from East Hills Mall as "the worst commercial of all time."

title: Amazon's Struggle In The Grocery Retail Sector 2023
summary: Amazon is attempting to reduce its reliance on its investee business, More Retail, for grocery deliveries as both companies develop separate strategies for their supermarket businesses in India. This move comes amidst plans to potentially float More Retail on the stock market, and both Amazon Retail and More Retail have experienced growing losses despite increased sales. Amazon's overall struggle in the grocery sector, despite acquisitions like Whole Foods and services like Amazon Fresh, is attributed to consumer habits, logistical challenges with perishable goods, and established competition.

title: Amazfit Cheetah, the first smartwatch in the Amazfit series for runners, monitors eSports too!
summary: The Amazfit Cheetah smartwatch is designed for runners but also monitors eSports activities, offering extensive exercise tracking and health features. It includes a "Zepp Coach" function to create personalized exercise plans and supports monitoring of over 150 different sports, including e-sports. The review highlights its comfortable design, easy setup, comprehensive data tracking through the Zepp App, and customizable watch faces.

title: Building a Retail Media Business – Challenges and Solutions for Retailers
summary: This article discusses the challenges retailers face in building and scaling a retail media business, dubbed the "third wave of digital advertising." It emphasizes understanding goals, strategic technology integration, and developing strong delivery teams. Experts from Kellogg's, Criteo, and GoPuff Ads contribute insights on the importance of robust tech ecosystems, data access, and tailored approaches for success.

title: Smucker’s return-to-office plan is a full embrace of remote work—with a big catch
summary: J.M. Smucker has implemented a unique hybrid return-to-office plan that requires employees to come into its Orrville, Ohio, headquarters for 22 "core weeks" per year, scheduled annually. This model allows employees to live remotely, even thousands of miles away, while still fostering in-person collaboration during designated periods. While employees are responsible for their own travel costs, the flexibility to avoid relocating has been positively received, offering a new approach to hybrid work that CEO Mark Smucker believes will become a permanent fixture.

title: Plug Power settles with SEC, wins dismissal of lawsuit
summary: Plug Power has agreed to pay a $1.25 million civil fine to settle SEC charges related to accounting and control failures that led to a restatement of financial results for over two years. In a related development, a U.S. District Judge dismissed a lawsuit against the company accusing it of defrauding shareholders. Plug Power did not admit or deny wrongdoing in the settlement and faces a potential additional fine if internal control weaknesses are not fixed within a year.

title: The Fishbowl: Lydia Parker, Samsung Ads Europe
summary: This article features an interview with Lydia Parker, Head of International Agencies at Samsung Ads Europe, as part of The Media Leader's "Fishbowl" series. She discusses her career progression, professional experiences, leadership strategies in a hybrid work environment, changes in client conversations due to the booming CTV market, and her favorite ad.

title: 3 Online Retailers To Revamp Your Shopping List With
summary: The online retail sector, as seen through the ProShares Online Retail ETF (ONLN), is showing signs of recovery after a challenging period. With consumer confidence appearing to have bottomed out and the holiday season approaching, three online retailers are highlighted as potential additions to investors' watchlists: Amazon.com Inc (AMZN), Shopify Inc (SHOP), and Etsy Inc (ETSY). Amazon and Shopify have already seen significant rallies, while Etsy, despite recent struggles, is presented as a high-upside opportunity for risk-tolerant investors.

title: Summer's Eve Introduces New On-The-Go Collection
summary: Summer's Eve has launched a new "on-the-go" collection featuring travel-sized intimate care products. The line includes Refresher Mists, a travel-size Simply Sensitive Cleansing Wash, and individually wrapped Cleansing Cloths, designed for convenience and freshness for busy consumers. These products are gynecologist-tested, dye- and paraben-free, and are available nationwide.

title: EU, trasnacionales y los más ricos de México, detrás del grupo de presión IMCO
summary: The Mexican Institute for Competitiveness (IMCO), presented as a non-partisan think tank, is reportedly a pressure group funded by American corporations, wealthy Mexican businessmen, and the US government through USAID and NED. Since López Obrador's presidency began, IMCO has consistently criticized his administration's policies, aligning with the interests of its corporate and ultracapitalist donors like Atlas Network. Its financial records show that between 2019 and 2022, IMCO managed 180 million pesos for its "investigations," with over 90% of its annual income coming from donations, primarily from the Mexican Business Council and foreign entities.

title: From Dallas to Hyderabad, in Pursuit of Drones
summary: Joseph Thumma, CEO of Magellanic Cloud (Scandron), relocated from Dallas to Hyderabad to leverage the growing opportunities in India's drone sector. The article details how his company addresses diverse applications, from military logistics in tough terrains like Siachen to agricultural spraying and potential last-mile delivery solutions. Thumma emphasizes the importance of safety, purpose-based ROI, and the challenges of integration with enterprise software, while also discussing the future affordability of drones amid "Make-in-India" initiatives and chip scarcity.

title: Vodafone teams up with Amazon's Project Kuiper to extend 5G reach
summary: Vodafone announced a partnership with Amazon's Project Kuiper to expand the reach of its 4G and 5G networks in Europe and Africa. This collaboration will use Kuiper's low Earth orbit satellites to connect mobile base stations in remote areas, aiming to provide high-speed broadband services to underserved communities and businesses. This initiative complements Vodafone's existing work with AST SpaceMobile for space-based mobile networks.

title: Press Release: Rachel Kobetz Joins PayPal as Chief Design Officer
summary: PayPal has announced Rachel Kobetz as its new Senior Vice President and Chief Design Officer (CDO), reporting to John Kim, Executive Vice President and Chief Product Officer. Kobetz brings over 20 years of experience from leadership roles at Expedia Group, Bank of America, Amazon, and Samsung. In her new role, she will be responsible for the design strategy and execution across all PayPal experiences for consumers, merchants, developers, and employees globally.

title: Better Buy: Opendoor Technologies vs. Amazon
summary: This article evaluates Opendoor Technologies and Amazon as investment options, contrasting their performance since Opendoor's 2020 IPO. Opendoor, once dubbed the "Amazon of real estate," has seen its stock plummet due to a cooling housing market and rising interest rates, missing its financial targets and facing existential challenges. In contrast, Amazon, despite a growth slowdown in 2022, has shown signs of stabilization in its e-commerce and cloud segments and is considered a more resilient investment navigating cyclical headwinds.

title: Bandwidth Partners with Google and Cognigy to Launch AIBridge, Integrating Artificial Intelligence with Contact Centers Using Maestro
summary: Bandwidth has announced a partnership with Google and Cognigy to launch AIBridge, a new capability within its Maestro platform. AIBridge enables enterprises to deploy voice-based AI tools to their contact centers, allowing for faster and more efficient call resolution in the communications cloud without direct agent intervention. This integration is designed to improve customer experience, increase agent satisfaction, and significantly reduce contact center operating costs by leveraging conversational AI from partners like Cognigy.AI and Google Cloud's Dialogflow.

title: Cineverse Horror Channel SCREAMBOX TV Launches on Amazon Freevee
summary: Cineverse Corp. has launched its horror channel, SCREAMBOX TV, on Amazon Freevee, expanding its reach to a wider audience. SCREAMBOX has become a premier and rapidly growing streaming brand in the horror genre, adding over 550 new films and TV series since its acquisition. This move aims to make top-quality horror entertainment more accessible and attractive to advertisers.

title: Shark® Detect Pro™ Technology Outsmarts Dirty Carpets and Floors
summary: SharkNinja (NYSE: SN) has introduced its new Detect Pro technology, featured in a new line of intelligent cordless and robot vacuums. This technology includes DirtDetect, EdgeDetect, FloorDetect, and LightDetect to automatically adjust suction, clean edges, optimize for floor type, and illuminate hidden dirt, making cleaning easier and smarter for consumers. The Shark® Cordless Detect Pro™ Auto Empty System is now available, with more products, including a robot vacuum, to follow.

title: 2023 Entrepreneur Of The Year Winners
summary: This article highlights the 2023 Entrepreneur Of The Year winners from nine Minnesota companies, recognized through EY's Entrepreneur of the Year Awards program. It features profiles of leaders like Cheri Beranek of Clearfield Inc., Michele Henry of Face Foundrié, Patrick Hawkins of Hawkins Inc., and others, detailing their entrepreneurial journeys, company growth, financial achievements, and leadership philosophies. The entrepreneurs represent diverse industries including fiber optics, facial spas, chemical distribution, mental health services, functional beverages, airlines, sushi ventures, education technology, and RV manufacturing.

title: The Supply Side: Entrepreneur’s allergy-friendly snacks land in 1,800 Walmart stores
summary: Blake Sorenson, founder of Blake's Seed Based, successfully launched his allergy-friendly snack brand in 1,800 Walmart stores. Sorenson, an athlete with a nut allergy, developed the seed-based snacks to solve a personal and broader social need. His persistence over several years, including raising initial capital via Kickstarter and securing placement in major grocery chains, led to this significant expansion with Walmart.

title: Tesla’s market value could surge by $500 billion because of its Dojo supercomputer, Morgan Stanley says
summary: Morgan Stanley believes Tesla's Dojo supercomputer could increase the company's market value by as much as $500 billion. The firm upgraded Tesla's stock to overweight with a $400 price target, citing Dojo's potential to enable faster adoption of robotaxis and network services, similar to Amazon Web Services' impact on Amazon. Analysts anticipate that Dojo will make software and services the primary value driver for Tesla going forward.

title: Canada's Telesat taps SpaceX to launch its broadband satellites in orbit
summary: Canada's Telesat has partnered with SpaceX to launch its Lightspeed low-Earth-orbit (LEO) satellites starting in 2026, aiming to provide global broadband service by late 2027. This agreement covers 14 launches for Telesat's constellation of 198 satellites, with each Falcon 9 rocket deploying up to 18 satellites. While the deployment schedule is three years later than initially planned, the partnership with SpaceX was chosen for its optimal combination of price, performance, reliability, and schedule.

title: Amprius and Tenergy Launch New Drone Battery Pack
summary: Amprius Technologies and Tenergy have partnered to launch a new high-performance drone battery pack, unveiled at the Commercial UAV Expo. This innovative battery solution offers a 31% reduction in weight and a 6% greater energy capacity compared to competitors, featuring Amprius's high-energy density Silicon Anode Li-ion batteries. The companies anticipate this "drop-in" solution will significantly increase adoption in the Unmanned Aircraft Systems market, which is projected to reach $38 billion by 2025.

title: Direct Air Capture Company CarbonCapture Inc. Announces Equity Investment from Amazon’s Climate Pledge Fund
summary: CarbonCapture Inc., a direct air capture company, announced it has received an equity investment from Amazon’s Climate Pledge Fund.
