title: FedEx Challenges Amazon with Homegrown Marketplace
summary: FedEx is launching its own digital marketplace, fdx, to compete with Amazon by leveraging its extensive logistics infrastructure and shipping data. This move allows FedEx to monetize its delivery metadata by connecting merchants with customers, similar to how Amazon used customer behavior data to fuel its growth. The platform aims to serve both retail and B2B sellers, mirroring the evolution of Amazon's marketplace.

title: Netflix Tests Streaming Deal with Carrefour to Boost Subscribers (NFLX)
summary: Netflix is piloting a joint subscription plan with French retailer Carrefour to increase subscribers for its ad-supported tier. This initiative offers customers in Bordeaux and Rouen a discounted Netflix subscription along with benefits on Carrefour products and free shipping for orders over €60. The trial aims to replicate Amazon's success in bundling services to attract more streaming customers and may expand across France if successful.

title: Unlocking The Potential Of Pure Research At InterDigital
summary: This article examines InterDigital's role as a pure research facility, led by CEO Liren Chen, and its significant contributions to wireless technology, including current work on 5G advanced and future plans for 6G with AI integration. It highlights InterDigital's impressive growth, long-standing partnerships with companies like Apple and Amazon, and its unique approach to fostering innovation among its largely inventor-based workforce. Chen emphasizes the importance of curiosity, competency, and impact in driving both individual and company success, showcasing InterDigital's continued influence on global digital communication.

title: 5 key themes from the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show
summary: The 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) highlighted five key technological themes: AI as a pervasive enabler across all tech, advancements in smart home devices with unified communication standards like Matter, the evolution of immersive technology anticipating the Apple Vision Pro, the future of the automotive industry focusing on driver experience and electric vehicles, and the widening scope of health technology, including AI-powered mental wellness tools and wearables. These innovations provide a glimpse into the technological future.

title: NetApp Hires AWS Executive To Manage Global Cloud Sales
summary: NetApp has hired Ashish Dhawan, formerly AWS's managing director and worldwide head of enterprise workload sales, as its new senior vice president of global cloud sales. This new role aims to strengthen NetApp's commitment to cloud sales and strategy, with Dhawan responsible for developing and executing the company's cloud sales strategy across all major cloud providers. This is NetApp's second high-profile hire this year, following Riccardo Di Blasio as SVP of North America sales.

title: Billionaire couple gives Spelman College largest-ever single donation to an HBCU
summary: Billionaire Ronda Stryker and her husband William Johnston have donated $100 million to Spelman College, marking the largest-ever single donation to a Historically Black College and University (HBCU). The funds will primarily support scholarships ($75 million), with the remainder allocated to developing an academic focus on public policy and democracy, and improving student housing. This donation is part of a larger trend of increased giving to HBCUs following racial justice protests, and highlights Spelman's strong endowment compared to other HBCUs.

title: LiveRamp Acquires Habu, As Walled Gardens Put The Squeeze On Indie Data Collaboration Tech
summary: LiveRamp has acquired Habu for $200 million, consolidating the data clean room category. This acquisition combines Habu's cross-cloud clean room expertise for walled gardens with LiveRamp's identity graph and open web capabilities, aiming to contend with major players like Google and Amazon. The deal addresses the challenges faced by independent data collaboration vendors in a market increasingly dominated by large platforms, providing LiveRamp with activation capabilities that Habu previously lacked.

title: Your S&P 500 Index Fund Has Higher Risk Than You May Think
summary: This article argues that the S&P 500 index fund, traditionally seen as diversified, now carries higher risk due to its concentration in a few technology stocks. It highlights concentration, valuation, and inflation risks, suggesting investors re-evaluate their S&P 500 holdings and consider more value-oriented, truly diversified investments.

title: LQR: Leveraging Digital Marketing to Grow e-Commerce Platform & Advertising Operations as Sector Online Channel Expands
summary: LQR House Inc. (NASDAQ:LQR) is expanding its online marketplace, CWSpirits.com, and digital marketing agency, LQR House, to capitalize on the growing online alcohol sales market. The company employs a three-pronged strategy involving e-commerce, growing its product portfolio (including the SWOL tequila brand), and providing full-service digital marketing, leveraging influencers and AI-backed tools. With the upcoming closure of Drizly, LQR anticipates significant market share gains and aims to grow its revenue and marketing contracts, potentially diversifying into brand incubation and a wine club.

title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Stock Rocketed Over 200% in 2023
summary: Polen Capital's "Polen Focus Growth Strategy" fund saw significant returns in 2023, with NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) being a key contributor as its stock rocketed over 200%. The article highlights NVIDIA's crucial role in the generative AI boom, providing the processing power for large language models and AI factories. NVIDIA was held by 180 hedge fund portfolios at the end of Q3 2023, up from 175 in the previous quarter.

title: On theCUBE Pod: Is 2024 the year of AI ROI, and Broadcom’s next moves
summary: Industry analysts John Furrier and Dave Vellante discuss their predictions for artificial intelligence in 2024, focusing on deployment and economic impacts. They believe 2024 will be the year of AI deployment, leading to rationalized product portfolios and increased tech literacy among consumers. The podcast also delves into Broadcom's strategic moves, particularly after its acquisition of VMware, positioning it as a formidable player in the chip-to-app space.

title: eBay cutting 1,000 jobs, will reduce contract workforce
summary: eBay is cutting approximately 1,000 full-time jobs, representing about 9% of its workforce, and will also reduce its contract workforce to become more nimble and efficient. CEO Jamie Iannone stated that the company's headcount and expenses had outpaced business growth, despite strategic progress. This move follows similar workforce reductions by other major e-commerce retailers like Amazon and Wayfair amidst a cooling e-commerce boom.

title: AT&T's Q4 Profit Likely Fell as Subscriber Growth Slowed
summary: AT&T's Q4 profit is expected to have declined due to rising service and sales costs, despite a marginal revenue increase and persistent subscriber growth, albeit at a slower rate. Bank of America suggests that the company's stock underperformed last year despite strong operational performance, but anticipates improved performance in 2024 due to reduced capital spending and expanding cash flow.

title: Online retailer eBay is cutting 1,000 jobs. It’s the latest tech company to reduce its workforce
summary: Online retailer eBay is cutting approximately 1,000 jobs, or 9% of its full-time workforce, citing that its employee count and costs have outpaced its business growth in a slowing economy. This move follows a trend of layoffs across the tech industry, where companies that rapidly expanded during the COVID-19 pandemic are now trimming expenses. CEO Jamie Iannone informed employees that the company will also reduce its alternate workforce contracts and highlighted the need for better organization and nimbleness.

title: Boeing’s history reveals connections and disconnections in the West’s economy
summary: This article examines Boeing's history to illustrate the evolving relationship between corporations and the communities they are rooted in. It highlights how Boeing's shifts, from its early growth in Seattle powered by government contracts and regional resources to its later decision to move headquarters and the recent 737 Max crisis, demonstrate a trend where corporate loyalty to specific locales diminishes. The author argues that this placelessness often creates an asymmetrical relationship, forcing communities to adapt to companies rather than the other way around.

title: Analog Devices finds permanent CFO from Amazon Web Services
summary: Analog Devices has appointed Richard Puccio, formerly CFO of Amazon Web Services, as its new permanent Chief Financial Officer. Puccio served as AWS's CFO for almost three years before joining the Wilmington-based semiconductor firm. This appointment follows previous reports regarding Analog Devices' search for a permanent CFO.

title: IonQ Achieves Technical Milestone One Year Ahead of Schedule
summary: IonQ announced it achieved its 35 algorithmic qubits (#AQ) technical milestone a full year ahead of schedule on its IonQ Forte system. This innovation enables larger and more sophisticated quantum circuits, making the systems more valuable for applications like quantum machine learning and chemistry. The company's CEO, Peter Chapman, highlighted this achievement as a testament to IonQ's technical leadership and aggressive roadmap in quantum computing.

title: Historic $10B planned investment to benefit Entergy Mississippi customers
summary: Entergy Mississippi announced a historic $10 billion investment by Amazon Web Services (AWS) in Mississippi will bring new jobs and economic development, while supporting long-term power reliability and affordability for all Entergy Mississippi customers. This significant capital investment, finalized by Mississippi lawmakers, is expected to help keep electricity rates lower and drive advancements in the power grid and generation across the state. Entergy Mississippi, which serves 461,000 customers in 45 counties, emphasized its readiness to support AWS and other growing companies, highlighting its century-long commitment to providing reliable power.

title: Amazon.com Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) stock rose 0.87% to $159.12 on Friday, marking its fourth consecutive day of gains. This performance occurred on a mixed trading day where the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) increased by 0.16% and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) fell by 0.07%. The article highlights Amazon's strong individual performance relative to broader market trends.

title: Amazon taps Kueski for buy now, pay later service in Mexico
summary: Amazon Mexico has partnered with venture capital-backed fintech Kueski Inc. to offer a buy now, pay later service, Kueski Pay, allowing customers to pay in bi-weekly installments without needing a credit card. This initiative addresses the large unbanked population in Mexico and expands flexible payment options for Amazon customers. Kueski, a leading BNPL provider in Latin America, has secured over $300 million in funding for its services.

title: Amazon, Roomba-parent iRobot abandon $1.4 billion merger deal
summary: Amazon and iRobot have abandoned their $1.4 billion merger deal due to opposition from EU and U.S. antitrust regulators. iRobot announced a significant restructuring, including cutting 350 jobs and the stepping down of its CEO, Colin Angle, while Amazon will pay iRobot a $94 million termination fee. Regulators were concerned Amazon could use the acquisition to hinder iRobot's rivals on its online marketplace.

title: Amazon.com Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) shares dropped 1.40% on Tuesday, closing at $159.00, breaking a five-day winning streak. Despite this decline, the stock still outperformed the broader S&P 500 Index, which also saw a slight decrease, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose.

title: 2027 the Target for Transition Away from 1D GTIN, Toward Digital Link 2D Barcodes
summary: The packaging industry is targeting 2027 for a significant transition from the traditional 1D GTIN barcode to the more versatile Digital Link 2D barcode. This shift, driven by increasing consumer demand for information, regulatory requirements, and supply chain traceability needs, will enable a single QR code to provide different information to various stakeholders (consumers, retailers, supply chain partners). The "Sunrise 2027" program aims to prepare the necessary infrastructure, primarily software changes, for this industry-wide adoption.

title: SellersFi, Amazon team up to offer up to $10M in credit lines
summary: SellersFi has partnered with Amazon Lending to provide credit lines of up to $10 million to eligible Amazon sellers, particularly targeting larger businesses with revenues exceeding $2.5 million annually. These credit lines are facilitated through Citi, which, alongside Fasanara Capital, provided a $300 million credit facility to SellersFi. The initiative addresses the challenges e-commerce businesses face in securing traditional financing, utilizing SellersFi's cash-flow-based underwriting to approve credit lines rapidly while allowing Amazon to retain sellers within its ecosystem.

title: Downtown Nashville is getting another music venue
summary: Developers have unveiled plans for a new 4,500-seat music venue called "The Pinnacle" coming to Nashville Yards in early 2025, with Pinnacle Financial Partners as the naming rights sponsor and AEG running operations. This addition will provide another large-scale ticketed live music option downtown, competing with existing venues like the Ryman and Bridgestone Arena. Pinnacle Financial Partners is also moving its headquarters to Nashville Yards.

title: Plug Power Completes First Customer Fill of Liquid Green Hydrogen at its Georgia Plant
summary: Plug Power has successfully completed the first customer fill of liquid green hydrogen produced at its Woodbine, Georgia plant, with the hydrogen being transported to sites for Walmart, Amazon, and Home Depot. This milestone follows the official commencement of operations at the plant, which houses the largest proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolyzer deployment in the U.S. The Georgia plant can produce up to 15 tons per day of liquid green hydrogen, demonstrating a significant step towards a cost-effective green hydrogen economy.

title: Sonos Era 100 vs. Era 300: Budget or brawn?
summary: This article compares the Sonos Era 100 and Era 300 smart speakers, highlighting their differences in price, size, features, and sound quality. The Era 100 is presented as a more budget-friendly option with excellent sound for most users, while the pricier Era 300 offers Dolby Atmos spatial audio and greater power for larger rooms or audiophiles. The decision between the two depends on budget, the importance of Dolby Atmos, and room size.

title: Amazon’s Jeff Bezos Filed Plan to Sell Up to $8.6 Billion of Stock
summary: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has filed a plan to sell up to 50 million shares of Amazon stock by January 2025, which could amount to up to $8.6 billion. This marks his first planned stock sale in over two years. Bezos currently serves as the executive chair of the company.

title: Bezos plans on selling 50 million Amazon shares during next 12 months
summary: Jeff Bezos, the executive chair of Amazon, plans to sell up to 50 million shares of Amazon stock within the next year, according to a recent SEC filing. This prearranged trading plan, adopted in November, indicates sales will conclude by January 25 of next year. Bezos currently owns over 988 million Amazon shares, which constitute a significant portion of his substantial net worth.

title: Snap, the owner of Snapchat, is laying off about 10% of its global workforce
summary: Snap Inc., the company behind Snapchat, is reducing its global workforce by approximately 10%, impacting about 530 employees. This marks the latest in a series of layoffs for the tech company, with estimated charges of $55 million to $75 million primarily for severance. Snap plans to report its Q4 and full-year financial results soon, joining other tech giants like Microsoft, eBay, and Google in recent workforce reductions.

title: FNDX ETF: Fundamentals Are Attractive, Performance Is Not (NYSEARCA:FNDX)
summary: The Schwab Fundamental U.S. Large Company Index ETF (FNDX) tracks the Russell RAFI US Large Company Index and holds 730 companies, offering attractive valuation metrics and sector diversification. However, since its inception in 2013, FNDX has underperformed its Russell 1000 benchmark and several factor-based ETFs, despite its strong fundamental attributes. The article suggests that while the ETF's holdings show promise, its overall market performance has been lacking.

title: Were Our 2023 Cloud Predictions Correct?
summary: In this article, Tracy Woo reviews Forrester's 2023 cloud predictions, evaluating their accuracy. While they successfully predicted a significant move towards cloud-native strategies, they acknowledge a notable miss regarding the adoption of third-party cloud cost management tools, underestimating the advanced DIY capabilities of sophisticated FinOps users. The author emphasizes Forrester's commitment to accountability in their predictions and hints at promising outlook for 2024 predictions.

title: What Ideas Do We Need To Change The World? 100 Issues of Socialist Alternative
summary: This article marks the 100th issue of Socialist Alternative, reflecting on their decade-long journey to provide a working-class perspective often ignored by corporate media. It highlights the consolidation of media ownership and explains how mainstream outlets fail to address issues important to working people. The piece emphasizes the newspaper's role as an "instrument of struggle," written by and for working-class individuals, to fight for a socialist world.

title: Ingles Sees Net Sales, Income Decline in Q1
summary: Ingles Markets Inc. reported declines in net sales and net income for Q1 of its fiscal 2024, with net sales decreasing by 0.8% to $1.48 billion and net income falling to $43.4 million. Despite increased operating expenses, the regional grocer managed to narrow its total debt to $546.9 million. The company asserts its financial resources are sufficient to meet future capital expenditures, debt service, and working capital needs.

title: After Earnings, Is Amazon Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar views Amazon (AMZN) stock as fairly valued after its better-than-expected Q4 earnings. The company's strong performance in AWS, advertising, and e-commerce leadership reinforces its wide economic moat. While results were good, Morningstar maintains a high uncertainty rating due to a mixed macroeconomic picture that could affect enterprise cloud spending and consumer discretionary purchases.

title: Natural Grocers Encouraged by Q1 Results, Raises 2024 Guidance
summary: Natural Grocers reported strong first-quarter earnings for fiscal 2024, with net sales increasing 7.6% to $301.8 million and daily average comparable-store sales up 6.2%. The company's {N}power rewards program also saw significant growth, reaching over 2.1 million members. Based on these positive results, Natural Grocers has raised its fiscal 2024 outlook for daily average comparable-store sales growth and diluted earnings per share, and plans to open four to six new stores.

title: DLN: Large-Cap Monthly Dividend Payer Not So Hot
summary: The WisdomTree US LargeCap Dividend ETF (DLN) is rated a "Hold" due to its lower dividend yield and growth rate compared to other dividend-paying ETFs. While DLN offers monthly income and is well-diversified across approximately 300 companies, its current concentration in technology and large-cap focus present a limitation given the potential for future small-cap growth. The article suggests that while large-cap growth has dominated, changing market dynamics could favor value and small-cap opportunities.

title: Kroger links with AI retail analytics firm to boost marketplace listings
summary: Kroger has partnered with Intelligence Node, an AI retail analytics firm, to enhance product listings on its third-party online marketplace. This collaboration aims to provide clearer and more informative product descriptions, improving the customer experience and offering Kroger better insights into product performance. Intelligence Node's use of AI and machine learning, including generative AI for copy optimization, will help sellers on the Mirakl platform stand out and optimize their content.

title: Exclusive: Cisco to cut thousands of jobs as it seeks to focus on high growth areas
summary: Cisco is reportedly planning to restructure its business, which will involve laying off thousands of employees to focus on high-growth areas. An announcement could come as early as next week, ahead of its earnings call on Feb. 14. This move follows a previous restructuring in late 2022 and comes as many other tech companies are also cutting jobs.

title: Jeff Bezos sells $2 billion of Amazon shares as stock surge puts him within reach of becoming the world’s richest person
summary: Jeff Bezos sold 12 million Amazon shares worth over $2 billion this week, marking his first stock sale since 2021. This move comes as Amazon's stock surge has increased his fortune to $199.5 billion, positioning him to potentially reclaim the title of the world's richest person. Amazon had previously announced Bezos's plan to sell up to 50 million shares over the next year.

title: Nvidia Overtakes Amazon in Market Value
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Amazon in market value, making it the fourth most valuable US-listed company earlier in the day. The chipmaker's rally, driven by exploding demand for AI chips, has seen its shares jump 46% this year. While Nvidia closed slightly below Amazon's valuation, the event marks a significant milestone fueled by the AI boom.

title: AI frenzy puts Nvidia briefly ahead of Amazon in market value
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Amazon.com in market capitalization on Monday, driven by the ongoing euphoria around artificial intelligence. The chipmaker reached a record high of $734.96, giving it a market value of $1.82 trillion, slightly ahead of Amazon's $1.81 trillion. This event marks Nvidia's highest valuation relative to Amazon since 2002, with investors keenly awaiting its upcoming quarterly results on February 21st.

title: Fleet owner pleads guilty after opening sealed trailers, illegally consolidating freight
summary: A Columbus, Ohio-based trucking company owner, Gurtej Singh, pleaded guilty to wire fraud for illegally overbilling shippers and consolidating loads. Singh admitted to opening sealed trailers, removing goods, and failing to deliver many items to their final destinations, resulting in hundreds of thousands of dollars in losses for companies like Bath and Body Works and Amazon. The scheme involved altering seals and paperwork to conceal the illicit activities.

title: BREAKING: Arlington lands HQ of another publicly traded company
summary: CoStar Group, a publicly traded real estate information and analytics provider, is moving its corporate headquarters from Washington D.C. to Arlington, Virginia. The move to the Central Place building in Rosslyn will bring 650 employees and involves nearly $5 million in state economic incentives and a $14 million payment to Arlington County for exclusive use of an observation deck. This relocation continues Arlington's streak of attracting major company headquarters.

title: Nvidia replaces Alphabet as Wall St's third most valuable company
summary: Nvidia has surpassed Google-parent Alphabet to become the third most valuable U.S. company, with a market capitalization of $1.825 trillion. This milestone follows its recent overtaking of Amazon, driven by strong investor interest in the dominant AI chipmaker ahead of its upcoming quarterly report. Nvidia's stock has surged due to its control of about 80% of the high-end AI chip market and strong demand for its products.

title: Seattle office tower vacated by Amazon is drawing interest, owner says
summary: The West 8th tower in downtown Seattle, which Amazon vacated in April 2023 leaving it 80% empty, is now reportedly attracting significant interest from potential new tenants. Kilroy Realty had purchased the building for $490 million in 2021. Amazon's departure led to the building's occupancy dropping to 20%.

title: Chase Coleman's Tiger Global Management Reduces Stake in ATRenew Inc
summary: Chase Coleman's Tiger Global Management has reduced its stake in ATRenew Inc (RERE) by selling over 3.8 million shares, representing a 33.33% decrease in its holdings. This transaction lowers ATRenew Inc's portfolio presence to 0.06% and reflects a strategic adjustment for Tiger Global, which typically focuses on larger technology and communication services companies. Despite ATRenew Inc having stable financial health, its poor profitability and growth prospects, coupled with a significant stock price decline, likely influenced Coleman's decision.

title: Stock market concentration is on the rise. Will this continue?
summary: Stock market concentration in the U.S. has reached multi-decade highs, largely driven by the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks. While these mega-caps have benefited from market rotation and AI excitement, historical trends suggest that steep rises in concentration tend to reverse, and this could be influenced by future liquidity and the economic cycle. Investors should consider the implications of this concentration on future performance, especially in different economic scenarios like a soft landing.

title: Amazon.com Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) stock experienced a slight dip of 0.17% on Friday, closing at $169.51, amid a generally down market where the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also fell. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for Amazon's stock. Despite these declines, the article headline suggests that Amazon's stock performed better relative to its competitors during this period.

title: Amazon.com Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Amazon.com Inc. stock (AMZN) closed down 0.17% on Friday at $169.51, marking its second consecutive day of losses. Despite the dip, Amazon's performance still outperformed the broader market, as both the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced larger declines on what was a generally dismal trading day.

title: Nvidia CEO Wears Fuchsia Leather Jacket At Insight Sales Event
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang wore a fuchsia leather jacket, Insight's signature color, at the company's Amplify sales event, signaling a strong partnership in generative AI. An Insight executive stated that Huang's virtual presence and choice of attire underscored the significance of their collaboration, especially as Nvidia expands its AI solutions. The event also featured Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger and focused on generative AI use cases, infrastructure upgrades, and the growing demand for AI PCs amidst a broader PC refresh cycle.

title: Amazon Set to Replace Walgreens in Dow Jones Industrial Average
summary: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) is set to replace Walgreens Boots Alliance Inc. (WBA) in the 30-stock Dow Jones Industrial Average. The change will be effective prior to the open of trading on Monday, February 26th. The e-commerce giant's inclusion diversifies the blue-chip index.

title: Hedge Fund and Insider Trading News: Boaz Weinstein, Bill Ackman, Elliott Investment Management, Rokos, Capital Management, Segantii Capital Management, Pantera Capital, AutoNation, Inc. (AN), and More
summary: This article covers recent news in the hedge fund and insider trading world, featuring major players such as Boaz Weinstein, Bill Ackman, Elliott Investment Management, Rokos Capital Management, Segantii Capital Management, and Pantera Capital. Key updates include the launch of new funds, asset changes for Segantii, Rokos's significant gains from interest rate bets, and Pantera's outlook on Bitcoin DeFi opportunities. Additionally, notable insider selling in Amazon and AutoNation is highlighted.

title: Home Depot ban on worker's Black Lives Matter apron was illegal, US agency rules
summary: A federal labor board ruled that Home Depot illegally banned a worker from wearing a "BLM" apron, linking it to protected activity due to previous racial discrimination complaints at the store. The ruling orders reinstatement and back pay for the worker, Antonio Morales, who quit after being told not to return with the apron. This decision comes as the labor board is also considering similar cases against Whole Foods and Kroger regarding Black Lives Matter apparel.

title: Retail Universe for U.S. Premium Natural Organic Food, Supplement, and Personal Care Sales
summary: The U.S. natural organic retail sector achieved over $103.6 billion in sales, an increase of 4.38% year-over-year, and now constitutes 10.13% of total U.S. food-store sales. While six of the eight tracked retail channels saw growth, pharmacy and vitamin chains experienced sales declines. Record efficiencies were noted, with natural products demonstrating 133.57% more sales per square foot compared to the overall retail average, indicating strong consumer embrace of healthier lifestyles despite inflation.

title: Vice Media to stop publishing on its website, lay off 'several' hundreds
summary: Vice Media has announced it will cease publishing content on its website, vice.com, and lay off several hundred of its employees. CEO Bruce Dixon stated that distributing digital content is no longer cost-effective, and the company will focus on other distribution channels. This move reflects the ongoing financial struggles in the media industry, with many legacy and digital media brands facing job cuts or shutdowns.

title: SPY First ETF to Reach $500B in Assets
summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) made history by becoming the first ETF to reach $500 billion in assets under management. This milestone follows the S&P 500 surpassing the 5,000 mark, driven by strong performance from large technology companies. Despite significant outflows, the fund's liquidity and broad investor base are cited as key drivers of its growth.

title: What's Cathie Wood been selling?
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Investment Management has sold shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. and Nvidia Corp., diversifying holdings amidst the AI frenzy. This marks Wood's first sale of TSMC stock in over two years and follows previous sales of Nvidia. The move comes as both TSMC and Nvidia have seen significant gains, with Wood's funds betting on growth potential in less-talked-about software companies like UiPath and Twilio, despite earlier predictions of AI's transformative impact.

title: Fidelity Fundamental Large Cap Core ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (BATS:FFLC) $FFLC
summary: This article provides detailed information on the Fidelity Fundamental Large Cap Core ETF (FFLC), including its current price, performance, key statistics, top holdings, and expense ratios. FFLC is an actively managed ETF investing in large-cap stocks based on fundamental factors, with major holdings in tech giants like NVIDIA, Alphabet, and Amazon. The ETF has seen a 1.9% increase in stock value since the beginning of the year.

title: Pioneering carbon capture initiative launched at International Paper's Vicksburg mill
summary: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) is funding a groundbreaking carbon capture project at International Paper's Vicksburg Containerboard Mill, in partnership with RTI International, SLB, and Amazon. This initiative aims to capture 120,000 metric tons of CO2 annually using RTI's non-aqueous solvent (NAS) technology, significantly reducing emissions in the pulp and paper industry. The project will generate valuable data on carbon capture efficacy and economic impacts, contributing to global decarbonization efforts and potentially fostering wider adoption of the technology.

title: EU lawmakers back draft rules on patents for connected cars, telecom equipment
summary: EU lawmakers have approved draft rules concerning patents for technologies in connected cars and telecom equipment, despite criticism from patent holders like Nokia and Ericsson. The proposed rules aim to resolve expensive and prolonged patent disputes, while industry groups like IP Europe argue they could stifle innovation, whereas the Fair Standards Alliance supports them for promoting fair licensing terms. The rules will now proceed to negotiations between the European Parliament and EU member countries.

title: Amazon's Stock Looks Cheap (NASDAQ:AMZN)
summary: Long-term investors in Amazon have seen significant returns, with the stock proving to be a "10-bagger" since 2014. The article suggests that Amazon's consistent aggressive reinvestment strategy, backed by substantial financial resources and talent, indicates that the stock's growth is far from over. A valuation analysis indicates Amazon is currently undervalued by 20%, leading to a "Strong Buy" rating.

title: KT teams up with Amazon to bolster B2B generative AI
summary: South Korean telecom provider KT Corp. has partnered with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to enhance its B2B generative AI services, aiming to transform into an AI-focused ICT company. This collaboration involves developing generative AI based on Amazon Bedrock and offering cloud-based private 5G network services. KT plans to hire more AI and ICT experts, integrate AI into performance reviews, and upgrade its internal generative AI platform "Gen.AIDU" using various LLMs.

title: The Rise of Humanoids, Explained
summary: This article discusses the rapid advancements and growing market potential of humanoid robots, driven by technological convergence of robotics and AI, and decreasing hardware costs. It highlights how humanoids are poised for mainstream adoption in industrial and household settings, potentially creating a multi-trillion dollar market by 2035, and identifies investment opportunities across the supply chain. The piece also details the challenges and improvements needed for widespread adoption, comparing the trajectory of humanoids to that of early automobiles and collaborative robots.

title: Citadel's Ken Griffin Oversees $500 Billion in Managed Assets: These Are His Top 5 Holdings
summary: Ken Griffin's Citadel Advisors manages $500 billion in assets and is a top-performing hedge fund. SEC filings reveal its top non-derivative holdings, which include the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon, and Advanced Micro Devices. These investments highlight a strategy of betting on market indexes and major players in tech and AI while acknowledging potential risks and sector trends.

title: Magnificent Seven vs. the ‘Granolas’: How does Europe’s version stack up?
summary: This article compares the U.S. "Magnificent Seven" stocks with Europe's "Granolas," highlighting their differences in market value, sector diversity, and profitability. While the Magnificent Seven are largely tech-driven and exceptionally profitable, the Granolas offer broader sector representation and solid returns, often trading at a discount despite their strong performance. The piece examines historical returns, valuation ratios, and individual stock assessments for both groups.

title: Phase II of Willow Grove Shopping Center construction complete: Amazon Fresh, other tenants on the way
summary: Phase II of the Willow Grove Shopping Center's construction is complete, with Amazon Fresh still planning to open in the space. A Dave's Hot Chicken restaurant will also be a tenant, while three to four other tenants are yet to be announced. Developer Federal Realty Investment Trust described the 211,000 square-foot shopping center, which will feature new walkways, public amenities, outdoor dining, and an 18,000 square-foot outparcel building.

title: F1 owner Liberty Media eyeing ‘€4bn’ MotoGP acquisition
summary: Liberty Media, owner of Formula One, is reportedly a frontrunner in the bidding to acquire MotoGP's commercial rights holder, Dorna, for an estimated €4 billion. Bridgepoint, which holds a significant stake in Dorna, is exploring a sale, with other interested parties including Amazon, Netflix, and Disney. The acquisition could raise competition concerns similar to those faced by CVC Capital Partners after buying Formula One.

title: New Target Membership Plan Will Offer Free Same-Day Delivery
summary: Target is launching a new paid membership program called Target Circle 360, offering free same-day delivery and two-day shipping for an annual fee, aiming to compete with Amazon Prime and Walmart+. The program will cost $49 initially, rising to $99, and is part of an expanded loyalty initiative. This move deepens customer loyalty and generates additional revenue, though Target is a late entrant to the subscription market.

title: Target Starts $49 Membership Program to Compete With Amazon, Walmart
summary: Target Corp. is launching "Target Circle 360," a new paid membership program offering unlimited free same-day delivery for orders over $35 and free two-day shipping. The program, priced at a promotional rate of $49 annually, aims to compete with established membership programs from Amazon and Walmart. Target is also rebranding its RedCard as the Target Circle Card and expanding its existing free loyalty program.

title: Why Mega-Cap Tech Stocks’ Dominance Is a Risk
summary: The "Magnificent 7" tech stocks now comprise a significant 30% of the S&P 500 Index’s market capitalization, creating concentration risks for investors. This historical top-heaviness makes portfolios vulnerable to significant drops if these stocks, highly correlated and sensitive to interest rates, decline. The article suggests strategies like investing in U.S. bonds, non-U.S. equities, equal-weighted S&P 500 strategies, and alternative assets to mitigate this risk.

title: Turn $10k Into a Fortune With These 3 High-Risk, High-Reward Space Stocks
summary: This article highlights three high-risk, high-reward space stocks—Rocket Lab USA (RKLB), Planet Labs PBC (PL), and AST SpaceMobile (ASTS)—that offer significant growth potential in the burgeoning $1 trillion space industry. Despite short-term financial challenges like stock dilution and cash burn, each company presents a strong long-term value proposition through substantial backlogs, key contracts with government agencies, or strategic partnerships.

title: Electric Hydrogen turns to ‘really efficient capital’ to expand manufacturing
summary: Electric Hydrogen, a green hydrogen company, secured $50 million in equipment financing from Trinity Capital to expand manufacturing for its first large-scale factory. The company's strategy relies on using widely proven equipment from other industries, which allowed it to secure non-dilutive, lower-cost financing. This funding adds to over $600 million raised previously and will support the production of 100 MW electrolyzers, with the factory aiming for full production volume of 1.2 gigawatts per year by 2025.

title: Fortune 500 list: The top 10 companies dominating business
summary: This article details the top 10 companies on the 2023 Fortune 500 list, ranked by revenue, ahead of the 2024 list release. Walmart maintains the top spot for the eleventh consecutive year, followed by Amazon, Exxon Mobil, and Apple. The report provides key financial figures, strategic moves, and recent developments for each company, highlighting their influence on the global economy.

title: Roku Forces Customers to Give Up Basic Legal Rights In New TOC
summary: Roku's updated terms of service now stipulate that users waive their right to participate in class-action lawsuits, instead requiring binding arbitration. Furthermore, Roku has implemented a convoluted and restrictive arbitration process, making it difficult and uneconomical for individual consumers to pursue claims against the company. While consumers can technically opt-out of these new terms, the process is intentionally cumbersome, highlighting a growing trend among companies to limit user recourse for legal issues.

title: Megacap tech stocks dominate many ESG funds. Here’s why
summary: Megacap tech stocks frequently dominate ESG funds because these funds still aim for top performers within economic groups, and the tech sector is considered "cleaner" regarding environmental impact. While ESG funds saw net outflows in Q4 2023 due to advisors pulling back, individual investor interest remains strong, viewing ESG as a long-term allocation strategy rather than a momentum play. Despite misconceptions, ESG funds can and do invest in energy companies, as explained by experts from DWS Group and former VettaFi.

title: 1How AI Enabled Smart Carts Can Bring Retail Into The 21st Century
summary: This article explores how AI-enabled smart carts can revolutionize the retail industry by addressing challenges like labor shortages, theft, and the demand for personalized shopping experiences. Despite past failures and slow adoption, new innovations in smart cart technology are making them more affordable, user-friendly, and capable of integrating with in-store media and CRM systems. The author suggests that these advancements, combined with aligning market conditions and a focus on positive user experience, could finally push smart carts into widespread use, potentially replacing self-checkout stations and funding adoption through new revenue channels.

title: Code and Theory, a Stagwell (STGW) Network Company, Named Ad Age Business Transformation Agency of the Year
summary: Code and Theory, a Stagwell (STGW) network company, has been named Ad Age Business Transformation Agency of the Year due to its technology-first approach and ability to drive significant results for clients. The agency is unique in its equal balance of engineers and creatives, which provides a competitive advantage in building brands, experiences, platforms, and capabilities to accelerate growth. They have achieved notable successes with clients like Amazon Ads, YETI, and Con Edison, distinguishing themselves through innovative partnerships and a focus on technology-led creative solutions.

title: 15 Best eBay Alternatives to Try in 2024
summary: This article lists the 15 best eBay alternatives for 2024, discussing why eBay is a popular choice for sellers and introducing other major e-commerce platforms like Walmart, Amazon, and Target. It also provides a detailed ranking of 15 alternatives based on site visits, including information on their offerings and seller programs.

title: Philips and AWS collaborate to scale digital pathology
summary: Royal Philips announced an expanded collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to enhance digital pathology solutions using cloud technology. This partnership aims to improve clinical workflows, efficiently store and manage large volumes of digital pathology data, and facilitate collaboration among specialists. The initiative is set to accelerate decision-making in diagnostics, support large-scale clinical trials, and integrate AI to further advance patient care, particularly in cancer diagnosis.

title: Lilly partners with Amazon to deliver Zepbound and other drugs
summary: Eli Lilly has partnered with Amazon Pharmacy to deliver prescriptions for its direct-to-consumer service, LillyDirect, which offers drugs like the weight-loss medicine Zepbound. This expands Lilly's delivery network, previously handled by Truepill, and aims to meet the surging demand for obesity medications while ensuring proper application of financial assistance and preventing counterfeit sales. Lilly is also exploring partnerships with retail pharmacies for pick-up options.

title: Eli Lilly taps Amazon Pharmacy to help deliver weight loss drug Zepbound, other medicines to patients
summary: Eli Lilly has partnered with Amazon Pharmacy to facilitate the home delivery of prescription drugs, including the weight loss medication Zepbound, through its direct-to-consumer website, LillyDirect. This collaboration aims to enhance patient access by providing two-day delivery for Amazon Prime members and offering 24/7 access to clinical pharmacists. The move reflects efforts to streamline drug distribution and reduce consumer costs, especially as demand for Zepbound and similar drugs continues to soar.

title: Markets Showing Signs of Bubble on Big Tech, Crypto Surge, BofA’s Hartnett Says
summary: Bank of America’s Chief Investment Strategist Michael Hartnett believes that the current market surge in "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks and cryptocurrencies exhibits characteristics of a bubble. He points to re-accelerating inflation, soft growth, and the narrowness of gaining assets as signs of a "bubble mentality."

title: Watch BofA’s Hartnett Sees Characteristics of a Bubble in Mag 7, Crypto
summary: Bank of America Corp.'s Chief Investment Strategist, Michael Hartnett, believes that the record-setting performance of the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks and the all-time highs in cryptocurrencies exhibit characteristics of a bubble. He shared these insights during a discussion with Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua at the Bank of America Global Investor Summit conference in Rome. The article highlights his assessment of current market conditions.

title: Nasdaq listing is a strategic move aimed at bringing value to Noventiq: CEO Hervé Tessler
summary: Noventiq CEO Hervé Tessler discusses the company's strategic move to list on Nasdaq from the London Stock Exchange, highlighting the benefits of increased access to capital and enhanced brand prestige. He emphasizes Noventiq's strong presence in emerging markets, its partnerships with tech giants like Microsoft and AWS, and its focus on digital transformation, cybersecurity, and AI solutions. Tessler also addresses challenges like navigating diverse global regulations and the imperative for continuous innovation in the rapidly evolving technology sector.

title: First over-the-counter birth control pill now for sale online
summary: Opill, the first oral contraceptive pill available without a prescription in the U.S., is now for sale online and will soon be in stores. Manufactured by Perrigo, the progestin-only pill costs $19.99 for a month's supply and is 98% effective when taken correctly. This marks a significant accessibility improvement for pregnancy prevention, especially for those who face barriers to seeing a healthcare provider.

title: Tough stuff: this rugged Corsair gaming headset just scored its biggest discount yet
summary: The Corsair HS80 Max Wireless gaming headset is currently available at its lowest price ever, costing AU$189, a AU$90 discount from its RRP of AU$279. The headset, which earned four stars in its initial review, is praised for its rich feature set, including long battery life (up to 130 hours with Bluetooth), multi-platform compatibility, and Dolby Atmos Spatial Audio, making it a compelling deal for gamers despite minor sound quality considerations.

title: Amazon Beats University’s Patent Lawsuit Over Alexa Voice Tech
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has successfully defended against a patent infringement lawsuit filed by Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI) concerning its Alexa voice assistant technology. A federal judge invalidated RPI's patent, US Patent No. 7,177,798, which covered a system for understanding and responding to language using a limited dictionary. This ruling concludes a nearly six-year legal dispute, granting Amazon's motion for summary judgment and denying RPI's competing request.

title: E-commerce giant Amazon inks huge lease in West Valley
summary: Amazon has signed a significant industrial lease in metro Phoenix for a new warehouse at the Cubes at Glendale industrial park. This marks one of the largest leases in the area since 2022, following a slowdown in e-commerce absorption. The move indicates renewed activity in the industrial real estate market for large facilities.

title: 12 Best Beach Towels of 2024, Tested by Experts
summary: The Good Housekeeping Institute Textiles Lab tested over a dozen beach towels in the lab and at the beach to identify the best options for summer 2024. They evaluated factors like absorbency, feel, durability, appearance, and sand retention. The article highlights 12 top picks, including the Lands' End Cabana Stripe Beach Towel as the Best Overall and various specialized towels for value, sand-free use, travel, and personalization, detailing their pros, cons, and material specifics.

title: Impact Shares YWCA Women’s Empowerment ETF (WOMN) Reaches $50M in Assets Under Management
summary: The Impact Shares YWCA Women’s Empowerment ETF ($WOMN) has surpassed $50 million in Assets Under Management (AUM), demonstrating investor interest in socially conscious funds. This ETF provides broad market exposure to U.S. companies promoting gender equity, with all net advisory profits donated to the YWCA to support its mission. The partnership between Impact Shares, Tidal Financial Group, and the YWCA highlights an innovative approach to aligning financial returns with social impact.

title: GraniteShares 2x Long AMZN Daily ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:AMZZ)
summary: This article provides details on the GraniteShares 2x Long AMZN Daily ETF (AMZZ), including its current price ($25.28), key statistics, and an overview of its investment strategy. The ETF aims to provide 2x leveraged exposure to the daily price movements of Amazon stock. It also lists top holdings, recent news headlines, and answers frequently asked questions about the ETF's performance and institutional ownership.

title: Get to know QQQ: Charting 25 years of performance
summary: The article celebrates 25 years of the Invesco QQQ ETF, tracing its evolution from its launch in 1999 to becoming one of the largest ETFs today. It highlights key milestones such as reaching significant assets under management (AUM), changes in sponsorship and naming, and its consistent outperformance compared to the S&P 500. QQQ is presented as a proven way for investors to gain exposure to highly innovative, non-financial companies listed on the Nasdaq-100 Index.

title: ABM General Services lays off more than 100 workers at Northeast Ohio Amazon facility
summary: ABM General Services Inc. has laid off all 127 employees who worked at Amazon's fulfillment center in North Randall, Ohio. This development affects workers in the Northeast Ohio region. The article, however, does not specify the reasons behind these layoffs.

title: Northrop Grumman awards 70 suppliers for delivering excellence
summary: Northrop Grumman recognized over 70 suppliers for their outstanding contributions in 2023 across four categories: Performance Excellence, Quality Excellence, Strategic Excellence, and Resilience Excellence. These awards highlight the crucial role of strong supplier relationships in achieving performance, quality, and technology leadership for customer mission success. The company emphasized its partners' dedication to speed, affordability, and overall excellence.

title: More Canadians ditch traditional TV as streamers are ‘winning the battle’: report
summary: A new report indicates a significant shift in Canadian television viewing habits towards streaming platforms and away from traditional cable and satellite. By the end of 2023, 42% of Canadian households had no traditional TV subscription, a figure expected to reach 50% by 2026. Conversely, over 80% of households subscribe to a streaming service, with streaming revenue increasing while traditional TV revenue declined, prompting calls for regulatory changes from Canadian broadcasters to level the playing field.

title: S&P Global Commodity Insights and Amazon Web Services Empower Digital Transformation in Upstream Oil & Gas Industry
summary: S&P Global Commodity Insights and Amazon Web Services (AWS) are collaborating to accelerate digital transformation in the upstream oil and gas industry. This partnership will provide energy companies with access to comprehensive upstream data on AWS Data Exchange and facilitate the development of cloud-based AI and machine learning tools, as well as access to S&P Global Commodity Insights' Kingdom™ Geoscience Software. The initiative aims to enhance decision-making, efficiency, and data interoperability for energy companies, supporting both traditional operations and the energy transition.

title: POET Enters Optical Module Market with 800G Transceiver for Artificial Intelligence Networks
summary: POET Technologies Inc. announced its entry into the optical module market with the Wavelight™, an 800G pluggable transceiver designed for artificial intelligence and cloud data centers. This marks a significant commercial milestone for the company, showcasing its POET Optical Interposer™ technology. The Wavelight modules will be debuted at OFC 2024, with customer sampling beginning in Q3 2024, targeting a rapidly growing market for high-speed transceivers.

title: News | Amazon's HQ2 Takes Shape at Metropolitan Park
summary: Amazon's HQ2 in Arlington, Virginia, is taking shape with the opening of Metropolitan Park, the first phase of its sprawling redevelopment. This project, recognized with a CoStar Impact Award, includes two 22-story office towers, retail, and public spaces designed with sustainability in mind. Judges lauded the development for its positive impact on the community, cutting-edge architecture, and the creation of new jobs and amenities.

title: A new product allows 5x leveraged bets on the Magnificent Seven. Traders should be wary.
summary: A new exchange-traded product allows investors to make 5x leveraged bets on the Magnificent Seven group of stocks, raising concerns among analysts. While levered exchange-traded products are gaining popularity, traders are warned about the significant risks associated with such highly leveraged instruments. ETF analyst Todd Sohn from Strategas Securities notes that this product appears to be the most heavily levered available in any developed market.

title: PREORDER! Transformers X Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Party Wallop Official Images & Details
summary: Hasbro has officially revealed the new Transformers x Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Party Wallop figure, available for preorder at $49.99. This unique collaborative figure transforms from the iconic TMNT Party Wagon into a robot and includes four swappable heads, allowing collectors to customize it to represent Donatello, Michelangelo, Leonardo, or Raphael. The figure is set to be released on July 21, 2024, and comes with various ninja weapon accessories.

title: This Is How Much Money Amazon Makes Every Minute
summary: Amazon, a colossal conglomerate that started as an online bookseller, generated nearly $575 billion in revenue last year, making an average of $1,093,588.28 per minute. After accounting for expenses, the company's net income for 2023 was $30.43 billion, translating to $57,886.23 in profit every minute. Amazon's stock has seen remarkable growth since its early days, with shares trading near all-time highs and benefiting from strong analyst recommendations.

title: Pixie Dust Technologies “kikippa” Speaker Ranked First on the Rakuten Sound Bar Ranking
summary: Pixie Dust Technologies' "kikippa" speaker achieved the top position on the Rakuten Sound Bar Ranking in February 2024. The speaker, which processes and outputs "gamma wave sound" with 40Hz modulation, is widely distributed on major e-commerce platforms. This recognition on Rakuten, a prominent Japanese online marketplace, significantly boosts the product's visibility and market acceptance.

title: Esports Investing: Opportunities & Strategies
summary: Esports is a rapidly growing industry with significant investment opportunities despite its relative newness. Investors can gain exposure through companies involved in game development, streaming platforms, hardware/software manufacturing, or through dedicated esports ETFs. While growth prospects are strong, investors should be prepared for volatility and perform thorough due diligence.

title: F5 Networks (FFIV) Appoints Kunal Anand as Chief Technology Officer
summary: F5, Inc. announced the appointment of Kunal Anand as Executive Vice President and Chief Technology Officer (CTO), alongside Lyra Schramm as Executive Vice President and Chief People Officer (CPO). Anand will be responsible for F5's technology vision, leveraging his expertise in cybersecurity and data to accelerate the company's leadership in application and API security. Schramm will focus on driving people initiatives and fostering an innovative workplace culture.

title: Why The Honest Company believes their customers are 'brilliant'
summary: Carla Vernón, CEO of The Honest Company, emphasizes transparency and treating customers as "brilliant" by informing them of price changes and offering trustworthy products. The company, co-founded by Jessica Alba, saw a 10% revenue increase in Q4 2023, attributing success to its "Honest Standard" and commitment to clean, sustainably designed personal-care items. Vernón discusses her role as a female Afro-Latina CEO, her high standards, and the company's strategic focus on data-driven decisions and financial discipline to grow responsibly and meet consumer needs.

title: Spotify to raise prices of plans in some markets, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Spotify is reportedly planning to increase its subscription prices by $1 to $2 per month in several markets, including the UK, Australia, and Pakistan, by the end of April. Prices in the United States are expected to rise later in the year, alongside the introduction of a new basic tier offering music and podcasts but no audiobooks. This move aims to boost profitability for the company, which forecasts strong subscriber growth and is diversifying its offerings beyond music.

title: Alger Launches 2 Transparent Actively Managed ETFs
summary: Fred Alger Management has launched two new transparent actively managed ETFs: the Alger AI Enablers & Adopters ETF (ALAI) and the Alger Concentrated Equity ETF (CNEQ). ALAI focuses on companies involved with artificial intelligence, while CNEQ invests in large-cap growth companies, with a significant allocation to technology. These additions bring Alger's ETF family to five funds, building on their earlier entry into the ETF market in 2021.

title: Uber Freight hires CH Robinson, Amazon veterans
summary: Uber Freight has expanded its leadership team by hiring two seasoned transportation veterans: Dan Annunziata as head of commercial and Hany Elkordy as head of logistics and last mile solutions. Annunziata, formerly a VP at C.H. Robinson, will focus on unifying commercial and sales operations, while Elkordy, with experience from Amazon, Walmart, and Chewy, will lead parcel modality enhancements. These appointments follow other strategic hires as Uber Freight continues to enhance its competitive edge in the brokerage space.

title: Target rolls out Target Circle 360 paid membership
summary: Target has officially launched its new paid membership program, Target Circle 360, costing $99 annually. This program offers same-day delivery with Shipt on orders over $35, an extended return window of 30 days, and free two-day shipping. Target is running a promotional offer of $49 for the first year if customers sign up by May 18, with this price also applying to Target Circle Card holders for more than just the first year.

title: As Amazon's stock hovers near a record, its bonds are also looking attractive
summary: Amazon's stock is nearing an all-time high, but its corporate bonds are also presenting an attractive opportunity due to increased yields. These higher yields are attributed to movements in Treasury yields, not credit issues, as the bonds hold strong ratings from Moody's and S&P Global Ratings. The company has over $66 billion in outstanding bonds, with a significant portion maturing in 2027.

title: As Amazon's stock hovers near a record, its bonds are also looking attractive
summary: Amazon's bonds are becoming attractive to investors as their yields have increased since the beginning of the year, tracking Treasury yields. The company's bonds are highly rated (A1 by Moody's, AA- by S&P) and one specific note with a May 2028 maturity has seen its yield climb to 4.71%, offering a solid return. Amazon currently holds over $66 billion in outstanding bonds, with a significant portion maturing in 2027.

title: Prevention through Design (PtD) Award
summary: The Prevention through Design (PtD) Award, presented by NIOSH and partners, recognizes individuals, teams, and organizations for eliminating or reducing hazards through design efforts or contributing to the body of knowledge enabling PtD solutions. Nominations are due the first weekday of March and highlight successful implementations of PtD principles, with recent winners including Bruce Main and Jacobs Solutions in 2024. The article details the award criteria, nomination process, and provides examples of past awardees and honorable mentions, showcasing diverse PtD applications.

title: News | Macy's Burning Buyout Question: How Much Is the Retailer's Real Estate Worth?
summary: Macy's is facing a buyout attempt from investors Arkhouse Management and Brigade Capital Management, who are significantly interested in the retailer's extensive real estate portfolio. Analysts estimate Macy's property to be worth between $5 billion and $14 billion, with its Herald Square flagship alone valued over $1 billion. The activists' offer has increased to $6.6 billion, and discussions continue about a potential sale, with Macy's already taking steps to unlock real estate value, such as closing underperforming stores and exploring sales of flagship properties.

title: Amazon appears to be moving forward with long-planned delivery station
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has received approval to build a $73 million last-mile delivery station in Round Rock, an Austin suburb. The project is expected to create up to 200 jobs and construction is slated to begin by the end of the year, with completion within 12 months. The site may also include a future data center.

title: Implications of AI for cloud computing companies
summary: The article explores the significant impact of AI on cloud computing companies, noting that while AI has driven much of the equity market's recent gains, particularly for the "magnificent seven" tech companies, the broader economic benefits may take time to materialize. It highlights cloud computing as a key beneficiary, where AI integration can enhance operational efficiency, decision-making, cybersecurity, and personalized services. The piece also introduces the Fidelity Cloud Computing UCITS ETF as a means for investors to gain exposure to this theme.

title: Birmingham-area stocks pace gainers
summary: Birmingham-area stocks experienced moderate gains on Thursday, leading the local stock market. First US Bancshares, ProAssurance, and Warrior Met Coal were among the top performers. Amazon also saw a record high, reaching $189.05 a share.

title: Have a Fiver? 3 Under-$5 Stocks That Are About to Take Off
summary: This article highlights three under-$5 stocks — VerifyMe (VRME), CXApp (CXAI), and WISeKey (WKEY) — that are poised for significant growth. These companies are strategically capitalizing on emerging trends such as traceability platforms, AI-driven applications, and satellite-enabled IoT initiatives. The author details each company's financial performance, technological advancements, and strategic plans to demonstrate their potential for delivering strong returns.

title: Abbott Laboratories to close Bay Area site; almost 200 jobs to be cut
summary: Abbott Laboratories is closing its Fairfield, California location later this year, leading to the layoff of 195 employees. The company, known for brands like Similac and Pedialyte, attributed a decrease in its annual sales to an anticipated decline in COVID-19 testing-related sales, despite overall sales growth in the fourth quarter. Fairfield Mayor Cathrine Moy expressed frustration over the timing of the layoffs and the company's decision to discontinue the nutritional bar previously made at the plant.

title: Akamai expands cloud services with dedicated GPUs for media workloads
summary: Akamai Technologies Inc. has launched a new industry-optimized cloud service utilizing Nvidia RTX 4000 Ada generation GPUs, specifically designed for media workloads to enhance video encoding, decoding, and processing. This service aims to provide faster and more cost-effective solutions for media providers compared to standard virtual machines, leveraging Akamai's global edge network. Although optimized for media, the service is also capable of supporting other demanding tasks like generative AI training and inference.

title: Cloud Software Engineering Veteran Jeremy Ung Joins BlackLine as Chief Technology Officer
summary: BlackLine, a leader in digital finance transformation, has announced the appointment of Jeremy Ung as its new Chief Technology Officer, effective April 15. Ung, a cloud software engineering veteran with over 20 years of experience from companies like Apptio, Amazon Web Services, and Microsoft, will be responsible for guiding the technology direction of BlackLine's market-leading cloud platform. This strategic hire aims to enhance BlackLine's solutions through connected data and AI-powered platforms to meet the growing demand for automation and risk management in the Office of the CFO.

title: Amazon Prime Memberships in US Gain 8% to New High
summary: Amazon's Prime subscription service reached a new high of 180 million U.S. shoppers in March, an 8% increase from the previous year, according to Consumer Intelligence Research Partners. This growth indicates strong appeal for Amazon's fast delivery, despite inflation and increased online competition. Analysts project a 12% gain in Amazon's subscription services revenue for the period ended March 31.

title: J.P. Morgan Chase, Amazon, Wells Fargo among LinkedIn’s Top Companies of 2024
summary: J.P. Morgan Chase & Co., Amazon, Wells Fargo, Deloitte, and PwC lead LinkedIn's 8th annual Top Companies list for 2024, highlighting 50 best large workplaces in the U.S. The list is compiled using LinkedIn data to measure career success based on promotion rates and skill development. This year's honorees are recognized for their investment in employee experience, including upskilling initiatives and flexible working arrangements.

title: JEPI: Do Not Make The Same Mistake Two Years In A Row, Buy This Covered Call ETF Instead
summary: The article compares the 2023 and Q1 2024 performance of three S&P 500 covered call ETFs: JEPI, XYLD, and SPYI, highlighting their total returns and tax efficiency. It argues that SPYI consistently outperformed its peers due to its strategy of writing out-of-the-money covered calls and favorable Section 1256 tax treatment. The author advises investors to choose SPYI for better price appreciation, income, and tax efficiency, particularly given the S&P 500's continued rally.

title: Cisco debuts new AI-focused security system after $28 billion deal to buy Splunk
summary: Cisco launched HyperShield, a new AI-focused security product, after its $28 billion acquisition of Splunk, aiming to protect applications, devices, and data across various IT environments. The technology is designed to address the challenges posed by new digital AI assistants like ChatGPT and improve vulnerability patching times from days to minutes. This move is part of Cisco's strategy to become a serious AI player and boost its cybersecurity business as it faces competition in its traditional market.

title: How to Find Cloud Software Company Stocks to Trade and Invest
summary: This article provides guidance on investing in cloud computing stocks, detailing different types of cloud services (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) and key factors influencing stock performance like market demand and financial health. It offers investment strategies, including individual stocks (e.g., ServiceNow, Zoom) and ETFs (e.g., Global X Cloud Computing ETF), and lists major players like Amazon, Microsoft, and Adobe in the cloud software industry.

title: Hyperscale data center capacity to double every four years - report
summary: A new report by Synergy Research Group indicates that hyperscale data center capacity will double every four years for the next decade, driven primarily by demand for cloud services and AI tools. The number of active hyperscale facilities has surpassed 1,000 globally, with AI accelerating the scale of new data centers. The US accounts for 51 percent of worldwide capacity, while Amazon, Microsoft, and Google collectively represent 60 percent of all hyperscale data center capacity.

title: HanesBrands Announces Substantial Progress Toward Reaching Sustainability Goals Around People, Planet and Product
summary: HanesBrands Inc. announced significant progress toward its 2025/2030 sustainability goals in its 2023 Sustainability Summary Report, encompassing People, Planet, and Product pillars. The company has reduced greenhouse gas emissions by 53% since 2019 and aims for zero waste by 2025 and 100% renewable electricity by 2030. HanesBrands has also recognized substantial savings from its sustainability investments and advanced efforts to improve lives globally.

title: Amazon Introduces Low-Cost Grocery Delivery Subscription for Prime Members and Customers Using EBT
summary: Amazon has launched a new grocery delivery subscription in over 3,500 U.S. cities and towns. Prime members can subscribe for $9.99 per month, offering unlimited deliveries on orders over $35 from Whole Foods Market, Amazon Fresh, and other local retailers. Customers with EBT cards can access the same benefits for a discounted rate of $4.99 per month, with a free 30-day trial available.

title: Here’s a look at the world’s first civil space traffic coordination system
summary: The article discusses the development of the Traffic Coordination System for Space (TraCSS), the world's first civil space traffic coordination system. Managed by the U.S. Commerce Department's Office of Space Commerce (OSC) and running on AWS, this system aims to transfer U.S. space coordination from military to civil authority. The project emphasizes minimal government infrastructure and an agile acquisition process to define safety metrics without competing with commercial space situational awareness companies.

title: Graham Holdings moving headquarters to trophy high-rise building in Rosslyn
summary: Graham Holdings, the company that previously owned The Washington Post, is relocating its corporate headquarters to a 35-story trophy building at 1812 N. Moore Street in Rosslyn, Virginia. The company has signed a 15-year lease for the entire 21st floor of the building, which also houses Nestle USA's headquarters. This move highlights the strong demand for Class A and top-tier office spaces in the D.C. area, despite high overall vacancy rates.

title: Amazon fined by Italian antitrust regulator
summary: Amazon has been fined €10 million by Italy’s antitrust regulator for engaging in unfair business practices. The regulator alleged that Amazon's automatic "Subscribe and Save" function significantly restricted consumers' freedom of choice by pre-setting recurring purchases. Amazon plans to appeal the decision, asserting that the program helps customers save money and time on regular deliveries.

title: Newegg creates free membership program, offering perks to participants
summary: Newegg has launched Newegg+, a free customer membership program offering perks such as free shipping, expedited returns, and exclusive deals. The electronics retailer aims to enhance the shopping experience and gather customer feedback to expand the program in the future. This initiative aligns with the growing trend of loyalty programs among Top 1000 retailers, which are used to gather data and drive customer behavior.

title: Focus: UPS, FedEx transition to electric vans slowed by battery shortages, low supply
summary: UPS and FedEx are facing significant hurdles in electrifying their delivery fleets due to battery shortages, high EV prices, and the failure of some electric van manufacturers. This transition is crucial for President Biden's climate goals, but the companies also face reduced access to California's EV purchase vouchers, further complicating their efforts to replace gas-guzzling vehicles. Despite these challenges, both companies are exploring alternative fuel options and continuing their electrification plans.

title: Netgear Orbi 970 mesh router review: A groundbreaking mesh with huge potential for the future
summary: The Netgear Orbi 970 is reviewed as a very fast but expensive Wi-Fi 7 mesh system, offering phenomenal speeds over Wi-Fi 6 and multiple multi-gigabit Ethernet ports. While it possesses huge potential with Wi-Fi 7, critical drivers and software support are still maturing, meaning its full capabilities aren't yet realized. The article advises holding off on purchase for now due to the high cost and the technology's early stage, despite its strong performance.

title: CEOs of Microsoft, Nvidia and other tech giants join federal AI advisory board
summary: A federal AI advisory board has been established, comprising prominent tech executives, academics, and civil rights leaders, including CEOs from Microsoft, Nvidia, and OpenAI. Led by Secretary of Homeland Security Alejandro Mayorkas, the board will advise the Department of Homeland Security on the safe application of AI in critical infrastructure and issue recommendations for organizations like power grid operators and manufacturers. This initiative stems from President Biden's executive order on AI, which also mandates data sharing on new AI models' safety with the government.

title: Why I Just Added This Ultra-High-Yield Dividend ETF to My Retirement Account
summary: The author recently added the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) to their retirement account, aiming for consistent passive income and price appreciation with lower volatility. JEPQ offers a high dividend yield (9.2% over 30 days, 10.8% over 12 months) through a strategy of holding a high-quality equity portfolio and selling out-of-the-money call options on the Nasdaq-100 index. This approach is designed to generate monthly income and provide relatively steady returns, making it an attractive investment for retirement planning.

title: Fox’s New Ad-Sales Chief Will Tout Tom Brady, Super Bowl LIX in TV Upfront
summary: Fox's new ad-sales chief, Jeff Collins, plans to leverage the upcoming Super Bowl LIX broadcast and Tom Brady's debut as an NFL announcer to attract advertisers during the annual TV upfront market. Collins emphasizes Fox's strategy of focusing on being "best in class" and offering distinct audiences across its diverse media portfolio, including broadcast, sports, Fox News, and Tubi, to counter market pressures from streaming services. He anticipates strong demand for Super Bowl LIX ad slots, despite increasing competition in the advertising landscape.

title: How to Buy Alphabet (Google) Stock Online in 2025
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to buy Alphabet (Google) stock online in 2025, detailing steps, top brokers, and reasons to invest in the company. It covers Google's diverse services, its financial performance, business strategy, and historical stock information, emphasizing its growth and expansion into new technologies like AI. The article also addresses common FAQs regarding Alphabet stock.

title: Amazon’s stock inches up as cost-cutting measures and AI investment boost AWS profits
summary: Amazon's stock increased after reporting better-than-expected first-quarter earnings, driven by strong performance in its cloud computing unit, Amazon Web Services (AWS), and its advertising business. The company's profitability soared due to extensive cost-cutting measures and significant investments in AI, particularly in AWS, which has now become a $100 billion annual business. Despite these successes, Amazon remains the only trillion-dollar market cap internet company not to issue a dividend.

title: Amazon sales surge as company trains focus on artificial intelligence
summary: Amazon reported record first-quarter sales, with revenue rising 13% to $143.3 billion and profit surging to $10.4 billion, exceeding Wall Street expectations. This growth was largely driven by a renewed focus on artificial intelligence, particularly in its cloud-computing unit, Amazon Web Services (AWS), which saw sales increase over 17%. CEO Andy Jassy highlighted AI as a critical building block for Amazon's future growth, aiming to generate "tens of billions of dollars" of revenue from it in the coming years.

title: Amazon signs lease for building at new Kannapolis industrial park
summary: Amazon has leased an industrial building of just over 198,000 square feet at a new industrial park under construction in Kannapolis, Charlotte region. This move signifies Amazon's continued expansion in the area. Related articles discuss other projects and developments by Amazon and industrial space in the region.

title: AEP faces 15 GW of new load, driven by Amazon, Google, other data centers: interim CEO Fowke
summary: American Electric Power (AEP) is facing a substantial increase in electricity demand, primarily driven by data centers from companies like Amazon and Google, projected to reach 15 GW by 2030. Interim CEO Ben Fowke noted this load growth is sparking discussions in Ohio about utilities potentially owning power plants again and highlighted AEP's intent to sue the EPA over new environmental regulations. The company anticipates requiring significant capital investments in transmission and generation to meet this rising demand while keeping rates affordable.

title: Industrial Same-store NOI Saw Highest Increase of Reporting Sectors Through 2023
summary: The industrial REIT sector demonstrated strong operational performance through 2023, with year-over-year same-store net operating income increasing by 7.8%, the highest among reporting sectors. This growth occurred despite a softening in fundamentals like net absorption, driven by robust demand, especially from e-commerce giants like Amazon. Industrial REITs maintained high occupancy rates and attractive pricing compared to private real estate, indicating a favorable investment position.

title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy broke labor law with union remarks, judge rules
summary: A US National Labor Relations Board judge ruled that Amazon CEO Andy Jassy violated federal labor law with comments made in 2022 regarding the downsides of unionization. The judge stated that Jassy's remarks, which implied slower progress and bureaucracy with a union, lacked an objective basis and suggested that Amazon should be required to post a notice informing employees of their rights. Amazon disputes the ruling and plans to appeal, while the Amazon Labor Union celebrated the decision.

title: Dominion connected 15 data centers totaling 933MW in Virginia in 2023, 15 more expected in 2024
summary: Dominion expects to connect 15 more data centers to the grid in Virginia in 2024, after connecting 15 facilities totaling 933MW in 2023.
