This move comes despite warnings from some experts about potential employee dissatisfaction and signaling employer mistrust regarding all-or-nothing work arrangements.

title: EXEC: Hydro Flask Adds Velocity with Expanding Amazon, Target and Costco Programs
summary: Helen of Troy Limited, parent company of Hydro Flask, Osprey, and OXO brands, reported positive momentum in its Home & Outdoor segment for Q3 fiscal year 2025, partially offsetting declines in Beauty & Wellness. Hydro Flask saw significant distribution gains at Target and Costco, alongside strong e-commerce performance, including being Amazon's #1 Best Seller during Prime Day. The company also detailed its Project Pegasus restructuring plan, aimed at improving operating margins, and provided an updated fiscal 2025 outlook including the acquisition of Olive & June.

title: Lumen’s Finke Charts Network, AI Intersections
summary: Josh Finke, Lumen's Senior Vice President, Public Sector, discusses how Lumen Technologies is supporting Federal government agencies with network modernization and AI integration. The company is focusing on simplifying technology consumption, enhancing network security and reliability, and building an "AI fabric" to support data-intensive AI workloads. Finke also shared Lumen's perspective on Federal AI policy, emphasizing a multi-phase approach to AI adoption for measurable outcomes.

title: Macy's closing 66 stores, including four in Michigan
summary: Macy's Inc. announced it will close 66 underperforming stores as part of its "Bold New Chapter" strategy, aiming for sustainable growth. Four Michigan locations, including those in Traverse City, Sterling Heights, Troy, and Flint, are among the closures. This move reflects a broader trend of declining department store interest and increased online shopping, according to retail experts.

title: JPMorgan tells staff to return to the office five days a week
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has mandated that all employees return to the office five days a week starting in March, ending its hybrid work policy. While some locations currently lack the capacity, the bank aims to accommodate all employees, confirming feasibility by the end of the month. This move aligns JPMorgan with other major companies like Amazon and Walmart, despite acknowledging that not all employees will agree with the decision.

title: Why Have Dividend Stock Strategies Been Underperforming?
summary: Dividend stock strategies significantly underperformed the broader market in 2024, with the Morningstar Dividend Composite Index rising 15.7% compared to the Morningstar US Market Index's 24.1% gain. This underperformance is primarily attributed to the low exposure to technology stocks within dividend strategies, as tech and communication services were the best-performing sectors. Additionally, many top market contributors like Nvidia, Apple, and Amazon have low or no dividend payouts, favoring share repurchases as a way to return cash to shareholders, which dividend indexes typically do not capture.

title: What’s new with Matter: how Matter 1.4 is reshaping interoperability and energy management
summary: Matter 1.4 is the latest update to the smart home interoperability standard, introducing support for new device types like solar panels, heat pumps, and water heaters, alongside enhanced energy management features. This update also streamlines the multi-admin experience, allowing users to connect devices to several smart home systems more efficiently. While adoption is gradual, Matter 1.4 further solidifies the protocol's goal of creating a unified and user-friendly smart home ecosystem.

title: Premier Protein® Launches NEW Line of Indulgent High Protein Shakes
summary: Premier Protein has launched a new line of "Indulgence" high protein shakes, offering four dessert-inspired flavors: Decadent Dark Chocolate, Salted Caramel Truffle, Chocolate Covered Strawberry, and White Chocolate Raspberry. Each shake contains 30g of protein, 3g of sugar, and 170 calories, aiming to satisfy sweet cravings while supporting health and wellness goals. The new shakes are available at Walmart and Amazon, with more information on premierprotein.com.

title: Energy Vault Shares Rise On NuCube Partnership For Modular Nuclear Reactors: Retail Applauds Carbon-Free Energy Push
summary: Energy Vault Holdings Inc. (NRGV) saw its stock jump over 9% following an announcement of a partnership with NuCube Energy. This collaboration aims to accelerate the deployment of NuCube’s small, modular microreactor, NuSun, designed to power industrial and data-center applications, by late 2028 or early 2029. Energy Vault will contribute expertise in material science and integrate its VaultOS platform to manage energy storage and the NuSun hardware, addressing the growing demand for sustainable and reliable energy solutions for hyperscalers.

title: AI revolution: Impact Series
summary: The article discusses the significant infrastructure demands of the AI revolution, highlighting the strain on resources like power, land, water, and materials due to increasing AI adoption and model training. It emphasizes the need for responsible and sustainable development of AI, examining energy consumption by data centers and the growing demand for "firm" and "dispatchable" power sources like nuclear and geothermal energy. Authors Christian Keller, William Thompson, and Akash Utsav contributed to this Impact Series report from Barclays Research, stressing the global challenge of balancing economic opportunities with environmental sustainability in AI development.

title: Milka chocolate sold in Canada recalled for containing undeclared nuts
summary: Milka chocolate bars, specifically the Bubbly Alpine Milk product, have been recalled across Canada due to undeclared hazelnuts, posing a risk to those with allergies. The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) issued the recall, noting the product was distributed in several provinces and sold online. Mondelez International, owner of Milka, stated the product was not authorized for sale in Canada by their Canadian entity and attributed the issue to an incorrect label applied by an unauthorized distributor.

title: MicroStrategy Sets Sights on Amazon and Alphabet’s Common Shares Dominance
summary: MicroStrategy is seeking shareholder approval to dramatically increase its authorized Class A common shares from 330 million to 10.3 billion, a move that would position it with more shares outstanding than many Nasdaq 100 tech giants. This expansion is designed to fuel its aggressive Bitcoin acquisition strategy, which has already seen its stock surge over 2,500% since 2020. Despite potential dilution concerns, investors appear to prioritize the company's Bitcoin-centric growth.

title: Amazon Adds Teladoc Health to Health Benefits Program
summary: Amazon has expanded its Health Benefits Connector by adding Teladoc Health for cardiometabolic support. This program links Amazon customers to digital health companies offering various services, with Amazon promoting Teladoc Health's services for diabetes, hypertension, pre-diabetes, and weight management. While this partnership offers a new access point for Teladoc Health's services, the company remains cautious about its immediate revenue impact.

title: 3 Fidelity ETFs You Can Buy and Hold Forever to Generate $100,000 in Yearly Dividend Income, Starting in 2025
summary: This article examines three Fidelity ETFs that could generate significant dividend income. It details the Fidelity International Value Factor ETF (FIVA), the Fidelity International High Dividend ETF (FIDI), and the Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF (FYEE), outlining their dividend yields, expense ratios, holdings, and potential risks. The piece calculates the approximate investment needed in each ETF to achieve $100,000 in annual dividend income.

summary: Spotify is showing strong profitable growth, marked by record-high gross margins, solid cash flow, and strategic expansions into various audio content. Despite a high Price-to-Sales ratio, its rapid revenue growth, improved profitability, and free cash flow justify its premium valuation. Key drivers include increased ad revenue, international market expansion, and advanced ad-targeting tools, though risks like licensing costs, competition, and regulatory scrutiny remain.

title: Oracle Named a Leader in the 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud Database Management Systems
summary: Oracle has been recognized as a Leader in the 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud Database Management Systems, highlighting its converged database architecture and continuous innovation. The company's database services, including Oracle Autonomous Database and Oracle Database 23ai, offer flexible intercloud and multicloud deployment choices, enabling customers to leverage various data and application development models for enterprise data and AI workloads. Oracle Database is lauded for its rapid pace of innovation and industry-leading multicloud capabilities, now available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

title: Sonos takeover speculation grows, with Spotify or Amazon tipped as potential suitors (report)
summary: Speculation is mounting that audio technology company Sonos could be acquired following a problematic app rollout in 2024 and recent leadership changes, including the departure of CEO Patrick Spence. Bloomberg's Mark Gurman identifies Spotify and Amazon as the most likely suitors. While Amazon could integrate Sonos' premium audio into its Echo devices, Spotify could gain immediate entry into the hardware market, despite its past struggles with products like the "Car Thing."

title: As Netflix stock surges, an ‘anti-ESPN’ sports strategy emerges
summary: Netflix's stock is surging due to its "anti-ESPN" sports strategy, focusing on high-profile, "can't-miss" special events rather than regular-season packages. The company's successful Christmas Day NFL games and Paul-Tyson boxing match significantly boosted subscriber numbers. With its substantial market cap and global audience, Netflix can strategically acquire premium sports rights like the Women's World Cup, potentially leading to future UFC or Men's World Cup deals, challenging traditional sports broadcasters.

title: IUSV: A Cost-Effective Value ETF With Strong Downside Protection And Growth Potential
summary: The iShares Core S&P U.S. Value ETF (IUSV) offers cost-efficient value investing with a portfolio of 700 U.S. large-cap and mid-cap value stocks and a low expense ratio of 0.04%. Its significant allocation to large-cap stocks and a 23% exposure to the technology sector, including Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, contribute to strong downside protection and growth potential. IUSV has demonstrated superior performance, outperforming its peer IWD with a 167.2% total return over the past decade and 597.5% since inception.

title: Walmart Boosts Regional Manager Pay As Retailers Struggle To Find Leaders
summary: Walmart is significantly increasing bonus and stock awards for regional store managers, with top performers potentially earning over $600,000, in response to difficulties retailers face in filling managerial roles. This move comes as the company also reduces perks for office staff, including phasing out remote work and restructuring health insurance plans. The article details the pay increases for market managers, contrasting it with tech giant layoffs and Walmart's recent financial performance.

summary: Amazon is set to close one of its Amazon Go convenience stores in Woodland Hills, California, by February 26, following a "portfolio assessment." This closure continues a trend that has seen Amazon reduce its Go store count by about 50% since early 2023, bringing the current total to 16 locations. Despite these closures, Amazon states it will continue to invest in its physical retail strategy and is attempting to find new roles within the company for the 10 employees affected by this closure.

title: How to Leverage AI for Innovation & Growth in Your Consumer-Facing Organization: 3 Real-world Use Cases
summary: This article discusses how consumer-facing organizations can leverage AI, particularly generative AI (GenAI), for innovation and growth. The piece presents three real-world use cases: personalized image creation, content creation, and "talk to your data" scenarios, demonstrating AI's potential to enhance customer experiences and operational efficiency.

title: Tech’s TikTok Dilemma: Follow the Law or President Trump? (Published 2025)
summary: A federal law banning TikTok in the U.S. has created a schism among American tech companies, forcing them to choose between complying with the law or adhering to President-elect Trump's executive order to pause its enforcement. Companies like Amazon, Apple, and Google have moved to comply with the ban, while others like Oracle, Akamai, and Fastly continue to support TikTok. This situation highlights the legal and political complexities surrounding the TikTok ban and the varying approaches taken by major tech firms.

title: Data Center Market to Grow by USD 535.6 Billion (2025-2029), Driven by Multi-Cloud Adoption and Network Upgrades, Report on AI's Impact on Market Trends - Technavio
summary: The global data center market is projected to grow by USD 535.6 billion between 2025 and 2029, achieving a CAGR of 15.6%. Previously, Wong led corporate VC initiatives at Honeywell, iRobot, and Rockwell Automation. At Amazon, he will focus on investments in customer fulfillment, logistics, supply chain technology, robotics, warehouse automation, transportation, and last-mile delivery for the $1 billion fund.

This app replaces the previous CVS Pharmacy app and will manage prescriptions, delivery options, immunization scheduling, and provide benefit details for eligible members. It aims to improve the healthcare experience by offering seamless access to various CVS operations and potentially open locked merchandise displays in stores.

title: West Coast owners optimistic about return-to-office policies
summary: Apartment REIT executives are optimistic about the performance of properties in San Francisco and Seattle, following major companies like Amazon and Salesforce implementing return-to-office mandates. This shift has led to increased demand for rental units in these cities, driving up touring activity and occupancy rates. Despite past struggles, particularly in San Francisco, the return-to-office trend, coupled with minimal new apartment construction, is expected to boost rental markets in 2025 and beyond.

title: FY25 Q2 - Press Releases - Investor Relations
summary: Microsoft announced strong second-quarter fiscal year 2025 results, driven by significant growth in Microsoft Cloud and AI. The company reported $69.6 billion in revenue, a 12% increase, and a 17% increase in operating income to $31.7 billion. Microsoft's AI business alone surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $13 billion, marking a 175% year-over-year increase.

title: AMZY ETF: No Longer An Attractive Entry Point (Rating Downgrade) (NYSEARCA:AMZY)
summary: The YieldMax AMZN Option Income Strategy ETF (AMZY) has delivered significant returns primarily through dividends since its last "Buy" rating, but its price has remained largely unchanged. This reassessment suggests caution and a potential shift in investment strategy despite the fund's past performance.

title: Publishing Market to Grow by USD 18.9 Million (2025-2029), Increase in Demand for Diversity in Content to Boost Revenue, Report on AI Impact on Market Trends - Technavio
summary: The global publishing market is projected to grow by USD 18.9 million between 2025 and 2029, driven by an increased demand for content diversity and a rise in self-published authors. The industry is adapting to digital transformation with new business models and technologies to enhance reader engagement and content delivery.

title: Atlassian’s shares surge 18% after it smashes earnings expectations in latest quarter
summary: Atlassian's shares surged 18% after the company exceeded earnings and revenue expectations in its fiscal 2025 second quarter. The collaboration software company reported adjusted earnings of 96 cents per share on revenue of $1.286.5 million, significantly outperforming analyst predictions. Atlassian also highlighted strong customer growth and strategic advancements in cloud scalability and partnerships, including an expanded collaboration with Amazon Web Services.

summary: This article features an interview with the CEO of Omada Health, Sean Duffy, discussing critical topics impacting the health tech industry such as initial public offerings (IPOs), Amazon's role in healthcare, and the rise of GLP-1 medications. The piece also includes mentions of other relevant healthcare news regarding legal actions, AI in prior authorization, and challenges faced by rural clinics.

title: BlackRock, Inc. Expands Stake in Apollo Global Management Inc
summary: BlackRock, Inc. has significantly increased its stake in Apollo Global Management Inc by acquiring an additional 12,008,761 shares, boosting its total holdings to 35,519,094 shares. This strategic move, valued at $165.16 per share, diversifies BlackRock's portfolio and strengthens its presence in the alternative investment management sector. Despite Apollo Global Management being currently considered significantly overvalued based on its GF Valuation, its strong profitability, growth metrics, and high market capitalization make it an attractive long-term investment.

summary: Goldman Sachs Asset Management foresees a robust technology sector in 2025, driven by AI momentum and a broadening of opportunities beyond the "Magnificent 7." Despite being modestly overvalued with a GF Value of $66.97, SS&C Technologies boasts a high GF Score of 91/100 and strong growth, attracting other prominent investors as well.

summary: Goldman Sachs Research anticipates a significant surge in global data center power demand, projecting a 50% increase by 2027 and up to 165% by 2030 compared to 2023, primarily driven by the expansion of artificial intelligence. This growth will strain infrastructure, necessitating substantial investment in the electric grid and potentially affecting supply-demand balance for data centers, with occupancy rates expected to peak around 95% in late 2026 before moderating. The market expansion presents diverse investment opportunities across hyperscalers, asset managers, utilities, and data center operators.

title: Is There An Opportunity With Amazon.com, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:AMZN) 37% Undervaluation?

The analysis uses a 2-stage DCF model, projecting future cash flows and discounting them. Despite the apparent undervaluation, the article emphasizes that valuation is only one aspect of investment analysis and encourages further examination of financial health, future earnings, and other business fundamentals.

title: 5 Charts on How Investors Are Addressing Race at Shareholder Meetings
summary: Investor engagement on race and ethnicity at shareholder meetings has evolved, with a significant increase in anti-DEI resolutions but consistently low support for them. While overall support for conventional resolutions addressing race has declined, there's a growing focus on workforce-related issues, aligning with broader human capital management trends. Requests for pay gap reports and anti-harassment efforts at major companies like Apple, Amazon, Nike, Oracle, and Tesla garnered significant shareholder backing in 2024.

title: China blacklists parent company of Calvin Klein, Tommy Hilfiger
summary: China has blacklisted PVH Corp., the parent company of Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger, placing it on its "Unreliable Entities List." This move is seen as a response to President Donald Trump's new tariffs on Chinese imports, escalating a trade war between the two countries. PVH Corp. stated its intention to work with Chinese authorities to resolve the situation.

title: AG Schwalb Secures $3.95 Million from Amazon to Resolve Lawsuit Over Stolen Tips Intended for Delivery Workers
summary: Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb announced a $3.95 million settlement with Amazon over allegations it misled DC consumers by diverting tips intended for Amazon Flex delivery drivers to cover its own labor costs. Amazon had already reimbursed drivers for misappropriated wages through a previous FTC settlement, but this new agreement requires additional financial penalties and mandates clear disclosure of how tips are used. The settlement aims to deter unlawful behavior and ensure transparency in tipping practices for consumers and fair compensation for workers.

title: Report Shows 22.2% Year-Over-Year Increase In Black Businesses, But Room For Improvement Remains
summary: A new report shows a 22.2% year-over-year increase in Black-owned businesses since 2019, with Atlanta, GA, remaining a primary hub. Despite this growth and increased support from companies following 2020's racial awakening, the number of Black-owned businesses is still disproportionately low compared to the Black population. The article also discusses potential shifts in Black business ownership due to changes in corporate diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) commitments, which could lead to both challenges and new growth opportunities.

title: International Business Machines (IBM): Among the Best Stocks to Invest In According to AI
summary: International Business Machines (IBM) is highlighted as one of the best stocks to invest in according to AI, ranking 10th on a list compiled by Insider Monkey. IBM's recent Q4 2024 earnings beat expectations, with strong performance in free cash flow, Generative AI bookings, and Red Hat's growth, leading analysts to maintain bullish views and raise price targets.

title: Trump appoints VP JD Vance to oversee TikTok negotiations as US ownership talks intensify
summary: President Donald Trump has reportedly appointed Vice President JD Vance to lead negotiations for TikTok's U.S. operations, as the app faces a ban under the Protecting Americans from Foreign Adversary Controlled Applications Act. Companies like Oracle, Microsoft, and Amazon are potential suitors, while TikTok is also proposing a joint venture with American investors to ensure data security. Meanwhile, Elon Musk has publicly stated he is not interested in acquiring TikTok.

Despite investor scrutiny and concerns about the speed of AI adoption, WPP has invested over $300m in its platform, integrating various AI and cloud solutions, and reshaping its business model by blurring traditional marketing silos. This beta experience, currently available to a subset of U.S. customers, aims to expand selection and convenience by linking users to external brand sites for purchases. If brands have "Buy with Prime" enabled, Prime members can still benefit from fast delivery and support for items bought off-Amazon.

title: DeepSeek引发美股剧烈动荡 “科技七巨头”今年盈利增速预计放缓

title: News | Amazon, WeWork extend office partnership to Dallas
summary: Amazon is expanding its office presence in North Texas through a new partnership with WeWork, taking over space in a McKesson-owned building in Irving, Texas. This move will accommodate hundreds of Amazon employees and represents an extension of their flexible office strategy, previously seen in New York City and Silicon Valley. The expansion comes as Amazon encourages its employees to return to the office five days a week, highlighting the company's need for more physical workspace.

title: Walmart takes over former Amazon space in Bellevue tower
summary: Walmart has leased former Amazon office space in Bellevue's Skyline Tower, expanding its local footprint. This move comes as Amazon exits the building, which is now nearly fully leased. The article highlights a rival company taking over Amazon's previous office area.

summary: Amazon, Exxon Mobil, and Microsoft have joined a new expert panel to enhance the voluntary carbon market's credibility. The initiative comes as political shifts occur and companies seek market-based solutions for climate change.

summary: As companies like Kroger, Amazon, and JP Morgan Chase implement return-to-office mandates, employees are pushing back, citing increased financial burdens like higher childcare and commuting costs. An economics professor notes that while Zoom proficiency has improved, only about 10% of workers remain fully remote.

The company also maintains a modest but consistently growing dividend with a safe payout ratio, further contributing to its total shareholder return.

title: Costco chairman reveals surprising luxury items that are selling out
summary: Costco's chairman, Hamilton "Tony" James, highlighted that the retailer successfully sells a surprising range of luxury items, from Porsches to Rolex watches, Dom Perignon, and gold bars. He noted that despite a generally average-income customer base, the presence of affluent members with double the average income allows Costco to move high-end products rapidly due to their appreciation for good deals. James also mentioned the recent quick sale of gold bars, questioning whether affluent individuals or those wary of the economy are driving these purchases.

title: Why Is Amazon Stock Down Today? AMZN Price Hits 2-Month Lows Amid High Price Predictions
summary: Amazon (AMZN) stock has fallen to two-month lows, currently at $216.58, despite strong Q4 2024 results, due to a Q1 2025 sales forecast below Wall Street expectations and concerns over a $100 billion spending plan for AWS and AI. Analysts remain bullish for the long term, with price targets ranging from $260-$280 for 2025 and a potential $3 trillion market cap by 2026, driven by growth in AWS, AI, and e-commerce despite short-term volatility and macroeconomic factors. The article provides a detailed analysis of Amazon's financial performance, technical indicators, and market factors influencing its stock price.

title: Earnings call transcript: Navitas Semiconductor Q4 2024 revenue miss hits stock
summary: Navitas Semiconductor reported its Q4 2024 earnings, with an EPS meeting expectations but revenue significantly missing forecasts ($18 million vs. $23.99 million). This shortfall led to a 16.95% after-hours stock drop. Despite this, the company maintains a strong cash position, anticipates a recovery starting in Q2 2025, and aims for positive EBITDA by 2026, driven by growth in data center, EV, and mobile markets.

title: Work-from-home warning as more bosses drag staff back into the office: ‘Domino effect’
summary: A "domino effect" is leading more Australian employers to mandate full-time office work, with nearly two in five requiring five days a week in the office. This trend, driven by companies like Amazon and Dell, is increasing the average required office days and reducing remote work flexibility. Despite some employee pushback and demands for higher salaries to compensate for commuting costs, employers are banking on improved office environments and career development to entice staff back.

title: Number Sense: Cracking the code behind Sprouts’ striking success
summary: Sprouts Farmers Market has shown remarkable financial success, with significant increases in same-store sales and stock price, contrasting with other grocers. This growth is attributed to its strategy of targeting health-focused shoppers willing to pay higher prices, shifting to smaller, more profitable store formats, and effectively expanding its private label offerings. Originally underperforming, Sprouts has successfully pivoted to achieve strong financial returns and investor confidence.

title: NEOS Nasdaq-100 Hedged Equity Income ETF (QQQH) Price, Holdings, & News
summary: This article provides detailed information about the NEOS Nasdaq-100 Hedged Equity Income ETF (QQQH), including its current price, key statistics, overview, and expense ratios. It also lists recent news headlines related to QQQH and answers frequently asked questions about its performance and share activity. The ETF aims to generate current income with downside protection by investing in Nasdaq-100 stocks combined with an options collar.

title: After waiting with bated breath, Wall Street absorbs Nvidia's latest earnings
summary: Nvidia's latest earnings report reveals $39.3 billion in revenue, a 78% increase for the quarter ending in January, surpassing analyst expectations. The chipmaker's performance is crucial for Wall Street due to its significant market valuation and central role in the AI sector. Despite strong revenue, Nvidia's forward-looking statements signaled slowing profit-margin growth, and concerns about sustaining the rapid pace of AI investment and recent economic slowdowns have impacted its stock.

title: Is Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) the Best Magic Formula Stock for 2025?
summary: This article examines Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) in the context of Joel Greenblatt's "Magic Formula" investment strategy, which uses earnings yield and return on capital. While AMZN ranks 4th on a list of best magic formula stocks for 2025, the article notes the strategy's historical performance, potential risks, and Greenblatt's own fund's recent underperformance against the S&P 500. It also highlights an analyst's positive outlook for AWS's generative AI revenue growth.

title: After Earnings, Is Walmart Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar maintains a fair value estimate of $58 per share for Walmart (WMT) and rates it as significantly overvalued following its fiscal first-quarter earnings report. Despite strong performance in key metrics like comparable sales and high-margin profit streams, underwhelming fiscal 2026 guidance led to a stock sell-off. Morningstar believes Walmart possesses a wide economic moat due to its brand, cost advantage, and extensive physical footprint, but faces risks from e-commerce competition.

title: Pearson and AWS Announce Collaboration to Unlock AI-Powered Personalized Learning for Millions of People Globally
summary: Pearson and Amazon Web Services (AWS) are expanding their collaboration to accelerate AI-powered personalized learning for millions globally. Building on a 13-year partnership, Pearson will significantly increase its use of AWS cloud infrastructure and Amazon Bedrock to enhance and scale its higher education products, virtual schools, and English-language learning. This initiative aims to make learning more effective, efficient, and accessible, providing AI-powered tools for educators and enriching content experiences for learners.

title: Progyny projects slower revenue growth after losing Amazon
summary: Progyny, a fertility benefits company, anticipates slower revenue growth in 2025 due to losing Amazon, its largest client, which accounted for 12% of its previous year's revenue. Despite this, Progyny reported strong 2024 earnings of $1.2 billion and saw its stock significantly increase on Thursday. The company also highlighted potential positive implications from a recent Trump administration executive order aimed at bolstering access to IVF treatments.

title: Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FYEE)
summary: The Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF (FYEE) holds an aggregate "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $29.02. This rating is based on 758 analyst ratings for 24 companies, which constitute 52.7% of the ETF's portfolio. Top holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, with NVIDIA showing a potential upside of 53.4%.

title: QQQX: 8%-Plus Yield, 9% Discount, Long-Term NAV Growth (NASDAQ:QQQX)
summary: QQQX is a closed-end fund that generates an 8%+ yield by selling covered calls on NASDAQ 100 stocks, aiming for attractive returns with reduced volatility. The fund boasts a 35% NAV/share increase since inception and currently trades at a 9.11% discount to NAV, providing regular quarterly distributions with potential tax advantages. Despite risks like price volatility and call option risks, QQQX is considered a speculative Buy for investors seeking high yield.

title: The 10 Stocks Hedge Funds Love—and Hate—the Most
summary: Hedge funds are favoring established blue-chip companies like Walmart, Philip Morris International, and Amazon.com, expecting them to continue generating strong returns. Conversely, they are showing disfavor towards clean-energy companies such as Enphase Energy and Albemarle in a volatile market environment. This indicates a shift in investment strategy, prioritizing stable performers over speculative growth in the current economic climate.

title: STMicroelectronics Expands Silicon Photonics
summary: STMicroelectronics has announced advancements in silicon photonics and bipolar CMOS (BiCMOS) technologies to meet the growing demand for high-speed, energy-efficient data transmission in AI and cloud computing. These innovations aim to enable 800Gb/s and 1.6Tb/s optical interconnects with improved power efficiency, with mass production planned for its facility in Crolles, France. The company is collaborating with partners like Amazon Web Services (AWS) to refine its PIC100 silicon photonics platform, positioning itself in a market projected to grow significantly, with silicon photonics anticipated to account for 60% of the optical transceiver market by 2030.

title: First Class Action Filed Under Washington’s MY Health MY Data Act Draws Parallels to Previous SDK Litigation
summary: The first class action lawsuit under Washington's MY Health MY Data Act (MHMDA) has been filed against Amazon, alleging improper collection and use of consumer location data via Software Development Kits (SDKs). The lawsuit, Maxwell v. Amazon.com, Inc., draws parallels to prior SDK class actions, but introduces MHMDA's broad private right of action for violations related to "consumer health data," which includes precise location information. Businesses are advised to be mindful of SDK use as more states enact privacy legislation and similar lawsuits are expected.

title: Kornit Apollo for American Classics Apparel
summary: American Classics Apparel, a major wholesaler of licensed music, film, and gaming apparel, has adopted the Kornit Apollo platform to transition from traditional screen printing to high-speed digital production. This move is aimed at meeting increasing customer demand and leveraging the expanding e-tailer marketplace. The integration of Kornit Apollo will enhance the company's speed, efficiency, and quality in delivering licensed apparel.

title: Philips just launched its first Roku OLED TV and it looks like a steal for PS5 owners
summary: Philips has introduced its first Roku OLED TV, the 65-inch Class 974 Series, which offers a 4K 120Hz refresh rate for $1,299. This model is presented as a cost-effective option for PS5 owners, providing premium gaming features at a lower price point than comparable LG or Sony OLED TVs, though it is currently exclusive to Sam's Club members. The TV includes Dolby Vision IQ, HDR10, Dolby Atmos, and AMD FreeSync Premium, making it a strong contender in the gaming TV market.

title: CISO Global Continues to Advance AI-Powered Security Solutions within Microsoft and Amazon Cloud
summary: CISO Global is advancing its AI-powered security solutions within Microsoft Azure and Amazon AWS ecosystems. The company successfully developed a Proof of Concept for integrating its Argo Secure platform with Microsoft Security Copilot, enhancing asset management and threat detection. Additionally, CISO Global's TIGRIS GRC solution is now available on the AWS Marketplace, offering cloud-based security assessment and compliance tools.

title: Armlogi Holding Corp. Announces Letter of Intent to Acquire Leopard Transnational Inc.
summary: Armlogi Holding Corp. (Nasdaq: BTOC) has announced a non-binding Letter of Intent to acquire Leopard Transnational Inc., a California-based logistics provider. This strategic acquisition aims to expand Armlogi's warehousing footprint by 360,000 square feet and enhance its e-commerce fulfillment capabilities, particularly benefiting cross-border e-commerce sellers and major online marketplaces. The deal, which involves Armlogi common stock and potential earn-out payments, is expected to be finalized within 60-90 days, pending due diligence and necessary approvals.

title: Polar announces new AI-ready data center powered by 100 percent renewable energy in Norway
summary: Polar has announced the construction of a new multi-phase, AI-ready data center in Tørdal, Norway, powered entirely by renewable hydroelectric energy. The first phase, DRA01, will provide 12MW of capacity and is expected to be operational in the second half of 2025, addressing the increasing demand for AI computing infrastructure in Europe. The facility leverages Norway's natural resources for energy efficiency and features advanced liquid cooling and high-density rack configurations optimized for AI applications.

title: Grayscale Investments LLC Grayscale Zcash Trust (ZEC) (ZCSH) reports earnings
summary: Grayscale Investments LLC Grayscale Zcash Trust (ZEC) (ZCSH) has filed its annual 10-K report for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024. The Trust aims to track the value of ZEC, holding approximately 2.4% of ZEC in circulation as of year-end, and the report details its operations, risks associated with digital assets, and the role of Grayscale Investments LLC as the Sponsor.

title: Defined Outcome ETFs: Good For Conservative Investors, Not Necessarily Conservative Investments
summary: Defined Outcome ETFs utilize options to provide a buffer against downside risk while capping potential upside, making them appealing to conservative investors. This article argues that these strategies are particularly effective when applied to volatile markets like China's internet sector, where they can offer higher upside potential compared to similar strategies for more stable assets such as the S&P 500 Index. The KraneShares 100% KWEB Defined Outcome January 2027 ETF (KPRO) and the KraneShares 90% KWEB Defined Outcome January 2027 ETF (KBUF) are highlighted as examples that apply this strategy to the volatile but high-growth China internet sector.

title: Coinbase to Debut Combined Equities & Crypto Index Futures
summary: Coinbase Derivatives is set to launch Mag7 + Crypto Equity Index Futures on September 22, offering the first US-listed derivative that provides combined exposure to traditional equities and cryptocurrency ETFs. This innovative product aims to provide thematic exposure to innovation and growth assets, diversification, and strategic risk management. The index will include the "Magnificent 7" stocks (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, META, TSLA), Coinbase Global Inc. (COIN), and cryptocurrency ETFs (IBIT, ETHA), with an even-weighting methodology and quarterly rebalancing.

title: Whole Foods at The Shops at Sun Vet to open earlier than planned, developer says
summary: The Shops at SunVet in Holbrook, formerly Sun Vet Mall, is over 70% leased and on track for completion this year, with Whole Foods Market opening earlier than expected in fall 2025 instead of spring 2026. This $93 million redevelopment by Regency Centers Corp. is transforming the struggling 1970s mall into an upscale open-air shopping center with tenants like Nordstrom Rack, Shake Shack, and Sephora. Mild winter weather allowed for accelerated construction, contributing to the earlier opening of Whole Foods.

title: ARM & HAMMER™ Laundry and Meghan Trainor Hit All the Right Notes with the 'Toss Like This' Laundry ‘Re-Trainor’ Campaign!
summary: ARM & HAMMER Laundry has partnered with GRAMMY® winning artist Meghan Trainor for a campaign called “Toss Like This,” aimed at simplifying laundry routines with ARM & HAMMER POWER SHEETS™ Laundry Detergent. Trainor transforms her hit song into a playful jingle to highlight the convenience and eco-friendliness of the new detergent sheets, which eliminate bulky plastic bottles and offer powerful cleaning. The POWER SHEETS are available on Amazon and Walmart, packaged in recyclable cartons and designed for easy use at home or on the go.

title: Ulta Beauty’s new CMO on balancing cultural pizzazz with socially minded initiatives
summary: Ulta Beauty's new CMO, Kelly Mahoney, discusses the brand's strategy of blending high-profile cultural marketing, such as Super Bowl and Golden Globes sponsorships, with a continued commitment to DEI initiatives and customer accessibility. Mahoney emphasizes the importance of authentic social media engagement and the strategic balance required to navigate economic uncertainties like tariffs while maintaining brand relevance and purpose.

title: Is It Time to Buy the Magnificent Seven?
summary: After a recent selloff in the US stock market, the valuations of the "Magnificent Seven" mega-cap tech stocks have decreased. Morningstar analysts now consider Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet to be undervalued, while Nvidia and Tesla are fairly valued, and Apple remains overvalued. Despite these changes, Morningstar strategists continue to recommend underweighting growth stocks, favoring a rotation into value stocks.

title: Druva debuts fully integrated support for Microsoft Azure
summary: Druva, a SaaS platform for data security and backup, has introduced integrated support for Microsoft Azure, expanding its services beyond its decade-long AWS exclusivity. This move allows Druva customers to backup Microsoft Azure and Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud VMs to Azure Storage, with further services planned through a strategic partnership with Microsoft. The development addresses the growing multi-cloud environment used by enterprises and positions Druva to meet broader market demands.

title: Is it time to buy the Magnificent Seven?
summary: The "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks have experienced a significant selloff, making some of them appear undervalued according to Morningstar's fair value metrics. While Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet are now rated as buys, Nvidia and Tesla are fairly valued, and Apple remains overvalued. Morningstar strategists still recommend underweighting growth stocks overall, favoring value stocks due to market volatility and slowing economic growth.

title: The maker of Roomba has ‘substantial doubt’ about surviving
summary: iRobot, the maker of Roomba, has expressed "substantial doubt" about its ability to continue as a company, according to its latest quarterly earnings report. This dire warning follows the collapse of a planned $1.7 billion acquisition by Amazon due to regulatory concerns. The company's stock plummeted 30% in premarket trading, and its board is now exploring strategic alternatives amidst mounting debt.

title: E-Commerce Automotive Aftermarket Size, Share | CAGR of 22.4%
summary: The global E-Commerce Automotive Aftermarket is projected to grow from USD 67.4 Billion in 2024 to USD 508.7 Billion by 2034, exhibiting a CAGR of 22.4%. This growth is driven by digitalization, increasing vehicle ownership, and consumer preference for online shopping, with North America currently dominating the market. Key trends include AI-powered recommendations, mobile-friendly platforms, omnichannel strategies, and the emergence of eco-friendly auto parts.

title: Roomba maker says 'substantial doubt' about future of company
summary: iRobot, the company behind Roomba vacuums, has issued a warning of "substantial doubt" about its ability to continue as a going concern, citing a significant revenue decrease and annual net loss in fiscal 2024. The company is evaluating a "broad range of alternatives" for its business, including debt refinancing and transactions, after facing aggressive competition and the termination of a proposed acquisition by Amazon. These challenges come even as iRobot launched new robot vacuum models and implemented an operational restructuring to focus on profitability.

title: Applied Optoelectronics Soars Following Share Purchase Agreement With Amazon
summary: Shares of Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) are experiencing a significant surge, climbing over 35 percent, following the announcement of a share purchase agreement with Amazon. Amazon.com, Inc. has been issued a warrant to acquire approximately 8 million shares, as disclosed in a recent SEC filing. This news has led to a boost in AAOI's stock price, which traded at $21.60 on Friday morning.

title: GE Vernova, Amazon Expand Effort to Address Surge in Global Energy Demand
summary: GE Vernova and Amazon Web Services (AWS) are expanding their collaboration to address rising global energy demand and advance decarbonization goals. GE Vernova will provide essential electrical equipment and construction support to AWS for its data center expansion, while also working on onshore wind development projects. AWS, in turn, will offer cloud services to GE Vernova, aiding its cloud migration, digital innovation, and the use of generative AI to optimize operations and accelerate technology development.

title: NEOS Nasdaq-100 Hedged Equity Income ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (QQQH)
summary: The NEOS Nasdaq-100 Hedged Equity Income ETF (QQQH) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" based on analysis of its holdings. MarketBeat calculates this rating and a price target by considering Wall Street analyst ratings for the individual stocks within the ETF's portfolio. The aggregate price target for QQQH is $53.32, with its top holdings including NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon.com.

title: Western Digital unveils 26TB 3.5-inch Red Pro SATA NAS HDD
summary: Western Digital has launched a new 26TB 3.5-inch WD Red Pro hard drive (WD260KFGX) designed for network-attached storage (NAS) devices. This new drive uses conventional magnetic recording (CMR) technology, offering high capacity and reliability with a 2.5-million-hour mean time between failure (MTBF) rating and 550 TB per year of data transfers. It is priced at $569.99 and is optimized for demanding, always-on NAS usage, including RAID settings, with anti-vibration technology and OptiNAND.

title: F5 CEO: AI Is Sparking Cloud Repatriation Movement
summary: F5 CEO François Locoh-Donou states that enterprises are increasingly repatriating application workloads from the cloud due to rising costs and data sovereignty concerns, with AI accelerating this trend. An IDC report suggests 80% of organizations will see some level of repatriation within the next year, indicating a shift towards hybrid and multi-cloud strategies rather than full cloud adoption. This movement creates significant opportunities for partners to help businesses manage and secure applications across diverse environments efficiently.

title: Forever No More. Operator of mall staple Forever 21 files for bankruptcy protection
summary: Forever 21 has filed for bankruptcy protection for a second time, announcing plans to close its U.S. business due to declining mall traffic and aggressive competition from online retailers. F21 OpCo stated it would wind down U.S. operations under Chapter 11, exploring partnership or asset sale options, while international stores continue to operate normally. The company cites competition benefitting from the "de minimis" tax exemption as a significant factor in its inability to find a sustainable path forward.

title: AWS and Accenture to bring cloud skills training to KSA and UAE
summary: AWS and Accenture have partnered to launch the AWS Cloud Institute in Saudi Arabia and the UAE, aiming to address the global shortage of skilled cloud professionals. This initiative utilizes Accenture's LearnVantage platform to deliver comprehensive training and certifications, preparing individuals for careers as cloud developers within approximately nine months. The program includes digital learning, instructor-led sessions, interactive labs, and preparation for AWS certifications, building upon AWS's significant investments in the Middle East.

title: Adobe Announces New Offerings with AWS and Amazon Ads for Marketers and Creatives to Deliver Impactful Customer Experiences at Scale
summary: Adobe has announced a strategic collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Amazon Ads to enhance customer experience orchestration. This partnership aims to provide marketers and creative teams with new tools and integrations, leveraging AWS's cloud services, generative AI, and Amazon Connect, to deliver personalized customer experiences at scale. The offerings include enhanced omnichannel experiences, AI-driven interactions, boosted marketing performance, and streamlined creative workflows through integrations with Adobe Experience Platform, Amazon Marketing Cloud, and Adobe Creative Cloud.

title: Eschew Magnificent Seven Stock-Picking With This ETF
summary: This article suggests that investors consider actively managed ETFs like the Invesco Top QQQ ETF (QBIG) instead of trying to pick individual Magnificent Seven stocks. The ETF focuses on the Nasdaq-100 Mega Index, which includes the Magnificent Seven plus Broadcom, offering flexibility in the current market. The article highlights that the Magnificent Seven's valuation is at an eight-year low relative to the S&P 500, with companies like Amazon and Alphabet presenting strong potential for growth.

title: Target, Amazon boycotted after DEI rollbacks. Where do NJ corporations stand on DEI?
summary: Consumers are initiating boycotts against major corporations like Target and Amazon following their decisions to roll back Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) policies, with further boycotts planned for other companies like Nestlé, Walmart, General Mills, and McDonald's. The article also examines the DEI policies of South Jersey corporations, specifically detailing the robust DEI commitments made by Campbell's Co. and Subaru of America Inc., both based in Camden. These companies demonstrate their commitment through various initiatives, employee resource groups, and community partnerships.

title: MSCI World ETFs too volatile for you? Three investment alternatives
summary: This article explores alternatives to MSCI World ETFs for investors concerned about their volatility or heavy US stock concentration. It highlights three specific ETF options: MSCI World ex USA, MSCI World Equal Weighted, and MSCI USA ex Mega Cap Select, detailing their compositions and past performance. The piece also advises investors on maintaining a calm approach during market fluctuations, recommending against impulsive selling or excessive buying.

title: 4 tech giants look cheap ahead of earnings
summary: Ahead of their earnings reports, four major tech companies—Apple, Amazon, Facebook, and Microsoft—are considered undervalued. Analysts see Apple as undervalued despite recent revenue guidance adjustments, Amazon as continuing its impressive growth, Microsoft showing broad-based strength, and Facebook maintaining its competitive advantages in digital advertising despite recent controversies.

title: How AWS opened the floodgates for modern day cloud computing
summary: In 2006, Amazon Web Services (AWS) launched its first service, Amazon S3, pioneering the concept of scalable, cost-effective data storage and effectively creating the cloud computing industry before the term even existed. Initially met with some skepticism, AWS rapidly grew, introducing services like EC2, and distinguishing itself through a first-mover advantage, a "customer-obsession" approach, and consistent price reductions. Today, AWS maintains market leadership but faces new challenges in the AI race, where competitors like Microsoft and Google have made significant investments, prompting AWS to focus on long-term AI innovation and in-house chip development.

title: The 10 best global wide-moat stocks of 2020
summary: This article reviews the top 10 best-performing global wide-moat stocks of 2020, all of which returned over 50%. While none are currently screaming buys, four are fairly valued and make good watchlist candidates. The article provides a brief overview of Veeva Systems, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, Amazon.com, and Tencent Holdings, highlighting their competitive advantages and future growth prospects according to Morningstar analysts.

title: Lands' End's retail cliffhanger - is there time to reverse away from the edge and into an omni-channel future?
summary: Lands' End is exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale or merger, as CEO Andrew McLean works to boost e-commerce and personalization despite declining sales. The company has a history of struggling to adapt to the online retail landscape, having been spun off from Sears in 2013, and faces challenges in its physical store presence and partnerships. McLean highlights efforts in marketing, AI-driven personalization, updated websites, and third-party collaborations, while also re-evaluating its traditional catalog strategy as a personalized marketing tool.

title: Equities in 2025: Embracing a Broader Landscape
summary: Goldman Sachs Asset Management foresees a broadening landscape of equity market opportunities for active investors in 2025, moving beyond the dominance of US mega-cap tech. The report highlights potential in small-cap companies, developed markets like Japan and Europe, and emerging markets such as India and specific sectors in China. Investors are encouraged to pursue diversified, active strategies and explore long-term themes like reshoring and increased energy demand.

title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Among the 10 Goldman Sachs Tech Stocks to Buy
summary: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) is listed among Goldman Sachs' top 10 tech stocks to buy, despite recent market volatility affecting the tech sector. The company reported strong financial results for 2024, with net sales growing 11% year-over-year, and plans significant capital expenditures in AI and AWS for 2025. Analysts maintain a bullish outlook on AMZN, with a consensus price target suggesting substantial upside potential.

title: Amazon, Tesla and 18 other US companies that bagged maximum H-1B visas in 2024
summary: Amazon and Tesla, along with numerous other US companies, were among the top recipients of H-1B visa approvals in 2025, according to USCIS data. Amazon led with 9,265 approvals, followed by Cognizant and Google, while Indian IT giants like Infosys and TCS also secured a significant number of visas. These companies rely heavily on skilled foreign workers for specialized roles in technology, engineering, research, and development.

title: Can Nuclear Energy Help Big Tech Meet Data Center Needs?
summary: The growing electricity demands of data centers, particularly due to AI, are leading Big Tech companies to consider nuclear energy, including small modular reactors (SMRs). While wind and solar are carbon-free, they don't provide the consistent power required, and natural gas, though quick and cheap, has carbon emissions. However, SMRs are expensive and take a long time to come online, posing challenges for utilities trying to meet this new demand while managing costs and timelines.

title: EJH Share Surge: An Unexpected Upside?
summary: E-Home Household Service Holdings Limited (NASDAQ: EJH) saw its stock surge 16.18% on Wednesday, reportedly due to a new partnership announcement in the home services sector. The article details EJH's financial performance, including a closing price of 1.185, a Price-to-Sales ratio of 0.13, and significant total assets with no massive debts. Investors are debating whether this uptick is a fleeting rally or a strategic opportunity, with analysts emphasizing careful evaluation over hasty decisions.

title: What is the dividend yield of the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV)?
summary: The article discusses the dividend yield of the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV), noting its excellent capital returns over the past five years driven by US tech giants, despite a relatively low dividend yield of 1.01% as of February 2025. It emphasizes that while IVV isn't ideal for large investment income, its overall net return and low management fee make it attractive for long-term growth due to its exposure to strong US businesses benefiting from trends like digitalization and AI.

title: Amazon Autos wants to sell used cars—here’s what it can’t screw up
summary: Amazon Autos is planning to expand its platform to include used car sales, building on its existing partnership with Hyundai for new car sales. The article highlights three critical non-negotiables for Amazon to succeed in this venture while aligning customer experience with dealer profitability: preserving dealer F&I profits, reinforcing the value of used car listings beyond just price, and minimizing dealer exposure to return policies. The success hinges on Amazon understanding and supporting the complex mechanics that make used car retailing profitable for dealerships.

title: PLDT Strengthens Digital Growth with Data Center Expansion
summary: PLDT is heavily investing in data center expansion as a core part of its digital growth strategy in Southeast Asia. Through its subsidiary ePLDT, the company operates a network of VITRO data centers, including the largest in the Philippines, VITRO Sta. Rosa, which is set to become the nation's AI hub. PLDT is also preparing for an AI-powered future by offering GPU-as-a-Service, further solidifying its leadership in digital transformation.

title: Amazon Smile SALE: New Life FINAL" Underway! AI Gadgets Like Voice Recorder "PLAUD" and Smartwatch "Amazfit" Also on Sale
summary: Amazon is holding its "Smile SALE: New Life FINAL" until April 1, 2025, offering discounts on over a million items including AI gadgets. Featured products include the PLAUD NOTE voice recorder with AI transcription, various Amazfit smartwatches (Active 2, Balance with GPT-4o, and T-Rex 3), Dangbei projectors (Atom and DBOX02), and 1MORE audio products (SonoFlow Pro HQ51 headphones and Open Earbuds S51). The sale aims to support individuals starting new phases in life with useful tech for business, study, and leisure, and also includes a point-up campaign.

summary: STAG Industrial (STAG) is recommended as a buy due to its strong fundamentals, consistent dividend history, and resilience in a challenging interest rate environment for REITs. Despite a modest dividend increase, STAG's robust coverage rate and long history of payouts make it an attractive option for long-term income, with Wall Street analysts projecting potential for double-digit returns.

title: AI²: How radical innovations are revolutionizing tech and health care
summary: This article discusses the transformative impact of "AI²" — artificial intelligence (AI) and artificial incretins (GLP-1s) — on the technology and healthcare sectors. title: The 12 Best Pellet Grills, According To Reviews
summary: This article reviews the 12 best pellet grills on the market, categorized by features such as portability, budget-friendliness, and suitability for beginners or pros.
