The selection is based on comprehensive reviews from various trusted retail sites.

title: Abrdn Total Dynamic Dividend Fund Q4 2024 Commentary (AOD)
summary: The abrdn Total Dynamic Dividend Fund (AOD) underperformed its benchmark in Q4 2024, primarily due to stock selection and sector allocation, with Tesla, Amazon, and Meta Platforms being key detractors. Despite global equity market gains driven by optimism over Trump's policies, the fund maintains a cautious outlook given macroeconomic factors and recession risks. The investment team focuses on stock-level diversification and capital preservation.

title: The rise of analog travel & the age of disconnection
summary: In an increasingly digital world, a growing trend towards "analog travel" is emerging, focusing on disconnection, authentic interactions, and sensory engagement. While travelers seek tech-free experiences like digital detox retreats and slow travel, sophisticated technology invisibly operates behind the scenes to ensure seamless personalization, booking, and security, creating a balance between intentional unplugging and luxurious convenience. This approach leverages discretionary technology to serve guests without demanding their attention, defining the future of high-end hospitality.

title: YMAG ETF Holdings List — AMEX:YMAG
summary: This article provides a detailed list of the holdings for the YMAG (YieldMax Magnificent 7 Fund of Option Income ETFs) ETF as of February 27, 2026. The fund primarily invests in option income strategy ETFs for major tech companies like Amazon, Microsoft, Meta, Tesla, Google, Apple, and Nvidia, alongside small allocations to U.S. Dollar reserves and a Government Obligations Fund. Each holding's weight, share count, and market value are presented.

title: XLY ETF Holdings List — AMEX:XLY
summary: This article provides a detailed list of the top holdings for the XLY ETF (State Street Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF) as of February 27, 2026. It includes the weight percentage, number of shares, and market value for each company held within the ETF. Key holdings include Amazon.com, Inc., Tesla, Inc., Home Depot, Inc., and McDonald's Corporation, among others.

title: GPIQ ETF Holdings List — NASDAQ:GPIQ
summary: This article provides the current holdings list for the Goldman Sachs Nasdaq-100 Premium Income ETF (GPIQ) as of February 27, 2026. It details the top holdings, including NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, and Tesla, along with their respective weight percentages, number of shares, and market values. The data offers insights into the fund's composition and its major investments.

title: Salesforce on Its New Contact Center Integration Program & the Future of Service Cloud
summary: Salesforce is expanding its Service Cloud integrations to include deeper contact center capabilities beyond just voice, partnering with CCaaS vendors like Amazon Connect, Genesys, and Five9. This initiative, part of the "Bring Your Own Channel for CCaaS" program, aims to streamline agent experiences, centralize service data, and enhance AI innovation by allowing open integration with various contact center platforms. The company is also making Service Cloud more predictive and prescriptive through tools like Salesforce Customer Success Score and My Service Journey, which help customers identify and implement high-impact features and create tailored transformation roadmaps.

title: Rockwell Automation and AWS Collaborate to Transform Manufacturing Through Advanced Industrial Automation Solutions at Hannover Messe 2025
summary: Rockwell Automation and Amazon Web Services (AWS) are collaborating to accelerate the digital transformation of the manufacturing industry by combining Rockwell's operational technology with AWS's cloud services. This partnership aims to provide manufacturers with scalable, secure, and flexible cloud solutions to optimize asset performance, increase operational visibility, and deliver actionable insights from data. Key aspects of the collaboration include expanding Rockwell's FactoryTalk® Hub software-as-a-service offerings to AWS Marketplace and showcasing new cloud-enabled industrial solutions at Hannover Messe.

title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA): Our Calculation of Intrinsic Value
summary: This article presents a discounted cash flow (DCF) analysis for NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA), concluding that the stock is overvalued. Based on conservative assumptions, the intrinsic value is estimated at $36.07 per share, significantly below the current market price of $110.42, indicating a -67.34% margin of safety. The author acknowledges criticism regarding the conservative growth rate assumptions and provides clarification on the methodology.

title: Technology: Stocks Are Selling Off, Creating More Investment Opportunities
summary: The technology sector experienced a significant sell-off in the first quarter of 2025 after strong performance in 2024, leading Morningstar to view it as an attractive investment opportunity. Despite the downturn, secular tailwinds like cloud computing, AI, and semiconductor demand remain strong, with generative AI identified as a major growth driver. Morningstar highlights NXP Semiconductors, Adobe, and Microsoft as top picks, citing their strong market positions and potential for future growth.

title: Factor This Friday finance and development roundup: Invenergy, EDP Renewables, TotalEnergies, Energy Vault, Avangrid
summary: This article provides a roundup of recent financial and development news in the renewable energy sector, highlighting major projects and investments from companies like Invenergy, EDP Renewables, TotalEnergies, Energy Vault, and Avangrid. Key announcements include Invenergy's new solar and battery storage facility in Arizona, EDP Renewables launching a 100 MW solar park, TotalEnergies acquiring several renewable portfolios, Energy Vault securing financing for a hybrid hydrogen and battery storage project, and Avangrid bringing a solar project online in Ohio. The report underscores the continued growth and strategic developments within the renewable energy industry.

title: Rockwell Automation and AWS Expand Cloud Integration to Advance Industrial Manufacturing
summary: Rockwell Automation and Amazon Web Services (AWS) have announced an expanded collaboration aimed at accelerating digital transformation for manufacturers. This initiative integrates Rockwell Automation’s operational technology with AWS’s cloud services, offering secure and scalable solutions to improve asset performance, enhance visibility, and translate operational data into actionable insights. The partnership also includes new Software-as-a-Service offerings from Rockwell Automation on AWS Marketplace, such as DataMosaix™ and Fiix® CMMS, with additional tools expected to be added throughout the year.

title: AI's Potential for Supply Chain
summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) offers immense potential for optimizing supply chain processes and enhancing decision-making, yet many organizations struggle with successful, scalable deployment. To overcome these challenges, chief supply chain officers (CSCOs) need to define a clear AI strategy, create a common language for operations, prioritize use cases, and measure the value of their AI investments. Incorporating advanced AI approaches like Generative AI (GenAI), Composite AI, and Agentic AI can further accelerate this transformation, requiring continuous adaptation and cross-organizational support.

title: L3Harris, Kuiper Government Solutions Partner on Resilient S
summary: L3Harris Technologies (NYSE: LHX) and Kuiper Government Solutions LLC (KGS), a subsidiary of Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN), have partnered to deliver integrated, resilient Satellite Communications (SATCOM) solutions globally for governments and militaries. The collaboration aims to provide hybrid SATCOM with high-speed and low-latency connectivity by combining L3Harris's tactical networks and communication systems with KGS's global satellite network. This partnership is expected to enhance communication capabilities, resiliency, and security for various applications.

title: SPY Signal Says Patience as Tariff Turmoil Mounts
summary: The article discusses the significant market downturn following new tariffs, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite experiencing their worst daily percentage drops since 2020. It analyzes past SPY (SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust) performance after similar large pullbacks, suggesting that while initial reactions can be harsh, long-term stability may return, potentially by May 2025, if historical patterns hold. The analysis highlights that severe drops are often followed by bullish rebounds, even amidst significant negative events.

title: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest buys $13M tumbling tech stock, dumps Bitcoin ETFs
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest has been strategically buying shares of Coinbase, Amazon, and Nvidia, amounting to over $26 million, following President Donald Trump’s "reciprocal tariffs" announcement. Concurrently, the firm has divested $12 million from its ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF (ARKB). This move comes as tech stocks have stumbled and Bitcoin ETFs have experienced significant outflows.

title: Chunghwa Telecom to cooperate with US satellite firm
summary: Chunghwa Telecom announced a strategic cooperation with US-based Astranis Space Technologies Corp for satellite procurement to enhance network resilience against geopolitical risks and natural disasters. This move follows the approval of a satellite investment program by its board of directors and is part of a broader effort to secure more satellite bandwidth and ensure communication network stability. The collaboration with Astranis, a producer of small and low-cost telecommunications satellites, will complement Chunghwa Telecom's existing low and medium Earth orbit satellite services.

title: LeBron James is taking his talents to Mattel
summary: LeBron James has become the first professional athlete to have a Ken doll made in his likeness through a new collaboration with Barbie and the LeBron James Family Foundation. The "Kenbassador" doll, which is one inch taller than standard Ken dolls, features details reflecting James's career milestones and commitment to his hometown. It will be available for $75 at select retailers and online platforms.

title: Live Nation, Ticketmaster can’t shake antitrust class action
summary: A federal judge denied Live Nation and Ticketmaster's attempt to dismiss an antitrust class action lawsuit. Consumers claim they paid excessive prices for concert tickets due to the companies' alleged monopoly. The judge found that consumers had antitrust standing, rejecting arguments that their harm was indirect or speculative, and will revisit a recent Ninth Circuit decision.

title: Dow Jones Today: DIA ETF Down 2%, Amazon (Nasdaq: AMZN) and American Express (NYSE: AXP) Drop
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF (DIA) is down about 2% today, with Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) dropping 3.5% and American Express (NYSE: AXP) falling 4.5%. This market decline is primarily attributed to ongoing trade tensions, specifically the 125% tariff on Chinese imports by the U.S. and China's retaliatory 84% tariffs, despite a temporary 90-day tariff reprieve from both the U.S. and EU on other goods.

title: Amazon trained Prime drivers to use defibrillators
summary: Amazon conducted a small-scale trial called Project Pulse in Europe, training over 100 volunteer Prime delivery drivers to use automated external defibrillators (AEDs) for emergency response. Internal documents suggest that Prime vans equipped with AEDs could potentially respond to cardiac emergencies faster than traditional EMS. Although Project Pulse has concluded, Amazon may expand similar initiatives into other markets, aiming to further integrate into daily life and emergency services.

title: Türkiye: TikTok content moderators sue Telus over allegations of poor conditions & union retaliation; incl. co. response
summary: A group of 15 TikTok content moderators in Türkiye are suing Telus, alleging they were dismissed in retaliation for unionizing. The workers, who were reportedly low-paid and exposed to disturbing content, claim severe mental health issues and poor working conditions. Telus has provided a statement in response to these allegations, which are said to be part of a broader issue in outsourced content moderation.

title: Is Marvell Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MRVL) a NASDAQ Stock with the Highest Upside Potential?
summary: Marvell Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MRVL) is identified as a NASDAQ stock with significant upside potential, ranking first on a list compiled using analysts' upside potential and hedge fund sentiment. The article discusses Wedbush Securities' Dan Ives' concerns about tariffs and geopolitical tensions impacting tech supply chains and earnings, while highlighting Marvell's strong position in AI and cloud markets through partnerships like with Amazon Web Services. Bank of America reiterated a Buy rating on MRVL due to its AI opportunities and growth trajectory.

title: iShares MSCI World ETF: Ob das eine gute Idee ist?
summary: The iShares MSCI World ETF has experienced significant price drops, leading investors to hope for a recovery. However, the article raises concerns about a concentration risk due to the high proportion of US tech stocks (over 70% US equities, with more than 10% in leading tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Amazon, Meta, Alphabet, and Tesla). It suggests that a US recession could heavily impact the ETF and advises investors to diversify by adding other ETFs, such as those tracking the MSCI ACWI, to their portfolios.

title: Kohl's axes popular 'returns' perk after 8 years & it's a headache for shoppers
summary: Kohl's has officially begun to axe its popular Amazon return service at select locations after an eight-year run, causing inconvenience for shoppers. The retailer is conducting a "test" in these stores to assess the impact of discontinuing third-party returns, a move supported by some Kohl's employees due to the perceived workload and cost associated with the service. While many Kohl's stores will no longer accept Amazon returns, shoppers can still use other locations like Whole Foods, Amazon Fresh, Amazon Go, Amazon Lockers, Staples, and The UPS Store.

title: Amazon.com (AMZN) Sold Off in Q1 Along with Peers
summary: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) experienced a sell-off in Q1 2025, along with other cloud computing companies, contributing to negative returns for Vulcan Value Partners' composites. Despite this, the investment management company noted Amazon's dominant position, powerful secular tailwinds in e-commerce, digital advertising, and cloud transition, and its strong Q4 global revenue growth. However, Vulcan Value Partners suggests that AI stocks might offer greater returns in a shorter timeframe compared to Amazon.

title: Freedom Cloud brings Amazon technologies to Kazakhstan
summary: Freedom Cloud, a subsidiary of Freedom Holding Corp., has partnered with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to bring AWS solutions to the Kazakhstani market. This collaboration grants Freedom Cloud's clients, including companies, startups, and government agencies, access to a wide range of AWS features like cloud computing, data storage, AI, and ML services. The partnership aims to position Kazakhstan as an active player in the global digital landscape and enhance the competitiveness of Kazakhstani businesses and government services.

title: 10 Dirt Cheap Stocks To Invest In Now
summary: This article highlights PDD Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: PDD) as one of the dirt-cheap stocks to invest in, emphasizing its strong revenue and operating profit growth in Q4 2024. PDD, operating Pinduoduo and Temu, is praised by GreenWood Investors for its innovative business model and rapid growth, outperforming Amazon in sales growth and valuation attractiveness. The company's relentless corporate culture and efficiency in the supply chain are also noted as key strengths.

title: South Korea’s Telecom Giants Ramp Up Data Center Expansion Amid AI Demand Surge
summary: South Korea's major telecom companies, including KT, LG Uplus, and SK Telecom, are significantly expanding their data center infrastructure to meet the surging demand for AI technology and cloud services. They are investing in new facilities, securing advanced GPUs like Blackwell, and developing GPU-as-a-Service offerings to solidify their positions in the AI infrastructure market. This strategic expansion aims to diversify revenue streams beyond traditional telecommunications and leverage their network expertise to support South Korea’s digital transformation.

title: Amazon.com to Webcast First Quarter 2025 Financial Results Conference Call
summary: Amazon.com, Inc. announced it will host a conference call on Thursday, May 1, 2025, at 2:00 p.m. PT/5:00 p.m. ET to discuss its first-quarter 2025 financial results. The event will be webcast live on its investor relations website, with audio and slides available for at least three months. The company also recently expanded same-day medication delivery and introduced Health Insights for lab results.

title: Etsy touts 'shopping domestically' as Trump tariffs threaten price increases for imports
summary: Etsy is encouraging "shopping domestically" to counteract the potential price increases resulting from President Donald Trump's tariffs on imports. The company is highlighting local sellers and providing guidance to its merchants to minimize disruptions. This strategy aims to help Etsy remain resilient compared to competitors like Shein and Temu, which are planning price hikes due to the new tariffs.

title: Industrial IoT Market Size, Growth, Share & Analysis, 2033
summary: The global Industrial IoT (IIoT) market was valued at USD 375.7 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 1034.34 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 11.91% from 2025. Key drivers include increased IoT-related regulations, automation, and AI integration, while restraints include interoperability issues, security concerns, and high implementation costs. Significant opportunities exist in co-creation through IoT, cybersecurity, and 5G deployment, with North America and Asia-Pacific expected to lead regional growth.

title: 10 cloud programming languages developers need to know
summary: This article identifies ten essential cloud programming languages for developers, detailing their use cases, advantages, and limitations. It emphasizes the importance of choosing the right language based on factors like cloud client and service integration, targeted stack layer, team familiarity, and risk aversion. The languages covered include JavaScript, Node.js, Python, C, Go, .NET, Java, Kotlin, Swift, and Rust.

title: Cart Abandonment Rate: What It Shows and How to Reduce It
summary: This article defines cart abandonment rate as the percentage of customers who add items to their online cart but leave without completing the purchase, framing it as an opportunity rather than a failure. It explains how to calculate this rate and identifies key causes such as frustrating shopping experiences, hidden fees, limited payment options, and mandatory account creation. The article then provides actionable strategies to improve the cart abandonment rate, including simplifying the checkout process, offering cheap or free shipping, optimizing the site/app, and sending follow-up emails with incentives.

title: General Mills Boycott: Full List of Products Organizers Say to Avoid
summary: People's Union USA has organized a week-long boycott of General Mills products, starting April 21 and ending April 28. The activist group alleges the multinational company, which owns brands like Cheerios and Häagen-Dazs, is responsible for unchecked corporate power, unhealthy food choices, and environmental harm. The boycott targets a wide range of General Mills products, including pet food, ice cream, cereals, snacks, baking items, dairy, and frozen foods.

title: Lumen’s love affair with cloud hyperscalers could reshape the internet
summary: Lumen Technologies is partnering with Google Cloud to rebuild the internet's U.S. national architecture, creating new 400G cloud on-ramps. This initiative aims to replace outdated infrastructure, provide multi-cloud access through a single Lumen port, and support the growing demands of AI applications. Lumen's CTO Dave Ward emphasizes the "co-opetition" strategy, where Lumen offers its access network to hyperscalers who lack it, while gaining a global-scale partner in return.

title: Beauty Has Long-Term Glow Despite Near-Term Woes – AHEAD OF THE CURVE®
summary: TD Cowen's analysis forecasts a 5% five-year CAGR for the beauty sector, inclusive of aesthetics and wellness, driven by hybridization with wellness, fragrance adoption, haircare innovation, and momentum in "masstige" and prestige categories. The US beauty market is sized at $115 billion, with emerging trends reshaping the total addressable market and channel dynamics. Innovation is crucial, with experts anticipating a rebound in the second half of 2025 and continued mid-single-digit growth.

title: Data Center Market Size Hits USD 1,008.65 Billion by 2034
summary: The global data center market is projected to reach USD 1,008.65 billion by 2034, growing from USD 386.71 billion in 2025 at a CAGR of 11.24%. This growth is driven by the increasing applications of AI, cloud computing, and the demand for data storage and processing. North America currently dominates the market, but Asia Pacific is expected to show significant growth, with key trends including generative AI solutions, hybrid cloud adoption, and multicloud strategies.

title: Old Dominion doesn’t see Amazon’s LTL service as a threat
summary: Old Dominion Freight Line's CEO Marty Freeman stated that Amazon's new LTL (less-than-truckload) service, primarily geared towards its own suppliers, is seen as an opportunity rather than a threat. Old Dominion believes its existing network can support Amazon's suppliers, and the growth of e-commerce, despite new competitors, presents a tailwind for their LTL services. Despite this optimistic outlook, Old Dominion reported a 5.8% year-over-year decrease in Q1 2025 revenue, reflecting broader economic softness partially offset by an increase in LTL revenue per hundredweight.

title: GameStop Short Seller Andrew Left Goes Long On China And These 2 US Stocks Amid Market Correction
summary: Andrew Left of Citron Research, known for shorting GameStop, has revealed his long positions in First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR), Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN), and the iShares China Large-Cap ETF (NYSE:FXI). He is bullish on First Solar due to new U.S. tariffs on solar imports, Amazon for its consumer resilience and low P/E, and Chinese markets for their oversold status, low multiples, and government support. Left believes these investments are well-positioned for growth amid current market conditions.

title: Choice Hotels International Introduces Mews Cloud Technology As Newest Property Management System Option For International Franchiseees
summary: Choice Hotels International is integrating Mews cloud technology as a new property management system option for its international franchisees. This move aims to enhance guest experiences, streamline operations, and empower franchisees by providing robust integrations, intuitive workflows, and a cloud-native architecture that connects seamlessly with Choice's proprietary tools like choiceEDGE. The partnership will offer international hotel owners full data visibility, operational efficiencies through automation, scalable growth, and an enhanced guest experience.

title: NTNX Stock Price, News & Analysis
summary: Nutanix, Inc. (NASDAQ: NTNX) operates in the information sector as a software publisher focused on hybrid multicloud computing, offering a unified, cloud software platform for running applications and AI and managing data across different environments. The company emphasizes its Nutanix Cloud Platform (NCP) designed to support hybrid multicloud infrastructure, including deployments in core data centers, at the edge, and in public clouds with a consistent operating model. Nutanix highlights its subscription-based business model, global customer base, and focus on AI, security, and compliance, with offerings like Nutanix Kubernetes Platform (NKP) and Nutanix Enterprise AI (NAI).

title: The Magnificent Seven receives another ETF focused on covered calls
summary: Roundhill Investments has launched a new exchange-traded fund, the Roundhill Magnificent Seven Covered Call ETF (MAGY), which focuses on covered calls for the mega-cap tech stocks known as the Magnificent Seven. This actively managed ETF is the latest financial product inspired by the strong performance of these prominent technology companies.

title: ASAN Stock Price, News & Analysis
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of Asana (ASAN), a work management platform specializing in human and AI collaboration. It details Asana's business model, customer metrics, financial reporting using both GAAP and non-GAAP measures, corporate governance, and key features like AI Studio, Smart Workflows, and AI Teammates. The company's efforts to serve public sector and regulated industries with Asana Gov are also highlighted, alongside its stock performance and financial metrics.

title: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT): Among Billionaire Steve Cohen’s Large-Cap Stock Picks With Huge Upside Potential
summary: Billionaire investor Steve Cohen's Point72 Asset Management has identified Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) as a top large-cap stock pick with significant upside potential. Despite Cohen's warnings about a potential economic slowdown due to tariffs and slower immigration, he remains optimistic about the artificial intelligence sector, where Microsoft is a key player. Analysts at Mizuho project impressive long-term growth for Microsoft, particularly from its Azure cloud unit and AI investments.

title: How cloud technology and storage are helping power AI
summary: This article explains how cloud computing and data storage are fundamental to the advancement of artificial intelligence (AI), providing the necessary scalability and data processing capabilities. It highlights key companies within the Invesco QQQ ETF, such as Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, and Micron Technology, that are leading innovations in cloud and storage solutions for AI. The piece concludes by emphasizing the deepening relationship between AI and cloud technology, and their increasing demand for greater infrastructure and storage capacity in the future.

title: MercadoLibre proposes to move US legal home to Texas from Delaware
summary: MercadoLibre's board has asked shareholders to approve moving its U.S. operations from Delaware to Texas, citing a desire for greater legal certainty. This move, echoing similar actions by companies like Tesla, is scheduled for a shareholder vote on June 17, and if successful, could benefit MercadoLibre while posing a challenge to Asian e-commerce competitors. The company believes Texas's recently enacted rule for codifying business judgments will provide more predictable decision-making for its innovative business model.

title: PayPal Brings Together Developers, AI Leaders to Power Agentic Commerce at Dev Days
summary: PayPal is leading the agentic commerce revolution, providing developers and merchants with innovative AI tools to create next-generation shopping experiences. The company is hosting Dev Days, collaborating with AI leaders like Amazon Web Services, Anthropic, Google Cloud, and Microsoft, to demonstrate the power of agentic experiences. PayPal's goal is to enable AI agents to transact on behalf of customers and businesses, providing secure and efficient commerce operations.

title: Risks of Using AI Models Developed by Competing Nations
summary: The proliferation of open and offline AI models presents significant risks, particularly when these models are developed by rival nations or communities. Beyond basic security and privacy concerns, issues like political bias embedded in training data and the potential for malicious fine-tuning can lead to unpredictable degradation of model performance or even introduce hidden backdoors. Organizations must prioritize rigorous vetting of model lineage, conduct thorough security audits, and stay informed about emerging threats to mitigate these dangers while leveraging the benefits of offline AI for privacy and IP protection.

title: Sinclair names former Amazon exec Jeff Blackburn as Tennis Channel CEO and Chairman
summary: Sinclair Inc. has appointed former Amazon executive Jeff Blackburn as the new Chairman and CEO of Tennis Channel. Blackburn, known for his work in expanding Amazon's streaming and sports ventures, will focus on growing Tennis Channel across digital, streaming, and global platforms. His extensive experience at Amazon, including leading Prime Video and launching "Thursday Night Football," is expected to be instrumental in enhancing the channel's reach and direct-to-consumer offerings.

title: EPAM Advances Strategic Collaboration Agreement with AWS Focusing on Generative AI
summary: EPAM Systems, Inc. announced an expanded strategic collaboration agreement with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to develop generative AI solutions. This partnership will help enterprises migrate workloads to AWS and modernize applications using AWS's advanced generative AI services like Amazon Bedrock. The collaboration aims to accelerate the adoption of specialized AI agents and GenAI solutions to address engineering and industry-specific challenges, enhancing efficiency and competitive advantage for clients.

title: New Philips Norelco Shaver i9000 Prestige Ultra Introduces the Brand's First "Root-Level" Shave, for Closest Shave Yet
summary: Philips Norelco has launched its most advanced electric shaver, the Norelco Shaver i9000 Prestige Ultra, featuring AI-powered "root-level" shave technology that cuts hair up to -0.08mm below the surface. Key features include SenseIQ Pro technology with live coaching, five smart shaving modes, a 360° Precision Flexing System, and 72 self-sharpening NanoTech Dual Precision blades. Priced between $329.99-$499.99, the shaver also comes with a 7-year warranty upon registration, addressing consumer needs for precision, comfort, and longevity.

title: Amazon.com, Inc. 2025 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation (NASDAQ:AMZN) 2025-05-01
summary: This article provides the earnings call presentation from Amazon.com, Inc. for its Q1 2025 results. The company reported an EPS of $1.59, beating estimates by $0.23, and revenue of $155.67 billion, an 8.62% year-over-year increase, beating by $546.51 million. The article highlights that the slide deck was published in conjunction with their earnings call.

title: Chunghwa Telecom seeking further satellite deals – report
summary: Chunghwa Telecom is actively pursuing additional satellite partnerships to bolster its capacity and network resilience, engaging in discussions with Amazon's Project Kuiper and Canada's Telesat. This move follows previous agreements with OneWeb and Astranis, and comes as the Taiwanese telco aims to diversify international capacity after incidents involving offshore cables and faces delays in cable infrastructure projects. The company has ruled out a partnership with Starlink due to "regulatory restrictions" and security concerns related to Elon Musk's ties to China.

title: Apple, Amazon, Block, Take-Two, Dexcom, MicroStrategy, Duolingo, Roku, and More Movers
summary: Stocks continued to rally on Friday, influenced by robust U.S. jobs data and promising signs of impending trade negotiations between the U.S. and China. This positive momentum overshadowed mixed earnings reports from tech giants Apple and Amazon. The article lists several companies, including Block, Take-Two, Dexcom, MicroStrategy, Duolingo, and Roku, as significant movers in the market.

title: Hasbro Pulse Star Wars Fanstream Event May 2nd Reveals
summary: Hasbro hosted a Star Wars Fanstream event on May 2nd, revealing new collectibles for The Black Series and The Vintage Collection lines, ahead of Star Wars Day. Pre-orders for most items begin on May 4th at 1 PM ET. The new products include a Force FX Elite Ezra Bridger Lightsaber, several Vintage Collection figure packs like Stormtroopers and Cantina Adventure, and Black Series figures such as Aayla Secura, Magnaguard, Shoretrooper & Death Trooper, Sandtrooper, and Clone Lieutenant Galle.

title: BlackRock, Inc. Reduces Stake in TTM Technologies Inc.
summary: BlackRock, Inc. has reduced its stake in TTM Technologies Inc. by 2,886,132 shares, a 15.77% decrease, though TTM Technologies still represents only 0.01% of BlackRock's portfolio. The transaction occurred at $20.51 per share, and despite the reduction and TTM Technologies being considered overvalued with a low GF Score, its stock price has increased by 22.04% since the transaction. First Eagle Investment Management, LLC remains TTM Technologies' largest holder, indicating continued investor interest.

title: Results: Amazon.com, Inc. Beat Earnings Expectations And Analysts Now Have New Forecasts
summary: Amazon.com (NASDAQ: AMZN) recently reported its first-quarter results, beating analyst expectations for earnings per share, though revenues were in line with forecasts. Despite the strong performance, analysts have largely reconfirmed their previous outlook, with no major changes to revenue or EPS estimates for 2025, and the price target remains at US$240. The company's revenue growth is expected to slow but remain consistent with the broader industry.

title: Vita Coco Expands Coconut Juice Line Up with New Piña Colada Flavor
summary: Vita Coco has launched a new Piña Colada flavored coconut juice with pulp, timed for the summer season, aiming to provide a tropical escape. This new flavor joins their existing product line, offering a blend of pineapple and coconut water, and is available nationwide and online. The company emphasizes its mission to provide healthy and great-tasting products.

title: The Resurgence of the Beauty Marketplace
summary: Beauty marketplaces are experiencing a resurgence, driven by Amazon's success in prestige beauty and the rise of TikTok Shop. Retailers like Ulta Beauty and Walmart are investing in these platforms to expand their reach, drive revenue, and offer a broader assortment of products, despite past failures in the sector. The key to success lies in competitive pricing, strong customer loyalty, and a comprehensive product selection.

title: Earnings call transcript: Viant Technology Q1 2025 earnings beat expectations
summary: Viant Technology Inc. (DSP) reported a significant beat in its Q1 2025 earnings, with an EPS of $0.03 against a forecasted loss of $0.07 and revenue of $70.6 million, far exceeding expectations. The company saw a 32% year-over-year revenue increase and a 76% increase in adjusted EBITDA, driven by strong performance in connected TV (CTV) advertising and AI solutions. Viant's stock rose 4.96% in aftermarket trading, reflecting investor confidence and an outlook for continued growth, despite minor macroeconomic uncertainties causing some Q2 campaign activations to shift to the second half of 2025.

title: PepsiCo and AWS collaborate to accelerate digital transformation
summary: PepsiCo has announced a strategic, multi-year agreement with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to accelerate its digital transformation. This collaboration focuses on leveraging AWS's infrastructure and services to drive AI innovation, transform supply chain and go-to-market strategies, and create personalized consumer experiences. The partnership involves cloud migration, enhancement of PepsiCo's generative AI platform PepGenX with Amazon Bedrock, and utilizing real-time insights for targeted marketing.

title: PepsiCo Announces Strategic, Multi-year Agreement With Amazon Web Services
summary: PepsiCo has announced a strategic, multi-year agreement with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to accelerate its enterprise-wide digital transformation. This collaboration will leverage AWS's infrastructure and services to drive AI innovation, transform supply chain and go-to-market strategies, and enhance personalized consumer experiences. Both companies emphasize the importance of cloud technology and AI in meeting evolving consumer expectations and achieving scalability.

title: PepsiCo Makes Alliance With AWS to Strengthen Digital Infrastructure
summary: PepsiCo has entered into a multi-year strategic alliance with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to enhance its digital infrastructure across AI, supply chain, and customer experience globally. This collaboration will leverage AWS's cloud services, including Amazon Bedrock for generative AI, to boost PepsiCo's operational efficiencies, digital supply chain capabilities, and personalized customer interactions. Despite this forward-looking move, PepsiCo's shares have recently underperformed due to weaker North American consumer demand, product recalls, geopolitical tensions, and adverse currency rates.

title: EPAM Systems: Strategic Moves with AWS
summary: EPAM Systems' stock has risen by 13.4% due to strategic partnerships, particularly a deepened collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AWS) focusing on generative AI solutions and workload migration using Amazon Bedrock. Despite positive sentiment, several analysts like Mizuho, HSBC, Stifel, and Scotiabank have lowered price targets for EPAM Systems, citing market uncertainty and macro concerns, while still largely maintaining "Overweight" or "Outperform" ratings. The company's recent financial performance shows consistent revenue growth but mixed profitability indicators, with its strategic alliances, especially with AWS, expected to offset growth tapering concerns.

title: EXEC: Peloton CEO Highlights Four “Objectives” to Drive Growth
summary: Peloton's CEO, Peter Stern, outlined four key objectives to drive growth after the company raised its full-year guidance for 2025. These objectives include enhancing member outcomes through improved products and services, expanding reach to new members and locations, fostering deeper connections among existing members, and streamlining operations for greater efficiency and revenue maximization. The company reported positive Adjusted EBITDA for the fifth consecutive quarter and raised its full-year guidance for various metrics, signaling a strong financial recovery.

title: Here’s Where To Shop All Of Molly-Mae’s ‘Behind It All’ Outfits, Including Her Exact Pair Of Glasses
summary: This article details Molly-Mae Hague's outfits from her docuseries "Molly-Mae: Behind It All," providing shopping links for exact items and similar alternatives. It covers everything from high-street finds to designer pieces, reflecting her "elevated casual vibe." The piece also touches on her personal life shared in the documentary, including her relationship with Tommy Fury, motherhood, and her brand Maebe.

title: IBM Is America’s Worst Tech Company
summary: The article argues that IBM has fallen dramatically from its former prominence as a leading tech company, citing its consistently flat revenue growth and significantly smaller market capitalization compared to competitors like Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft. It highlights IBM's failure to succeed in key tech sectors such as cloud computing and AI, where its market share is negligible. The author concludes that IBM, despite attempts by its CEO to project AI success, is now among America's worst tech companies, especially when compared to its more successful peers.

title: Invesco Global Core Equity Fund Q1 2025 Commentary
summary: The Invesco Global Core Equity Fund's Q1 2025 commentary reflects a shift from post-election optimism to uncertainty, citing a lack of coherent policy direction from President Trump's administration. The fund has consequently built a diversified portfolio of high-quality businesses with strong balance sheets to navigate the current environment. Investors are encouraged to remain open-minded about US policy direction.

title: 13 billion mattress empire to establish offices in Texas
summary: Somnigroup International, the new corporation formed by the acquisition of Houston-based Mattress Firm by Tempur Sealy International, will establish its corporate headquarters in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. This move follows a $4 billion deal that created the $13 billion company. While Tempur Sealy will maintain its Kentucky base and Mattress Firm its Houston operations, Dallas was chosen for the new overarching corporate office, further solidifying North Texas as a growing business hub.

title: 3 Stocks to Sell and 3 Stocks to Buy Instead
summary: Morningstar's Chief US Market Strategist, Dave Sekera, discusses the market's reaction to potential trade breakthroughs between the US and China, the Fed's decision to hold rates, and upcoming economic data. He provides insights on several company earnings, identifies three stocks to sell (Philip Morris International, Exelon, American Water Works) due to being overvalued, and three stocks to buy (Amazon, Ingersoll Rand, Marvell Technology) that are currently undervalued according to Morningstar's analysis. Sekera also advises investors to maintain a market-weight position, favoring value and small-cap stocks, and to prepare for potential market volatility.

title: The Gig Trap: Algorithmic, Wage and Labor Exploitation in Platform Work in the US | HRW
summary: This Human Rights Watch report details how digital labor platforms in the US exploit workers through algorithmic management, wage opacity, and misclassification as independent contractors, denying them crucial labor protections and social security benefits. The report, based on extensive interviews and surveys with gig workers in states like Texas, reveals widespread financial insecurity, low wages—often below minimum wage after expenses—and vulnerability to arbitrary deactivations and on-the-job hazards. It calls for urgent regulatory reforms, including proper employment classification using the "ABC test," algorithmic fairness, guaranteed living wages, adequate social protection, and strengthened worker bargaining power to ensure platform workers' human rights are upheld.

title: Amazon and FedEx reach delivery deal following pullback by UPS
summary: Amazon and FedEx have re-established a package delivery agreement following Amazon's decision to significantly reduce shipments handled by UPS. This new deal will see FedEx transporting select Amazon packages, including large items, with a focus on heavier weights and all US ZIP codes. FedEx anticipates this partnership will be beneficial and accretive to its domestic market yield, though Amazon specifies it's not meant to replace UPS.

title: PepsiCo signs multi-year contract with AWS
summary: PepsiCo has entered into a multi-year agreement with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to migrate its applications and workloads to AWS' cloud platform, aligning with its "cloud-first" strategy. This collaboration aims to enhance agility, intelligence, and scalability across PepsiCo's global operations, including integrating generative AI with Amazon Bedrock and exploring predictive maintenance in its supply chain. The move follows previous cloud migrations with Microsoft Azure and highlights the ongoing trend of major corporations adopting cloud solutions.

title: Here's Why Amazon.com, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:AMZN) CEO May Deserve A Raise
summary: Amazon.com, Inc.'s CEO Andy Jassy received a total annual compensation of US$1.6m in 2024, significantly below the industry median for similar companies, reflecting a relatively modest remuneration despite the company's strong performance. Over the past three years, Amazon has seen an 83% total shareholder return and a 43% annual EPS growth. Given these impressive results and Jassy's substantial shareholdings in the company, shareholders are likely satisfied, and a discussion about his compensation at the upcoming AGM may not be a primary focus.

title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) and HUMAIN to Invest $5B+ in Saudi Arabia’s AI Zone Development
summary: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) and HUMAIN, a newly established AI startup in Saudi Arabia, plan to jointly invest over $5 billion to develop an "AI Zone" in the country. This initiative is part of a broader shift in U.S. policy under the Trump administration, which aims to deepen tech ties with Middle Eastern partners by providing access to advanced AI chips and technology, contrasting with previous restrictions. The AI Zone will feature AWS AI infrastructure, UltraCluster networks, and services to position Saudi Arabia as a leading AI hub, specifically supporting developments in Large Language Models, including Arabic variants.

title: Things To Be Wary Of When Buying Netflix Stock
summary: Netflix stock has seen a significant surge, rising 25% this year to around $1,100 per share, driven by strong financial performance, subscriber growth from ad-supported plans, and price increases. Despite strong fundamentals in growth, profitability, and financial stability, the stock is considered volatile and expensive with high valuation ratios compared to the S&P 500, making it a tricky investment. The article advises caution due to its high valuation and sensitivity to adverse events, even though its operational performance and financial health are robust.

title: Box expands its toolbox with more AI agents for search, deep research and data extraction
summary: Box Inc. is expanding its artificial intelligence capabilities by launching new AI agents for search, deep research, and data extraction, along with a Box AI Agent for Microsoft 365 Copilot. These new agents aim to automate various tasks for users and integrate Box content seamlessly into Microsoft applications. Box emphasizes its vision of an "intelligent content management platform" and highlights the need for clean data architecture to maximize AI benefits.

title: 'He Won't Sell One Toy In The United States,' Says Donald Trump Suggesting A 100% Tariff On Mattel Which He Seemed To Mistake For A Country
summary: Donald Trump has reiterated his aggressive stance on tariffs, proposing a 100% tariff on Mattel Inc. while seemingly mistaking the company for a country. This comes as Mattel aims to diversify its supply chain away from China, a move Trump criticized, advocating instead for U.S.-based manufacturing. The potential tariffs could lead to higher toy prices and a significant shortage, especially during the holiday season.

title: Netflix Is Turning Into an Advertising Company
summary: Netflix, once a pioneer of ad-free streaming, is rapidly transforming into an advertising company. Its ad-supported tier now boasts 94 million global monthly active users and is its fastest-growing segment. The company is actively developing new ad formats, including AI-powered contextual ads, to compete directly with major digital advertising players like Google, Meta, and Amazon, signaling a significant shift in its business model.

title: Prologis Stock: The Warehouse King With A 3.8% Yield And Room To Run (NYSE:PLD)
summary: Prologis (PLD) presents a strong buy-on-weakness opportunity, currently trading 15% below its intrinsic value and offering an attractive 3.8% dividend yield. The company demonstrates robust operational performance with high occupancy rates, strong tenant retention, and impressive rent growth despite macroeconomicchallenges. Its exceptional dividend growth, with a 9% trailing rate and 13.2% three-year CAGR, further enhances its appeal, while a diversified, investment-grade client base provides insulation against economic risks.

title: How to Start an Etsy Shop in 10 Easy Steps
summary: This article provides a step-by-step guide on how to open an Etsy shop and begin selling handmade crafts or vintage items online. It covers everything from setting up an account and choosing a shop name to photographing products, adding listings, selecting payment preferences, and understanding Etsy seller fees. The guide emphasizes the ease of setting up an online storefront on Etsy for new sellers.

title: U.S. Defense and AI Companies Poised to Dominate Middle East Spending Wave
summary: The article discusses how U.S. defense and AI companies are set to benefit significantly from a new wave of spending in the Middle East, particularly spurred by President Trump's recent diplomatic efforts. Key agreements include a massive arms deal with Saudi Arabia and substantial investments in AI infrastructure using American semiconductors across Gulf states. Aviation also plays a crucial role with a large deal between Boeing and Qatar Airways, all against a backdrop of rising global military spending.

title: Tom Alberg Net Worth
summary: This article details the net worth of Tom Alberg, a renowned lawyer and businessman, at the time of his passing. It highlights his entrepreneurial ventures, including founding Madrona Venture Group and Novelty Hill Winery, and his significant director role at Amazon.com. The piece also discusses his substantial investments and real estate holdings, attributing his wealth to his successful business acumen.

title: Laird Superfood expands functional product range with six new mushroom powders
summary: Laird Superfood has launched six new organic mushroom powders, expanding its functional product range. These include two blends, Calm & Relaxation and Focus & Memory, which combine mushrooms with botanical ingredients for specific wellbeing benefits. Additionally, four individual organic mushroom powders (lion’s mane, reishi, turkey tail, and oyster) have been introduced, all organic, US-grown, and free from fillers, and will be available online and in select US retail locations.

title: Meta to buy 200 MW of wind energy from TransAlta as it expands footprint
summary: Meta has signed a long-term agreement to purchase 200 MW of wind energy and environmental attributes from TransAlta's Horizon Hill Wind Power Project in Oklahoma. This deal supports Meta's goal of powering global operations solely with wind and solar energy as its footprint expands. The project, expected to cost between US$290 million and US$310 million, contributes to TransAlta's goal of adding 2 GW of renewables by 2025.

title: Edible Garden Stock Skyrockets on Big News
summary: Edible Garden AG Incorporated (NASDAQ: EDBL) stock surged by over 55% following promising developments including the acquisition of NaturalShrimp Farms’ assets for $12M, product expansion into sports nutrition via Amazon, and increased distribution with Stop & Shop. Despite a net income loss and revenue dip, the company's strategic shift toward higher-margin products and sustainable initiatives, coupled with reduced expenses, signals a positive future outlook. Investors are optimistic about the company's growth potential through strategic acquisitions and e-commerce expansion.

title: Modine records highest sales in company history, supported by data center demand
summary: Modine, a thermal management technology manufacturer, reported its highest sales and profitability for the third consecutive year in fiscal 2025, with net sales growing 7% to $2.6 billion and net earnings increasing 14% to $185.5 million. This record performance was largely driven by its data center business, prompting a $38 million investment to expand production capacity in Grenada, Mississippi. The company expects continued strong growth in its Climate Solutions segment, particularly for data centers, and has developed relationships with five hyperscalers.

title: Big Mag 7 Rally Pays Off for QBIG
summary: The Invesco Top QQQ ETF (QBIG), holding Magnificent Seven stocks and Broadcom, has seen a 16% gain in the month ending May 15, outperforming the S&P 500 Growth Index. This rebound is attributed to clearer international trade policy and potential AI advancements, despite the ETF still being in the red year-to-date. A recent thaw in U.S./China trade relations also bodes well for QBIG’s holdings.

title: The IT wingspan of JPMorgan Chase & Co.
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPMC), the largest US bank, employs a complex hybrid IT infrastructure strategy balancing enterprise data centers with multi-cloud solutions (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) for resilience and leading-edge capabilities. Infrastructure CIO Darrin Alves highlights JPMC's commitment to technology, significant investments, and plans to consolidate its global data center estate from 32 to 17 facilities while rigorously applying legal, security, and resiliency criteria before cost optimization. The bank is also investing in AI and quantum computing research, leveraging cloud for generative AI experimentation, and utilizing mainframes for critical systems.

title: Dolby Atmos now available across the entire Mercedes fleet, but there’s a catch
summary: Mercedes-Benz has expanded the availability of Dolby Atmos across its entire car fleet, moving beyond its previous restriction to top-tier models like the S-Class and Maybach. To experience Dolby Atmos, customers must opt for an upgraded Burmester sound system, which often comes as part of a premium package. This integration offers an immersive audio experience, significantly enhancing in-car music and even audiobooks from sources like Apple Music, Amazon Music, Tidal, and Audible.

title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Faces Lawsuit Over Claims it Sold Contaminated Rice
summary: Amazon.com, Inc. is being sued by consumers claiming the company sold various rice products tainted with arsenic and other heavy metals. The proposed class action targets 18 rice varieties, including Ben's Original and Whole Foods' 365, alleging that Amazon sold these products with dangerously high levels of heavy metals without warning, especially concerning for children. This lawsuit follows a study revealing widespread heavy metal contamination in rice across the US, and the plaintiffs are seeking at least $5 million in damages.

title: Amazon pauses plan to build data center near Becker
summary: Amazon has paused its plans to build a large data center near Xcel Energy's Sherco site in Becker, Minnesota. This decision comes amidst discussions in the Minnesota Legislature to remove a sales tax exemption on electricity used by data centers, a move opposed by Republicans but supported by Governor Tim Walz to balance the state's budget. Xcel Energy confirmed Amazon's pause, stating it was due to factors independent of their electricity readiness, while Amazon emphasized its continuous evaluation of new locations based on customer demand.

title: Gannett’s SWOT analysis: digital growth amid print decline shapes stock outlook
summary: Gannett, the largest U.S. newspaper publisher, is navigating a challenging media landscape by transitioning from print to digital, as detailed in an InvestingPro SWOT analysis. While facing overall revenue decline and difficulties in its Digital Marketing Solutions (DMS) segment, the company shows strength in its growing digital audience, subscriptions, cost discipline, and strategic AI partnerships. Future success hinges on accelerating digital revenue growth and managing debt as asset sale opportunities diminish.

title: The Trade Desk vs. Criteo: Which Ad Tech Stock is the Better Buy Now?
summary: This article compares two ad tech companies, The Trade Desk (TTD) and Criteo (CRTO), to determine which is a better investment. While TTD is gaining from strong revenue growth in the first quarter of 2025, it faces challenges from macroeconomic uncertainties, intense competition, and regulatory scrutiny. Criteo, on the other hand, is transitioning towards high-growth areas like Retail Media with its AI-driven platform and direct retailer access, and is considered the smarter pick due to its stronger valuation and focus on partnerships.

title: Surging Data Center Growth to Help Lower Energy Costs for PG&E Customers
summary: PG&E anticipates that the significant growth in data center electricity demand, projected to reach 8.7 GW over the next decade, will actually help lower energy costs for its existing customers. The company estimates that for every 1 GW of new data center demand, residential monthly electric bills could decrease by 1-2% due to better utilization of existing infrastructure and spreading costs over more consumption. PG&E is actively working on numerous data center projects, particularly in Silicon Valley and the Bay Area, and is conducting further studies across California to accommodate future growth and integrate these large loads efficiently.

title: How Restaurants Can Move from AI Hype to Real Results
summary: This article discusses how restaurants can move from the hype of AI to achieving tangible results. It highlights the challenges companies face with disjointed systems and bad data in AI implementation and emphasizes the importance of data quality and understanding the specific problems AI needs to solve. Examples from AWS and Yum! Brands illustrate successful AI applications in boosting productivity, managing operations, and enhancing customer engagement.

title: State utility officials approve AEP Ohio's building onsite energy generators at two data centers
summary: Ohio utility regulators have approved AEP Ohio's plan to install onsite energy generators at two large data centers belonging to Amazon Web Services and Cologix. This decision aims to provide a secondary power source for the expanding data centers without drawing further from the grid, addressing concerns about increasing energy demand versus available supply. The systems will utilize solid oxide fuel cells and natural gas, with costs borne solely by AWS and Cologix, ensuring other AEP Ohio customers do not incur these expenses.

title: Analyst Sees ‘Wait-and-See’ for Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL) as EPS Drops and Competitors Loom
summary: Redburn Atlantic initiated coverage on Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL) with a "Neutral" rating and a $67 price target, citing concerns about its potential involvement in future Amazon Web Services (AWS) Trainium chips. Competitors like Alchip or AWS's Annapurna Labs might take a larger share of AWS's accelerated ASICs work, impacting Marvell. Despite a nearly 50% stock price decline in 2025 and a 16% reduction in three-year forward GAAP EPS, analysts project Marvell to return to profitability in FY2026 with an estimated EPS of $2.83, leading to a "wait-and-see" approach for investors.

title: Talkspace Strikes Deal with Amazon Pharmacy, Expanding Medication Access
summary: Talkspace has partnered with Amazon Pharmacy, making it the first behavioral health provider to integrate with the retail giant's pharmaceutical offerings. This collaboration simplifies medication management for Talkspace providers by allowing them to send prescriptions directly to Amazon Pharmacy with one click and aims to improve medication adherence and access to care for mental health patients. The partnership builds on an earlier arrangement with Amazon Health Services and is expected to drive Talkspace's growth by increasing awareness and facilitating comprehensive care.

title: What’s Happening With IONQ Stock?
summary: IONQ stock has surged 400% in the last year, reflecting growing interest in quantum computing, despite the technology still being in a developmental stage. The company specializes in trapped-ion quantum computers, offering solutions like IonQ Forte and Aria, and plans to launch Tempo. While quantum computing holds immense promise for various industries, IonQ faces significant financial hurdles, including a small revenue base and operating losses, and its stock has shown high volatility compared to the broader market.

title: The Best Pellet Smoker Grill
summary: This article reviews the top pellet smoker grills, identifying the Camp Chef Woodwind Pro 24 as the best overall for its precision and robust smoke flavor. The Pit Boss Navigator 1300 is recommended as the budget-friendly option with ample cooking space, while the Traeger Woodridge suits those preferring milder smoke flavors and simpler functionality. The author extensively tested these grills, smoking various meats and detailing their features, performance, and maintenance.

title: Are Investors Undervaluing Maplebear Inc. (NASDAQ:CART) By 34%?
summary: Simply Wall St estimates Maplebear Inc. (NASDAQ:CART) is undervalued by 34%, with a projected fair value of US$69.12 based on a 2-stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, while its current share price is US$45.67. This fair value estimate is also 32% higher than analysts' average target of US$52.30. The analysis uses a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and highlights the importance of future cash flow projections and discount rates in valuation.

title: In brief: Best Buy restructures, McKesson spins off, BOC responds, women reign
summary: Best Buy is undergoing a major restructuring in its health business, incurring $109 million in charges. McKesson plans to spin off its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment to focus on oncology and biopharma, having recently acquired CORE Ventures for $2.49 billion. BOC faces a three-year pause on new accreditations in certain states due to a former contractor's misconduct, while Amazon Pharmacy has enhanced its services for medication management.

title: Interim Kohl's CEO Michael Bender outlines strategy for national retailer - Bizwomen
summary: Interim Kohl's CEO Michael Bender announced that the national retailer will largely continue with the existing strategies set by previous management. While the business is studying its approach, key focus areas include offering curated assortments, reestablishing Kohl's as a value and quality leader, and delivering a frictionless shopping experience. CFO Jill Timm emphasized the importance of value messaging for middle and low-income customers under financial pressure.

title: Will the MAG 7 Stocks Continue to Lead the Stock Market?
summary: The article discusses the continued influence of the "Magnificent Seven" (MAG 7) stocks—Microsoft, NVIDIA, Apple, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta Platforms, and Tesla—on the US stock market. It highlights their market capitalization leadership and their resilience following a market correction caused by tariff fears. The author suggests that the MAGS ETF offers a diversified way to invest in these leading technology companies and expects them to maintain their market leadership.

title: Top Active Managers Love These ETFs’ Holdings
summary: This article highlights that top active managers are keen on the holdings of ETFs like the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). Despite a 2025 pullback, these managers leveraged first-quarter retrenchment in mega-cap growth stocks like Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft, and Nvidia to increase their positions, signaling strong confidence in these companies' fundamental outlooks, especially in areas like cloud computing and advertising. The ETFs allocate about 28% of their rosters to these popular names, suggesting potential benefits for QQQ/QQQM investors.

title: Amazon.com (AMZN) Turns Up the Heat in CTV Ad Wars With Fresh DSP Discounts
summary: Amazon (AMZN) is intensifying competition in the Connected TV (CTV) advertising market by offering discounts on its Demand-Side Platform (DSP) to attract marketers. While Google is using ad credits for its DV360 service, Amazon's aggressive strategy aims to capitalize on the growing CTV advertising momentum. Despite strong competition from The Trade Desk, Amazon is closely watched by investors as it navigates the digital advertising landscape.

title: 1 ETF to Buy Hand Over Fist to Follow the TACO Trade
summary: The article discusses the "TACO" trade strategy, which involves buying into the market on the assumption that Donald Trump will reverse course on tariffs. It highlights the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) as a key ETF for this strategy due to its liquidity, the strong momentum of the consumer discretionary sector, and improved consumer confidence. The XLY ETF invests in U.S. consumer discretionary companies across various industries.

title: Cathie Wood Snaps Up Amazon and AMD Stocks but Offloads Palantir and CoreWeave
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs recently acquired significant shares of Amazon (AMZN) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), signaling confidence in these tech giants' growth potential despite market concerns. Conversely, ARK offloaded shares of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and CoreWeave (CRWV), with analysts noting valuation concerns for both. Wood also made other notable purchases including Guardant Health Inc. (GH) and 10X Genomics Inc. (TXG).

title: Walmart plans to expand drone deliveries to three more states
summary: Walmart announced plans to expand its drone delivery service to 100 stores in Atlanta, Charlotte, Houston, Orlando, and Tampa within the next year, bringing the total number of states offering the service to five. This expansion aims to enhance convenience for customers, who typically order urgent items like groceries or medicines. While Walmart and Amazon have had slow rollouts of drone delivery, Walmart has completed over 150,000 drone deliveries since 2021.

title: Amazon robotics program expansion to unlock billions in savings, boosting margins
summary: Amazon's growing investment in robotics, including the potential use of humanoid robots for package delivery, is expected to generate significant long-term cost savings and enhance margins, according to Bank of America analysts. They project over $7.1 billion in annual savings by 2032 from delivery robotics, excluding humanoid robots, and believe this strategy could lift Amazon's retail margins to 11%. This expansion also strengthens Amazon's position in AI beyond AWS to its retail operations.

title: eBay Charges Sellers Ad Fees Months After Sales In Apparent Attribution Snafu
summary: eBay is reportedly charging sellers ad fees months after sales have completed, which the company claims is due to a "system error" that missed previous attribution for promoted listings. This issue has led to frustration among sellers, who are questioning eBay's transparency and demanding refunds, especially given past instances of similar technical issues with fee collection. Critics suggest the multi-billion dollar corporation should absorb these costs rather than penalize sellers, urging those affected to report the issue to the Federal Trade Commission.

title: Why Is Everyone Talking About Coupang's Stock?
summary: Coupang, often called the "Amazon of South Korea," has gained significant attention from investors due to its strong performance, including a 25% stock price increase in the last year and consistent revenue growth. The company is known for its customer-centric approach, extensive logistics, and expansion into new services and geographies. Coupang's recent $1 billion share buyback plan also signals management's confidence in its future and commitment to long-term shareholder value.

title: Exclusive / Netflix buys Daily Beast TV pilot as it races against YouTube
summary: Netflix has acquired a pilot television series from The Daily Beast, produced with Bright North Studios, a move signaling its interest in quickly produced, topical documentary-style content. This initiative suggests Netflix is looking to compete more directly with YouTube for audience eyeballs, despite its CEO Ted Sarandos previously downplaying YouTube as a talent competitor. The potential success of The Daily Beast series could be a significant win for the publication's new leadership, which has focused on cost reduction and an editorial overhaul, leading to its recent profitability.

title: Maximus Updates CX Platform with AWS Integration
summary: Maximus has launched an updated version of its Total Experience Management (TXM) platform, now integrated with Amazon Web Services (AWS). This FedRAMP-authorized solution aims to help federal agencies modernize customer experience through AI-assisted interactions, intelligent document processing, and sentiment analysis, utilizing AWS services like Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Lex, and Amazon Textract. The platform has already demonstrated success in accelerating service delivery and boosting efficiency for agencies like the Office of Personnel Management, and the Department of Veterans Affairs.

title: Op-Ed: Why AI Companies Should Pay Media Organizations For Their Content
summary: The article argues that AI companies should pay media organizations for using their content, citing The New York Times' licensing deal with Amazon as a successful example. It highlights the shift from AI companies using copyrighted material without permission to paying for intellectual property, which creates accountability and helps publishers struggling with traffic. The author suggests that more artists and publishers should pursue similar deals to protect their work and ensure they are compensated for their contributions to AI training.

title: TOPT ETF: Historical Outperformance Can Continue (NYSEARCA:TOPT)
summary: The iShares Top 20 U.S. Stocks ETF (TOPT) provides concentrated exposure to the largest U.S. companies and has shown strong performance despite higher volatility. The author is bullish on TOPT's major holdings, believing the ETF is well-positioned to benefit from companies that leverage their scale. Consequently, the article rates TOPT as a Buy.

title: Progyny Joins Amazon’s Health Benefits Connector Program as First Women’s Health and Family Building Solution
summary: Progyny, a global leader in women’s health and family building solutions, announced its collaboration with Amazon’s Health Benefits Connector. This partnership aims to expand access for eligible Amazon customers to discover and enroll in Progyny's comprehensive women's health services, including fertility, family building, and menopause benefits. The initiative addresses the challenge of individuals not understanding their available health benefits, simplifying the process of accessing high-quality, inclusive care.

title: Untapped potential of AI in the insurance sector: Genpact
summary: A Genpact report, in collaboration with AWS, reveals that despite 69% AI adoption in the insurance sector, only 36% of US customers experience improved digital services, highlighting a significant gap in scaling AI for customer experience and growth. Challenges include low customer trust in AI-driven quotes, underutilization of AI for monetization, governance issues, data privacy concerns, and a talent gap in AI fluency among insurance executives. The report proposes a six-point strategic blueprint for insurers to effectively scale AI and build trust.

title: NVIDIA DGX Cloud Lepton Connects Europe’s Developers to Global NVIDIA Compute Ecosystem
summary: NVIDIA has expanded its DGX Cloud Lepton platform, a global compute marketplace for AI application developers, by welcoming new cloud providers including Mistral AI, Nebius, and Scaleway, in addition to AWS and Microsoft Azure. This expansion aims to increase access to high-performance NVIDIA Blackwell and other architecture GPUs in Europe while allowing data to remain local for sovereign AI requirements. To further support the AI ecosystem, Hugging Face is integrating DGX Cloud Lepton into its Training Cluster as a Service, and NVIDIA is partnering with European venture capital firms to offer marketplace credits to startups.

title: First stage of Amazon’s 4 GWh Californian solar-plus-storage site is built
summary: AES Corp has completed the first 1 GW solar plus 1 GW/2 GWh battery storage stage of the Bellefield project in California for Amazon. The second stage, which will double the capacity to 1 GW solar and 1 GW/4 GWh storage, is expected to be completed next year. This project is projected to be the largest solar-plus-storage facility in the United States, powering approximately 467,000 homes and offsetting over a million tons of carbon emissions annually.

title: Estée Lauder Launches in the Amazon.ca Premium Beauty Store
summary: Estée Lauder has officially launched its brand in the Amazon.ca Premium Beauty store, expanding access to its skincare, makeup, and fragrances for Canadian customers. This partnership brings hero products like Advanced Night Repair Serum and Double Wear Stay-In-Place foundation to a wider audience through a convenient online shopping experience. The move builds on a successful relationship with Amazon.com, allowing Prime members to enjoy fast, free delivery on Estée Lauder products in Canada.

title: Oracle's stock closes out best week since 2001 on cloud momentum
summary: Oracle's stock experienced its best week since 2001, surging 24% after a strong earnings report indicated significant momentum in its cloud business. The company's CEO, Safra Catz, provided revenue guidance exceeding consensus expectations, fueled by increasing demand for Oracle's cloud infrastructure, particularly from clients needing to operate AI models. Oracle is rapidly expanding its data center capacity to meet this demand, outpacing other tech megacaps in stock performance this year.

title: IonQ's Accelerated Roadmap: Turning Quantum Ambition into Reality
summary: IonQ is accelerating its quantum computing roadmap towards fault-tolerant systems, bolstered by strategic acquisitions of Lightsynq and an agreement to acquire Oxford Ionics. These acquisitions enhance IonQ's trapped ion technology, allowing for significantly increased qubit density and entanglement rates, and setting a path to achieve over 2 million physical qubits by 2030. The company is already demonstrating real-world applications in drug development, computational fluid dynamics, and AI, underscoring the practical and scalable potential of its quantum platform.

title: Synopsys Stock Falls on China Ban, But Long-Term Outlook Holds
summary: Synopsys (NASDAQ: SNPS) shares dropped after the Trump administration ordered a halt to EDA software sales to China, impacting about 10% of the company's revenue, though sales in the region were already declining. This ban also adds complexity to Synopsys's pending $35 billion acquisition of ANSYS (NASDAQ: ANSS), which still requires Chinese regulatory approval. Despite near-term uncertainty and a moderate "Hold" rating from analysts, the company's long-term outlook remains strong due to its indispensable role in chip design and the enduring secular trends in advanced chip development.

title: Could Amazon Become the Next Great Dividend Stock?
summary: Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) is financially capable of paying dividends, as evidenced by its strong first-quarter 2025 results, but the company currently prioritizes growth initiatives over dividend payments. While an Amazon dividend announcement is unlikely in the near future, investors seeking dividend-like income from AMZN might consider the YieldMax AMZN Option Income Strategy ETF (NYSEARCA:AMZY), which offers significant cash distributions through a covered call strategy, though it carries higher risks.

title: SK, AWS to build South Korea’s largest, multibillion-dollar AI data center
summary: SK Group and Amazon Web Services (AWS) are collaborating to build South Korea's largest AI data center, a 103-megawatt facility in Ulsan, equipped with 60,000 GPUs. This multibillion-dollar project, involving various SK subsidiaries and a significant investment from AWS, aims to establish a leading AI hub in Northeast Asia. The strategic location near SK Gas's power plant ensures a reliable energy supply, highlighting the growing competition in the AI computing market despite strong local players.

title: Apple supplier Jabil plans $500M venture for AI infrastructure
summary: Jabil, an electronics component maker and Apple supplier, is investing $500 million over the next several years to expand its cloud and artificial intelligence data center infrastructure in the Southeastern U.S. This investment includes large-scale production facilities expected to be operational by mid-2026. The move aligns with similar U.S. infrastructure investments by tech giants like Apple, Nvidia, and Foxconn, aimed at strengthening domestic manufacturing and diversifying Jabil's commercial portfolio to meet surging AI infrastructure demand.

title: Georgia’s PSC is in the game on data centers, Mississippi’s has been benched
summary: The article criticizes the Mississippi Public Service Commission (PSC) for being "benched" by the state's Governor and Legislature, allowing Entergy Mississippi to build data centers for Amazon with concessions that may lead to significant electricity rate increases for small customers. In contrast, Georgia's PSC has implemented rules to protect residential customers from similar costs associated with data center development. The author warns Mississippi legislators about potential voter backlash due to unchecked rate increases.

title: ‘매그 7’을 넘어서: 테마 ETF가 AI 투자의 다음 물결을 이끌 수 있을까?

summary: Brandy Melville has filed a federal lawsuit against Temu, accusing the e-commerce platform of systematically selling counterfeit versions of its apparel and infringing on its trademarks and copyrights. The lawsuit challenges Temu's claim of being a neutral marketplace, alleging that Temu controls nearly every aspect of its sellers' activities, including product listings and pricing, and actively promotes infringing goods. Brandy Melville seeks injunctions, destruction of counterfeit inventory, and an accounting of Temu's profits from these alleged illicit sales, potentially reshaping how courts view marketplace liability for intellectual property infringement.

title: Nestlé considers selling supplement assets
summary: Nestlé is considering divesting some of its vitamins, minerals, and supplements (VMS) brands acquired under the Bountiful Company in 2021 as part of a strategic review aimed at improving "sluggish" performance and cutting costs. The company plans to focus its vitamin business on higher-end products like Garden of Life, Pure Encapsulations, and Solgar, while shedding brands such as Nature’s Bounty, Osteo Bi-Flex, and Puritan’s Pride due to weaker performance and a competitive "race to the bottom" in the mainstream supplement market. This move follows a $5.75 billion acquisition and a previous FTC complaint against Bountiful for deceptive review practices.

The company is actively implementing AI across its operations for grid modernization, predictive maintenance, and efficient management of renewable energy, distinguishing its approach from competitors focused on more siloed AI applications.

The brand aims to balance modern messaging with the glamour consumers desire, following last year's relaunched show which featured a diverse cast and emphasized comfort over traditional sexiness, while also integrating shoppable elements with Amazon. Despite recent efforts to regain market share and cater to a female gaze, customer comments indicate a strong desire for the "old glamour" and iconic wings from previous shows.

summary: United Parcel Service (UPS) held its Q2 2025 earnings call, discussing Q2 financial results, strategic actions, and outlook. The company reported consolidated revenue of $21.2 billion and operating profit of $1.9 billion, with a focus on improving revenue quality and managing challenges like decreased Ground Saver volume and lower-than-anticipated employee attrition.
