summary: Etsy is focusing on its mobile app and leveraging AI to combat persistent declines in its flagship marketplace. In Q2, the company saw a 5.4% year-over-year drop in gross merchandise sales and a 4.6% decrease in active buyers, though its Depop marketplace performed better. summary: SAP announced its plan to acquire San Francisco-based HR startup SmartRecruiters for an undisclosed fee. SmartRecruiters specializes in AI-powered applicant tracking and recruitment analytics, which SAP intends to integrate into its SuccessFactors platform to enhance hiring and workforce planning capabilities. While the term is largely used in jest, it highlights ongoing anxieties about technological advancements and the increasing presence of AI and robotics.

summary: Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson's proposed 2026 budget includes an increase to the city's cloud tax, raising it from 11% to 14%. The tax hike is projected to generate $333 million and is seen as a short-sighted solution to the city's financial challenges, failing to address underlying issues like rising pension obligations.

summary: Amazon has surpassed Walmart in annual revenue for the first time, ending Walmart's 13-year reign atop the Fortune 500 list, with Amazon generating $716.9 billion compared to Walmart's $713.2 billion. While Amazon is known for reshaping e-commerce, its diversified business approach, including its Prime media ecosystem, played a significant role in this growth. Amazon's faster growth rate, roughly three times Walmart's between 2018 and 2025, ultimately closed the revenue gap.

title: Amazon Fails to Stop Reinstated Teen Suicide Substance Claims
summary: The Washington Supreme Court has reinstated negligence claims against Amazon Inc., ruling that families of teens who died by suicide after buying a toxic substance on its platform can sue. The court stated that suicide was not a superseding cause precluding claims under Washington's Product Liability Act, and Amazon had a duty of care in selling sodium nitrite. This decision follows a surge in suicides facilitated by sodium nitrite ingestion after 2018, leading to numerous lawsuits against the retailer.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Price Targets Cut as $200 Billion Capex Plan Weighs on Sentiment
summary: Amazon's stock price targets have been cut by Benchmark and Piper Sandler following the company's announcement of a $200 billion capital expenditure plan, which is $40 billion more than anticipated. Despite this, Amazon's AWS cloud segment continues to show strong growth and efficiency, and the company remains focused on cost reduction and AI investment. The market reacted subdued to the investment estimates, but analysts acknowledge Amazon's continued strategic direction.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Price Targets Cut as $200 Billion Capex Plan Weighs on Sentiment
summary: Amazon's stock price targets have been cut by Benchmark and Piper Sandler following the company's announcement of a $200 billion capital expenditure plan, which is $40 billion more than anticipated. Despite the increased spending, Amazon's AWS cloud segment showed significant growth, and the company continues to focus on retail efficiency and AI investments. Analysts believe certain other AI stocks might offer better upside potential with less risk for investors.

title: With 62% institutional ownership, Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) is a favorite amongst the big guns
summary: Institutional investors hold a significant 62% ownership in Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN), indicating their confidence in the company's future and potentially influencing its stock price. While insiders retain an 11% stake, with Jeffrey Bezos as the largest individual shareholder, the diverse ownership by a large number of institutional investors suggests that no single entity holds a majority interest. This structure implies that decisions are not concentrated but also highlights the potential for collective impact from institutional trading activities.

title: E-commerce giant Amazon inks huge lease in West Valley
summary: Amazon (Nasdaq: AMZN) has secured a significant 1.2 million-square-foot industrial lease for 10 years at The Cubes at Glendale industrial park in metro Phoenix. This move expands Amazon's already substantial presence in the Valley, joining other major tenants like Williams-Sonoma Inc. in the developing 5.5 million-square-foot industrial project. The site is in an industrial hotbed alongside major employers such as Nestle, Red Bull, White Claw, and Walmart.

title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Named Top AI Pick as Morgan Stanley Sees AWS Acceleration and Agentic Upside
summary: Morgan Stanley has named Amazon (AMZN) a top AI pick, reiterating an "Overweight" rating and a $300 price target, seeing its AWS and Retail divisions as underappreciated GenAI winners. The firm anticipates AWS revenue growth to exceed 30%, supported by strong enterprise demand and increased capital expenditures. Additionally, Agentic Commerce and AI-powered shopping assistants are expected to drive further upside, despite the stock trading at a discount compared to peers.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Feels Misunderstood, Says Jim Cramer
summary: Jim Cramer believes Amazon (AMZN) feels misunderstood, especially concerning its negative free cash flow and significant capital expenditures, which analysts have questioned. Despite strong operating income and AWS growth, firms like Bernstein and Benchmark have reduced price targets for Amazon shares due to these concerns. Cramer emphasizes his strong position in Amazon, while also noting the public perception issues around data centers.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Navigates Investment Cycle and Strategic Developments
summary: Amazon (AMZN) is undertaking a significant investment cycle, increasing capital spending by over 50% to nearly $200 billion to expand AWS and meet AI demand. Moody's Ratings revised its outlook on Amazon to stable from positive due to these expenditures, which are expected to weigh on cash generation, though the company's underlying operations and financial strategies remain strong. Additionally, Amazon-backed X-energy's fuel subsidiary received a US NRC license to commercially manufacture HALEU nuclear fuel, marking a notable development for the company's diversified interests.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Stock Drops as $200B AI Spending Plan Drives AWS Optimism
summary: Amazon shares fell 2.54% following news of its planned $200 billion capital expenditure by 2026, primarily for AI data centers to support AWS growth. Despite the drop, AWS's strong financial performance with $128.7 billion in 2025 sales and significant advertising revenue provide a positive outlook, supported by CEO Andy Jassy's confidence in monetizing the AI infrastructure investment. Investor attention also focuses on new institutional holdings, strategic expansion in India, and the impact of evolving trade policies.

title: Amazon Pledges $12 Billion For Louisiana Data Centers, Aims To Create Over 2,000 Jobs
summary: Amazon announced a $12 billion investment in northwest Louisiana for data centers, expected to create 540 full-time and 1,710 full-time equivalent jobs. This expansion continues Amazon's aggressive investment in data center infrastructure to support growing AI demand, despite Wall Street's skepticism over related capital expenditures. The company is collaborating with Southwestern Electric Power Company (SWEPCO) to finance necessary energy infrastructure.

title: Is Amazon.com Stock Underperforming the Dow?
summary: Amazon.com (AMZN) stock has underperformed the Dow Jones Industrial Average over the past three months, year-to-date, and the last 52 weeks, despite its status as a mega-cap company. This underperformance is largely attributed to investor concerns over its aggressive spending plans, especially a significant investment in AI infrastructure and cloud expansion in 2026. Despite these concerns, Wall Street analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating for AMZN, projecting a substantial upside.

title: Amazon Stock Just Flipped Again: What Smart Money Is Doing Now
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has released new financial data, prompting Wall Street to reassess the stock. This analysis delves into recent changes, what professional investors are doing, and the implications for everyday Amazon users and investors, emphasizing its diversified revenue streams, particularly in cloud services and advertising, alongside its retail dominance. The article highlights Amazon's strategic shift towards profitability and AI investment, discussing expert opinions on both its strengths and potential risks.

title: Silicon Sovereignty: Bank of America Reaffirms Amazon ‘Buy’ Rating as Custom Chips Power AWS Re-Acceleration
summary: Bank of America has reiterated its "Buy" rating on Amazon, designating it as a top pick for 2026 with a bullish price target of $303. This endorsement is driven by the significant re-acceleration of Amazon Web Services (AWS) revenue and the rapid adoption of Amazon's proprietary AI chips, Trainium and Inferentia. The report positions Amazon as an AI powerhouse, challenging traditional semiconductor firms and poised for continued growth through its vertically integrated strategy and substantial capital expenditure plan.

title: HORAN Wealth LLC Boosts Holdings in Amazon.com, Inc. $AMZN
summary: HORAN Wealth LLC significantly increased its stake in Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) by 216.8% in the third quarter, acquiring 13,332 additional shares to reach a total of 19,482 shares valued at approximately $4.28 million. This move is part of broader institutional investor activity, with hedge funds and other institutional investors now controlling 72.20% of Amazon's stock. Analysts currently rate Amazon as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $287.29, driven by factors like AWS capacity expansion and a $12 billion data-center commitment, despite concerns over substantial AI-related capital expenditure and recent insider selling.

title: SBI Securities Co. Ltd. Reduces Holdings in Amazon.com, Inc. $AMZN
summary: SBI Securities Co. Ltd. has reduced its stake in Amazon.com, Inc. by 4.8% in the third quarter, now holding 400,404 shares valued at approximately $87.9 million. The report also highlights recent insider selling from Amazon's CEO and other executives, totaling over $15 million in the last 90 days. Despite a mixed outlook influenced by AWS expansion plans and ongoing debates about AI/data-center capital expenditure, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the company.

title: Amazon Plans In-House AI Chips to Cut Costs
summary: Amazon (AMZN) intends to utilize its proprietary Trainium and Inferentia processors for developing artificial intelligence models. This move aims to significantly reduce costs associated with AI technology development, as revealed in a Wall Street Journal report, and is part of a broader strategy to gain more control over its AI technology stack and manage expenses for large-scale model training and deployment. The company plans to dedicate a substantial portion of its $200 billion capital expenditure in 2026 towards AI, processors, and related infrastructure.

title: Amazon.com (AMZN) Data Center Push Continues with $12B Investment in Louisiana
summary: Amazon.com (AMZN) plans to invest $12 billion in northwest Louisiana to build data center campuses, creating 540 full-time jobs. This move supports cloud computing technologies and aligns with the company's expected capital expenditures of $200 billion for the year. Wells Fargo reiterated an Overweight rating on AMZN but lowered its price target to $304, citing anticipated growth in hyperscaler capacity due to AI-driven demand.

title: Guinness Asset Management LTD Trims Stock Position in Amazon.com, Inc. $AMZN
summary: Guinness Asset Management LTD reduced its stake in Amazon.com, Inc. by 4.6% in the third quarter, selling 10,070 shares and ending with 208,983 shares valued at $46.43 million. Meanwhile, Amazon is investing heavily in AI and cloud strategy, including a potential $50 billion commitment to OpenAI and expanding AWS data centers. Insider selling has occurred, yet analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with a price target of $287.29.

title: Trace Wealth Advisors LLC Trims Stake in Amazon.com, Inc. $AMZN
summary: Trace Wealth Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in Amazon.com, Inc. by 58.3% in the third quarter, selling 6,753 shares and now holding 4,832 shares worth $1.06 million. Despite this, other major institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc. and State Street Corp increased their holdings in Amazon. The article also provides a summary of recent news impacting Amazon, including positive sentiment around its investment in OpenAI and expanding data center capacity, alongside negative sentiment regarding massive near-term spending and legal risks in the UK.
